  tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Carrier assignments can be to 3PL providers for outsourced legs (distinct from asset carriers and intermediary agents). Enables 3PL assignment tracking, performance measurement, cost allocation, and c']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Carrier assignments specify the exact vehicle/container executing the shipment. Essential for execution tracking, carrier performance measurement, and asset utilization. vehicle_number and container_n']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: A carrier assignment is operationally executed within a fleet trip. Linking enables trip-level carrier cost aggregation, on-time performance per trip, and operational reconciliation between carrier bo']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier arrived at the destination with the shipment.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the carrier departed with the shipment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date on which the carrier was assigned to this shipment leg.']
  assignment_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the carrier was assigned to this shipment leg. Auto-optimized indicates algorithmic carrier selection via SAP TM or Oracle TMS; manual override indicates planner intervention; contract based indicates assignment per existing carrier contract; spot market indicates ad-hoc market rate booking; tender indicates competitive bidding process; preferred carrier indicates selection from preferred carrier list.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the carrier assignment. Confirmed indicates the carrier has accepted the booking; tentative indicates provisional assignment pending confirmation; cancelled indicates the assignment was revoked; pending indicates awaiting carrier response; rejected indicates carrier declined; expired indicates assignment lapsed without confirmation.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the carrier assignment was created in the system.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier assignment was cancelled.']
  carrier_iata_code varchar [note: 'IATA two or three-character airline designator code for air carriers.']
  carrier_performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Performance rating assigned to the carrier for this shipment leg, typically on a scale of 0.00 to 5.00. Used for carrier scorecard and future selection optimization.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code uniquely identifying the carrier in North American freight systems. 2-4 uppercase letters.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the carrier confirmed acceptance of the booking.']
  contract_rate_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rate applied is a contracted rate (true) or a spot market rate (false).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier assignment record was first created in the system.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or container used by the carrier (e.g., 20ft container, 40ft container, refrigerated, flatbed, dry van, tanker).']
  estimated_arrival_date date [note: 'Estimated date of arrival for this carrier leg as provided by the carrier.']
  estimated_departure_date date [note: 'Estimated date of departure for this carrier leg as provided by the carrier.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number assigned by the airline for air freight transport.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) or fuel surcharge applied by the carrier to account for fuel cost fluctuations.']
  mode_of_transport varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this carrier assignment (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal, courier).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this carrier assignment, including special instructions, exceptions, or operational remarks.']
  on_time_delivery_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier delivered the shipment on time per the agreed service level agreement (true) or late (false).']
  rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Agreed freight rate amount charged by the carrier for this shipment leg. Base rate before surcharges and adjustments.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  rate_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the rate (e.g., per kilogram, per cubic meter, per TEU, per shipment, per pallet, per mile, per kilometer). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_kg|per_cbm|per_teu|per_shipment|per_pallet|per_mile|per_km — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or trailer to ensure cargo integrity.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of carrier service: Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), parcel, express, standard, or economy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ftl|ltl|fcl|lcl|parcel|express|standard|economy — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  total_carrier_charge decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total amount charged by the carrier for this shipment leg, including base rate, fuel surcharge, and other carrier fees.']
  train_number varchar [note: 'Train service number assigned for rail freight transport.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier assignment record was last modified in the system.']
  vessel_imo_number varchar [note: 'Unique seven-digit IMO number assigned to the vessel for identification and tracking.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ocean vessel assigned for sea freight transport.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage or sailing number for ocean freight, identifying the specific vessel journey.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of a carrier (airline, ocean carrier, road haulier, rail operator) to a specific shipment leg. Captures carrier SCAC/IATA code, carrier name, service product code, booking reference number with the carrier, assigned vehicle/vessel/flight number, assignment date, assignment method (auto-optimized via SAP TM carrier selection / manual override), rate agreed, and assignment status (confirmed/tentative/cancelled). Enables carrier performance tracking, freight audit, and carrier spend analysis per shipment.'
}

Table shipment.delivery_instruction {
  delivery_instruction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery instruction record. Primary key.']
  alternative_address_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address_book. Business justification: Alternative delivery addresses in delivery instructions should reference validated entries in the customer address_book to ensure address accuracy, enable reuse of pre-validated addresses, and support']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment or consignment to which this delivery instruction applies. Links delivery preferences to the specific shipment being transported.']
  customer_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Delivery instructions frequently specify customer contact person for access coordination, delivery authorization, and issue resolution. Normalizes denormalized contact fields. Critical for last-mile d']
  delivery_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Delivery instructions are executed by the assigned delivery agent. Linking enables agent-specific instruction compliance tracking, authority-to-leave policy enforcement by agent territory, and last-mi']
  last_mile_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.last_mile_dispatch. Business justification: Delivery instructions (safe place, signature required, access codes) must be accessible to last-mile dispatch operations. Linking delivery_instruction to last_mile_dispatch normalizes the plain-text d']
  authority_to_leave_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery driver has authority to leave the shipment unattended at the delivery location without obtaining a signature. True if authority is granted, False otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery instruction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  delivery_notification_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred method for receiving delivery notifications. Specifies whether the consignee wants to be notified via SMS, email, phone call, mobile app push notification, or no notification.']
  id_verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether government-issued identification must be verified at delivery. Typically required for age-restricted or high-value shipments.']
  instruction_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this delivery instruction becomes effective. Supports future-dated instructions and instruction scheduling.']
  instruction_expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this delivery instruction expires and is no longer valid. Nullable for instructions with no expiration.']
  instruction_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this delivery instruction. High priority instructions take precedence in case of conflicting instructions or operational constraints.']
  instruction_source varchar [note: 'The origin or channel through which the delivery instruction was captured. Indicates whether the instruction came from the shipper, consignee, customer portal, call center, mobile app, or Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) system.']
  instruction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery instruction. Tracks whether the instruction is active, has been cancelled, completed, superseded by a newer instruction, or is pending activation.']
  instruction_type varchar [note: 'The type of delivery instruction specified by the shipper or consignee. Defines the primary delivery handling requirement for last-mile execution.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user, system, or process that last modified this delivery instruction record. Supports audit and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this delivery instruction record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  locker_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parcel locker or automated pickup point where the shipment should be delivered if locker delivery is selected.']
  locker_location_code varchar [note: 'Geographic or facility code identifying the location of the parcel locker. Used for routing and last-mile optimization.']
  minimum_age_requirement varchar [note: 'Minimum age of the recipient required for delivery. Used for age-restricted goods such as alcohol, tobacco, or pharmaceuticals.']
  neighbour_delivery_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment can be delivered to a neighbor if the consignee is not available. True if neighbor delivery is permitted, False otherwise.']
  preferred_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the consignee prefers to receive the delivery. Used for appointment scheduling and delivery planning.']
  preferred_neighbour_address varchar [note: 'Address of a preferred neighbor to whom the shipment can be delivered if the consignee is unavailable.']
  preferred_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'The end of the preferred delivery time window specified by the consignee. Defines the latest acceptable time for delivery within the preferred window.']
  preferred_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'The start of the preferred delivery time window specified by the consignee. Enables time-definite delivery services and appointment-based deliveries.']
  return_if_absent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment should be returned to the depot or origin if the consignee is not present. True if return is required, False if alternative delivery options should be attempted.']
  safe_place_description varchar [note: 'Description of a safe place where the parcel can be left if the consignee is not present. Examples include porch, garage, side door, or with a specific neighbor.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a signature is required upon delivery. True if signature is mandatory, False if delivery can be completed without signature.']
  signature_type varchar [note: 'The type of signature required for delivery. Specifies whether the consignee must sign, any adult can sign, an indirect signature is acceptable, or an electronic signature is permitted.']
  special_access_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions provided by the consignee for accessing the delivery location. May include gate codes, building entry procedures, parking instructions, or security protocols.']
  special_handling_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or special handling instructions for the delivery. May include fragile handling, temperature requirements, or other consignee-specific requests.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this delivery instruction record. Incremented each time the instruction is modified, supporting optimistic locking and change history.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user, system, or process that created this delivery instruction record. May be a user ID, system name, or API client identifier.']

  Note: 'Shipper or consignee-specified delivery instructions associated with a consignment. Captures instruction type (LEAVE_AT_DOOR, REQUIRE_SIGNATURE, DELIVER_TO_NEIGHBOUR, LOCKER_DELIVERY, APPOINTMENT_REQUIRED, CALL_BEFORE_DELIVERY, RETURN_IF_ABSENT), preferred delivery time window (start/end), special access instructions, safe place description, alternative delivery address, contact phone for delivery, and instruction source (shipper/consignee/customer portal). Enables last-mile delivery agents to execute D2D delivery per customer preferences and reduces failed delivery attempts.'
}

  Note: 'Shipment-level charge record capturing all freight charges, surcharges, accessorial fees, and customs duty references applied to a consignment. Captures charge code, amount, currency, tax, and any linked customs declaration references.'

  flight_number varchar [note: 'Airline flight number for air freight manifests, combining carrier code and flight number (e.g., AA1234).']
  gps_tracking_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time GPS tracking is enabled for this manifest via FourKites or other visibility platforms.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this manifest record was last updated or modified.']
  load_plan_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the load optimization plan generated by Oracle TMS or SAP TM that produced this manifest.']
  manifest_closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manifest was closed and locked for departure, preventing further additions or changes.']
  manifest_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manifest was marked as completed after successful delivery and unloading at destination.']
  manifest_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total transportation cost allocated to this manifest, including carrier charges, fuel surcharges, and accessorial fees.']
  manifest_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the manifest cost amount.']
  manifest_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this manifest record was first created in the TMS or WMS system.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique manifest number assigned by the carrier or TMS for this consolidated load. Used for carrier handover documentation and regulatory compliance.']
  manifest_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the manifest indicating its operational state from creation through completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|open|closed|departed|in_transit|arrived|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the manifest load is expected to arrive at the destination facility.']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time when the manifest load is planned to depart from the origin facility.']
  predicted_eta_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Real-time predicted arrival timestamp generated by visibility platforms such as FourKites based on GPS tracking and predictive analytics.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique seal number applied by the carrier to secure the container or trailer, used for tamper detection and security compliance (C-TPAT, AEO).']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this manifest includes temperature-sensitive cargo requiring refrigerated or climate-controlled transport.']
  total_chargeable_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total chargeable weight for billing purposes, calculated as the greater of actual weight or dimensional (DIM) weight, summed across all consignments.']
  total_consignments varchar [note: 'Total number of individual consignments (AWBs, BOLs, HAWBs) consolidated into this manifest.']
  total_pieces varchar [note: 'Total number of individual pieces, packages, or handling units loaded onto this manifest across all consignments.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of all consignments on this manifest, measured in cubic meters, used for load optimization and capacity planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of all consignments on this manifest, measured in kilograms.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this manifest (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal, or parcel express).']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ocean vessel or aircraft carrying this manifest for sea or air freight.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Vessel voyage identifier for ocean freight manifests, assigned by the shipping line.']

  Note: 'Freight manifest record consolidating all consignments loaded onto a specific transport vehicle, vessel, or aircraft for a given departure. Captures manifest number, transport mode, vehicle/vessel/flight reference, origin facility, destination facility, departure date, total pieces, total weight, total CBM, number of consignments, dangerous goods indicator, temperature-controlled indicator, manifest status (open/closed/departed/arrived), and carrier seal number. Sourced from SAP TM and Oracle TMS load optimization outputs. Enables load reconciliation and carrier handover documentation.'
}

Business justification: Shipping documents (commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin) are generated per fulfillment order and required for customs clearance and merchant document retrieval. Compliance teams an']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Shipment documents of type IMPORT_LICENSE or EXPORT_PERMIT correspond to license_permit records. Compliance verification workflows require direct document→license_permit linkage to validate permit uti']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Shipping documents (commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading) are generated against purchase orders in international trade. Customs compliance and trade finance require tracing documents to or']
  shipment_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to shipment.shipment_leg. Business justification: Transport and trade documents are frequently leg-specific in multi-modal shipments — customs declarations, airway bills, bills of lading, and carrier-specific documents apply to individual legs rather']
  superseded_by_document_shipment_document_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment_document_id of the newer version that supersedes this document. Nullable if this is the current version.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the document was approved or accepted by the reviewing authority (customs, carrier, compliance officer). Nullable if not yet approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the document meets all regulatory and carrier compliance requirements. True=compliant, False=non-compliant or pending review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this shipment document record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the document in the shipment workflow. Pending=awaiting upload or review, Submitted=sent to customs or carrier, Approved=accepted by authority, Rejected=not accepted, Expired=past validity date, Cancelled=voided by shipper or system.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the document expires or is no longer valid for use in customs clearance or carrier acceptance. Nullable for documents without expiration. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format or MIME type of the stored document. PDF=Portable Document Format, JPEG/PNG/TIFF=image formats, XML=structured data, EDI=Electronic Data Interchange message.']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the document file in bytes. Used for storage management and upload validation.']
  file_uri varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) or file path pointing to the stored digital copy of the document in the document management system or cloud storage. Confidential as it may expose internal storage architecture.']
  hash varchar [note: 'Cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256) of the document file content, used for integrity verification and tamper detection.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the document was officially issued or certified by the issuing authority. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  issuing_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization, government agency, or entity that issued or certified the document (e.g., Chamber of Commerce, Ministry of Agriculture, Insurance Company).']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the document was issued.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language in which the document is written (e.g., en=English, es=Spanish, zh=Chinese).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this shipment document record was last updated or modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  mandatory_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether this document is mandatory for the shipment to proceed (e.g., commercial invoice for customs clearance). True=mandatory, False=optional or supplementary.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'The unique reference or control number assigned to this document by the issuing authority or system. Used for tracking and retrieval.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for document rejection. INCOMPLETE=missing required fields, EXPIRED=document past validity, INVALID=does not meet regulatory requirements, MISMATCH=data does not match shipment details, ILLEGIBLE=poor quality scan, UNAUTHORIZED=issuer not recognized.']
  rejection_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the reviewing authority detailing why the document was rejected and what corrective action is required.']
  rejection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the document was rejected by the reviewing authority. Nullable if not rejected. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  retention_expiry_date date [note: 'The date after which the document may be archived or purged according to regulatory retention policies (e.g., 7 years for customs records). Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  reviewed_by_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the customs office, carrier, or compliance authority that reviewed and approved or rejected the document.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the document was officially submitted to customs, carrier, or other external authority for review. Nullable if not yet submitted. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  translation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the document requires translation into the destination country language for customs clearance or carrier acceptance. True=translation required, False=no translation needed.']
  type_code varchar [note: 'Classification code identifying the type of document. CINV=Commercial Invoice, PLIST=Packing List, COO=Certificate of Origin, PHYTO=Phytosanitary Certificate, DGM=Dangerous Goods Manifest, INSC=Insurance Certificate. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CINV|PLIST|COO|PHYTO|DGM|INSC|LOC|EXPL|IMPP|CUST|HAWB|MAWB|BOL|AWB|POD|CMR|TIR — promote to reference product]']
  upload_source varchar [note: 'The channel or system through which the document was uploaded or received. Shipper_portal=customer web portal, EDI=Electronic Data Interchange, API=Application Programming Interface integration, Email=email attachment, Manual=manual entry by operations, Carrier_system=received from carrier system.']
  upload_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the document was uploaded or received into the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the document if it has been revised or resubmitted. Starts at 1 for the original submission and increments with each revision.']

  Note: 'Registry of all transport and trade documents associated with a consignment beyond the primary AWB/BOL. Captures document type (commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin, phytosanitary certificate, dangerous goods manifest, insurance certificate, letter of credit, export license, import permit), document reference number, issuing authority, issue date, expiry date, document file URI, upload source, and document status (pending/submitted/approved/rejected). Provides a complete document envelope for a shipment enabling customs compliance and carrier acceptance checks.'
}

Table shipment.manifest_line {
  manifest_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this manifest line record. Primary key.']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the consignment being loaded onto this manifest']
  freight_manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the freight manifest on which this consignment is loaded']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this manifest line record was first created in the TMS.']
  handling_unit_type varchar [note: 'Type of handling unit used for this consignment on this manifest (e.g., pallet, cage, loose, container). Relevant for load planning and warehouse operations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this manifest line record was last updated or modified.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number indicating the order in which this consignment was loaded onto the manifest. Used for load planning and unload sequencing.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this manifest line indicating operational state: planned (scheduled for loading), loaded (physically loaded), departed (manifest departed), in_transit (en route), arrived (manifest arrived), delivered (consignment delivered from this leg), exception (issue detected).']
  loaded_pieces varchar [note: 'Number of pieces from this consignment actually loaded onto this manifest. May differ from consignment total pieces in multi-leg scenarios where a consignment is split across manifests.']
  loaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this consignment was physically loaded onto the manifest. Captured from WMS or TMS scan events.']
  loaded_volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual volume in cubic meters (CBM) of this consignment loaded onto this manifest. Used for load optimization and capacity planning.']
  loaded_weight_kg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual weight in kilograms of this consignment loaded onto this manifest. Used for load reconciliation and carrier billing.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating any special handling requirements for this consignment on this manifest leg (e.g., fragile, temperature-controlled, hazmat, priority).']
  unloaded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this consignment was unloaded from the manifest at the destination facility. Captured from WMS or TMS scan events.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the loading record between a freight manifest and a consignment. It captures the operational act of loading a specific consignment onto a specific manifest, including line-level loading details such as sequence, loaded pieces, weight, volume, and line status. Each record links one freight manifest to one consignment with attributes that exist only in the context of this loading relationship. This is a core operational entity in TMS and freight systems, enabling load reconciliation, carrier handover documentation, and multi-leg shipment tracking.. Existence Justification: In freight logistics operations, manifest consolidation is a core operational process where multiple consignments are physically loaded onto a single transport vehicle, vessel, or aircraft (the manifest). Reciprocally, a single consignment can appear on multiple manifests across its multi-leg journey (e.g., origin terminal → hub → destination terminal). The manifest line is a recognized operational entity in TMS and WMS systems, representing the loading record with line-level data such as sequence, loaded pieces, weight, volume, and status that belongs to neither the manifest nor the consignment alone, but to the specific loading relationship.'
}

Table customer.customer_account {
  customer_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the Transport Shipping logistics platform. Primary key for the customer_account data product in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  parent_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent customer account record, enabling hierarchical B2B enterprise account structures (e.g., subsidiary accounts rolling up to a global parent). Null for top-level accounts. Supports consolidated billing and group-level SLA management.']
  preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Account-level preferred carrier drives automated shipment routing and rate application in TMS. Account managers configure this for volume-based carrier negotiations. A logistics domain expert expects ']
  preferred_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Strategic customers negotiate preferred lanes for their shipments. Drives TMS routing decisions, contract-specific lane assignments, and dedicated capacity allocation. Essential for customer-specific ']
  preferred_service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: Enterprise customers negotiate corridor-level service agreements for regional trade lanes. Required for corridor capacity reservations, multi-leg service commitments, and strategic account corridor pe']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalizes the customer segmentation classification. Currently customer_account has account_segment as a STRING (likely storing segment_code). Adding customer_segment_id FK allows proper referential i']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric account number assigned to the customer at onboarding. Printed on invoices, Air Waybills (AWBs), and Bills of Lading (BOLs) as the billing reference. Unique across the logistics network.']
  account_source varchar [note: 'Channel or method through which the customer account was originally created: Salesforce CRM opportunity conversion, web portal self-registration, EDI partner onboarding, manual entry by operations team, data migration from legacy system, or partner referral.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the customer account. \'active\' indicates the account is in good standing and can generate shipments; \'suspended\' indicates a temporary hold (e.g., credit breach); \'closed\' indicates the account has been permanently deactivated; \'pending\' indicates onboarding in progress; \'prospect\' indicates a pre-onboarding opportunity from Salesforce CRM.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial segmentation tier assigned to the customer account based on revenue contribution, shipment volume, and strategic value. Determines priority handling, dedicated account management, and access to premium services such as guaranteed capacity and priority customs clearance.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the customer account into its primary commercial relationship category: B2B (enterprise shipper), B2C (individual sender), 3PL (Third-Party Logistics provider), 4PL (Fourth-Party Logistics provider), CONSIGNEE (receiving party), or BROKER (customs/freight broker). Drives SLA entitlements, pricing tiers, and service eligibility.']
  aeo_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official AEO certificate number issued by the customs authority to the customer. Required for customs declaration pre-population in Descartes Customs GTM and for claiming AEO mutual recognition benefits on cross-border shipments. Null if aeo_certified is False.']
  aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer holds a valid Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification issued by a customs authority. AEO-certified customers benefit from expedited customs clearance, reduced inspection rates, and mutual recognition benefits under WCO SAFE Framework agreements.']
  api_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has an active API integration for real-time shipment booking, tracking, and rate queries via Transport Shipping\'s logistics platform. API-enabled accounts can connect their ERP or e-commerce systems directly.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the customer\'s registered billing address (street number and name). Used for invoice generation, tax compliance, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the customer\'s registered billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination, freight rate zone assignment, and regulatory reporting.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the customer\'s registered billing address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives VAT/GST applicability, customs compliance rules, trade sanctions screening, and currency assignment.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the customer\'s registered billing address. Used for freight rate zone calculation, tax jurisdiction mapping, and address validation.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer account was permanently closed. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis, regulatory data retention scheduling, and financial period-end reconciliation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer account record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Salesforce CRM Account CreatedDate. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR processing records.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit and outstanding balance are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency accounts.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance permitted for this customer account in the account\'s billing currency. Enforced by SAP S/4HANA Finance credit management module. Exceeding this limit triggers automatic shipment booking blocks.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current credit standing of the customer account as assessed by the Finance team in SAP S/4HANA. \'approved\' allows standard credit terms; \'on_hold\' blocks new shipment bookings pending payment; \'suspended\' indicates credit facility withdrawn; \'under_review\' indicates active credit reassessment; \'cash_only\' requires prepayment for all services.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'External account identifier sourced from Salesforce CRM Account object. Used to trace the master record back to the system of record and support bi-directional synchronisation with Salesforce.']
  ctpat_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is a validated member of the US Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism (C-TPAT) program. C-TPAT membership enables expedited US Customs and Border Protection processing and reduced examination rates for US-bound shipments.']
  ddp_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is approved for Delivered Duty Paid (DDP) shipments, where Transport Shipping assumes responsibility for import duties and taxes on behalf of the customer. Requires credit approval and customs power of attorney on file.']
  denied_party_screen_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent denied party and sanctions screening was performed for this customer account in Descartes Customs GTM. Used to enforce periodic re-screening compliance and audit trail requirements.']
  denied_party_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has passed the most recent denied party and sanctions screening check in Descartes Customs GTM. Must be True before any shipment booking is permitted. Automatically reset to False when screening expires or a watchlist update occurs.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) number uniquely identifying the customer\'s legal business entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, supplier onboarding, and enterprise account hierarchy resolution.']
  edi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account is configured for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) integration for automated shipment booking, Advanced Shipping Notices (ASN), and status updates. EDI-capable accounts bypass manual data entry workflows.']
  eori_number varchar [note: 'Economic Operators Registration and Identification (EORI) number assigned by EU customs authorities to the customer for cross-border trade within the European Union. Required on all EU import/export customs declarations filed via Descartes Customs GTM.']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The primary industry sector of the customer\'s business (e.g., Automotive, Retail, Pharmaceutical, Technology, Perishables, Energy). Used for industry-specific SLA configuration, dangerous goods handling rules, and targeted sales analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Automotive|Retail|Pharmaceutical|Technology|Perishables|Energy|Aerospace|Consumer Goods|Industrial|Healthcare — promote to reference product]']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC verification was last completed for this customer account. Used to enforce periodic KYC renewal cycles and regulatory audit compliance.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer account has completed Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification as required by anti-money laundering (AML) regulations and Transport Shipping\'s customer onboarding policy. Required for high-value accounts and cross-border trade.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Sourced from Salesforce CRM Account LastModifiedDate. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Silver Layer lakehouse pipeline.']
  last_shipment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent shipment booked or executed under this customer account. Used for dormancy detection, account health monitoring, and proactive retention outreach by the account management team.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the customer entity as recorded with the relevant national business registry. Used for customs declarations, trade compliance screening, and financial invoicing. For B2C individuals this is the full personal name.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer account was formally activated and approved for shipment booking. Marks the start of the commercial relationship. Used for customer tenure analysis, anniversary-based tier reviews, and SLA effective date calculations.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code assigned to the customer account (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer master. Determines invoice due dates and early payment discount eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NET30|NET60|NET90|COD|PREPAID|EOM|NET15 — promote to reference product]']
  preferred_incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Customer\'s default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code governing risk and cost transfer in shipments (e.g., DDP, FOB, CIF, EXW). Pre-populated on new shipment bookings and freight orders in SAP TM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  preferred_service_mode varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred primary freight transport mode: air (express/freight), ocean (FCL/LCL), road (FTL/LTL), rail, or multimodal. Used by SAP TM for default carrier and route selection and by Blue Yonder for demand forecasting.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the account\'s main operational contact. Used for shipment notifications, Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) alerts, invoice delivery, and service case communications via Salesforce CRM.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary operational contact person at the customer account. Used for shipment documentation, customs declarations, and account management correspondence.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the account\'s main operational contact in E.164 international format. Used for last-mile delivery coordination, exception management, and Proof of Delivery (POD) confirmation.']
  salesforce_owner_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM User ID of the account owner record. Retained for bi-directional synchronisation and audit traceability back to the Salesforce system of record.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the contracted Service Level Agreement (SLA) tier applicable to this customer account. Determines On-Time Delivery (OTD) targets, exception escalation thresholds, and guaranteed transit time commitments. Linked to the SLA entitlement configuration in SAP TM.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax Identification Number (TIN) assigned by the national tax authority (e.g., IRS EIN for US entities). Used for financial reporting, 1099/W-9 compliance, and customs importer of record identification.']
  trading_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) name used commercially by the customer, which may differ from the registered legal name. Used in customer-facing communications and shipment documentation.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'Customer\'s VAT or GST registration number issued by the relevant tax authority. Required for cross-border invoicing, customs declarations, and EU VAT compliance. Null for B2C customers or entities in non-VAT jurisdictions.']

  Note: 'Master record for all customer accounts in the logistics network, covering B2B enterprise shippers, B2C individual senders, consignees, and 3PL/4PL partners. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Account object. Stores account identity, legal entity name, tax registration (VAT/GST/EIN), classification (B2B/B2C/3PL/4PL), industry vertical, credit status, account tier, onboarding date, account manager assignment, and operational flags such as DDP eligibility, AEO certification status, and active/suspended/closed lifecycle state.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record in the Transport Shipping data platform. Primary key for the contact entity.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent customer account this contact is associated with. Links the contact to the B2B enterprise account, B2C shipper, or 3PL/4PL partner account in the customer domain.']
  mailing_address_book_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.address_book. Business justification: Contact persons have mailing addresses used for correspondence, GDPR notifications, and regulatory communications. The contact table currently stores mailing_city, mailing_country_code, mailing_postal']
  reports_to_contact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the contact\'s managerial or supervisory contact within the same account. Mirrors the Salesforce CRM \'Reports To\' relationship. Enables organizational hierarchy navigation for escalation routing and account planning.']
  contact_description varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or description about the contact person, capturing additional context such as areas of responsibility, special handling instructions, relationship history, or account-specific notes entered by the account management team in Salesforce CRM.']
  contact_role varchar [note: 'Functional role the contact plays in the customer relationship with Transport Shipping. Determines routing of communications, invoices, and service cases. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing|operational|escalation|authorized_signatory|procurement|customs|compliance|executive — promote to reference product]']
  contact_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts receive communications and are eligible for service interactions. Inactive contacts have left the organization or are no longer relevant. Do_not_contact respects opt-out or legal restrictions per GDPR.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact record indicating its priority and purpose within the account relationship. Primary contacts receive all standard communications; emergency contacts are activated for critical shipment exceptions or SLA breaches.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contact record was first created in the source Salesforce CRM system. Represents the start of the contact\'s lifecycle in the customer relationship management process. Used for cohort analysis and data lineage.']
  crm_contact_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for the contact record in Salesforce CRM (Contact object ID, e.g., 003XXXXXXXXXXXXXXX). Used for cross-system reconciliation and lineage tracing back to the operational system of record.']
  data_retention_expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the contact\'s personal data must be reviewed for deletion or anonymization in compliance with GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure) and the organization\'s data retention policy. Calculated based on the last active engagement date plus the applicable retention period.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or business unit the contact belongs to within their company (e.g., Logistics, Procurement, Finance, Operations, Supply Chain). Supports targeted communications and service routing.']
  do_not_call boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted out of phone-based outreach. When true, outbound calls to this contact are suppressed in CRM workflows. Supports GDPR consent management and national do-not-call registry compliance.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address of the contact person. Used for shipment notifications, EDI communications, invoice delivery, and CRM-driven customer service case correspondence. Governed under GDPR as personal data.']
  email_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contact has opted out of marketing and non-transactional email communications. When true, only transactional emails (e.g., shipment confirmations, POD notifications) are permitted. Enforces GDPR Article 7 consent requirements.']
  fax varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact, used in legacy customs documentation, trade compliance filings, and formal freight forwarding communications where fax remains an accepted channel per regulatory requirements.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Used for personalized communications, correspondence, and identity verification.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Full display name of the contact person (typically first_name + last_name). Sourced directly from Salesforce CRM Name field. Used as the primary human-readable identity label for the contact across logistics operations, EDI communications, and customer service interactions.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the contact provided or last updated their GDPR data processing consent. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance demonstration under GDPR Article 7(1).']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current GDPR data processing consent status for the contact. Tracks whether the contact has given, withdrawn, or not yet provided consent for personal data processing. Mandatory for contacts in EU/EEA jurisdictions. Drives data retention and communication eligibility.']
  is_authorized_signatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is an authorized signatory for shipping contracts, customs declarations, power of attorney for customs brokerage, and trade compliance documents. Critical for Descartes Customs and AEO/C-TPAT compliance validation.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive invoices, freight charges, and financial communications. Billing contacts receive freight invoices, duty and tax statements, and payment reminders from SAP S/4HANA Finance AR processes.']
  is_customs_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated point of contact for customs clearance, trade compliance queries, ISF (Importer Security Filing), and AMS (Automated Manifest System) communications. Used by Descartes Customs for routing compliance notifications.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated primary contact for the associated customer account. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary. Drives default routing for shipment notifications, invoice delivery, and account management communications.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Professional job title or designation of the contact person within their organization (e.g., Shipping Coordinator, Procurement Manager, Logistics Director, Finance Controller). Used for routing communications to the appropriate decision-maker and for account segmentation analytics.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact\'s preferred language for communications, shipping documents, and customer service interactions (e.g., \'en\', \'fr\', \'zh-CN\', \'de\'). Ensures localized AWB, BOL, and customs documentation.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent logged activity (call, email, meeting, task) associated with this contact in Salesforce CRM. Used to identify dormant contacts, prioritize re-engagement campaigns, and measure account health.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contact record in the source Salesforce CRM system. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person as recorded in Salesforce CRM. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for communications and reporting.']
  lead_source varchar [note: 'Original source or channel through which this contact was acquired or entered the CRM system. Used for marketing attribution, sales pipeline analysis, and customer acquisition cost reporting in Salesforce CRM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|referral|trade_show|partner|cold_call|existing_customer|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn profile URL for the contact person. Used by account management and sales teams for relationship intelligence, opportunity tracking, and professional network engagement within Salesforce CRM.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for SMS-based shipment tracking alerts, last-mile delivery notifications, and ePOD (Electronic Proof of Delivery) confirmations.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number of the contact in E.164 international format. Used for shipment exception notifications, customs clearance coordination, and direct customer service interactions.']
  portal_user_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the contact\'s user account in the Transport Shipping customer self-service portal. Links the CRM contact to the portal login for shipment tracking, document retrieval, and service case submission. Enables single-sign-on (SSO) correlation.']
  preferred_contact_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred communication channel for receiving shipment updates, service notifications, and account communications. Drives automated notification routing in TMS and CRM systems.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Honorific or salutation prefix for the contact person (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal correspondence, shipping documentation, and customer communications to ensure professional address.']
  shipment_notification_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is subscribed to receive automated shipment status notifications including ETA updates, departure confirmations, POD (Proof of Delivery) alerts, and exception notifications from FourKites or the TMS.']
  sla_notification_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is configured to receive SLA breach notifications and KPI performance alerts. When true, the contact is included in automated SLA exception communications from the TMS and FourKites visibility platform.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier representing the contact\'s local time zone (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Singapore\'). Used to schedule communications, SLA notifications, and delivery window alerts at appropriate local times.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with customer accounts, including shipping coordinators, procurement managers, finance contacts, and authorized signatories. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact object. Captures full name, job title, department, communication preferences, language, time zone, and contact role (e.g., billing contact, operational contact, escalation contact).'
}

Table customer.account_hierarchy {
  account_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each account hierarchy relationship record in the Transport Shipping customer domain. Primary key for the account_hierarchy data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master contract or framework agreement governing the enterprise account hierarchy relationship. Links to the contract and agreement domain for SLA entitlements, pricing terms, and service scope.']
  child_account_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the child (subsidiary, affiliate, or regional) account in the hierarchy. References the subordinate account linked to the parent for group-level management and revenue roll-up.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Multinational logistics customers manage AEO/C-TPAT compliance at the corporate hierarchy level for group-wide certification. Account hierarchy records must reference the group compliance program for ']
  primary_customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent account in the hierarchy. References the account that sits one level above the child account, enabling enterprise roll-up for consolidated billing and group SLA management.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Account hierarchies with pricing_inheritance_flag=true require a reference to the rate card that applies at the hierarchy level. Consolidated pricing for global corporate accounts — where a parent ent']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Account hierarchies in enterprise logistics are associated with a market segment that governs consolidated commercial terms, pricing inheritance, and SLA inheritance across the hierarchy. The account_']
  annual_contracted_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total annual contracted revenue committed by the enterprise account group under the master agreement. Used for group-level P&L reporting, EBITDA analysis, and account tier classification in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the account hierarchy relationship was formally approved by the designated authority. Supports SOX audit trail and change management governance.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the Transport Shipping commercial or finance authority who approved the account hierarchy relationship. Required for SOX internal controls and audit trail compliance.']
  child_account_count varchar [note: 'Number of direct child accounts linked to the parent account at this hierarchy level. Provides a structural summary for account management and hierarchy governance without requiring recursive queries.']
  consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account\'s shipment charges and invoices are consolidated into the parent account\'s billing statement. When true, enables group-level invoice generation and payment management in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  country_of_domicile varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the parent account\'s legal domicile. Determines applicable trade compliance regulations, customs requirements, and tax jurisdiction for the enterprise group.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the account hierarchy relationship record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record provenance and SOX compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credit_consolidation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account\'s credit exposure is pooled with the parent account for group-level credit limit management. When true, credit checks in SAP S/4HANA Finance are evaluated at the parent account level.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure approved for the enterprise account group at the parent hierarchy level. Applied when credit_consolidation_flag is true. Managed in SAP S/4HANA Finance credit management module.']
  crm_account_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM system identifier for the account hierarchy record. Enables cross-system traceability between the lakehouse silver layer and the Salesforce CRM source of record for account management and opportunity tracking.']
  data_privacy_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country or region code indicating the primary data privacy jurisdiction applicable to the account hierarchy record. Determines GDPR, CCPA, or other regional data protection obligations for customer data handling.']
  denied_party_check_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent denied party and sanctions screening was performed for the enterprise account group. Ensures compliance screening currency per regulatory requirements.']
  denied_party_check_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent denied party and sanctions screening check for the enterprise account group. Managed in Descartes Customs denied party screening module. Flagged accounts require compliance review before shipment execution.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the account hierarchy relationship becomes binding and operationally active. Used to manage time-bound corporate restructuring, mergers, and acquisitions in the customer master.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the account hierarchy relationship ceases to be binding. Nullable for open-ended relationships. Populated upon corporate restructuring, divestiture, or account termination.']
  erp_customer_hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Finance customer hierarchy node identifier for the account group. Enables reconciliation between CRM account structures and ERP financial hierarchies for consolidated billing and revenue recognition.']
  global_account_manager varchar [note: 'Name of the Transport Shipping global account manager responsible for the enterprise account hierarchy. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account ownership for escalation routing and relationship management.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the account hierarchy relationship, used in Salesforce CRM and SAP S/4HANA Finance for cross-system reference and consolidated billing linkage.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the child account within the corporate hierarchy tree, where level 1 represents the ultimate parent (global account). Enables multi-tier enterprise account navigation and reporting.']
  hierarchy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for the account hierarchy relationship, typically reflecting the corporate group name (e.g., \'Acme Corp Global Group\'). Used in CRM dashboards and enterprise account reporting.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Materialized path string representing the full ancestry chain from root to child account (e.g., \'/1001/2045/3087\'). Enables efficient tree traversal queries for enterprise account reporting and consolidated billing without recursive joins.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the account hierarchy relationship. Controls whether the parent-child linkage is operationally active for billing consolidation, SLA management, and enterprise reporting.']
  hierarchy_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the purpose of the hierarchy structure. Corporate hierarchies reflect legal ownership; billing hierarchies drive consolidated invoicing; operational hierarchies manage service delivery; 3PL/4PL hierarchies manage logistics partner structures.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to the enterprise account group\'s shipments. Defines risk and cost transfer points for cross-border logistics. Sourced from SAP TM and Oracle TMS shipment configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_vertical varchar [note: 'The primary industry sector of the enterprise account group (e.g., Retail, Automotive, Pharmaceutical, Technology, Energy). Used for vertical-specific SLA configuration, compliance requirements, and targeted service offerings in Salesforce CRM.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the account hierarchy relationship record. Supports change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and audit trail compliance.']
  max_hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Maximum number of levels in the account hierarchy subtree rooted at this relationship node. Used for hierarchy validation, UI rendering optimization, and enterprise account structure governance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special instructions, or business commentary related to the account hierarchy relationship. Used by global account managers and finance teams for relationship management.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership or equity stake held by the parent account in the child account. Relevant for subsidiary and joint venture relationships. Used for IFRS 10 consolidation threshold determination and financial reporting.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to the enterprise account group (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD). Inherited by child accounts when consolidation is active. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance accounts receivable configuration.']
  pricing_inheritance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account inherits the contracted pricing, rate agreements, and GRI exemptions negotiated at the parent account level. Drives tariff application in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Classifies the nature of the parent-child relationship within the account hierarchy. Drives how billing consolidation, SLA inheritance, and revenue roll-up are applied. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: parent|subsidiary|affiliate|regional_division|branch|joint_venture — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  restructure_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the creation or modification of the account hierarchy relationship. Supports audit trail and change management for corporate restructuring events. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: merger|acquisition|divestiture|reorganization|new_subsidiary|account_migration|other — promote to reference product if additional reasons are needed]']
  revenue_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the annual contracted revenue amount. Ensures consistent multi-currency financial reporting across global enterprise accounts.']
  revenue_roll_up_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account\'s revenue is rolled up to the parent account for enterprise-level P&L reporting and EBITDA analysis. Enables group revenue visibility in SAP S/4HANA Finance and CRM dashboards.']
  sla_inheritance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the child account inherits the SLA entitlements (e.g., OTD targets, OTIF commitments, priority service levels) from the parent account. Drives SLA enforcement and KPI reporting at the group level.']
  voting_rights_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of voting rights held by the parent account in the child entity. Determines control classification under IFRS 10 (control threshold typically >50%) for financial consolidation and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Defines parent-child relationships within enterprise customer account structures, supporting multi-national corporate hierarchies, subsidiary relationships, and regional account groupings. Enables consolidated billing, group-level SLA management, and enterprise revenue roll-up across global accounts. Tracks hierarchy level, relationship type (parent/subsidiary/affiliate), effective dates, and consolidation flags.'
}

Table customer.shipper_profile {

  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Shipper AEO/C-TPAT compliance program membership governs authorized shipper status, security screening bypass, and expedited export release. Logistics systems must link shipper profiles to their activ']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent customer account record that owns this shipper profile. Links the shipper operational defaults to the master customer identity.']

The shipper\'s default pickup/origin address should be stored in the centralized address_book rather']

  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID) used for automated shipment booking, ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice), and status message exchange between the shipper\'s systems and the TMS/WMS platforms.']
  export_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: shipper_profile has exporter_trade_party_id → customs domain but no network agent FK. The export customs broker/freight agent is a network entity handling export declarations and AES filing. Role-pref']

Required for: export declarations, AEO/CTPAT certification validation, denied party screening, and origin determination. Essential for outbound']
  origin_tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Shippers use origin 3PLs for pickup, consolidation, and cross-docking. shipper_profile has default_origin_network_node_id but no 3PL FK. Role-prefixed as origin_tpl_provider_id to distinguish from con']

Business justification: Shipper-level default carrier preference drives automated carrier selection in booking systems, rate shopping prioritization, and capacity allocation. Replaces denormalized preferred_carrier_code with']

Business justification: Shipper profiles define preferred outbound service corridors used in TMS lane selection and rate card application. No existing FK covers corridor preference on shipper_profile. Logistics domain expert']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: In TMS auto-rating, the system resolves the applicable rate card directly from the shipper_profile at shipment creation. Logistics rating engines require shipper_profile→rate_card to apply correct neg']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shipper account by business model. B2B for enterprise shippers, B2C for individual consumers, 3PL/4PL for logistics partners, NVOCC for Non-Vessel Operating Common Carriers, and government for public sector entities. Drives service tier and pricing logic.']
  aeo_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the shipper\'s AEO certification. Used to trigger renewal alerts and to validate AEO status at the time of shipment booking in Descartes Customs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shipper profile record was first created in the system. Captured in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR data retention management.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding credit amount extended to the shipper for freight charges. Enforced during shipment booking to prevent over-limit tendering. Managed in SAP S/4HANA Finance accounts receivable.']
  ctpat_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the shipper\'s C-TPAT certification. Monitored to ensure continuous compliance eligibility for US cross-border shipments.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the shipper\'s default billing and invoicing currency. Used in freight charge calculation, COD amounts, and SAP S/4HANA Finance revenue recognition.']
  default_incoterms varchar [note: 'Default Incoterms rule applied to shipments originated by this shipper. Defines the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between shipper and consignee. Pre-populated on freight orders in SAP TM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  default_packaging_type varchar [note: 'Shipper\'s default packaging type used for shipment booking and warehouse operations. Drives packaging material selection, DIM weight calculation, and stacking rules in Manhattan WMS and SAP TM.']
  eori_number varchar [note: 'EU-issued Economic Operators Registration and Identification number used for customs declarations and cross-border trade compliance within the European Union. Required for EU import/export filings in Descartes Customs.']
  hazmat_certification_expiry date [note: 'Expiry date of the shipper\'s hazmat handling certification. Shipments tendered after this date will be blocked from hazmat routing until recertification is confirmed.']
  hazmat_certification_number varchar [note: 'Regulatory certification or registration number authorizing the shipper to tender dangerous goods. Required for IMDG (ocean) and IATA DGR (air) hazmat shipments. Validated in Descartes Customs during shipment booking.']
  iata_shipper_code varchar [note: 'IATA-assigned shipper identification code used in air freight documentation including House Air Waybills (HAWB) and Master Air Waybills (MAWB). Required for IATA-regulated air cargo bookings.']
  is_cod_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) is enabled for shipments from this shipper. When true, COD collection instructions are appended to delivery orders and ePOD workflows.']
  is_fragile_handling_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether shipments from this shipper require fragile handling by default. Triggers special handling instructions on freight orders and warehouse pick/pack operations in Manhattan WMS.']
  is_hazmat_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipper is authorized to tender hazardous materials shipments. When true, hazmat-specific handling, documentation, and carrier routing rules are applied. Validated against IMDG Code for ocean and IATA DGR for air shipments.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this shipper regularly tenders temperature-controlled (cold chain) shipments. When true, temperature-controlled handling, equipment, and carrier capabilities are applied by default in SAP TM routing.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the shipper profile record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and for audit trail compliance.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal registered name of the shipper as it appears on Air Waybills (AWB), Bills of Lading (BOL), and customs declarations. Required for trade compliance and carrier documentation.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for shipment status notifications and alerts (e.g., booking confirmation, ETA updates, POD). EDI indicates Electronic Data Interchange integration; webhook indicates API-based push notifications via FourKites or TMS integrations.']
  notification_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for shipment status notifications, booking confirmations, and exception alerts. Used when notification_channel is set to email. Subject to GDPR consent management.']
  notification_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile phone number for SMS-based shipment notifications and alerts. Used when notification_channel is set to sms. Must be in E.164 international format.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to freight invoices for this shipper (e.g., NET30, NET60, PREPAID). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer master and used in freight audit and invoice processing.']
  preferred_load_type varchar [note: 'Shipper\'s default load consolidation preference. FTL (Full Truckload) and LTL (Less Than Truckload) apply to road freight; FCL (Full Container Load) and LCL (Less Than Container Load) apply to ocean freight. Used in load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  preferred_service_mode varchar [note: 'Shipper\'s preferred primary transport mode for shipment booking. Influences carrier selection and routing logic in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Multimodal indicates no single-mode preference.']
  profile_effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this shipper profile becomes operationally active and eligible for shipment booking. Supports time-bounded profile management and contract-aligned activation.']
  profile_effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this shipper profile is no longer operationally active. Null indicates an open-ended profile with no planned expiry. Used to enforce time-bounded shipper authorizations.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shipper profile. Active profiles are eligible for shipment booking. Suspended profiles are temporarily blocked. Blacklisted profiles are permanently barred due to compliance violations or fraud. Drives booking eligibility checks in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM Account object identifier for the shipper. Enables cross-system traceability between the operational shipper profile and the CRM account record for opportunity tracking, service cases, and customer communications.']
  sap_customer_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP TM customer master number for the shipper. Used for freight charge calculation, accounts receivable posting, and revenue recognition. Enables reconciliation between TMS and ERP financial records.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA entitlement tier assigned to the shipper, determining priority handling, On-Time Delivery (OTD) targets, exception escalation thresholds, and dedicated account management levels. Sourced from Salesforce CRM contract records.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any additional special handling requirements for shipments from this shipper that are not covered by structured flags (e.g., \'Do not stack\', \'Keep upright\', \'Requires liftgate at delivery\'). Appended to freight orders in SAP TM.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the US, VAT number in the EU, GST number in other jurisdictions) for the shipper entity. Used for customs declarations, freight invoicing, and financial reporting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments from this shipper. Used alongside the minimum to define the acceptable cold chain envelope for carrier and equipment selection.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments from this shipper. Used to specify cold chain equipment requirements and validate carrier capabilities.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or doing-business-as (DBA) trade name of the shipper, which may differ from the legal name. Used for customer-facing communications and account management in Salesforce CRM.']
  webhook_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'HTTPS endpoint URL registered by the shipper to receive real-time shipment event push notifications via webhook. Used when notification_channel is set to webhook. Must use HTTPS for security compliance.']

  Note: 'Operational profile for customers acting as shippers (origin parties) in logistics transactions. Captures shipper-specific attributes including default origin addresses, preferred service modes (air/ocean/road/rail), FTL/LTL/FCL/LCL preferences, carrier preferences, packaging requirements, hazmat handling authorizations (IMDG/IATA DGR), IATA shipper codes, C-TPAT or AEO certification references, default Incoterms, COD preferences, special handling instructions (fragile, temperature-controlled), and notification preferences (SMS/email/EDI/webhook). Serves as the unified operational defaults profile for shipment booking automation and personalized service delivery.'
}

  delivery_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Consignees have delivery location nodes for last-mile routing. Required for delivery scheduling, route optimization, last-mile zone assignment, and delivery promise calculation. Replaces denormalized ']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID) for the consignee, enabling automated transmission of ASNs, delivery confirmations, and customs pre-arrival notifications.']
