  importer_trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Consignee profiles represent importers/receivers in customs. Required for: import declarations, customs broker assignment, duty assessment, and delivery clearance. Critical for inbound customs operati']
  preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Consignees in last-mile logistics designate preferred delivery carriers (e.g., residential consignees preferring specific couriers). This drives last-mile carrier selection in delivery planning. No ex']

Business justification: A consignee profile has a designated primary contact person for delivery coordination (signature requirements, appointment scheduling, ePOD). Linking to tpl_provider enables automated de']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consignee profile. Blacklisted status is set by Descartes Customs denied-party screening. Pending verification indicates incomplete KYC or trade compliance checks.']
  asn_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignee requires an Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) to be transmitted via EDI prior to shipment arrival. When True, ASN generation is triggered in the WMS/TMS integration layer.']
  consignee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consignee indicating the nature of the receiving party. Drives SLA entitlements, customs handling rules, and delivery workflows. B2B enterprise and 3PL/4PL partners are recurring; B2C individual may be one-time last-mile recipients. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: b2b_enterprise|b2c_individual|3pl_partner|4pl_partner|government|nvocc|freight_forwarder|broker — promote to reference product]']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding credit amount extended to this consignee for duty deferment, freight charges on account, or COD (Cash on Delivery) waiver arrangements. Managed in SAP S/4HANA Finance accounts receivable.']
  ctpat_status varchar [note: 'C-TPAT membership status of the consignee as maintained in Descartes Customs. Certified status qualifies the consignee for reduced US CBP examination rates and expedited cargo release.']
  ddp_authorized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignee has authorized Transport Shipping to arrange DDP (Delivered Duty Paid) shipments on their behalf, where the shipper or carrier pre-pays import duties and taxes. Relevant for cross-border e-commerce fulfillment.']
  denied_party_screen_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent denied-party screening check. A match_found or blocked result prevents shipment processing until compliance review is completed.']
  denied_party_screened_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent denied-party screening check performed against this consignee in Descartes Customs. Required for trade compliance audit trails under US EAR, OFAC, and EU dual-use regulations.']
  epod_contact_email varchar [note: 'Dedicated email address to which electronic proof of delivery (ePOD) documents are transmitted upon successful delivery. May differ from the primary contact email when a separate receiving or logistics team handles POD acknowledgment.']
  epod_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or direct phone number for the ePOD recipient contact, used by drivers and last-mile couriers to confirm delivery and obtain digital signature authorization.']
  geo_latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate of the consignee delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used by FourKites for geofencing, last-mile ETA prediction, and proof-of-delivery geo-stamping.']
  geo_longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate of the consignee delivery location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for geofencing and route optimization in SAP TM and FourKites.']
  id_verification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignee must present government-issued identification at the time of delivery. Applicable for high-value shipments, controlled goods, or regulatory requirements.']
  incoterms_preference varchar [note: 'Consignee\'s preferred Incoterms rule governing the transfer of risk and cost responsibility for import shipments. Incoterms 2020 standard. Drives duty and tax liability assignment in customs declarations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consignee is a recurring destination party (True) or a one-time recipient such as a B2C last-mile delivery addressee (False). Drives profile retention and SLA assignment logic.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the consignee as it appears on customs declarations, air waybills (AWB), and bills of lading (BOL). Used for trade compliance screening and import documentation.']
  notification_language_code varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 language tag representing the consignee\'s preferred language for shipment notifications, delivery alerts, and ePOD communications (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, zh-CN).']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to this consignee for freight charges billed to destination (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer master.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the consignee\'s preferred billing and invoicing currency for duty, tax, and freight charge settlements.']
  preferred_delivery_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of preferred delivery days of the week (e.g., MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI). Used by last-mile scheduling to avoid unattended delivery attempts and reduce failed delivery rates.']
  preferred_delivery_window_end varchar [note: 'Consignee-preferred latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for delivery attempts. Defines the close of the acceptable delivery window for last-mile scheduling.']
  preferred_delivery_window_start varchar [note: 'Consignee-preferred earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour format) for delivery attempts. Used by last-mile routing engines in Oracle TMS and SAP TM to schedule delivery slots within SLA windows.']
  profile_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consignee profile record was first created in the Transport Shipping data platform. Represents the record audit creation event for lineage and compliance tracking.']
  profile_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consignee profile record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer and audit trail compliance.']
  requires_appointment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consignee requires a pre-scheduled delivery appointment before a driver can attempt delivery. True triggers appointment scheduling workflow in Oracle TMS.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from Salesforce CRM linking this consignee profile to the corresponding CRM account record for B2B consignees. Null for one-time B2C recipients.']
  signature_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a consignee signature is mandatory for proof of delivery (POD). When True, drivers must capture a digital signature via ePOD before completing the delivery event.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'SLA entitlement tier assigned to this consignee profile, determining delivery time commitments, exception handling priority, and OTD/OTIF performance targets. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account entitlements.']
  special_delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing consignee-specific delivery instructions such as dock number, gate access codes, forklift requirements, temperature-controlled unloading bay, or security check-in procedures.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (TIN, VAT number, EIN, or equivalent) for the consignee. Required for customs declarations, duty invoicing, and trade compliance filings in Descartes Customs.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the consignee delivery location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used to convert delivery window times to UTC for scheduling in SAP TM and FourKites.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or doing-business-as (DBA) trade name of the consignee, which may differ from the legal name. Used for operational labeling and customer-facing communications.']
  warehouse_dock_number varchar [note: 'Specific dock door or bay number at the consignee\'s receiving facility. Used by drivers and Manhattan WMS for directed unloading and dock scheduling.']

  Note: 'Operational profile for parties designated as consignees (destination recipients) in logistics shipments. Stores consignee identity, delivery address preferences, customs clearance authorizations, import license numbers, preferred delivery windows, ePOD contact details, and special delivery instructions. Supports both recurring consignees (B2B) and one-time recipients (B2C last-mile). Distinct from shipper_profile as consignees have import-side compliance and delivery-side operational attributes.'
}

Table customer.address_book {
  address_book_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each address record in the centralized address book. Primary key for the address_book data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (shipper, consignee, or account holder) who owns or is associated with this address record. Links the address to the customer master.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Address book entries of type \'warehouse\' or \'distribution_center\' must link to actual facility records for dock scheduling, address validation, and facility-based shipment routing in transport logisti']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Customer addresses map to network nodes for routing and planning. Powers address-to-node geocoding, route optimization, network design, and facility assignment. Essential for translating customer loca']
  route_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Address book entries must be mapped to delivery zones for last-mile routing, surcharge calculation (residential, remote area), and service availability checks. Zone-to-address mapping is a fundamental']
  address_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier or short code assigned to this address record, used in shipment booking, EDI transactions, and TMS integrations (e.g., SAP TM location code, Oracle TMS address code). Enables cross-system address referencing.']
  address_label varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name or alias for this address record (e.g., \'London Heathrow Warehouse\', \'NYC HQ Billing\', \'Shanghai Port CFS\'). Used in shipment booking UIs, ePOD systems, and customer-facing communications to identify addresses without displaying full address details.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including building number, street name, and any suite or unit designation. This is the first and most critical line of the physical address used on shipping labels, AWBs, BOLs, and customs declarations.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for additional location details such as floor number, building name, industrial estate, or complex name. Optional field used when the primary address line is insufficient to uniquely identify the delivery location.']
  address_line_3 varchar [note: 'Tertiary address line for additional location context such as district, neighborhood, or landmark reference. Commonly used in international addresses where additional geographic context is required for customs or last-mile delivery.']
  address_role varchar [note: 'Functional role this address plays in logistics operations. An address may be classified as an origin pickup location, delivery destination, billing address, return address, warehouse address, or notify party address. A single physical address may have multiple roles across different shipments. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: origin|destination|billing|return|warehouse|notify_party — promote to reference product]']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the address record. Active addresses are available for shipment booking. Pending_validation addresses are awaiting address verification. Deprecated addresses are retained for historical reference but not available for new bookings. Blacklisted addresses are blocked due to compliance or fraud concerns.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the physical address by its operational nature. Drives last-mile delivery rules, customs declaration requirements, and carrier surcharge logic. Residential addresses may attract residential delivery surcharges; port/airport addresses trigger terminal handling charge (THC) logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: residential|commercial|warehouse|port|airport|border_crossing|free_trade_zone — promote to reference product]']
  aeo_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official AEO certificate number issued by the relevant customs authority (e.g., EU EORI-based AEO number, C-TPAT account number). Required for customs pre-clearance, reduced inspection, and trusted trader benefits. Validated in Descartes Customs during shipment processing.']
  aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the entity at this address holds Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, which grants expedited customs clearance and reduced inspection rates. AEO-certified addresses enable simplified customs procedures and are prioritized in Descartes Customs trade compliance workflows.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name of the address. Used in route planning, carrier zone determination, customs declarations, and last-mile delivery assignment. Critical for TMS routing and freight rate calculation.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the company, organization, or business entity at this address. Required for commercial shipments, customs declarations, and B2B delivery. Used on shipping labels, air waybills (AWB), bills of lading (BOL), and customs documentation.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact at this address. Used for shipment notifications, electronic proof of delivery (ePOD) dispatch, customs pre-arrival notifications, and delivery exception alerts.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person at this address. Used for last-mile delivery coordination, customs notifications, and proof of delivery (POD) capture. Required for residential and commercial delivery addresses.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the contact person at this address, in E.164 international format. Used for last-mile delivery notifications, exception management, and customs clearance coordination. Required by many carriers for residential delivery.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the address (e.g., USA, GBR, CHN, DEU). Mandatory for cross-border shipments, customs declarations, Incoterms application, trade compliance screening, and international freight rate determination.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this address record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for address master data governance, GDPR data subject access requests, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  customs_zone varchar [note: 'Customs and trade compliance zone classification for this address. Free Trade Zone (FTZ) and bonded warehouse addresses require special customs handling in Descartes Customs. Special Economic Zone (SEZ) addresses may have preferential duty treatment. Drives customs declaration routing and duty calculation.']
  dangerous_goods_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dangerous goods (DG) shipments are permitted at this address. When False, the TMS and booking systems will block DG shipments to/from this address. Governed by IMDG Code for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR/RID for road/rail transport.']
  delivery_window_close varchar [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format) when deliveries or pickups are accepted at this address. Used alongside delivery_window_open to define the acceptable delivery time window for route planning and SLA management.']
  delivery_window_open varchar [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM in 24-hour format) when deliveries or pickups are accepted at this address. Used by TMS route optimization to schedule delivery windows and ensure SLA compliance. Critical for warehouse and commercial addresses with restricted receiving hours.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this address record is valid and available for use in shipment bookings and logistics operations. Supports address lifecycle management and temporal validity for contract logistics, where addresses may be activated on a specific date aligned with contract start.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this address record is no longer valid for new shipment bookings. Null indicates an open-ended validity. Used for address lifecycle management when customers relocate, close facilities, or terminate contracts. Historical shipments referencing this address are retained for audit purposes.']
  eori_number varchar [note: 'EORI number assigned by EU customs authorities to identify economic operators in customs declarations and trade transactions. Required for all import/export customs filings within the EU. Stored at the address level to support multi-site customers with different EORI registrations per facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Type of logistics facility at this address when applicable. Drives specialized handling requirements in Manhattan WMS, carrier selection in SAP TM, and customs processing in Descartes. Cold storage facilities require temperature-controlled transport; bonded warehouses require customs bond management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: distribution_center|cross_dock|cold_storage|bonded_warehouse|cfs|icd|airport_cargo|seaport_terminal — promote to reference product]']
  geocoding_accuracy varchar [note: 'Precision level of the geocoded latitude/longitude coordinates. Rooftop indicates exact building-level precision; range_interpolated is street-level; geometric_center is centroid of a postal area; approximate is city/region level; unverified means coordinates have not been validated. Drives confidence in GPS-based routing and geofencing.']
  hs_code_restriction varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of HS Code prefixes (Harmonized System codes per WCO) that are restricted or prohibited at this address due to local regulations, facility licensing, or trade compliance requirements. Used by Descartes Customs to flag non-compliant shipments during booking.']
  is_default_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the customer\'s default billing address for invoice generation and accounts receivable processing in SAP S/4HANA Finance. When True, this address is used for all financial documents unless overridden at the shipment level.']
  is_default_destination boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the customer\'s default delivery destination. When True, this address is pre-populated as the destination in shipment booking workflows, supporting frequent lane customers and e-commerce fulfillment automation.']
  is_default_origin boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the customer\'s default pickup/origin location for shipment booking. When True, this address is pre-populated as the origin in shipment booking workflows in SAP TM and Oracle TMS, reducing data entry errors.']
  is_residential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is classified as a residential location. Residential addresses trigger carrier-specific residential delivery surcharges (e.g., FedEx, UPS residential fees), require different last-mile delivery protocols, and may affect SLA commitments for D2D (door-to-door) services.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this address record was most recently updated, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Used for change data capture (CDC) in ETL pipelines, optimistic locking in concurrent update scenarios, and audit trail compliance.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), ranging from -90 to +90. Used for GPS-based last-mile routing, geofencing in FourKites real-time visibility, proximity-based warehouse assignment, and route optimization in TMS.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum), ranging from -180 to +180. Used alongside latitude for GPS-based routing, geofencing, real-time shipment tracking, and network design optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the address. Used for carrier zone mapping, freight rate calculation, last-mile delivery routing, dimensional weight (DIM weight) zone surcharges, and address validation. Format varies by country.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this address record originated. Enables data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during master data synchronization, and audit compliance. Salesforce CRM is the primary source for customer-facing addresses; SAP TM and Oracle TMS for logistics locations.']
  source_system_address_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of this address record in the originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce CRM Address ID, SAP TM Location ID, Oracle TMS Address Code). Enables bidirectional synchronization, change data capture (CDC), and cross-system reconciliation during EDI processing.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field for special handling or access instructions at this address (e.g., \'Deliver to loading dock B\', \'Call 30 minutes before arrival\', \'Gate code: 1234\', \'No deliveries before 08:00\'). Transmitted to last-mile carriers and drivers via TMS dispatch notes.']
  standardized_address varchar [note: 'Full normalized and standardized address string as returned by the address validation provider, conforming to postal authority formatting rules. Used as the canonical address for printing on shipping labels, AWBs, and customs declarations to ensure deliverability.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the address using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes where applicable. Required for domestic routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and US/Canada customs filings (AMS, ISF).']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the address location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Shanghai\'). Used to correctly interpret delivery windows, calculate ETA/ETD in local time, and schedule last-mile delivery notifications across global operations.']
  validation_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party address validation service or postal authority database used to validate this address (e.g., USPS, Loqate, SmartyStreets, Google Maps, HERE). Enables audit trail for address quality and supports provider performance benchmarking.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the address standardization and validation process against postal authority databases (e.g., USPS, Royal Mail, Deutsche Post). Validated addresses have been confirmed deliverable. Failed addresses contain errors. Partial addresses are incomplete but usable. Used to enforce address quality gates in shipment booking workflows.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated against a postal authority or address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is required based on address data freshness policies.']

  Note: 'Centralized repository of all customer-associated addresses including origin pickup locations, delivery destinations, billing addresses, and warehouse addresses. Supports address validation, geocoding coordinates, address type classification (residential/commercial/warehouse/port), and address lifecycle management. Used across shipment booking, last-mile delivery, and customs declarations to ensure address standardization.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer segment record. Primary key for the customer_segment data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Customer segments are often governed by segment-level master agreements defining pricing models, SLA tiers, and volume commitments for all accounts in that segment. Commercial management and segment p']
  primary_trade_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Customer segments are scoped to primary trade lanes (e.g., Asia-Pacific exporters). Segment has primary_trade_lane as a denormalized text field — replacing with a proper FK enables lane-level segment ']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Customer segments reference standard published tariffs for regulatory compliance and baseline pricing. In transport logistics, segments often map to tariff classes (e.g., retail vs wholesale). Require']
  account_management_model varchar [note: 'Defines the assigned account management approach for customers in this segment: STRATEGIC = dedicated named account manager with executive sponsorship; COMMERCIAL = shared account management team; DIGITAL = digital-first with inside sales support; SELF_SERVE = fully automated self-service with no dedicated account manager.']
  api_access_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are entitled to direct API (Application Programming Interface) access to Transport Shipping\'s TMS and WMS platforms for automated shipment booking, tracking, and reporting.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the commercial authority (e.g., VP Commercial, Regional Pricing Director) who approved this segment definition. Required for SOX audit trail and commercial governance compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which this segment definition was formally approved by the designated commercial authority. Used for audit trail, governance reporting, and SOX compliance.']
  commercial_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification indicating the level of service, pricing, and account management investment assigned to this segment. STRATEGIC = top-tier enterprise accounts with dedicated account teams; PREMIUM = high-value accounts with enhanced SLAs; STANDARD = mid-market accounts; BASIC = transactional accounts; DIGITAL = self-serve digital-only accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the system of record (Salesforce CRM) and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per platform convention.']
  credit_limit_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit exposure in USD extended to customers within this segment. Used by SAP S/4HANA Finance Accounts Receivable for credit control and order release decisions. Confidential financial data.']
  crm_segment_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this segment record in Salesforce CRM (15 or 18-character Salesforce record ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and the Salesforce CRM source of record.']
  customs_facilitation_level varchar [note: 'Level of customs clearance facilitation entitlement for customers in this segment. AEO_FAST_TRACK applies to segments serving Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certified customers eligible for expedited customs processing via Descartes Customs.']
  dangerous_goods_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are eligible to ship dangerous goods (DG) under IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR) and IMDG Code. When TRUE, additional compliance checks and documentation requirements apply.']
  dedicated_warehouse_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment qualify for dedicated warehouse space and labour allocation within Transport Shipping\'s distribution network, as managed by Manhattan WMS.']
  discount_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the discount tier applicable to this segment, governing base rate reductions, General Rate Increase (GRI) exemptions, Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) caps, and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) waivers. Confidential as it reflects commercial negotiation outcomes.']
  ecommerce_fulfillment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are entitled to access e-commerce fulfillment services including last-mile delivery, returns management (RMA), and Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) processing.']
  edi_integration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are required to exchange transactional data (shipment orders, ASNs, invoices) via Electronic Data Interchange (EDI). Drives onboarding and integration requirements.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this segment definition becomes effective and can be used for customer assignment, pricing, and SLA entitlement. Supports temporal validity management in the Silver Layer.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this segment definition expires and is no longer valid for new customer assignments. NULL indicates the segment is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used for segment lifecycle management and historical reporting.']
  eligible_service_lines varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of logistics service lines that customers in this segment are eligible to access (e.g., \'EXPRESS_PARCEL,FREIGHT_AIR,FREIGHT_OCEAN,CUSTOMS_BROKERAGE,ECOMMERCE_FULFILLMENT\'). Governs service entitlement and product catalogue access. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXPRESS_PARCEL|FREIGHT_AIR|FREIGHT_OCEAN|FREIGHT_ROAD|FREIGHT_RAIL|CUSTOMS_BROKERAGE|WAREHOUSE|ECOMMERCE_FULFILLMENT|LAST_MILE|CONTRACT_LOGISTICS — promote to reference product]']
  erp_segment_code varchar [note: 'Segment code as recorded in SAP S/4HANA Finance for revenue allocation, cost center assignment, and P&L reporting by customer segment. Enables financial reporting alignment between the data lakehouse and ERP.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage scope of the segment, indicating whether it applies to global enterprise accounts, regional accounts, domestic-only shippers, or cross-border trade customers. Influences route and network planning in SAP TM.']
  incoterms_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to customers in this segment, defining the allocation of costs, risks, and responsibilities between shipper and consignee. Sourced from contract agreements and Salesforce CRM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product if full Incoterms 2020 set required]']
  invoice_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which invoices are generated and issued to customers in this segment. Drives billing cycle configuration in SAP S/4HANA Finance and impacts cash flow planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this segment record in the source system or Silver Layer. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit trail maintenance.']
  otd_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance target expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for customers in this segment. Used in KPI reporting, SLA compliance monitoring, and carrier performance management.']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractual On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance target expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00) for customers in this segment. Measures both delivery timeliness and order completeness. Used in supply chain performance reporting.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code applicable to customers in this segment (e.g., NET30, NET60, PREPAID, COD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance and governs invoice due dates and cash collection processes.']
  preferred_transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transport mode preference for customers in this segment (AIR, OCEAN, ROAD, RAIL, or MULTIMODAL). Used in SAP TM carrier selection and route planning to pre-configure default routing for segment accounts.']
  pricing_model_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pricing model applied to customers in this segment (e.g., CONTRACTED_RATE, SPOT_RATE, VOLUME_DISCOUNT, TARIFF_PUBLISHED). Drives charge calculation in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Confidential as it reflects commercial pricing strategy.']
  real_time_visibility_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are entitled to real-time shipment tracking and predictive ETA services via FourKites. Typically reserved for STRATEGIC and PREMIUM commercial tiers.']
  revenue_band_max_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum annualised revenue threshold in USD defining the upper boundary of this segment\'s revenue band. NULL indicates an open-ended upper bound (e.g., for the highest-tier segment). Used alongside revenue_band_min_usd for segment qualification.']
  revenue_band_min_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum annualised revenue threshold in USD that a customer account must meet to qualify for this segment. Used in segment assignment logic and periodic segment review cycles. Expressed in USD for global comparability.']
  revenue_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the revenue band thresholds (revenue_band_min_usd, revenue_band_max_usd) are denominated. Defaults to USD for global segments; may be set to local currency for region-specific segments.']
  review_criteria varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the criteria used during the segment review cycle to determine whether a customer account should be upgraded, downgraded, or retained in this segment (e.g., revenue performance, shipment volume, strategic partnership status).']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which customer accounts assigned to this segment are reviewed for re-segmentation based on updated revenue, volume, and strategic criteria. Drives the account management review calendar in Salesforce CRM.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the customer segment (e.g., ENT-GOLD, SME-SILVER, B2C-STANDARD). Used as the business key across CRM, TMS, and billing systems. Sourced from Salesforce CRM account segmentation module.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment\'s commercial profile, target customer characteristics, and strategic intent. Used for onboarding account managers and informing pricing strategy.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the customer segment (e.g., \'Enterprise Gold\', \'SME Silver\', \'B2C Standard\'). Used in reporting dashboards, account management workflows, and commercial strategy documents.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the segment definition: ACTIVE = in use for customer assignment and pricing; INACTIVE = suspended from new customer assignments; DRAFT = under review and not yet published; DEPRECATED = retired segment retained for historical reporting only.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the segment by customer relationship type: B2B (business-to-business enterprise accounts), B2C (individual consumer shippers), 3PL (Third-Party Logistics partners), 4PL (Fourth-Party Logistics orchestrators), GOVERNMENT (public sector entities), or INTERNAL (intra-company accounts).']
  shipment_volume_band_max varchar [note: 'Maximum annualised shipment volume (number of shipments) defining the upper boundary of this segment\'s volume band. NULL indicates open-ended upper bound for the highest-volume segment tier.']
  shipment_volume_band_min varchar [note: 'Minimum annualised shipment volume (number of shipments) threshold for segment qualification. Complements revenue thresholds for volume-based segmentation used in logistics pricing and SLA assignment.']
  sla_tier_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the SLA tier entitlement assigned to customers in this segment. Determines On-Time Delivery (OTD) targets, On-Time In-Full (OTIF) commitments, claims response times, and escalation thresholds. Linked to the SLA tier reference product.']
  sustainability_reporting_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customers in this segment are entitled to receive Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) and Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emissions reporting for their shipments. Supports customer ESG compliance and EU ETS reporting obligations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this segment definition, incremented each time the segment criteria, thresholds, or entitlements are revised. Supports change history tracking and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Market segmentation classification for customer accounts defining commercial tiers, revenue bands, and strategic groupings used for pricing, SLA assignment, and account management. Captures segment code, segment name, revenue threshold ranges, assigned account management model (strategic/commercial/digital), eligible service lines, and segment review cycle. Supports differentiated service delivery and targeted commercial strategies.'
}

Table customer.sla_entitlement {
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer-specific SLA entitlement record in the silver layer lakehouse.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: SLA entitlements in logistics are often fulfilled by specific customs brokers or freight agents contractually responsible for meeting transit and clearance SLAs. Agent-specific SLA measurement and bre']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent commercial contract or agreement under which this SLA entitlement is granted. Distinct from the entitlement itself — the contract domain holds the template; this record is the customer-specific instantiation.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Enterprise SLA agreements often specify carrier-specific performance guarantees (e.g., \'Carrier X: 99% OTD on EU lanes\'). Contract management, performance measurement, and penalty/incentive calculatio']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: SLA transit time guarantees are tied to specific carrier services (e.g., \'DHL Express Worldwide 2-day\'). SLA measurement and OTD reporting require knowing which carrier service the entitlement applies']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which this SLA entitlement is instantiated. Links to the customer master in the customer domain.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: SLA entitlements are lane-specific contractual commitments. Required for lane-level performance monitoring, OTD/OTIF measurement by lane, penalty/incentive calculation, and contractual compliance repo']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.partner. Business justification: Freight forwarders and 3PLs (network partners) are often contractually responsible for SLA delivery, distinct from carriers. SLA entitlements with freight forwarder partners require a partner FK for p']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: SLA entitlements are co-negotiated with rate cards in logistics contract management. The rate card defines pricing commitments while the SLA defines service commitments — both are referenced together ']
  reporting_contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary customer contact who receives SLA performance reports and is the designated recipient for escalation notifications under this entitlement.']
  service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: SLA entitlements are scoped to service corridors (e.g., Asia-Europe express). Corridor-level SLA reporting and customer commitment tracking require this link. Logistics domain experts expect entitleme']
  sla_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_sla_commitment. Business justification: Customer SLA entitlements must be traceable to the specific contract SLA commitment that backs them. Compliance audits, SLA breach investigations, and penalty calculations require verifying that each ']
  tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: E-commerce fulfillment SLA entitlements are commonly with 3PL providers (pick-pack-ship SLAs, same-day dispatch). No existing 3PL FK on sla_entitlement. Required for 3PL SLA breach reporting, penalty ']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: SLA compliance measurement requires linking customer entitlements to the published transit time standard they are measured against. Logistics KPI reporting (OTD, OTIF) depends on comparing actual vs. ']
  auto_renewal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA entitlement automatically renews at the end of the effective period without requiring explicit renegotiation. When True, the entitlement rolls over under the same terms unless terminated.']
  claims_resolution_sla_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days within which a cargo claim must be acknowledged, investigated, and resolved under this SLA entitlement. Drives claims team prioritization and escalation triggers.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA entitlement record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Provides audit trail for record provenance and data lineage in the silver layer.']
  crm_entitlement_code varchar [note: 'Source system identifier for this SLA entitlement record in Salesforce CRM. Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse silver layer and the operational CRM system of record.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields in this SLA entitlement record (penalty_cap_amount, incentive amounts). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency customer accounts.']
  delivery_time_window_end varchar [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which delivery must be completed to be considered on-time under this SLA entitlement. Defines the close of the delivery window for time-definite commitments.']
  delivery_time_window_start varchar [note: 'Earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) within which delivery must be attempted to be considered on-time under this SLA entitlement. Used for time-definite delivery commitments (e.g., AM delivery windows).']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination geography of the lane covered by this SLA entitlement. Null if the entitlement is not lane-specific.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this SLA entitlement becomes binding and enforceable. Performance measurement begins on or after this date.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this SLA entitlement expires and ceases to be enforceable. Null indicates an open-ended entitlement with no fixed expiry. Aligns with contract renewal cycles.']
  entitlement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive business name for this SLA entitlement, e.g., \'Gold Express OTD Commitment — APAC Lane\'. Used in customer-facing documents and CRM dashboards.']
  entitlement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for this SLA entitlement, used in customer communications, CRM service cases, and operational reporting. Format: SLA- followed by alphanumeric characters.']
  entitlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the SLA entitlement. \'active\' means the entitlement is in force; \'suspended\' means temporarily paused (e.g., force majeure); \'expired\' means the effective period has lapsed; \'pending_activation\' means approved but not yet in effect; \'terminated\' means cancelled before natural expiry; \'draft\' means under negotiation.']
  escalation_threshold_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours after a shipment misses its committed delivery time before an automatic escalation is triggered to the account management or operations team. Enables proactive exception management for high-value customers.']
  exclusion_conditions varchar [note: 'Free-text description of conditions under which SLA performance failures are excluded from penalty calculations, such as force majeure events, customs delays, natural disasters, consignee unavailability, or regulatory holds. Sourced from contract terms.']
  grace_period_hours varchar [note: 'Number of hours beyond the committed delivery time that are tolerated before a shipment is classified as a late delivery for SLA measurement purposes. Accounts for minor operational variances and is agreed upon at contract negotiation.']
  incentive_clause_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial incentive or rebate clause is in effect for over-performance against the SLA targets. When True, the incentive_rate_pct field defines the reward structure.']
  incentive_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the applicable freight charge or contract value awarded as a financial incentive or rebate for each percentage point of SLA over-performance above the committed target. Active only when incentive_clause_applicable is True.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this SLA entitlement record was most recently updated, in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset. Supports change detection, incremental processing, and audit compliance in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this SLA entitlement was last formally reviewed with the customer in a business review meeting. Used to track review cadence compliance and trigger upcoming review scheduling.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Description of the methodology used to measure SLA performance, including how on-time is defined (e.g., first delivery attempt, POD scan time, ePOD timestamp), exclusions (e.g., force majeure, customs delays, consignee unavailability), and data source (e.g., FourKites, SAP TM, Oracle TMS).']
  measurement_period varchar [note: 'Frequency at which SLA performance is measured and reported against the committed targets. Determines the rolling window for OTD, OTIF, and volume threshold calculations.']
  min_volume_shipment_count varchar [note: 'Minimum number of shipments the customer commits to tender per measurement period to qualify for the SLA entitlement terms. Failure to meet this threshold may trigger tier renegotiation or penalty clauses.']
  min_volume_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum volume in Twenty-Foot Equivalent Units (TEU) the customer commits to tender per measurement period. Applicable to ocean freight FCL/LCL services. TEU is the standard unit of container capacity measurement per IMO and FMC conventions.']
  min_volume_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum chargeable weight in kilograms the customer commits to tender per measurement period. Applicable primarily to air freight and express services where weight-based volume commitments are standard.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal SLA performance review with the customer. Derived from last_reviewed_date and review_frequency but stored explicitly to support scheduling and CRM activity management.']
  notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days advance notice required by either party to terminate or renegotiate this SLA entitlement before its effective_until_date. Governs contract exit obligations.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin geography of the lane covered by this SLA entitlement. Null if the entitlement is not lane-specific.']
  otd_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed On-Time Delivery (OTD) rate expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Defines the minimum proportion of shipments that must be delivered within the agreed transit time window to meet the SLA. Core KPI for logistics SLA performance measurement.']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contractually committed On-Time In-Full (OTIF) rate expressed as a percentage (0.00–100.00). Measures the proportion of shipments delivered both on time and with complete quantity/content as ordered. Stricter than OTD alone; commonly required by retail and e-commerce customers.']
  penalty_cap_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum financial penalty amount (in the contract currency) that can be levied against Transport Shipping in a single measurement period for SLA under-performance. Caps total financial exposure under the penalty clause.']
  penalty_clause_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a financial penalty clause is in effect for under-performance against the SLA targets. When True, the penalty_rate_pct and penalty_cap_amount fields define the financial exposure.']
  penalty_rate_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the applicable freight charge or contract value applied as a financial penalty for each percentage point of SLA under-performance. Active only when penalty_clause_applicable is True.']
  pickup_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which a shipment must be tendered for pickup to qualify for same-day transit under this SLA entitlement. Shipments tendered after this time are counted against the next business day for OTD measurement.']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Agreed frequency at which SLA performance is formally reviewed with the customer in a business review meeting. Drives scheduling of Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) and account management activities.']
  service_line varchar [note: 'Business service line or product family to which this SLA entitlement applies, e.g., Express Parcel Delivery, Freight Forwarding, E-Commerce Fulfillment, Contract Logistics, Customs Brokerage. Aligns with the core business processes of Transport Shipping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: express_parcel|freight_forwarding|supply_chain_mgmt|customs_brokerage|warehouse_distribution|ecommerce_fulfillment|last_mile|contract_logistics|cross_border — promote to reference product]']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'Transport or logistics service mode to which this SLA entitlement applies, e.g., Air Express, Ocean FCL, Road FTL, Road LTL, Rail, Parcel. Determines which operational processes are governed by this entitlement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air_express|ocean_fcl|ocean_lcl|road_ftl|road_ltl|rail|parcel|last_mile|warehouse — promote to reference product]']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'Commercial tier classification of the SLA entitlement, reflecting the level of service commitment and associated pricing. Platinum represents the highest commitment level; standard represents baseline service.']
  transit_time_guarantee_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of calendar days from shipment pickup to delivery guaranteed under this SLA entitlement. Defines the transit time window used to evaluate OTD compliance. Expressed in whole days; fractional-day commitments use transit_time_guarantee_hours.']
  transit_time_guarantee_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum number of hours from shipment pickup to delivery guaranteed under this SLA entitlement. Used for express and time-definite services where day-level granularity is insufficient (e.g., next-day AM, same-day delivery). Takes precedence over transit_time_guarantee_days when both are populated.']

  Note: 'Defines service level entitlements and volume commitments granted to a customer account, specifying OTD targets, OTIF commitments, transit time guarantees, pickup cut-off times, claims resolution SLAs, escalation thresholds, and minimum volume thresholds (TEU, kg, shipment count) by service line or lane. Captures SLA tier, applicable service modes, performance measurement methodology, penalty/incentive clauses for under/over-performance, measurement periods, and effective date ranges. Distinct from contract domain SLA templates — this is the customer-specific instantiated entitlement.'
}

Table customer.service_preference {
  service_preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer service preference record. Primary key for the service_preference data product within the customer domain.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Customer service preferences (incoterms, service level, transport mode) are contractually defined in a master agreement. Logistics ops teams must validate that preferences align with contracted terms;']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Customers with AEO or C-TPAT compliance program membership require specific service preferences (expedited clearance, reduced examination). Logistics operators configure service preferences directly a']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for whom these service preferences are defined. Links to the master customer record in the customer domain.']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'The customer\'s EDI interchange partner identifier (ISA qualifier + ID or EDIFACT interchange address) used for automated B2B document exchange including ASNs, invoices, and shipment status messages. Applicable for enterprise B2B customers.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s preferred warehouse or distribution center for order fulfillment and storage operations. Used in Manhattan WMS for inventory slotting and order routing.']
  preferred_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: customs_clearance_preference is plain text on service_preference. Customers designate preferred customs brokers/agents for import and export clearance. This drives automated agent assignment in custom']
  preferred_carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred_service_level as plain text. Automated booking systems require a FK to the actual carrier service record to apply correct rates, transit times, and equip']
  preferred_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred lanes for routing optimization. Powers TMS routing engine, enforces customer-specific lane selection rules, and ensures service level compliance. Critica']
  preferred_service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: Customer service preferences specify preferred corridors driving TMS lane selection and rate negotiation. Existing FK covers preferred_lane_id but not corridor. Logistics domain experts expect service']
  preferred_tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Customers in e-commerce logistics designate a preferred 3PL for warehousing and fulfillment. This preference drives automated warehouse assignment and WMS integration. No existing 3PL FK on service_pr']
  primary_service_preferred_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer\'s preferred carrier for shipment execution. Used in SAP TM carrier selection and Oracle TMS freight audit to prioritize carrier assignment when capacity and rates are comparable.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Service preferences specify which rate card applies to customer\'s shipments. Critical for multi-rate-card customers (different cards for air vs ocean, domestic vs international). Drives rating engine ']
  service_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_scope. Business justification: Service preferences must be validated against the contracted service scope to ensure customers only request services covered by their agreement. Booking validation and order acceptance processes depen']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: Customer service preferences define the applicable transit time standard (e.g., 3-day express vs. 7-day economy). TMS uses this during shipment planning to apply the correct standard. Logistics domain']
  aeo_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official AEO certificate number issued by the relevant customs authority. Used in Descartes Customs for automated declaration pre-population and expedited clearance routing. Applicable only when aeo_certified is true.']
  aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer holds an Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, entitling them to expedited customs processing and reduced inspection rates. Stored to enable AEO-based routing and compliance benefits in Descartes Customs.']
  asn_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires an Advanced Shipping Notice (ASN) to be transmitted prior to shipment departure. When true, ASN generation is triggered in Manhattan WMS and transmitted via EDI (X12 856 or EDIFACT DESADV) to the customer.']
  awb_type_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preference for Air Waybill (AWB) type on air freight shipments. \'HAWB\' (House Air Waybill) is issued by the freight forwarder; \'MAWB\' (Master Air Waybill) is issued by the airline; \'either\' allows system-driven selection. Impacts IATA cargo documentation and customs filing.']
  bl_type_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preference for Bill of Lading (BOL) type on ocean freight shipments. \'HBL\' (House Bill of Lading) is issued by the NVOCC or freight forwarder; \'MBL\' (Master Bill of Lading) is issued by the ocean carrier. Impacts FMC compliance and customs filing in Descartes Customs.']
  co2e_offset_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preference for carbon offset participation. \'none\' means no offset; \'voluntary\' means the customer opts into voluntary carbon offset programs; \'mandatory\' means offset is contractually required. Supports CORSIA compliance and sustainability reporting.']
  cod_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) collection is enabled for this customer\'s shipments. When true, delivery drivers are authorized to collect payment at the point of delivery. Relevant for B2C and e-commerce fulfillment scenarios.']
  cod_max_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary value authorized for COD collection per shipment for this customer. Enforced during last-mile delivery dispatch to manage cash handling risk. Expressed in the customer\'s default currency.']
  container_load_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preference for ocean freight container loading. \'FCL\' (Full Container Load) provides exclusive use of a container; \'LCL\' (Less Than Container Load) consolidates cargo with other shippers. Drives container booking logic in SAP TM and Oracle TMS load optimization.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service preference record was first created in the system. Sourced from Salesforce CRM customer preference creation event. Used for audit trail and data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  customs_clearance_preference varchar [note: 'Indicates how the customer prefers customs clearance to be managed. \'self_clearance\' means the customer handles their own customs filings; \'broker_managed\' means a third-party customs broker is used; \'transport_managed\' means Transport Shipping handles clearance as part of the service. Drives workflow routing in Descartes Customs.']
  declared_value_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is required to declare the cargo value on all shipments. When true, shipment booking validation enforces declared value entry, which is used for customs valuation in Descartes Customs and insurance calculation.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code representing the customer\'s preferred billing and invoicing currency for freight charges. Used in SAP S/4HANA Finance revenue recognition and SAP TM charge calculation.']
  default_incoterms varchar [note: 'Customer\'s default International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) governing risk and cost transfer for shipments. Applied automatically during shipment booking in SAP TM. Aligns with ICC Incoterms 2020 rules and WTO trade compliance requirements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  dim_weight_method varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate dimensional weight (DIM weight) for rating and billing purposes. Determines whether IATA volumetric divisor, carrier-specific divisor, or IMDG rules apply. Impacts freight charge calculation in SAP TM.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this service preference profile becomes active and is applied to shipment bookings. Supports seasonal and project-based preference management.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this service preference profile expires and is no longer applied. Nullable for open-ended preferences. Supports contract-aligned and seasonal preference windows.']
  epod_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires an electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) to be captured and transmitted upon shipment completion. When true, last-mile delivery drivers must capture digital signature or photo confirmation. Supports SLA compliance and invoice release workflows.']
  hazmat_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer is authorized to ship hazardous materials (hazmat) under IMDG Code (ocean), IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (air), or DOT regulations (road). When true, hazmat documentation and compliance checks are triggered in Descartes Customs.']
  insurance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires cargo insurance to be arranged by Transport Shipping for their shipments by default. When true, insurance coverage is automatically included in shipment booking and charge calculation in SAP TM.']
  label_format varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred shipping label output format for parcel and freight labels. ZPL (Zebra Programming Language) is standard for thermal printers; PDF for laser printing; EPL for legacy Eltron printers; PNG for digital/web display. Configured in Oracle TMS shipment execution.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service preference record was most recently updated. Tracks preference changes for audit compliance under SOX and GDPR accountability requirements. Used for incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred channel for shipment status notifications and alerts. Drives automated notification dispatch in FourKites real-time visibility platform and Salesforce CRM service communications. EDI applies to B2B enterprise customers using EDIFACT or X12 messaging.']
  notification_events varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of shipment lifecycle events that trigger customer notifications (e.g., \'booked,picked_up,in_transit,customs_cleared,out_for_delivery,delivered,exception\'). Configures FourKites exception management and Salesforce CRM service case alerts.']
  preference_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for this service preference profile, used in EDI integrations, API calls, and customer-facing communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM customer preference records.']
  preference_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable label for this service preference profile, enabling customers and operations staff to distinguish between multiple preference sets (e.g., \'Standard B2B Freight\', \'E-Commerce Express\').']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service preference record. \'active\' means preferences are applied to new shipment bookings; \'inactive\' means preferences are archived; \'pending\' means awaiting customer or operations approval; \'suspended\' means temporarily disabled.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the preference profile indicating its scope and application context. \'default\' applies to all shipments unless overridden; \'override\' supersedes defaults for specific lanes or periods; \'seasonal\' applies during defined date windows; \'project\' is tied to a specific contract or project.']
  preferred_destination_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer\'s default shipment destination country. Used in SAP TM route planning and Descartes Customs trade compliance screening for import declarations and HS code validation.']
  preferred_origin_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the customer\'s default shipment origin country. Used in SAP TM route planning and Descartes Customs trade compliance screening for export declarations.']
  preferred_packaging_type varchar [note: 'Customer\'s default packaging format for shipments, used to pre-populate booking forms and drive warehouse packing instructions in Manhattan WMS. Supports slotting optimization and labor management.']
  preferred_service_level varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred shipment service level tier governing transit time and cost trade-off. Maps to SLA entitlements and carrier product codes in SAP TM charge calculation and Oracle TMS load optimization.']
  preferred_transport_mode varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred primary mode of transport for shipments. Drives automated carrier and route selection in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Values align with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) modal classifications.']
  road_load_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preference for road freight load type. \'FTL\' (Full Truckload) provides exclusive truck capacity; \'LTL\' (Less Than Truckload) consolidates with other shipments. Used in SAP TM route planning and Oracle TMS load optimization.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this service preference record originated. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution in the Databricks Silver Layer. \'manual\' indicates direct data entry by operations staff.']
  special_handling_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of special handling instruction codes applicable to this customer\'s shipments by default (e.g., \'FRAGILE,TEMP_CTRL,HAZMAT,PERISHABLE,HIGH_VALUE,OVERSIZED\'). Drives warehouse handling procedures in Manhattan WMS and carrier booking requirements in SAP TM.']
  sustainability_reporting_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted into carbon emissions reporting for their shipments. When true, GHG (Greenhouse Gas) and CO2e (Carbon Dioxide Equivalent) data is calculated and included in customer sustainability reports, aligned with EU ETS and CORSIA requirements.']
  temp_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used to configure cold chain monitoring alerts in FourKites and warehouse environment controls in Manhattan WMS. Applicable only when temperature_controlled is true.']
  temp_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used to configure cold chain monitoring alerts in FourKites and warehouse environment controls in Manhattan WMS. Applicable only when temperature_controlled is true.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer\'s shipments require temperature-controlled handling and transport by default. When true, cold chain equipment and monitoring requirements are applied during booking in SAP TM and warehouse slotting in Manhattan WMS.']
  tracking_visibility_level varchar [note: 'Granularity of shipment tracking data shared with the customer via FourKites or customer portal. \'full\' provides GPS-level real-time updates; \'milestone\' provides key event updates (pickup, customs, delivery); \'summary\' provides ETA and final status only; \'none\' disables customer-facing tracking.']

  Note: 'Stores customer-level operational preferences governing how shipments are handled, routed, and communicated. Includes preferred transport modes, carrier preferences, packaging requirements, notification preferences (SMS/email/EDI), tracking visibility settings, default Incoterms, COD preferences, and special handling instructions (e.g., fragile, temperature-controlled, IMDG hazmat). Enables automated shipment booking defaults and personalized service delivery.'
}

Table customer.service_case {
  service_case_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer service case record in the Transport Shipping lakehouse. Primary key for the service_case data product.']
  cargo_claim_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to claim.cargo_claim. Business justification: service_case has claim_reference_number (denormalized) and claims_initiated flag, indicating service cases escalate into cargo claims. Linking enables case-to-claim lifecycle tracking, SLA breach esca']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Customer service cases often involve carrier performance issues (delays, damage, lost shipments). Direct carrier attribution enables escalation workflows, SLA breach tracking, root cause analysis, and']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: service_case has service_mode as plain text. Cases (claims, complaints, SLA breaches) are about specific carrier services. Linking to carrier_service enables root cause analysis by service type, SLA b']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment record associated with this service case, if the case relates to a specific shipment event or delivery failure.']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific customer contact person who submitted or is the primary point of contact for this service case.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that raised or is associated with this service case. Links to the customer master record.']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Service cases frequently address customs declaration issues (rejections, amendments, duty disputes). Required for: case-to-declaration traceability, root cause analysis, SLA tracking for customs clear']

Operations and compliance teams must link the service case directly to the scr']

  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Customer service cases for delivery failures, missing items, and SLA breaches are raised against specific fulfillment orders. service_case has tracking_number and consignment_id but no fulfillment_ord']

  network_event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_event. Linking to network_event enables ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_partner. Business justification: Service cases often involve non-carrier network partners (warehouse damage, customs clearance delays, 3PL errors). Customer service teams need direct partner attribution for escalation, performance tr']
  penalty_clause_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.penalty_clause.   plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_plan. Business justification: Service cases reference specific route plans that experienced execution failures. Essential for route execution issue tracking, delivery failure investigation, operational exception root cause analysi']
  route_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Last-mile service cases (delivery failures, surcharge disputes, window violations) are zone-specific. Linking service_case to route_delivery_zone enables zone-level exception analysis and SLA breach r']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Service cases are categorized by the customer\'s account segment for SLA prioritization, escalation routing, and performance reporting (e.g., enterprise-tier cases get different response targets than S']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: A service case is directly governed by a specific SLA entitlement — when a case is opened for SLA breach (sla_first_response_breached, sla_resolution_breached flags), it must reference the exact entit']
  spot_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.spot_quote. Business justification: Billing dispute and spot-quote-not-honored cases are a primary service case type in logistics. Customer service agents must reference the original spot_quote when resolving pricing disputes, overcharg']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Root-cause supplier attribution: when a service failure (damage, delay, mis-delivery) is caused by a procurement supplier (e.g., packaging vendor, IT provider, customs broker), the service_case must r']
  tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Fulfillment failure cases (wrong pick, late dispatch, damaged goods) involve 3PL providers as the responsible party. service_case links to carrier, agent, and partner but not 3PL. Required for 3PL per']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Air Waybill (AWB) number linked to this service case when the case relates to an air freight shipment. Follows IATA AWB format: 3-digit airline prefix followed by 8-digit serial number. Enables direct cross-reference to air freight documentation.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading (BOL) number linked to this service case when the case relates to an ocean or road freight shipment. Enables cross-reference to freight forwarding documentation and carrier records.']
  case_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service case record was first created in Salesforce CRM. This is the principal business event timestamp representing when the customer interaction was formally logged. Used as the baseline for all SLA (Service Level Agreement) calculations.']
  case_description varchar [note: 'Full narrative description of the customer\'s issue, complaint, or inquiry as captured at case creation. May include shipment details, service failure context, or customer-provided evidence.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable case reference number assigned by Salesforce CRM at case creation. Used in all customer-facing communications and SLA tracking. Format: CS- followed by 8–12 digits.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current workflow state of the service case lifecycle. Drives operational queues, SLA breach monitoring, and customer communication triggers. Transitions: new → in_progress → pending_customer | escalated → resolved → closed.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service case by the nature of the customer interaction. Drives routing, SLA assignment, and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipment_inquiry|service_failure|complaint|claims_initiation|billing_dispute|general_support|customs_query|returns_rma — promote to reference product if additional types are added]']
  claims_initiated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service case has triggered the initiation of a formal cargo claim in the claims domain. True when the case type is claims_initiation and a claim record has been created. Provides the handoff signal between customer service workflow and financial liability management.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service case was formally closed after customer confirmation or automatic closure policy. Distinct from resolved_timestamp — a case may be resolved but remain open pending customer acknowledgement.']
  customer_feedback_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text verbatim feedback provided by the customer as part of the post-case satisfaction survey. Complements the numeric CSAT score with qualitative insight for service improvement analysis.']
  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer upon case closure, typically on a 1–5 scale. Sourced from Salesforce CRM post-case survey. Key KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for service quality measurement and agent performance management.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment destination location referenced in this service case. Used for cross-border service failure analysis, last-mile delivery issue routing, and trade compliance case categorization.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'The highest escalation tier reached by this case. Populated when is_escalated is True. Used for escalation pattern analysis and workforce capacity planning. None indicates no escalation occurred.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or structured reason provided by the agent when escalating the case. Documents the business justification for escalation, such as SLA breach risk, high-value customer, or regulatory sensitivity.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the most recent escalation event was triggered for this case. Null if the case has never been escalated. Used for escalation response time analysis.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the first substantive agent response was sent to the customer after case creation. Used to calculate SLA (Service Level Agreement) first-response compliance and OTD (On-Time Delivery) of service commitments.']
  is_escalated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service case has been formally escalated to a senior agent, team lead, or management tier at any point in its lifecycle. True if any escalation event has occurred.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service case record was most recently updated in the source system (Salesforce CRM). Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail maintenance in the Silver layer.']
  last_reopened_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the case was most recently reopened after a prior resolution or closure. Null if the case has never been reopened. Used alongside reopen_count for resolution quality analytics.']
  origin_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel through which the customer submitted this service case. Used for channel analytics, staffing optimization, and omnichannel service reporting. EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) channel applies to B2B automated case submissions.']
