  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin location referenced in this service case. Used for cross-border service failure analysis, customs-related case routing, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  pod_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requested Proof of Delivery (POD) or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) document has been successfully retrieved and attached to this case. Used to track case resolution blockers.']
  pod_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Proof of Delivery (POD) or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) document has been requested as part of resolving this service case. Common for delivery dispute and non-receipt inquiry cases.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the service case, determining response time targets and escalation thresholds per the customer SLA entitlement. Critical cases typically involve time-sensitive freight or enterprise account failures.']
  reopen_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this service case has been reopened after being marked as resolved or closed. A non-zero value indicates resolution quality issues. Used for first-contact resolution (FCR) KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting.']
  resolution_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code describing the action taken to resolve the service case. Distinct from root cause — this captures what was done, not why it happened. Used for resolution effectiveness analytics and SLA compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: reshipped|refunded|credited|apology_issued|information_provided|escalated_to_claim|no_action_required|other — promote to reference product]']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative documenting the specific resolution actions taken, agent findings, and any commitments made to the customer. Captured at case closure and retained for audit and quality review.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the service case was first marked as resolved. Used to calculate total resolution time, SLA (Service Level Agreement) resolution compliance, and agent productivity metrics.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of the underlying root cause identified during case investigation. Critical for operational improvement, KPI (Key Performance Indicator) reporting, and recurring failure analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier_delay|customs_hold|documentation_error|damage_in_transit|lost_shipment|billing_error|system_error|weather_event|address_issue|other — promote to reference product]']
  service_mode varchar [note: 'The transport mode or service type associated with the shipment or service failure referenced in this case. Drives routing to specialized service teams (e.g., air freight team vs. last-mile delivery team).']
  sla_first_response_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the first response SLA (Service Level Agreement) target was breached for this case. True if the first_response_timestamp exceeded the sla_response_target_hours from case creation. Key KPI (Key Performance Indicator) for customer service quality reporting.']
  sla_resolution_breached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the resolution SLA (Service Level Agreement) target was breached for this case. True if the resolved_timestamp exceeded the sla_resolution_target_hours from case creation. Drives escalation workflows and executive service quality dashboards.']
  sla_resolution_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the case must be fully resolved, as defined by the applicable SLA (Service Level Agreement) for this account segment and priority combination.']
  sla_response_target_hours varchar [note: 'The contracted or policy-defined maximum number of hours within which the first agent response must be delivered to the customer, as defined by the applicable SLA (Service Level Agreement) for this account segment and priority combination.']
  source_system_case_code varchar [note: 'The native case identifier from Salesforce CRM (e.g., Salesforce Case ID / 18-character Salesforce record ID). Retained for lineage traceability, reconciliation with the source system, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration cross-referencing.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Short, descriptive title of the service case as entered by the customer or agent. Used for case list views, search, and initial triage. Typically 255 characters or fewer.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Customer-facing parcel or shipment tracking number associated with this service case. Used for last-mile delivery inquiries, POD (Proof of Delivery) disputes, and express parcel service failures.']

  Note: 'Customer service case records sourced from Salesforce CRM capturing complaints, service failures, shipment inquiries, claims initiation requests, and general support interactions. Tracks case number, case type, priority, status, root cause category, resolution details, SLA breach flag, escalation history, and linked shipment or AWB references. Distinct from the claim domain which manages financial liability — this entity manages the customer service workflow.'
}

Table customer.onboarding {
  onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the customer onboarding record. Primary key for the onboarding data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master service agreement or contract established for the customer during or prior to onboarding. Links the onboarding record to the governing commercial contract.']
  compliance_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.compliance_program. Business justification: Customer onboarding in logistics explicitly includes customs compliance program enrollment (AEO, C-TPAT, ISA). The onboarding record must reference the target compliance program to track enrollment pr']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account being onboarded. Links the onboarding lifecycle record to the master customer entity.']
  customs_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Customer onboarding has customs_setup_status and customs_registration_required. A specific customs agent/broker is assigned during onboarding for import/export registration. Role-prefixed as customs_a']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'The EDI interchange identifier assigned to the customer for electronic message exchange. Used as the sender/receiver qualifier in EDI transactions (e.g., ISA segment in ANSI X12 or UNB segment in EDIFACT).']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Customer onboarding includes WMS provisioning at a specific warehouse facility (onboarding.wms_provisioning_status tracks this). The assigned facility must be referenced to coordinate storage allocati']
  importer_trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Customer onboarding includes trade party registration for customs purposes. Required for: importer of record setup, EORI/tax ID validation, AEO/CTPAT pre-certification checks, and ensuring customer ca']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Customs license and permit verification is a mandatory onboarding compliance step for importers and exporters in logistics. The onboarding record must reference the verified license_permit to document']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.merchant. Business justification: In e-commerce logistics, customer onboarding for fulfillment services creates a merchant entity. The onboarding record must reference the resulting merchant to track provisioning completion, go-live r']
  origin_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Customer onboarding configures the origin network node (warehouse, port, hub) the customer ships from. Node-level provisioning tracking (WMS/TMS setup, customs bonding) requires this link. Logistics d']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Carrier setup is a named step in logistics customer onboarding (EDI configuration, rate loading, booking system provisioning). service_scope is plain text. Role-prefixed as primary_carrier_id. Require']
  primary_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Onboarding processes require tracking primary customer contact for implementation coordination, training scheduling, system provisioning, and go-live communication. Essential for customer onboarding w']
  primary_lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Customer onboarding in logistics includes activating specific trade lanes. The primary lane being onboarded must be tracked for lane-level provisioning status, TMS configuration, and go-live readiness']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the rate and pricing profile configured for the customer in the TMS rating engine during onboarding. Determines applicable tariffs, surcharges (BAF, GRI, THC), and discount structures.']
  segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: During customer onboarding, the customer is assigned to a market segment that governs their commercial tier, SLA entitlements, and service eligibility. The onboarding table currently stores customer_s']
  service_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_scope. Business justification: During customer onboarding, the contracted service scope defines which services to provision (WMS, TMS, EDI, customs). onboarding.service_scope is a plain-text denormalization. Replacing it with a FK ']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Customer onboarding in freight forwarding assigns the customer to a published tariff schedule (distinct from a negotiated rate card). Tariff-based onboarding is required for regulatory compliance and ']
  tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Customer onboarding includes wms_provisioning_status — WMS provisioning requires knowing which 3PL is being configured. The 3PL setup is a named onboarding checklist item in logistics. No existing 3PL']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Logistics-specific classification of the customer account role within the supply chain. Determines applicable service offerings, EDI message sets, and regulatory compliance requirements. Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier (NVOCC) and freight forwarder types trigger additional regulatory checks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: shipper|consignee|freight_forwarder|nvocc|broker|carrier|agent — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  actual_go_live_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the customer was activated and went live on Transport Shipping systems. Compared against target_go_live_date to measure onboarding cycle time adherence and SLA compliance.']
  aml_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Anti-Money Laundering (AML) screening check performed during onboarding. Includes sanctions list screening, denied party checks, and financial crime risk assessment. A flagged or rejected status blocks account activation.']
  api_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of the API integration setup for customers using direct API connectivity to Transport Shipping platforms for booking, tracking, and rate queries. Distinct from EDI for customers using REST/SOAP API channels.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer onboarding was initiated and managed. Distinguishes between digital self-serve (customer-led portal) and managed (assigned onboarding manager) approaches, supporting channel performance analytics.']
  checklist_completion_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of onboarding checklist items that have been completed. Provides a single numeric indicator of overall onboarding progress for dashboards and SLA monitoring. Calculated from checklist item statuses in the onboarding checklist sub-entity.']
  completed_checklist_items varchar [note: 'Number of onboarding checklist items that have been marked as completed. Used alongside total_checklist_items to track onboarding progress and identify bottlenecks.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding record was first created in the system. Serves as the audit trail anchor for the onboarding lifecycle. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  credit_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the credit risk assessment conducted during customer onboarding. Determines credit terms, payment conditions, and credit limit to be assigned. Conditionally approved may indicate a reduced credit limit or requirement for prepayment.']
  credit_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the credit limit and financial terms assigned during onboarding (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The approved credit limit (in the account currency) assigned to the customer following credit assessment. Governs the maximum outstanding receivables balance permitted before shipment holds are triggered.']
  customs_registration_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer requires customs brokerage registration and trade compliance setup as part of onboarding. Triggers Descartes Customs configuration, HS code profile setup, and AEO/C-TPAT compliance checks.']
  customs_setup_status varchar [note: 'Status of the customs and trade compliance configuration in Descartes Customs for the customer. Includes denied party screening profile, tariff classification preferences, and import/export license registration.']
  dangerous_goods_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been certified and approved to ship dangerous goods (DG) through Transport Shipping. Triggers IMDG/ICAO DG compliance checks and special handling configuration during onboarding.']
  data_consent_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer provided explicit consent for data processing. Required for GDPR compliance audit trail and consent management.']
  data_processing_consent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent for processing of their personal and business data in accordance with GDPR and applicable data protection regulations. Must be TRUE before account activation.']
  edi_setup_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) integration setup for the customer. EDI enables automated exchange of shipment orders, ASNs, invoices, and status messages between the customer\'s systems and Transport Shipping platforms.']
  initiation_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer onboarding process was formally initiated, typically coinciding with account creation or CRM opportunity conversion. Used as the start anchor for SLA and cycle time calculations.']
  kyc_aml_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which both the Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti-Money Laundering (AML) verification processes were successfully completed and approved for this customer onboarding.']
  kyc_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the Know Your Customer (KYC) identity verification process for the customer. Mandatory regulatory due diligence step before account activation. Tracks progression from pending submission through review, approval, or rejection.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the onboarding record. Used for change tracking, data freshness monitoring, and incremental ETL processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the assigned onboarding manager. Retained for operational reference and reporting without requiring a join to the HR system.']
  on_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the onboarding process has been placed on hold. Used for exception management, escalation tracking, and reporting on onboarding bottlenecks. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: kyc_aml_pending|credit_review|document_missing|customer_unresponsive|system_issue|legal_review|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the onboarding record, distinct from the granular stage. Indicates whether the onboarding is actively progressing, completed, paused, or terminated.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of business registration for the customer being onboarded. Used for regulatory jurisdiction determination, trade compliance, and AML risk profiling.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms code assigned to the customer account during onboarding (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD, PREPAID). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance and governs invoice due date calculations.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally communicable reference number assigned to the onboarding case. Used in customer communications and cross-system tracking (e.g., ONB-2024-000123).']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing regulatory due diligence observations, compliance exceptions, or special conditions noted during the onboarding process. Supports audit trail requirements under C-TPAT, AEO, and GDPR accountability obligations.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the reason for onboarding rejection or cancellation. Populated when onboarding_status is rejected or cancelled. Supports compliance audit trails and customer communication.']
  sla_tier varchar [note: 'The SLA tier assigned to the customer during onboarding, determining the service performance commitments, response times, and KPI targets applicable to the account. Drives SLA entitlement configuration in TMS and CRM.']
  stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the end-to-end customer onboarding lifecycle. Tracks progression from initial account creation through KYC/AML, credit assessment, system provisioning, training, and final activation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: initiated|kyc_aml_review|credit_assessment|system_provisioning|training|go_live_pending|active|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  target_go_live_date date [note: 'The planned date by which the customer is expected to be fully activated and operationally live on Transport Shipping systems. Used for SLA tracking and resource planning.']
  tms_provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Status of the customer account provisioning in the Transportation Management System (TMS — SAP TM or Oracle TMS). Tracks whether the customer has been set up for shipment booking, freight order management, and rate access.']
  total_checklist_items varchar [note: 'Total number of onboarding checklist items assigned to this customer onboarding record. Used as the denominator for checklist completion percentage and for workload tracking.']
  training_completed_date date [note: 'The date on which the customer completed all required onboarding training modules. Used to confirm readiness for go-live and to satisfy compliance documentation requirements.']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Status of the customer training program completion during onboarding. Training covers platform usage, booking procedures, compliance requirements, and service-specific processes. Waived status applies for experienced customers or self-serve digital onboarding.']
  wms_provisioning_status varchar [note: 'Status of the customer account provisioning in the Warehouse Management System (WMS — Manhattan WMS). Applicable for customers requiring warehousing, distribution, or e-commerce fulfillment services.']

  Note: 'Tracks the end-to-end customer onboarding lifecycle from initial account creation through activation, including KYC/AML verification, credit assessment, system provisioning (TMS/WMS access, EDI setup), and training completion. Captures onboarding stage, assigned onboarding manager, checklist item statuses, go-live date, and onboarding channel (digital self-serve vs. managed). Ensures compliance with regulatory due diligence requirements.'
}

Table customer.credit_profile {
  credit_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the customer credit profile record in the Transport Shipping lakehouse. Primary key for this entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Credit limits, payment terms, and credit status in a customer\'s credit profile are governed by the master service agreement. Credit underwriting and limit approval processes require tracing the credit']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account for which this credit profile is maintained. Links to the customer master record.']
  approved_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The formally approved credit limit as of the most recent credit review, which may differ from the currently active credit_limit if a pending change is awaiting activation. Maintains the audit trail of the last approved value.']
  available_credit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining credit headroom calculated as credit_limit minus outstanding_balance minus open_order_exposure. Stored as a business-critical operational field (not a derived KPI) because it is directly consumed by SAP TM shipment release checks and Oracle TMS load authorization gates.']
  billing_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which invoices are issued to this customer. May differ from credit_currency when the customer operates in a different functional currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer master.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this credit profile record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail start point required for SOX compliance.']
  credit_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external credit reference agency that provided the credit rating or score (e.g., Dun & Bradstreet, Experian, Equifax, Coface, Euler Hermes). Required for audit trail and credit review documentation.']
  credit_agency_reference varchar [note: 'Unique reference number or DUNS number assigned by the external credit agency for this customer. Used to cross-reference credit bureau reports and validate the source of the credit assessment.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP organizational unit (credit control area code) within which this customer\'s credit limit is managed. Defines the scope of credit monitoring and aligns with the company code structure in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  credit_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the credit limit, outstanding balance, and exposure amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Aligns with the customer\'s billing currency in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  credit_hold_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the credit hold was most recently applied to this account. Used for aging analysis of holds and SLA tracking for hold resolution.']
  credit_hold_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer account is currently under a credit hold, blocking the release of new shipments and freight orders in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. True = hold active; False = no hold. Operationally critical for shipment gating.']
  credit_hold_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the credit hold was applied. Used by finance and operations teams to prioritize resolution actions and communicate with customers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: overdue_invoices|credit_limit_exceeded|payment_dispute|bankruptcy_risk|manual_hold|compliance_review|other — promote to reference product]']
  credit_hold_released_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the most recent credit hold was lifted, allowing shipment release to resume. Null if the hold is still active. Used for hold duration analytics and finance KPI reporting.']
  credit_insurance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer\'s credit exposure is covered by a trade credit insurance policy (e.g., Euler Hermes, Coface, Atradius). True = insured; False = uninsured. Affects net credit risk exposure and provisioning under IFRS 9.']
  credit_insurance_policy_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number of the trade credit insurance policy covering this customer\'s receivables. Populated only when credit_insurance_flag is True. Used for claims processing and insurer reporting.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum approved credit exposure (in the credit currency) that Transport Shipping will extend to this customer at any point in time. Enforced by SAP S/4HANA Finance credit management to gate shipment release and invoice issuance.']
  credit_limit_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee ID of the finance officer or credit manager who formally approved the current credit limit. Required for SOX audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  credit_limit_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the approved credit limit currently utilized, calculated as (outstanding_balance + open_order_exposure) / credit_limit × 100. Stored as a business-critical operational field consumed by SAP TM shipment release checks and finance dashboards — not a derived analytics KPI.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'External or internal credit rating assigned to the customer, using a standardized rating scale (e.g., S&P/Moody\'s-aligned: AAA through D, or NR for Not Rated). Drives credit limit approval tiers and risk-based pricing decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|CC|C|D|NR — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  credit_score varchar [note: 'Numeric credit score assigned by an external credit agency or internal scoring model, typically on a scale of 0–1000 or 300–850 depending on the agency. Used alongside credit_rating for granular risk segmentation and credit limit setting.']
  credit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the credit profile. \'on_hold\' triggers shipment release blocks in SAP TM and Oracle TMS, preventing new freight orders from being executed until the hold is lifted by the finance team.']
  days_sales_outstanding decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of days taken by this customer to pay invoices, calculated from SAP S/4HANA Finance AR data. A key credit risk indicator stored as a business field (not a computed KPI) because it is sourced directly from the credit management system and used in credit limit decisions.']
  early_payment_discount_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from invoice date within which the customer must pay to qualify for the early payment discount. Paired with early_payment_discount_pct to define the discount window.']
  early_payment_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered to the customer for early payment of invoices within a specified discount period (e.g., 2% if paid within 10 days). Defined in SAP S/4HANA Finance payment terms configuration.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the current credit terms (limit, payment terms, rating) became effective for this customer. Supports bi-temporal data modeling and credit history tracking.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the current credit terms expire and a review is required. Null for open-ended profiles. Triggers automated credit review workflows in SAP S/4HANA Finance when the date approaches.']
  insured_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the total credit limit that is covered by trade credit insurance. May be less than or equal to credit_limit. Used to calculate net uninsured exposure for IFRS 9 provisioning.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recently completed formal credit review for this customer. Used to determine review frequency compliance and trigger overdue review alerts.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this credit profile record, in ISO 8601 format. Required for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver layer and SOX audit trail.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal credit review of this customer account. Automatically calculated based on review_frequency_months from the last_review_date. Drives workflow triggers in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  open_order_exposure decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of freight orders and shipments that have been accepted but not yet invoiced, representing committed but unbilled credit exposure. Sourced from SAP TM freight order management and used in real-time credit checks.']
  outstanding_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current total of unpaid invoices and open receivables against this credit profile, expressed in the credit currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance Accounts Receivable (AR) sub-ledger. Used to calculate available credit headroom.']
  overdue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total value of invoices that have passed their payment due date and remain unpaid, expressed in the credit currency. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance AR aging. Triggers credit hold workflows when thresholds are breached.']
  overdue_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the oldest unpaid overdue invoice has been past its due date. Used for collection prioritization, credit hold triggers, and IFRS 9 expected credit loss staging.']
  payment_behavior_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the customer\'s historical payment behavior based on Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) analysis from SAP S/4HANA Finance AR aging reports. Informs credit risk assessment and collection prioritization.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Finance payment terms key (e.g., NT30, NT60, NT90, ZB14) defining the contractual payment due date schedule for invoices raised against this customer. Governs net 30, net 60, net 90, or early-payment discount structures.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of the standard payment due period in calendar days (e.g., 30, 60, 90). Complements payment_terms_code for analytics, SLA reporting, and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) calculations in the finance domain.']
  profile_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the credit profile, used in correspondence with credit agencies, billing teams, and finance operations. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance customer credit management (FD32).']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the credit profile by customer segment: enterprise B2B accounts, small-to-medium enterprises (SME), individual B2C shippers, Third-Party Logistics (3PL) partners, Fourth-Party Logistics (4PL) partners, or government entities. Drives credit policy rules and approval workflows. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: enterprise|sme|individual|3pl_partner|4pl_partner|government — promote to reference product]']
  requested_credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Credit limit amount requested by the customer or proposed by the sales/account management team (Salesforce CRM opportunity data), pending formal credit review and approval by the finance team.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Frequency in calendar months at which this customer\'s credit profile must be formally reviewed (e.g., 3, 6, 12, 24). Determined by credit risk tier: high-risk accounts reviewed quarterly, standard accounts annually.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'Internal risk classification assigned to the customer based on credit score, payment history, and financial exposure. Drives credit limit approval authority levels, review frequency, and shipment release policies.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM Account record ID linked to this credit profile, enabling cross-reference between the finance credit management system and the CRM for account management, opportunity tracking, and service case correlation.']
  sap_credit_account_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA Finance customer credit account number (10-digit SAP debtor number) used in FD32 credit management transactions. The system-of-record identifier for reconciliation with SAP AR and credit management modules.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cash deposit or bank guarantee amount held by Transport Shipping as collateral against the customer\'s credit exposure. Reduces the net unsecured credit risk. Tracked in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  security_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial security or collateral provided by the customer to support their credit facility. Includes cash deposits, bank guarantees, letters of credit (LC), parent company guarantees, or none for unsecured accounts.']

  Note: 'Financial credit standing and payment terms for customer accounts, governing credit limit, payment terms (net 30/60/90), credit rating, credit hold status, outstanding balance thresholds, and currency of credit. Captures credit review dates, credit agency references, and approved credit limit history. Used by billing and finance domains to enforce payment controls and by operations to gate shipment release on credit-held accounts.'
}

Table customer.partner_relationship {
  partner_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the logistics partner relationship record in the Transport Shipping data platform. Primary key for this entity.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: When partner_type is \'agent\' (customs broker, freight agent), a direct FK to agent enables agent-specific relationship management (commission structure, territory, IATA accreditation). Required for ag']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Partner relationships (3PL, broker, agent) are governed by formal agreements. partner_relationship.contract_reference_number is a plain-text denormalization of the agreement entity. Replacing it with ']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: When a partner relationship is with a carrier, the specific carrier agreement (rates, capacity, liability terms) governs the relationship. This is distinct from the master agreement link and enables c']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: partner_relationship tracks customer-partner relationships by type. When partner_type is \'carrier\', a direct FK to carrier enables carrier-specific contract management, performance rating, and capacit']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that owns or initiated this partner relationship. Links the partnership to the customer master record.']
  edi_partner_id varchar [note: 'The partner\'s EDI interchange identifier used in ISA segment of X12 transactions or UNB segment of EDIFACT messages. Uniquely identifies the partner in electronic manifest and shipment data exchanges with carriers, customs, and freight platforms.']
  partner_sla_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.partner_sla. Business justification: partner_relationship has sla_tier_code as plain text. The governing partner SLA record defines penalty/incentive terms and measurement methodology. Linking to partner_sla enables automated SLA monitor']
  partner_trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Partners (customs brokers, freight forwarders) are trade parties. Required for: customs broker assignment, power of attorney validation, broker license verification, and tracking broker performance on']
  primary_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Every formal logistics partnership relationship has a designated primary contact person. The partner_relationship table currently stores primary_contact_email, primary_contact_name, and primary_contac']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Partner relationships (brokers, agents, freight forwarders) operate under specific rate cards governing commercial terms. Partner rate management and commission calculation processes require direct re']
  service_scope_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.service_scope. Business justification: partner_relationship.authorized_service_scope is a plain-text denormalization of the contracted service scope. Replacing it with a FK to contract.service_scope enables enforcement of authorized servic']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Dual-role entity management: in logistics, a partner (subcontractor, customs broker, 3PL) is frequently also a procurement supplier. Linking partner_relationship to supplier enables consolidated vendo']
  tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: When partner_type is \'3PL\', a direct FK to tpl_provider enables 3PL-specific relationship management (warehousing scope, WMS integration status, geographic coverage). edi_partner_id → partner exists b']
  aeo_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Official AEO certificate number issued by the relevant customs authority. Required for customs pre-clearance, reduced inspection rates, and C-TPAT mutual recognition processing in Descartes Customs GTM.']
  aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner holds a valid AEO (Authorized Economic Operator) certification issued by a customs authority, confirming compliance with WCO SAFE Framework security and compliance standards. Impacts customs clearance speed and C-TPAT mutual recognition benefits.']
  api_connectivity_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the API (Application Programming Interface) integration between Transport Shipping\'s TMS/WMS platforms and the partner\'s systems. Enables real-time shipment status, booking, and track-and-trace data exchange via FourKites or direct API.']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this partner relationship is configured for automatic renewal upon reaching the effective_until_date. When true, the relationship extends for the standard renewal period defined in the master service agreement unless explicitly cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner relationship record was first created in the Transport Shipping data platform. Populated at insert time and never updated. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  ctpat_member boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner is a certified member of C-TPAT (Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism), the US CBP voluntary supply chain security program. C-TPAT membership enables expedited customs processing and reduced examination rates for US-bound shipments.']
  data_sharing_permission_level varchar [note: 'Governs the level of Transport Shipping customer and shipment data the partner is authorized to access and receive. None = no data shared; shipment_status = track-and-trace only; full_visibility = complete shipment and inventory data; restricted = custom scope defined in data sharing agreement. Enforces GDPR data minimization principles.']
  denied_party_screen_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent denied party and sanctions screening was performed for this partner. Used to enforce periodic re-screening compliance intervals as required by trade compliance policy and Descartes Customs GTM workflows.']
  denied_party_screened boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has been screened against denied party, restricted entity, and sanctions lists (OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Security Council Sanctions) via Descartes Customs GTM. Must be true before activating any partner relationship.']
  edi_connectivity_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration channel between Transport Shipping and the partner. Active indicates live message exchange; pending_setup indicates configuration in progress; testing indicates UAT phase; inactive indicates EDI is not in use.']
  edi_partner_qualifier varchar [note: 'Two-digit EDI interchange qualifier code identifying the partner\'s ID type in EDI X12 or UN/EDIFACT message headers (e.g., 01 = DUNS, 08 = UCC, ZZ = mutually defined). Required for AMS (Automated Manifest System) and ISF (Importer Security Filing) submissions.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the partner relationship becomes legally and operationally binding. Used for SLA entitlement activation, EDI connectivity enablement, and authorized service scope enforcement.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the partner relationship expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null indicates an open-ended relationship with no fixed end date. Used for contract renewal alerts and SLA expiry management.']
  geographic_coverage_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional zone identifiers representing the geographic territories where the partner is authorized to operate under this relationship. Drives lane-level partner assignment in SAP TM route planning.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'ICC Incoterms code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between the customer and the partner for shipments executed under this relationship. Governs liability transfer points in cross-border shipping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_certificate_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the partner\'s cargo and liability insurance certificate. Required for high-value shipment authorization and claims processing. Validated against minimum coverage thresholds defined in the master service agreement.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner\'s cargo and liability insurance certificate expires. Triggers renewal alerts and may result in relationship suspension if insurance lapses before renewal is confirmed.']
  kyc_verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the KYC verification was last completed for this partner. Used to enforce periodic re-verification schedules and compliance audit trails in SAP S/4HANA Finance and Salesforce CRM.']
  kyc_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has completed the KYC (Know Your Customer) due diligence verification process, including identity verification, beneficial ownership confirmation, and anti-money laundering checks. Required for financial settlement and high-value freight partnerships.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this partner relationship record in the data platform. Updated on every write operation. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner completed the onboarding process, including KYC verification, denied party screening, EDI/API setup, and SLA agreement execution. Marks the start of operational readiness for shipment assignment.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the partner within Transport Shipping\'s TMS (Transportation Management System) and WMS (Warehouse Management System). Used in EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) message routing and SAP TM carrier/partner master.']
  partner_legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the third-party logistics partner organization as recorded in official trade and customs documentation. Used for AWB (Air Waybill), BOL (Bill of Lading), and customs declaration filings.']
  partner_role varchar [note: 'Operational role the partner plays within the customer\'s logistics network. Subcontractor performs services under the customer\'s brand; agent acts on behalf of the customer; co_loader shares freight capacity; principal is the primary contracting party; associate is a non-binding collaborative partner.']
  partner_trading_name varchar [note: 'Commercial or brand name under which the partner operates, which may differ from the legal name. Used in operational communications, shipment documentation, and customer-facing reporting.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the logistics partner by operational role. 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provides outsourced logistics services; 4PL (Fourth-Party Logistics) manages the entire supply chain; NVOCC (Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier) issues house bills of lading; freight_broker arranges carrier capacity; customs_broker handles trade compliance; co_loader shares container capacity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 3PL|4PL|NVOCC|freight_broker|customs_broker|co_loader — promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the agreed payment terms for financial settlements with this partner (e.g., NET30, NET60, COD). Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Finance vendor master and Coupa procurement system. Governs AP payment scheduling.']
  performance_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Composite performance score for the partner on a 0.00–5.00 scale, derived from KPI (Key Performance Indicator) measurements including OTD (On-Time Delivery), OTIF (On-Time In-Full), claims ratio, and SLA compliance. Updated periodically from operational data. Used for carrier/partner selection scoring in SAP TM.']
  performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this partner relationship. Used to schedule periodic KPI assessments and SLA compliance audits per contractual obligations.']
  preferred_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the preferred settlement currency used in financial transactions with this partner (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Used in SAP S/4HANA Finance for AP/AR processing and GRI (General Rate Increase) charge calculations.']
  relationship_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the partner relationship, used in EDI communications, contract references, and partner-facing correspondence. Sourced from Salesforce CRM or SAP TM partner master.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the partner relationship. Active indicates the partnership is operational; suspended indicates temporary halt pending resolution; terminated indicates the relationship has ended; pending indicates onboarding in progress; under_review indicates compliance or performance review is underway.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days before the effective_until_date that a renewal notice must be issued to the partner. Drives automated alerts in Salesforce CRM and Coupa procurement workflows to initiate contract renewal negotiations.']
  salesforce_relationship_code varchar [note: 'Salesforce CRM record identifier for this partner relationship, enabling bi-directional synchronization between the data lakehouse silver layer and the CRM system of record for opportunity tracking and service case management.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Actual date on which the partner relationship was formally terminated. Distinct from effective_until_date which is the planned expiry. Populated when relationship_status transitions to \'terminated\'. Used for audit trails and regulatory reporting.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for the termination of the partner relationship. Used for partner lifecycle analytics, compliance audit trails, and procurement risk reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: contract_expiry|performance_failure|compliance_breach|mutual_agreement|insolvency|regulatory_action — promote to reference product]']
  transport_modes_authorized varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited set of transport modes the partner is authorized to handle under this relationship. Aligns with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) regulatory frameworks. Used in SAP TM carrier selection and Oracle TMS load optimization.']

  Note: 'Defines formal logistics partnership relationships between customer accounts and third-party logistics providers (3PL/4PL), freight brokers, NVOCCs, customs brokers, and co-loading partners. Captures relationship type, partner role (subcontractor/agent/co-loader), authorized service scopes, geographic coverage, data sharing permissions, EDI/API connectivity status, performance rating, and relationship effective dates. Governs customer-side partner network topology for multi-party shipment orchestration.'
}

Table freight.freight_order {
  freight_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the freight forwarding order record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Freight orders are executed under master service agreements governing pricing, SLA, and liability. Operations teams must link each order to its governing agreement for rate validation, SLA compliance ']
  cargo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.cargo. Business justification: freight_order references cargo characteristics (commodity_description, hs_code, packaging_type) that are authoritatively defined in the cargo master reference table. Adding cargo_id normalizes these d']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Each freight order is fulfilled under a specific carrier agreement defining carrier obligations, contracted rates, and liability limits. Logistics teams require this link to validate carrier performan']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'FK to network.carrier']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: A freight order is placed against a specific carrier service (e.g., weekly FCL, express air). SLA compliance validation, transit time commitments, and rate applicability all require knowing which carr']
  consignee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.consignee. Business justification: Freight order execution requires the operational consignee record (geocodes, delivery preferences, blacklist status, COD eligibility) for last-mile handoff planning and SLA compliance. The existing co']
  consignee_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignee party profile designated to receive the freight at destination. Used for delivery instructions, customs clearance, and proof of delivery.']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Ocean and intermodal freight orders are fulfilled using specific containers. Critical for container tracking, utilization reporting, repositioning decisions, and demurrage management. Core container s']
  contract_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.contract_rate. Business justification: Contract rates are the negotiated sell-side rates applied to customer freight orders. Linking enables revenue recognition, contract utilization tracking, and deviation reporting. A logistics expert ex']
  credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Credit-to-cash process: freight orders are accepted only after validating available credit against the customer\'s credit_profile (credit_status, available_credit, credit_limit). AR teams use this link']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account that placed this freight order. Links to the customer domain for billing, SLA entitlement, and account management.']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: Direct traceability from freight execution to the originating fulfillment order is required for end-to-end SLA reporting, merchant billing reconciliation, and customer order status dashboards. Logisti']
  importer_trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_party. Business justification: Freight orders must identify the importer of record for customs purposes. Critical for duty payment, compliance verification, AEO/C-TPAT status checks, and denied party screening at booking stage.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: The freight order is the primary billable unit; its invoice_id enables order-to-cash tracking, revenue recognition at order level, and customer invoice reconciliation — fundamental to logistics AR ope']
  lane_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.lane_commitment. Business justification: Lane commitments define contracted capacity and minimum volumes per trade lane. Linking freight orders to lane commitments enables real-time volume utilization tracking against contracted commitments ']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination trade lane associated with this freight order. Used for rate lookup, carrier selection, and network planning.']
  notification_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Freight orders require a designated notification contact for shipment updates, exception alerts, and POD confirmation. Role-prefixed \'notification_contact_id\' because freight_order may have multiple c']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Freight orders originating from warehouse facilities (e.g., export consolidation, cross-dock) need origin_facility_id for cargo readiness verification, facility performance metrics, and origin-to-carr']
  origin_supplier_site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_site. Business justification: Inbound freight pickup planning: the supplier site is the physical origin location for supplier-to-DC freight orders. Logistics teams need the supplier site reference on the freight order to coordinat']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Rate card traceability on freight orders is essential for margin analysis, billing reconciliation, and contract compliance reporting. Operations teams must know which rate card priced each order. No e']
  service_preference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_preference. Business justification: Freight order execution validates and auto-populates parameters (incoterms, packaging, carrier, mode) against the customer\'s standing service preferences. Ops teams use this link for order defaults an']
  shipper_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipper party profile responsible for tendering the freight. Identifies the origin party for customs, documentation, and liability purposes.']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: Freight orders must validate against customer SLA entitlements to enforce transit time guarantees, OTD/OTIF targets, and penalty/incentive clauses. Operations teams need this link for real-time SLA co']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: The governing tariff (ocean/air/road) determines applicable charges on a freight order. Logistics operations require tariff traceability on orders for freight audit, FMC compliance, and charge validat']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: Freight orders are governed by a transit time standard that defines the SLA (ETA, service level). SLA compliance reporting, on-time delivery KPIs, and customer SLA breach analysis at order level requi']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Freight orders are executed using specific transport assets (trucks, aircraft, vessels). Essential for asset utilization analysis, maintenance correlation with freight activity, and operational perfor']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the freight was delivered to the consignee. Used for POD (Proof of Delivery) confirmation, OTD and OTIF KPI measurement, and SLA compliance reporting.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the freight departed from the origin port or facility. Used for transit time calculation, carrier performance measurement, and OTD reporting.']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'Air Waybill (AWB) number assigned for air freight shipments. Serves as the primary transport document and contract of carriage for air mode. Null for non-air shipments. Follows IATA AWB numbering format.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'Bill of Lading (BOL) number for ocean or road freight shipments. Serves as the contract of carriage, receipt of goods, and document of title. Null for air shipments. Includes both MBL (Master Bill of Lading) and HBL (House Bill of Lading) scenarios.']
  booking_date date [note: 'The business date on which the freight order was accepted and confirmed by the carrier or freight forwarder. Represents the principal real-world event time for this transaction and is used for revenue recognition and SLA measurement start.']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The weight basis used for freight charge calculation — the higher of gross weight and dimensional weight. Stored explicitly to support freight audit, carrier invoice reconciliation, and revenue recognition.']
  co2e_emissions_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions in kilograms of CO2 equivalent (CO2e) for this freight order. Calculated based on transport mode, distance, and cargo weight. Required for EU ETS reporting, CORSIA compliance (air), and customer sustainability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight order record was first created in the source system (SAP TM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA measurement.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this freight order (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency operations and financial consolidation under IFRS.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the freight destination. Used for customs import declarations, trade compliance, duty calculation, and lane-based rating.']
  destination_port_or_facility varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE or internal facility code identifying the specific port, airport, CFS, ICD, or delivery facility at the destination. Used for last-mile planning, customs clearance, and THC calculation.']
  dim_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Calculated dimensional weight in kilograms derived from shipment volume using the applicable DIM factor (e.g., 1:6000 for air). The chargeable weight is the higher of gross weight and DIM weight. Stored as a business field to support charge audit and dispute resolution.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) at the destination port or facility. Used for delivery scheduling, customs pre-clearance, and OTD (On-Time Delivery) KPI measurement. Updated dynamically by FourKites predictive ETA engine.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) from the origin port or facility. Used for vessel/flight scheduling, carrier coordination, and customer notification. Sourced from SAP TM and updated via FourKites real-time visibility.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base freight charge amount for this order before surcharges and adjustments, in the billing currency. Represents the core revenue line for freight forwarding. Used for revenue recognition, P&L reporting, and freight audit.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms as declared by the shipper. Used for freight charge calculation, carrier capacity planning, and customs declarations. Mandatory field per IATA and IMO regulations.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between shipper and consignee. Governs customs clearance obligations, insurance liability, and freight charge allocation. Aligned with ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shipment contains dangerous goods (hazardous materials) requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance under IMDG Code (ocean), ICAO Technical Instructions (air), or ADR (road).']
  is_nvocc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this freight order is executed under an NVOCC (Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier) arrangement, where the company acts as a carrier to the shipper but uses another vessel operator. Affects BOL issuance, liability, and FMC filing requirements.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable business identifier for the freight order as assigned by SAP TM freight order management. Used in customer communications, EDI messages, and operational tracking.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight order from booking acceptance through final delivery. Drives operational workflows, exception management, and SLA monitoring. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|booked|confirmed|in_transit|customs_hold|at_destination|delivered|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the freight origin location. Used for customs export declarations, trade compliance screening, and lane-based rating.']
  origin_port_or_facility varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE or internal facility code identifying the specific port, airport, CFS (Container Freight Station), ICD (Inland Container Depot), or warehouse from which the freight departs. Used for scheduling, carrier assignment, and THC calculation.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual packages, cartons, pallets, or handling units in this freight order. Used for loading plans, warehouse receiving, and proof of delivery reconciliation.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Freight charge payment arrangement indicating whether charges are prepaid by the shipper, collected from the consignee, or billed to a third party. Drives invoicing workflow and accounts receivable allocation.']
  pod_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Proof of Delivery (POD) or ePOD (Electronic Proof of Delivery) has been received and confirmed for this freight order. Required for invoice release, claims prevention, and OTIF measurement.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Contracted service level commitment for this freight order defining the speed and priority of delivery. Drives SLA measurement, carrier selection, and premium charge application. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: same_day|next_day|two_day|standard|deferred|economy|priority — promote to reference product]']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the freight service arrangement. FTL (Full Truckload) and LTL (Less Than Truckload) apply to road; FCL (Full Container Load) and LCL (Less Than Container Load) apply to ocean. Drives pricing, consolidation logic, and carrier assignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy|charter — promote to reference product]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system of record from which this freight order was sourced. SAP_TM for orders originating from SAP Transportation Management; ORACLE_TMS for Oracle TMS-sourced orders. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Industry-standard special handling code indicating specific requirements such as temperature control, fragile, live animals, or oversized cargo. Uses IATA Special Handling Codes (e.g., PER for perishables, AVI for live animals, HEA for heavy cargo). Multiple codes may be concatenated.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'Temperature control requirement for the shipment. Applicable for pharmaceutical, perishable, and cold-chain freight. Drives equipment selection, carrier qualification, and GDP (Good Distribution Practice) compliance.']
  teu_count decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of TEUs (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units) allocated to this freight order for ocean container movements. Applicable for FCL and LCL ocean shipments. Used for vessel capacity planning, GRI application, and port statistics reporting.']
  total_order_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total billable amount for this freight order including base freight charges, all surcharges, and applicable taxes. Represents the net total for invoicing and revenue recognition. Equals freight_charge_amount plus total_surcharge_amount plus applicable taxes.']
  total_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate of all applicable surcharges for this freight order including BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor), GRI (General Rate Increase), THC (Terminal Handling Charge), fuel surcharge, and security surcharge. Stored as a total to support charge breakdown analysis.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this freight order. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (IATA for air, IMO for ocean), charge components (BAF, GRI, THC for ocean; fuel surcharge for air), and documentation requirements (AWB for air, BOL for ocean).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the freight order record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental loads, and audit compliance.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters (CBM). Used alongside gross weight for dimensional weight (DIM weight) calculation, LCL rating, and container utilization planning.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing a freight forwarding order across all transport modes (air, ocean, road, rail). Captures the full order lifecycle from booking acceptance through final delivery, including mode type (FTL, LTL, FCL, LCL), service level, origin/destination locations, commodity details, weight, volume (CBM), dimensional weight, incoterms reference, special handling requirements, and current order status. Acts as the primary anchor entity linking transport documents, charges, milestones, and exceptions. Sourced from SAP TM freight order management module.'
}

Table freight.booking {
  booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the freight booking record in the lakehouse silver layer. Primary key for the booking data product.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: A booking is placed by or through a forwarding agent. Agent-level booking tracking drives commission calculation, agent performance reporting, IATA billing, and dispute resolution. Logistics experts e']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Bookings reference customer agreements for rate validation and capacity allocation. Business process: contract rate lookup at booking time, volume commitment tracking, service level verification again']
  booking_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node entity representing the pickup or origin point for this booking (e.g., shipper warehouse, Container Freight Station (CFS), Inland Container Depot (ICD), or airport/seaport terminal).']
  cargo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.cargo. Business justification: booking captures cargo_description and hs_code which are authoritatively stored in the cargo master reference table. Adding cargo_id normalizes these fields. cargo_description maps to cargo.cargo_desc']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: The carrier buy rate at booking determines the cost side for margin calculation. Freight forwarders must capture buy rate at booking stage for profitability analysis and carrier cost tracking. No exis']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the nominated carrier (airline, ocean carrier, trucking company, or rail operator) assigned to execute this booking. Populated upon carrier nomination or space reservation confirmation.']
  carrier_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.carrier_schedule. Business justification: Space bookings are confirmed against specific carrier schedules (vessel voyages or flights). Capacity utilization reporting, space confirmation tracking, and overbooking management all require linking']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Bookings reference specific carrier service offerings (e.g., Maersk AE7 weekly string, Lufthansa express flight service) with defined schedules and transit times. Sales and operations teams book capac']
  consignee_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignee profile entity representing the party designated to receive the cargo at the destination.']
  contract_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.contract_rate. Business justification: Contract rates applied at booking determine the customer sell price. Linking enables contract utilization tracking, booking-to-contract reconciliation, and revenue reporting against contracted volumes']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer account (shipper or freight forwarder) that submitted this booking request. Supports PARTY_REFERENCE requirement for TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  destination_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node entity representing the delivery or destination point for this booking (e.g., consignee warehouse, CFS, ICD, or airport/seaport terminal).']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_order. Business justification: A booking is a freight forwarding request that, when confirmed, generates a freight_order. This FK tracks the quote-to-order conversion lifecycle. Nullable until booking is confirmed and order is crea']
  lane_commitment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.lane_commitment. Business justification: Bookings consume contracted lane capacity. Linking bookings to lane commitments enables real-time capacity utilization monitoring against contracted minimums/maximums, supports space confirmation deci']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the route lane entity defining the origin-destination corridor for this booking. Used for rate lookup, capacity planning, and SLA determination.']
  notification_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Booking confirmations, space allocation updates, and amendment notifications are sent to a specific customer contact. Role-prefixed \'notification_contact_id\' to distinguish from other potential contac']
  origin_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Bookings for LCL/air cargo often originate from warehouse consolidation facilities (CFS). Tracking origin_facility_id enables facility-level capacity planning, dwell time analysis, and cargo handoff r']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.partner. Business justification: In NVOCC and co-loading operations, a booking is placed with or through a network partner who arranges carrier space. Partner-level booking tracking is essential for partner performance measurement, c']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_quote. Business justification: A booking may be created based on a freight quote. This FK tracks the quote-to-booking lineage. Nullable for direct bookings not based on a quote. No reverse FK exists, no bidirectional violation.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: At booking, the applicable rate card is selected to estimate freight charges and confirm space. This link supports booking cost estimation, rate audit, and booking-to-invoice reconciliation. No existi']
  service_preference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_preference. Business justification: Bookings are created against a customer\'s service preferences (preferred carrier, service level, incoterms, container type). Booking agents reference service_preference to auto-populate and validate b']
  shipper_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipper profile entity capturing the party responsible for tendering the cargo for transport. Distinct from the billing customer account.']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: At booking stage, the applicable SLA entitlement governs transit time guarantees, pickup cutoffs, and penalty clauses. Linking booking to sla_entitlement enables SLA compliance tracking from booking c']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: The tariff governing a booking determines applicable charges and regulatory requirements (FMC, IATA). Logistics teams reference the tariff at booking for charge validation and compliance. No existing ']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Bookings reserve capacity on specific transport assets (aircraft, vessels). Required for asset capacity planning, schedule optimization, and booking-to-execution reconciliation. Standard capacity mana']
  booking_date timestamp [note: 'The principal real-world event timestamp recording when the booking request was formally submitted by the shipper or freight forwarder. Distinct from system audit timestamps. Used for SLA measurement and capacity allocation.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the freight booking. Tracks progression from initial submission through carrier confirmation or rejection to execution. \'executed\' indicates a freight order has been raised against this booking.']
  cancellation_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this booking was cancelled by the shipper or operations team. Null if the booking has not been cancelled. Used for cancellation fee assessment, capacity release, and booking lifecycle analytics.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description provided when a booking is cancelled. Used for root cause analysis, carrier penalty assessment, and demand forecasting model calibration in Blue Yonder.']
  carrier_booking_ref varchar [note: 'The booking confirmation reference number issued by the carrier upon space reservation acceptance. For ocean shipments this is the carrier\'s booking number; for air it may be the airline\'s booking code. Used for cross-referencing with carrier systems.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the booking request was submitted. Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) and Application Programming Interface (API) indicate automated integrations; web_portal and manual indicate human-initiated entries. Used for channel analytics and SLA differentiation.']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The weight basis used for freight charge calculation, being the higher of actual gross weight and Dimensional Weight (DIM weight). Calculated per IATA volumetric conversion factor for air freight or carrier-specific rules for other modes.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'ISO container type code for ocean freight bookings (e.g., 20GP = 20-foot General Purpose, 40HC = 40-foot High Cube, 20RF = 20-foot Refrigerated). Null for non-ocean or non-containerized shipments. Drives Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU) calculation and Terminal Handling Charge (THC) assessment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 20GP|40GP|40HC|45HC|20RF|40RF|20OT|40OT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this booking record was first persisted in the system of record. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the estimated freight charge and surcharge amounts on this booking (e.g., USD, EUR, SGD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting under IFRS.']
  dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo contains dangerous goods or hazardous materials requiring special handling, documentation, and regulatory compliance. True triggers IMDG Code compliance checks for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR for road transport.']
  estimated_freight_charge decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated base freight charge for this booking in the billing currency, calculated at time of booking based on applicable rate tariff, chargeable weight, and lane. Excludes surcharges (BAF, GRI, THC). Used for shipper quotation and revenue forecasting.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) — the planned arrival timestamp of the vessel, aircraft, truck, or train at the destination port or terminal. Used for consignee notification, customs pre-arrival filing, and delivery scheduling.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) — the planned departure timestamp of the vessel, aircraft, truck, or train carrying this booking\'s cargo from the origin port or terminal. Used for schedule coordination, documentation cutoff management, and carrier slot reservation.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Indicates who is responsible for paying the freight charges: prepaid (shipper pays), collect (consignee pays), or third_party (a nominated third party pays). Drives accounts receivable routing and invoice generation in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo in kilograms as declared by the shipper at time of booking. Used for freight charge calculation, carrier capacity planning, and dangerous goods compliance. Basis for chargeable weight comparison against DIM weight.']
  hawb_number varchar [note: 'House Air Waybill (HAWB) number issued by the freight forwarder or NVOCC for air freight consolidations. Issued under the MAWB when Transport Shipping acts as consolidator. Null for direct carrier bookings or non-air modes.']
  hbl_number varchar [note: 'House Bill of Lading (HBL) number issued by the freight forwarder or NVOCC for ocean LCL consolidations or NVOCC arrangements. Issued under the MBL. Null for FCL direct carrier bookings or non-ocean modes.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) agreed between buyer and seller governing the transfer of risk, cost responsibility, and delivery obligations. Determines freight charge allocation (prepaid vs collect), insurance responsibility, and customs clearance party. Published by the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mawb_number varchar [note: 'Master Air Waybill (MAWB) number issued by the airline or ground handling agent for air freight bookings. Follows IATA 3-digit airline prefix plus 8-digit serial number format. Null for non-air transport modes. Used for cargo tracking and customs filing.']
  mbl_number varchar [note: 'Master Bill of Lading (MBL) number issued by the ocean carrier for ocean freight bookings. Serves as the primary transport document and title of goods for ocean shipments. Null for non-ocean transport modes.']
