  nvocc_arrangement boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking is executed under a Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier (NVOCC) arrangement, where Transport Shipping acts as a carrier to the shipper but contracts with an actual vessel operator. Relevant for FMC tariff filing and House Bill of Lading (HBL) issuance.']
  piece_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual pieces, packages, or handling units included in this booking. Used for cargo receipt verification, load planning, and Proof of Delivery (POD) reconciliation.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric booking reference number assigned at the time of booking submission. Communicated to the shipper and used across EDI, API, and manual channels for tracking and correspondence. Maps to Oracle TMS booking reference field.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason provided when a booking is rejected by the carrier or internal operations team. Populated only when booking_status is \'rejected\'. Used for exception management and carrier performance analysis.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date on which the shipper requests cargo to be delivered to the consignee at the destination. Basis for SLA commitment and On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance measurement.']
  requested_pickup_date date [note: 'The date on which the shipper requests cargo to be collected from the origin location. Used for capacity planning, driver/vehicle scheduling, and SLA commitment calculation.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the freight service requested. For road: Full Truckload (FTL) or Less Than Truckload (LTL). For ocean: Full Container Load (FCL) or Less Than Container Load (LCL). For air: express, standard, or economy. Drives pricing, capacity allocation, and consolidation logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy|charter|bulk|breakbulk — promote to reference product]']
  space_confirmation_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier confirmed space reservation for this booking. Null if space has not yet been confirmed. Used for SLA tracking and booking lead-time analytics.']
  space_confirmed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier has confirmed space reservation for this booking. True when the carrier has issued a booking confirmation; False when the booking is pending carrier acceptance or has been rejected.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling, stowage, or operational instructions provided by the shipper at time of booking (e.g., fragile, keep upright, top-load only, no stacking). Communicated to warehouse and carrier operations teams.']
  temp_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo. Null when temperature_controlled is False. Used for reefer container set-point configuration and cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temp_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo. Null when temperature_controlled is False. Used for reefer container set-point configuration and cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this booking requires temperature-controlled (reefer/cold chain) transport and storage. True triggers assignment of refrigerated containers (RF) or temperature-controlled vehicles and monitoring requirements.']
  teu_count decimal(6,2) [note: 'Number of Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEUs) represented by this booking. A 20-foot container = 1 TEU; a 40-foot container = 2 TEUs. Used for ocean capacity planning, vessel utilization reporting, and GRI/BAF charge calculation.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this freight booking. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (IATA for air, IMO for ocean), charge components (BAF for ocean, fuel surcharge for air), and document requirements (AWB for air, BOL for ocean).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp recording when this booking record was most recently modified in the system of record. Used for incremental data loading, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo in Cubic Meters (CBM) as declared by the shipper. Used for Dimensional Weight (DIM weight) calculation, container utilization planning, and LCL consolidation rating.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a freight booking request submitted by a shipper or freight forwarder. Captures booking reference number, requested pickup and delivery dates, ETD, ETA, carrier nomination, space reservation confirmation, booking channel (EDI, API, manual), booking status, and acceptance/rejection details. Represents the commercial commitment to move cargo before a freight order is fully executed. Integrates with Oracle TMS shipment execution module.'
}

Table freight.air_waybill {
  air_waybill_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the air waybill record in the Transport Shipping data platform. Primary key for the air_waybill product in the freight domain.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: In air freight, AWBs are issued by IATA-accredited agents. The issuing agent is required for IATA billing, commission calculation, and agent performance tracking. Air freight operations universally tr']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Air waybills execute under carrier agreements governing air freight rates and liability. Business process: carrier rate application, liability limit enforcement, operational SLA tracking for air trans']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: The airline buy rate on an AWB is needed to compute margin per shipment. NVOCC and freight forwarder operations require buy rate traceability on AWBs for profitability reporting and carrier cost analy']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: An AWB is issued by a specific airline/carrier. The existing carrier_name and iata_airline_code are denormalized text fields. A proper FK to carrier enables carrier performance tracking, interline bil']
  carrier_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.carrier_schedule. Business justification: AWBs are issued for specific flight schedules. flight_number and flight_date on air_waybill are denormalized from carrier_schedule. Flight-level cargo revenue reporting and schedule utilization analys']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: An AWB is issued for a specific airline service (e.g., express, deferred). Linking to carrier_service enables rate validation against service-specific tariffs, SLA transit time verification, and inter']
  consignee_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.consignee_profile. Business justification: The AWB identifies the consignee for customs clearance, delivery coordination, and import compliance. Linking to consignee_profile enables automated delivery notifications, customs contact lookup, and']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: In express air e-commerce logistics (DHL/FedEx model), an AWB is issued directly against a fulfillment order. Direct AWB-to-fulfillment-order traceability is required for IATA cargo tracking, merchant']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Rate card traceability on AWBs is required for freight audit and billing reconciliation. Auditors must verify that AWB charges match the rate card in effect at time of issue. No existing rate_card_id ']
  shipper_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.shipper_profile. Business justification: The AWB identifies the shipper for export compliance, IATA certification validation, and hazmat authorization. Only customer_account_id exists on air_waybill; a direct shipper_profile FK is needed for']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: AWBs for air freight are subject to SLA entitlements governing transit time guarantees and delivery windows. Linking AWB to sla_entitlement enables air freight SLA compliance reporting and penalty cal']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: IATA tariffs and carrier tariffs govern AWB charges. Linking AWB to the governing tariff is standard in air freight for charge validation, IATA compliance audit, and freight audit processes. No existi']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Air waybills document cargo transported on specific aircraft (modeled as transport_assets). Required for flight manifest reconciliation, aircraft utilization analysis, and cargo-to-flight traceability']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time the cargo arrived at the destination airport. Used for OTD (On-Time Delivery) KPI calculation, SLA compliance reporting, and claims assessment.']
  awb_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the air waybill. DRAFT: document being prepared; ISSUED: AWB formally issued and accepted by carrier; ACCEPTED: cargo accepted at origin; IN_TRANSIT: cargo in movement; ARRIVED: cargo arrived at destination airport; DELIVERED: cargo delivered to consignee; CANCELLED: AWB voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|ISSUED|ACCEPTED|IN_TRANSIT|ARRIVED|DELIVERED|CANCELLED — promote to reference product]']
  awb_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents a Master Air Waybill (MAWB) issued by the airline, a House Air Waybill (HAWB) issued by the freight forwarder, or a DIRECT AWB for a single-shipper direct airline shipment.']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The weight used for freight charge calculation, being the higher of the actual gross weight and the dimensional (DIM) weight. Calculated per IATA volumetric factor (typically 1:6000 cm³/kg for air freight). Core field for revenue calculation.']
  charges_collect_or_prepaid varchar [note: 'Indicates whether freight charges are to be paid by the shipper at origin (PREPAID) or by the consignee at destination (COLLECT). Determines billing party and accounts receivable assignment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the air waybill record was first created in the source system (SAP TM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 3-letter currency code in which all monetary amounts on this AWB are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, AED). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and CASS settlement.']
  declared_value_for_carriage decimal(15,2) [note: 'The value of the shipment declared by the shipper for the purpose of determining the carrier\'s liability in case of loss or damage. May be \'NVD\' (No Value Declared) in which case this field is null. Governs maximum liability per IATA conditions of contract.']
  declared_value_for_customs decimal(15,2) [note: 'The customs value of the shipment declared by the shipper for import duty and tax assessment at the destination country. Used by Descartes Customs for duty calculation and customs entry filing.']
  destination_airport_code varchar [note: '3-letter IATA airport code for the final destination airport where the cargo is to be delivered to the consignee (e.g., LAX, SIN, LHR). Used for routing, customs filing, and rate determination.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time of arrival of the carrying aircraft at the destination airport. Used for consignee notification, customs pre-arrival filing, and OTD performance measurement. Updated dynamically from FourKites.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time of departure of the carrying aircraft from the origin airport. Used for shipment tracking, customer notifications, and SLA monitoring via FourKites.']
  first_transit_airport_code varchar [note: '3-letter IATA airport code for the first intermediate transit/transfer point in the routing, if applicable. Null for direct flights. Used for multi-leg routing documentation on the AWB.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total base freight charge for the shipment calculated as chargeable weight multiplied by the rate per kg, in the billing currency. Represents the primary revenue line for the air freight service. Used for revenue recognition per IFRS 15.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total actual gross weight of the shipment in kilograms as declared by the shipper, including packaging and pallets. Used for load planning, aircraft weight and balance, and freight charge calculation.']
  hawb_number varchar [note: 'The House Air Waybill number issued by the freight forwarder or NVOCC to the individual shipper when consolidating multiple shipments under a single MAWB. Null when the AWB is a direct shipment not consolidated under a master. Sourced from SAP TM air freight module.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'The ICC Incoterms 2020 code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between shipper and consignee (e.g., EXW, FOB, CIF, DDP). Determines freight charge responsibility and customs clearance obligations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains dangerous goods as classified under IATA Dangerous Goods Regulations (DGR) and ICAO Technical Instructions. When true, additional DGR documentation and handling procedures are mandatory.']
  isf_filing_number varchar [note: 'The US Customs and Border Protection Importer Security Filing (ISF, also known as 10+2) reference number. Required for ocean and air cargo imports into the United States for advance security screening.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the air waybill was formally issued and signed, representing the principal business event timestamp for this contract of carriage. Corresponds to the execution date of the freight contract between shipper and carrier.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time (with timezone offset) when the air waybill was issued in SAP TM. Provides sub-day precision for operational sequencing and SLA measurement.']
  mawb_number varchar [note: 'The Master Air Waybill number issued by the airline or ground handling agent, formatted as the 3-digit IATA airline prefix followed by an 8-digit serial number (e.g., 176-12345678). Serves as the primary contract of carriage between the shipper and the airline. Sourced from SAP TM air freight module.']
  origin_airport_code varchar [note: '3-letter IATA airport code for the airport of departure where the cargo is tendered to the airline (e.g., DXB, FRA, JFK). Used for routing, rate determination, and regulatory reporting.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the goods were manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations, preferential tariff determination, and trade compliance screening.']
  pieces_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual pieces, packages, or handling units included in this shipment as declared on the AWB. Used for cargo acceptance, loading, and delivery verification.']
  pod_signatory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual who signed for receipt of the cargo at delivery, as captured on the electronic or paper proof of delivery (ePOD). Used for delivery confirmation and dispute resolution.']
  pod_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cargo was confirmed as delivered to the consignee and proof of delivery (POD) was captured. Used for OTD measurement, SLA compliance, and triggering final invoice settlement.']
  rate_class varchar [note: 'IATA rate class code indicating the type of rate applied to this shipment (e.g., N=Normal, Q=Quantity, M=Minimum, B=Basic, C=Specific Commodity, E=Express, R=Reduced, S=Surcharge, U=Unit Load Device, X=Express). Determines the applicable tariff structure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: N|Q|M|B|C|E|R|S|U|X — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_per_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'The agreed freight rate applied per kilogram of chargeable weight for this shipment, in the billing currency. Commercially sensitive pricing data used for revenue recognition and freight audit.']
  second_transit_airport_code varchar [note: '3-letter IATA airport code for the second intermediate transit/transfer point in the routing, if applicable. Null for direct or single-transit flights. Supports complex multi-leg air freight routing.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The service level or product type for this air freight shipment (e.g., EXPRESS for next-flight-out priority, STANDARD for regular scheduled service, DEFERRED for economy/consolidation, CHARTER for full aircraft charter, COURIER for express parcel). Determines SLA commitments and pricing tier.']
  special_handling_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA Special Handling Codes (SHC) applicable to this shipment (e.g., DGR=Dangerous Goods, PER=Perishables, VAL=Valuables, HUM=Human Remains, AVI=Live Animals, COL=Cold Chain). Drives handling instructions, storage requirements, and regulatory compliance checks.']
  total_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Grand total of all charges on the AWB including base freight charge and all other charges (surcharges, fees, taxes). Represents the total amount due from the shipper or consignee. Used for invoice generation and accounts receivable.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The 4-digit United Nations identification number for dangerous goods (e.g., UN1263 for paint, UN3480 for lithium ion batteries). Mandatory when is_dangerous_goods is true. Used for emergency response, handling, and regulatory compliance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the air waybill record in the source system. Used for change detection, incremental loads, and audit compliance.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,4) [note: 'Total volumetric measurement of the shipment in cubic meters (CBM). Used to calculate dimensional weight (DIM weight) and determine the chargeable weight when volume weight exceeds actual weight.']

  Note: 'Master document for air freight shipments serving as the contract of carriage between shipper and airline/freight forwarder. Stores MAWB and HAWB numbers, IATA airline codes, origin and destination airport codes, flight details, commodity description, HS codes, declared value, chargeable weight, freight charges, special handling codes (e.g., DGR, PER, VAL), and consignee/shipper details. Governed by IATA regulations. Sourced from SAP TM air freight module.'
}

Table freight.bill_of_lading {
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the bill of lading record in the Transport Shipping lakehouse. Primary key for the bill_of_lading data product. TRANSACTION_HEADER role.']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.booking. Business justification: A bill of lading is issued as a result of a confirmed ocean booking. The booking captures the shipper\'s space request and carrier confirmation, while the BOL is the formal contract of carriage and rec']
  cargo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.cargo. Business justification: bill_of_lading stores commodity_description and hs_code which are authoritatively defined in the cargo master reference table. Adding cargo_id normalizes these fields. Weight, volume, package_count, a']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Bills of lading execute under ocean carrier master agreements. Business process: contracted ocean freight rate application, carrier liability terms enforcement, service commitment verification for oce']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: Carrier buy rate on BOL enables cost-side margin analysis per ocean shipment. Ocean freight forwarders track buy rates at BOL level for profitability reporting and carrier cost benchmarking. No existi']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the NVOCC or freight forwarder acting as the contractual carrier on the house bill of lading. The NVOCC issues the HBL to the shipper and holds the MBL from the ocean carrier. Relevant for FMC-regulated NVOCC arrangements.']
  carrier_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.carrier_schedule. Business justification: BOLs are issued for specific vessel voyages which are carrier schedules. vessel_name and voyage_number on bill_of_lading are denormalized from carrier_schedule. Vessel-level cargo manifest reporting a']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: A BOL is issued for a specific vessel service (e.g., weekly Asia-Europe loop). Linking to carrier_service enables vessel schedule validation, service-level rate application, and SLA transit time compl']
  consignee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignee (importer/receiver) party to whom the cargo is consigned. Links to the consignee_profile in the customer domain.']
  consignee_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.consignee_profile. Business justification: The BOL identifies the consignee for ocean freight delivery and import customs. The existing consignee_id points to fulfillment.consignee, not customer.consignee_profile. A direct link to consignee_pr']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Bills of lading document cargo in specific containers. Fundamental ocean freight relationship for container tracking, cargo accountability, and container utilization. Replaces denormalized container_n']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: The BOL is a commercial and financial document tied to a customer account for billing, credit management, and account-level ocean freight reporting. No existing FK on bill_of_lading→customer_account; ']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.fulfillment_order. Business justification: A BOL issued for ocean/road freight must be traceable to the fulfillment order it serves for import customs reconciliation, customer order status updates, and merchant billing. Logistics domain expert']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Ocean freight BOLs trigger customer invoicing upon service completion. BOL-to-invoice link is essential for ocean freight billing reconciliation and supports proof-of-service validation in billing dis']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Rate card traceability on BOLs supports freight audit, billing dispute resolution, and margin reporting per shipment document. Logistics experts expect BOLs to reference the rate card used for pricing']
  shipper_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipper (exporter/consignor) party responsible for tendering the cargo. Links to the shipper_profile in the customer domain. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER PARTY_REFERENCE minimum category.']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: Ocean freight BOLs are subject to SLA entitlements governing transit time guarantees and penalty clauses. Linking BOL to sla_entitlement enables ocean freight SLA compliance reporting and contractual ']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Ocean tariffs (FMC-filed or carrier tariffs) govern BOL charges. Linking BOL to the governing tariff is required for FMC regulatory compliance, freight audit, and charge dispute resolution. No existin']
  trade_party_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the party to be notified upon arrival of the vessel at the port of discharge. Typically the customs broker or freight forwarder. Links to the customer domain.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date the vessel arrived at the port of discharge. Used for transit time calculation, demurrage and detention computation, customs import filing, and on-time delivery (OTD) KPI reporting.']
  actual_departure_date date [note: 'Actual date the vessel departed from the port of loading. Used for transit time calculation, carrier performance measurement, and customs export record finalization.']
  ams_filing_number varchar [note: 'US Customs and Border Protection (CBP) Automated Manifest System (AMS) filing reference number. Required for all ocean shipments destined for or transiting through US ports. Must be filed 24 hours prior to vessel loading under the 24-Hour Advance Manifest Rule.']
  bol_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the bill of lading document. Draft indicates pre-issuance; issued means the original has been released to the shipper; released indicates cargo release authorized; surrendered means original returned to carrier; amended reflects post-issuance corrections; cancelled indicates the BOL is void. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER LIFECYCLE_STATUS minimum category.']
  bol_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bill of lading indicating whether it is a master (MBL), house (HBL), seaway, straight (non-negotiable), to-order (negotiable), or surrendered BOL. Determines negotiability and title transfer rules. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: master|house|seaway|straight|order|surrender — promote to reference product if additional types emerge]']
  carrier_scac varchar [note: 'Four-letter Standard Carrier Alpha Code (SCAC) identifying the ocean carrier (vessel operating common carrier) that issued the master bill of lading. Used for AMS filing, EDI transactions, and carrier performance tracking.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Total number of containers covered by this bill of lading. Used for capacity planning, terminal handling charge (THC) calculation, and TEU-based reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bill of lading record was first created in the SAP TM ocean freight module and ingested into the Transport Shipping lakehouse silver layer. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED minimum category.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the cargo is destined. Used for import customs declarations, denied party screening, and trade compliance checks via Descartes Customs.']
  eta timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time the vessel is scheduled to arrive at the port of discharge. Used for consignee notification, customs pre-arrival filing, port berth planning, and on-time delivery (OTD) performance measurement. Updated dynamically via FourKites predictive ETA.']
  etd timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time the vessel is scheduled to depart from the port of loading. Used for cargo cut-off planning, shipper notification, and SLA monitoring. Sourced from SAP TM ocean freight module and updated via FourKites real-time visibility.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base ocean freight charge amount as stated on the bill of lading, before surcharges. Expressed in the currency indicated by freight_currency_code. Used for freight invoice reconciliation, revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Finance, and freight audit in Oracle TMS. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category (base amount component).']
  freight_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the freight charges are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Required for multi-currency freight invoice processing and foreign exchange revaluation in SAP S/4HANA Finance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category (currency code component).']
  freight_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total surcharge amount applied to the base freight, including Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF), General Rate Increase (GRI), Terminal Handling Charge (THC), and other accessorial charges. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category (adjustment component).']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Indicates who is responsible for paying the ocean freight charges: prepaid (shipper pays), collect (consignee pays), or third party (a nominated third party pays). Drives accounts receivable and accounts payable processing in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo including packaging, as declared by the shipper, expressed in kilograms. Used for vessel stability calculations, freight charge computation, customs declarations, and dangerous goods compliance under IMDG Code.']
  hbl_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier issued by the NVOCC or freight forwarder to the shipper for the house bill of lading. Represents the contract of carriage between the forwarder and the shipper. Distinct from the MBL number issued by the ocean carrier.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG hazard class of the dangerous goods (e.g., Class 1 Explosives, Class 3 Flammable Liquids, Class 6.1 Toxic Substances). Populated only when is_hazardous is true. Drives stowage segregation, emergency procedures, and port authority notifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  imo_number varchar [note: 'Unique 7-digit IMO vessel identification number assigned by IHS Markit on behalf of IMO. Provides a permanent, globally unique identifier for the vessel independent of name changes or flag changes. Used for regulatory compliance and vessel tracking.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'ICC Incoterms 2020 code defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between the shipper and consignee. Determines freight payment obligations, insurance responsibility, and customs clearance duties. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  is_hazardous boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the cargo contains dangerous goods as classified under the IMDG Code. When true, additional IMDG declarations, special stowage requirements, and emergency response information are mandatory.']
  is_negotiable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the bill of lading is negotiable (to-order) or non-negotiable (straight/waybill). A negotiable BOL serves as a document of title and can be transferred by endorsement; a non-negotiable BOL cannot be used to transfer title.']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date on which the bill of lading was officially issued by the carrier or NVOCC. This is the principal real-world business event date for the BOL document and is used for letter of credit compliance, customs filing deadlines, and freight payment terms. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP minimum category.']
  mbl_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique identifier issued by the ocean carrier (vessel operating common carrier) for the master bill of lading. Serves as the primary contract of carriage reference between the carrier and the NVOCC or freight forwarder. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER minimum category.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where the cargo originates. Used for customs declarations, trade compliance screening, rules of origin determination, and preferential tariff eligibility.']
  original_bol_count varchar [note: 'Number of original signed copies of the bill of lading issued to the shipper (typically 3). All originals must be surrendered to the carrier for cargo release when the BOL is negotiable. Used for document control and letter of credit compliance.']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual packages, pieces, or units of cargo covered by this bill of lading as declared by the shipper. Used for cargo receipt verification, customs declarations, and cargo claims assessment.']
  package_type varchar [note: 'Description of the type of packaging used for the cargo (e.g., cartons, pallets, drums, crates, bags). Used for cargo handling instructions, stowage planning, and customs declarations. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carton|pallet|drum|crate|bag|bundle|roll|cylinder — promote to reference product]']
  place_of_delivery varchar [note: 'Final destination where the carrier delivers the cargo to the consignee, which may differ from the port of discharge (e.g., an inland CFS, ICD, or door delivery address). Defines the end of the carrier\'s responsibility under the contract of carriage.']
  place_of_issue varchar [note: 'City and country where the bill of lading was issued by the carrier or NVOCC. Required for letter of credit compliance and legal jurisdiction determination in case of disputes.']
  place_of_receipt varchar [note: 'Location where the carrier takes custody of the cargo, which may differ from the port of loading (e.g., an inland container depot (ICD) or container freight station (CFS)). Defines the start of the carrier\'s responsibility under the contract of carriage.']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the cargo is discharged from the ocean vessel. Represents the destination port for the ocean leg. Used for customs import filing, terminal handling charge (THC) calculation, and delivery planning.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the port where the cargo is loaded onto the ocean vessel. Represents the origin port for the ocean leg of the shipment. Used for freight rate calculation, customs export filing, and carrier scheduling.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ocean freight service: Full Container Load (FCL), Less than Container Load (LCL), Roll-on/Roll-off (RORO), bulk, or break-bulk. Determines container allocation, consolidation requirements, and applicable tariff structures.']
  shipper_reference varchar [note: 'Shipper\'s own internal reference number or purchase order number associated with this shipment. Printed on the bill of lading for cross-referencing with the shipper\'s ERP or order management system. Facilitates EDI reconciliation.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total number of Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEUs) represented by the containers on this bill of lading. A 20-foot container = 1 TEU; a 40-foot container = 2 TEUs. Used for vessel capacity utilization, port statistics, and freight revenue reporting.']
  total_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight charges payable as stated on the bill of lading, being the sum of the base freight amount and all applicable surcharges. Used for freight invoice generation, letter of credit compliance, and revenue reporting. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER MONETARY_TRIPLET minimum category (net total component).']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the specific dangerous substance or article as assigned by the UN Committee of Experts on the Transport of Dangerous Goods. Populated only when is_hazardous is true. Required on shipping documents and package markings.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the bill of lading record was last modified in the source system or the lakehouse. Used for incremental data loading, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance. Satisfies TRANSACTION_HEADER RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED minimum category.']
  volume_cbm decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo expressed in cubic meters (CBM). Used for LCL freight rate calculation, container utilization assessment, and dimensional weight (DIM weight) comparison for rating purposes.']

  Note: 'Master document for ocean freight shipments serving as the contract of carriage, receipt of goods, and document of title. Stores MBL and HBL numbers, vessel name, voyage number, port of loading (POL), port of discharge (POD), place of delivery, container numbers, seal numbers, commodity description, number of packages, gross weight, CBM, freight terms (prepaid/collect), and NVOCC details. Governed by FMC and IMO regulations. Sourced from SAP TM ocean freight module.'
}

Table freight.consolidation {
  consolidation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consolidation. Primary key for the consolidation entity.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: In air freight consolidation (LCL air / ULD consolidation), multiple shipments are grouped under a Master Air Waybill (MAWB). The consolidation record represents the groupage operation, and the air_wa']
  blocked_space_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.blocked_space_agreement. Business justification: NVOCC consolidations are frequently built against blocked space agreements with carriers. Linking consolidation to BSA enables utilization tracking against guaranteed capacity, penalty/incentive calcu']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Consolidations operate under carrier master agreements for capacity and rates. Business process: contracted consolidation rate application, capacity utilization tracking against carrier commitments, N']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: Carrier buy rate on consolidation supports cost-side margin analysis for LCL/groupage consolidations. Freight forwarders must track the buy rate per consolidation to compute profitability. No existing']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: A consolidation is built around a specific carrier service departure (e.g., a weekly FCL or ULD service). Carrier service linkage is essential for cargo cutoff management, capacity planning against se']
  consolidation_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ocean carrier, airline, or surface carrier assigned to transport the consolidation. For NVOCC operations, this is the underlying vessel or flight operator.']
  consolidation_nvocc_operator_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the NVOCC or freight forwarder operating the consolidation. For direct carrier consolidations, this may be null.']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Ocean consolidations are stuffed into specific containers. Critical for container-level consolidation tracking and CFS operations. Replaces denormalized container_number string with proper FK to conta']
  destination_cfs_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination CFS or ICD facility where the consolidation will be deconsolidated and cargo distributed. Links to warehouse or facility master data.']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Consolidations (LCL/groupage) are billed as a unit to the NVOCC customer; the consolidation-level invoice covers all grouped shipments. Logistics billing teams require this link for consolidated freig']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Consolidations are planned and executed on specific contracted lanes between origin and destination ports. Master routing decisions, carrier selection, transit time commitments, and cost allocation al']
  origin_cfs_facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the origin CFS or ICD facility where cargo is received and consolidated. Links to warehouse or facility master data.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of a co-loading partner sharing space in this consolidation. Null if no co-loading arrangement exists.']
  pick_wave_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.pick_wave. Business justification: In 3PL consolidation operations, a pick wave produces the individual shipments that are consolidated into one freight movement. Linking consolidation to pick_wave enables cargo cutoff coordination and']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Consolidation services (LCL, groupage) are priced using specialized consolidation rate cards. Rate_card_id links the consolidation to the applicable rate card, enables consolidation pricing validation']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: The tariff governing a consolidation (LCL/ULD groupage) determines applicable charges and commodity restrictions. Needed for consolidation profitability analysis and tariff compliance. No existing tar']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Consolidations are transported on specific assets (aircraft for air consolidations, trucks for LCL). Essential for consolidation execution tracking, asset utilization, and operational coordination. St']
  actual_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual arrival date and time when the carrier arrived at the destination port.']
  actual_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual departure date and time when the carrier departed the origin port. ETD (Estimated Time of Departure) becomes actual once confirmed.']
  cargo_cutoff_datetime timestamp [note: 'Latest date and time by which cargo must be received at the origin CFS facility to be included in this consolidation.']
  carrier_booking_reference varchar [note: 'Booking or reservation number provided by the carrier for the consolidation space allocation.']
  consolidation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consolidation from planning through deconsolidation at destination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|booking_open|cargo_receiving|closed|departed|in_transit|arrived|deconsolidated — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consolidation_type varchar [note: 'Type of consolidation based on transport mode. Ocean LCL (Less Than Container Load) groups multiple shippers cargo into a single FCL container. Air groupage consolidates shipments into a single ULD (Unit Load Device). Road and rail groupage combine multiple LTL shipments.']
  container_size_type varchar [note: 'ISO container size and type code for ocean consolidations. 20GP = 20-foot General Purpose, 40GP = 40-foot General Purpose, 40HC = 40-foot High Cube, 45HC = 45-foot High Cube. Null for non-containerized modes.']
  contains_dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consolidation contains any dangerous goods (hazardous materials) requiring special handling and documentation per IMDG or IATA DGR.']
  contains_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consolidation contains temperature-sensitive cargo requiring reefer container or climate-controlled handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation record was first created in the system.']
  deconsolidation_complete_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all cargo was deconsolidated and available for onward delivery or pickup.']
  deconsolidation_start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when deconsolidation operations began at the destination CFS facility.']
  estimated_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Estimated arrival date and time at the destination port. Updated based on carrier tracking and real-time visibility data.']
  freight_charge_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for freight charges (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterms 2020 rule governing the transfer of costs and risks between shipper and consignee for the consolidation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes and special instructions for the consolidation, including handling requirements, stowage instructions, or exception documentation.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number for the consolidation, used in operational communications and documentation.']
  scheduled_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Scheduled departure date and time of the carrier vessel, flight, or vehicle transporting the consolidation from origin port.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Service level commitment for the consolidation, determining transit time and handling priority.']
  total_chargeable_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total chargeable weight for the consolidation, calculated as the greater of gross weight or volumetric weight (volume * dimensional weight factor). Used for freight charge calculation.']
  total_freight_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total freight charges for the consolidation, including base ocean or air freight, BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor), GRI (General Rate Increase), THC (Terminal Handling Charge), and other surcharges.']
  total_gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of all cargo in the consolidation, measured in kilograms.']
  total_piece_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual pieces or packages across all shipments in the consolidation.']
  total_shipment_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual shipper shipments (house shipments) consolidated into this master consolidation.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of all cargo in the consolidation, measured in cubic meters. Critical for LCL and air groupage space utilization.']
  uld_number varchar [note: 'IATA ULD identifier for air groupage consolidations. Format: 3-letter ULD type code + 5-digit serial + 2-letter owner code. Null for non-air consolidations.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of container or ULD capacity utilized by the consolidation, calculated as (actual volume / maximum capacity) * 100. Key metric for consolidation efficiency.']

  Note: 'Represents an LCL or groupage consolidation where multiple shippers\' cargo is grouped into a single FCL container or air ULD for cost-efficient transport. Captures consolidation reference, mode (ocean LCL, air groupage), origin and destination CFS facilities, consolidation status, cargo cut-off date/time, departure date, total CBM utilized, total weight, number of constituent shipments, and assigned carrier. Central to NVOCC co-loading operations and CFS management workflows.'
}

Table freight.load_plan {
  load_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the load plan record. Primary key.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: For air freight, a load plan defines how cargo is loaded onto an aircraft or ULD, and references the air waybill. One load_plan for one AWB. Nullable when load plan is for ocean or other modes.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.bill_of_lading. Business justification: For ocean freight, a load plan defines how cargo is loaded into a container or vessel, and references the bill of lading. One load_plan for one BOL. Nullable when load plan is for air or other modes.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: A load plan is created for a specific carrier\'s vehicle/vessel/aircraft, as carrier-specific weight limits, loading configurations, and hazmat segregation rules govern the plan. Logistics planners nee']
  consolidation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.consolidation. Business justification: A load plan can be created for a consolidation (defining how consolidated cargo is loaded into a container or ULD). One load_plan for one consolidation. This is an alternative to load_plan → freight_o']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Load plans for international shipments must be validated against customs declarations to ensure loaded cargo matches declared goods. This link supports pre-departure compliance checks and customs auth']
  driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: Load plans require driver sign-off for hazmat segregation verification and weight distribution compliance. Safety regulations and load acceptance audits require knowing which driver accepted and execu']
  freight_carrier_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_carrier_assignment. Business justification: A load plan is created in the context of a specific carrier assignment — the carrier and transport asset determine the load constraints (weight capacity, volume capacity, equipment type). Linking load']
  freight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_leg. Business justification: A load plan is operationally tied to a specific freight leg — it defines how cargo is loaded for that particular transport segment. Linking load_plan to freight_leg establishes the leg-level context f']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent freight order for which this load plan is created. Links to the freight order entity.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hold. Business justification: A customs hold can prevent a load plan from being executed when cargo is pending examination. Linking load_plan to hold enables load planning systems to automatically flag held cargo, preventing loadi']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: Load planning in express/last-mile operations requires parcel-level weight, dimension, and hazmat data to optimize vehicle loading and comply with weight regulations. A direct load_plan-to-parcel link']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.plan. Business justification: Load plan execution must be validated against the route plan it implements. Operations teams use this link for load-vs-plan compliance reporting and to confirm physical loading matches planned routing']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.storage_location. Business justification: Load plans specify staging locations within warehouses where cargo is assembled before container/vehicle loading. Tracking staging_location_id enables warehouse space utilization analysis, load sequen']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transport unit (vessel, aircraft, truck, railcar, container) onto which cargo is being loaded. May be vessel IMO number, aircraft registration, truck license plate, or container number.']
  actual_load_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading operations were completed. Critical for on-time performance tracking and operational efficiency analysis.']
  actual_load_start_time timestamp [note: 'Actual timestamp when loading operations commenced. Used for performance measurement and variance analysis against planned time.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the load plan was formally approved for execution. Marks transition from planning to operational phase.']
  cargo_placement_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for cargo placement within the transport unit, including stacking rules, orientation requirements, and securing methods.']
  center_of_gravity_x decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated X-coordinate of the load center of gravity relative to transport unit reference point. Critical for vessel and aircraft stability.']
  center_of_gravity_y decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated Y-coordinate of the load center of gravity relative to transport unit reference point. Critical for vessel and aircraft stability.']
  center_of_gravity_z decimal(10,3) [note: 'Calculated Z-coordinate (vertical) of the load center of gravity relative to transport unit reference point. Critical for vessel and aircraft stability.']
  contains_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the load plan includes hazardous materials requiring special handling and segregation. Triggers additional compliance checks.']
  contains_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the load plan includes temperature-sensitive cargo requiring refrigerated or climate-controlled transport.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the load plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard class of dangerous goods included in the load plan. Determines segregation requirements and handling procedures. Pipe-separated list if multiple classes present.']
  hazmat_segregation_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether hazmat segregation requirements have been verified and approved by qualified personnel. Mandatory for hazmat loads.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the load plan record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  load_optimization_method varchar [note: 'Method used to create the load plan. Automated plans use TMS optimization algorithms, manual plans are created by planners, hybrid combines both.']
  load_plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the load plan. Tracks progression from initial planning through execution completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|planned|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  load_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order in which cargo items are to be loaded onto the transport unit. Critical for LIFO/FIFO optimization and unloading efficiency.']
  loading_dock_bay varchar [note: 'Specific dock, bay, or gate number where loading operations are performed. Used for operational coordination and resource allocation.']
  loading_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the facility or terminal where loading operations take place. May be UN/LOCODE, IATA airport code, or internal facility code.']
  max_volume_capacity_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume capacity of the transport unit in cubic meters. Physical constraint for load planning.']
  max_weight_capacity_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the transport unit in kilograms. Regulatory limit that must not be exceeded.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the load plan, including special circumstances, deviations from standard procedures, or operational alerts.']
  planned_load_completion_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp when loading operations are expected to be completed. Used for departure scheduling and SLA management.']
  planned_load_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when loading operations are planned to begin. Used for resource planning and scheduling.']
  planned_load_start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when loading operations are scheduled to commence. Critical for dock scheduling and labor planning.']
  reference varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the load plan, used for external communication and documentation. Typically follows format LP-XXXXXXXX.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Service level for the load plan. FTL (Full Truckload), LTL (Less Than Truckload), FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less Than Container Load), or express/standard/economy for air and parcel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_handling_requirements varchar [note: 'Any special handling requirements for the load, such as fragile cargo, high-value goods, live animals, or oversized items requiring special equipment.']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature requirement in Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo in this load plan. Must be maintained throughout transport.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature requirement in Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo in this load plan. Must be maintained throughout transport.']
  total_actual_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total actual volume of all cargo loaded, measured in cubic meters. Captured post-loading for utilization analysis.']
  total_actual_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total actual weight of all cargo loaded, measured in kilograms. Captured post-loading for verification and compliance documentation.']
  total_planned_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total planned volume of all cargo to be loaded, measured in cubic meters. Used for space utilization and capacity planning.']
  total_planned_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total planned weight of all cargo to be loaded, measured in kilograms. Used for weight distribution calculations and regulatory compliance.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this load plan. Determines operational procedures, documentation requirements, and regulatory compliance.']
  transport_unit_type varchar [note: 'Type of transport unit for which the load plan is created. Determines applicable loading rules and constraints.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the load plan. Incremented when significant changes are made to the plan after initial creation.']
  volume_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of volume capacity utilized by the planned load. Key performance indicator for space optimization efficiency.']
  weight_utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of weight capacity utilized by the planned load. Key performance indicator for load optimization efficiency.']

  Note: 'Operational plan defining how cargo is loaded onto a transport unit (vessel, aircraft, truck, railcar). Captures load plan reference, transport unit identifier, planned vs actual load sequence, cargo placement instructions, weight distribution, center of gravity calculations, hazmat segregation requirements, load status, and planner details. Integrates with Oracle TMS load optimization module. Critical for FTL road and ocean vessel operations.'
}

Table freight.freight_carrier_assignment {
  freight_carrier_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier assignment record. Primary key.']

Carrier_buy_rate_id enables actual cost tracking at assignment level, supports margin calculation (sell r']

  carrier_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.carrier_payable. Business justification: Carrier assignments drive carrier payables (AP for carrier services). Assignment-to-payable link enables carrier cost tracking, rate validation, and carrier invoice reconciliation against contracted a']

Schedule-level carrier assignment reporting, capacity booking confirmation, and schedule fill-rate analysis all require']

  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: The tariff governing a carrier assignment determines applicable surcharges and base rates for that carrier. Needed for carrier cost validation, tariff compliance, and carrier selection audit. No exist']

Supports own-fleet vs subcontractor analysis and asset-level cost allocation. Operational tracking for inte']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: A carrier assignment results in one or more trips being dispatched. Linking assignment to trip enables cost-per-trip analysis, carrier performance measurement against contracted SLAs, and operational ']
  accessorial_charges_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total accessorial charges for additional services such as liftgate, inside delivery, detention, demurrage, Terminal Handling Charge (THC), or special equipment.']
  assignment_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier was assigned to the freight order. Represents the business event date of carrier selection.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the carrier assignment in its lifecycle: pending carrier confirmation, confirmed by carrier, rejected by carrier, cancelled by shipper, or expired due to timeout.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the carrier assignment was created in the system. Supports audit trail and carrier selection analytics.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for cancellation if the carrier assignment was cancelled. Includes carrier rejection reason, shipper cancellation reason, or system timeout. Null if not cancelled.']
  carrier_booking_reference varchar [note: 'Booking reference number or confirmation code provided by the carrier upon acceptance. Used for tracking and coordination with carrier systems.']
  carrier_iata_code varchar [note: 'IATA two-letter or three-character airline designator code for air carriers. Used for air waybill (AWB) and flight operations.']
  carrier_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions or requirements specific to this carrier assignment, such as handling requirements, delivery windows, contact information, or operational constraints.']
  carrier_scac_code varchar [note: 'Standard Carrier Alpha Code uniquely identifying the carrier in North American freight operations. 2-4 uppercase letters assigned by the National Motor Freight Traffic Association (NMFTA).']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'Date when the carrier confirmed acceptance of the freight assignment. Null if not yet confirmed.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the carrier confirmed the assignment. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking and carrier performance measurement.']
  container_count varchar [note: 'Number of containers assigned to this carrier for ocean or intermodal freight. Applicable for Full Container Load (FCL) and Less Than Container Load (LCL) shipments.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier assignment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight charge amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_broker_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this carrier assignment requires customs brokerage services for cross-border shipments. Integrates with Descartes Customs system.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or container assigned by the carrier, such as 20ft dry container, 40ft high cube, refrigerated container (reefer), flatbed trailer, or tanker.']
  estimated_delivery_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the carrier will deliver the freight to the destination. Used for customer communication and Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  estimated_pickup_date date [note: 'Estimated date when the carrier will pick up the freight from the origin location. Used for planning and coordination.']
  freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base freight charge amount quoted or contracted with the carrier for this assignment, excluding surcharges and accessorial fees.']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fuel surcharge or Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) applied by the carrier to account for fuel price fluctuations. Separate from base freight charge.']
  hazmat_certified boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the assigned carrier is certified to transport hazardous materials (dangerous goods) per International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code or ICAO Technical Instructions.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterms rule defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for this carrier assignment. Determines carrier liability and cost allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_intermodal boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this carrier assignment is part of an intermodal transport chain involving multiple modes (e.g., ocean to rail to road).']
  is_primary_carrier boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary carrier assignment for the freight order. True if primary, False if backup or secondary carrier.']
  master_awb_number varchar [note: 'Master Air Waybill number issued by the air carrier for air freight assignments. Format: 3-digit airline code followed by 8-digit serial number.']
  master_bol_number varchar [note: 'Master Bill of Lading number issued by the ocean carrier or Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier (NVOCC) for ocean freight assignments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier assignment record was last modified. Updated on any change to assignment status, confirmation, or carrier details.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this carrier assignment for operational sequencing and resource allocation: critical (urgent), high, normal, or low priority.']
  selection_criteria varchar [note: 'Business criteria or rules applied during carrier selection, such as lowest cost, fastest transit, preferred carrier, service level, or capacity availability.']
  selection_method varchar [note: 'Method used to select this carrier: manual selection by planner, automated rule-based selection, optimization algorithm, spot market quote, or pre-negotiated contract assignment.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of carrier service assigned: Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), express, standard, economy, or charter. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy|charter — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this carrier assignment requires temperature-controlled (refrigerated or heated) transport equipment.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total capacity in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) assigned to this carrier. Standard measure for container capacity where one 20-foot container equals 1 TEU and one 40-foot container equals 2 TEU.']
  total_carrier_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost payable to the carrier including base freight charge, fuel surcharge, and all accessorial charges. Used for freight audit and cost reconciliation.']
  tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether real-time shipment tracking is enabled for this carrier assignment via integration with FourKites or carrier tracking systems.']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected transit time in days from pickup to delivery as committed by the carrier. Used for performance measurement and On-Time Delivery (OTD) tracking.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this carrier assignment: air freight, ocean freight, road (truck), rail, or intermodal (combination of modes).']
  vehicle_count varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles (trucks, trailers, railcars) assigned by the carrier for this freight order. Applicable for road and rail transport modes.']

  Note: 'Records the assignment of a carrier (airline, ocean carrier, road haulier, rail operator) to a specific freight order or consolidation. Captures carrier SCAC/IATA code, service type, assigned lane, contracted rate reference, assignment date, confirmation status, carrier booking reference, and any carrier-specific instructions. Supports carrier selection logic from SAP TM and freight audit in Oracle TMS. Distinct from the contract domain which owns the carrier agreement terms.'
}

  transfer_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility.   transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Each freight leg is executed by a specific transport asset. Critical for multi-modal tracking, asset utilization per leg, and operational performance analysis. Replaces denormalized vehicle_registrati']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: Each freight leg is physically executed as a fleet trip. Linking freight_leg to trip enables actual vs. planned transit time analysis, fuel and emissions reporting per leg, and end-to-end shipment tra']
  actual_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual arrival date and time when the freight reached the destination location for this leg. Used for performance tracking and On-Time Delivery (OTD) calculation.']
  actual_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual departure date and time when the freight left the origin location for this leg. Used for performance tracking and On-Time Delivery (OTD) calculation.']
  actual_transit_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual transit time for this leg in hours. Calculated as the difference between actual arrival and actual departure timestamps. Used for On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance measurement.']
  baf_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Bunker Adjustment Factor surcharge for this leg. Fuel-related surcharge applied to ocean freight to account for fluctuating fuel costs.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type and size of container used for this leg. Indicates standard dry containers (20ft, 40ft, 40ft High Cube), refrigerated (reefer), tank, flat rack, or open top containers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 20ft|40ft|40ft_HC|45ft|reefer|tank|flat_rack|open_top — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this freight leg record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of delay for this leg in hours. Calculated as the difference between actual and scheduled arrival times when a delay occurs.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any delay on this leg. Used for root cause analysis and carrier performance evaluation.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility at the destination of this leg. Classifies the ending point as port, airport, warehouse, Container Freight Station (CFS), Inland Container Depot (ICD), terminal, or depot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port|airport|warehouse|CFS|ICD|terminal|depot — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total distance covered by this leg in kilometers. Used for mileage-based pricing, carbon footprint calculation, and route optimization.']
  dwell_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration in hours that freight remains at the destination location before moving to the next leg. Critical for intermodal transfer efficiency and Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring.']
  freight_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Base freight charges for this leg. Excludes surcharges such as Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF), General Rate Increase (GRI), and Terminal Handling Charge (THC).']
  freight_charges_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the freight charges amount. Indicates the currency in which base freight fees are denominated.']
  gri_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'General Rate Increase surcharge for this leg. Carrier-imposed rate adjustment applied to base freight charges, typically for ocean freight.']
  handling_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total handling charges incurred at the destination of this leg. Includes Terminal Handling Charge (THC), Container Freight Station (CFS) charges, and other transfer-related fees.']
  handling_charges_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the handling charges amount. Indicates the currency in which handling fees are denominated.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg carries hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance with International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code or ICAO Technical Instructions.']
  is_intermodal_transfer boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg involves an intermodal transfer (change of transport mode) at the destination. True if freight transitions between air/ocean/road/rail at the end of this leg.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this freight leg record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this freight leg in its lifecycle. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion.']
  origin_location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility at the origin of this leg. Classifies the starting point as port, airport, warehouse, Container Freight Station (CFS), Inland Container Depot (ICD), terminal, or depot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port|airport|warehouse|CFS|ICD|terminal|depot — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned arrival date and time for this leg at the destination location. Represents the Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) used for planning and customer communication.']
  scheduled_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned departure date and time for this leg from the origin location. Represents the Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) used for planning and customer communication.']
  sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this leg within the multi-leg freight movement. Indicates the position in the transport chain (e.g., 1 for first leg, 2 for second leg).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of freight service for this leg. Indicates Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), or express/standard/economy service levels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg requires temperature-controlled transport (refrigerated or heated). Applicable for perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, and temperature-sensitive cargo.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for this leg. Standard measure of container capacity where a 20ft container equals 1 TEU and a 40ft container equals 2 TEU.']
  thc_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Terminal Handling Charge for this leg. Fee charged for handling containers at the origin or destination terminal.']
  total_leg_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total charges for this leg including base freight, surcharges (BAF, GRI, THC), and handling charges. Represents the complete cost of this transport segment.']
  total_leg_charges_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total leg charges amount. Indicates the currency in which total leg costs are denominated.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Type of intermodal transfer occurring at the destination of this leg. Specifies the mode transition (e.g., ocean to road, air to road, rail to road) or none if no transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ocean_to_road|ocean_to_rail|air_to_road|road_to_ocean|road_to_rail|rail_to_road|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transit_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned transit time for this leg in hours. Calculated as the difference between scheduled arrival and scheduled departure timestamps.']
  transport_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the transport document for this leg. Can be an Air Waybill (AWB), Bill of Lading (BOL), House Air Waybill (HAWB), Master Air Waybill (MAWB), House Bill of Lading (HBL), or Master Bill of Lading (MBL) depending on mode.']
  transport_document_type varchar [note: 'Type of transport document issued for this leg. Identifies whether it is an Air Waybill (AWB), Bill of Lading (BOL), House/Master variants, or road/rail consignment note. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AWB|BOL|HAWB|MAWB|HBL|MBL|CMR|CIM — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for this leg. Defines whether the segment is moved by air, ocean, road, rail, or a combination (intermodal).']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the ocean vessel or aircraft used for this leg. Applicable for air and ocean transport modes.']
  voyage_flight_number varchar [note: 'Voyage number (for ocean) or flight number (for air) for this leg. Identifies the specific sailing or flight carrying the freight.']

  Note: 'Represents an individual transport segment within a multi-leg or intermodal freight movement, including intermodal transfer details at interchange points. Each leg captures mode of transport, origin and destination nodes (port, airport, ICD, CFS, warehouse), carrier for that segment, ETD/ETA, actual departure/arrival timestamps, leg sequence number, transport document reference, leg status, and transfer metadata (transfer type, dwell time, handling charges) when transitioning between modes. Enables end-to-end intermodal tracking, transfer management, and dwell time SLA monitoring across air, ocean, road, and rail.'
}

Table freight.intermodal_transfer {
  intermodal_transfer_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intermodal transfer event. Primary key.']
  accessorial_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.accessorial_charge. Business justification: Transfer handling charges (THC, drayage, transshipment fees) are accessorial charges applied at intermodal transfer points. Linking enables accurate transfer cost capture and accessorial charge audit.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: Intermodal transfers can involve air legs (e.g., air-to-road transfer at airport). Many transfers for one AWB. Nullable when transfer is for ocean or other modes.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.bill_of_lading. Business justification: Intermodal transfers can involve ocean legs (e.g., ocean-to-rail transfer at port). Many transfers for one BOL. Nullable when transfer is for air or other modes.']
  broker_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.broker_assignment.   carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: An intermodal transfer involves a specific carrier taking custody of the shipment at the transfer point. Carrier attribution at each transfer is essential for liability tracking, claims management, an']
  carrier_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.carrier_payable. Business justification: Intermodal transfers incur terminal handling, drayage, and transshipment charges payable to terminal operators or drayage carriers. AP teams require this link to process transfer-specific carrier paya']
