Business justification: Intermodal transfers can involve consolidated cargo (e.g., consolidated container transferred from vessel to rail). Many transfers for one consolidation. Nullable when transfer is for individual shipm']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Transfers move specific containers between transport modes. Core intermodal operation tracking. Replaces denormalized container_number string with proper FK to container_unit for referential integrity']
  corridor_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.corridor_leg. Business justification: An intermodal transfer executes a specific corridor leg transition in the planned network. Corridor leg performance tracking, transfer dwell time vs planned transit hours, and intermodal network optim']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Intermodal transfers at border crossings and bonded facilities require customs declarations for in-bond movement authorization. Linking intermodal_transfer to declaration enables clearance status trac']
  dock_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.dock_appointment. Business justification: Intermodal transfers at a facility require a dock appointment for the physical transfer operation (unloading from one mode, loading onto another). This link enables transfer scheduling, dwell time tra']
  freight_carrier_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.carrier_assignment. Business justification: Intermodal transfers involve carrier handoffs (inbound carrier to outbound carrier). Many transfers can be associated with carrier assignments. Nullable when transfer is internal (within same carrier ']
  freight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_leg. Business justification: An intermodal transfer occurs at the junction between two freight legs — it is the physical handoff event between transport modes. Linking intermodal_transfer to freight_leg establishes which leg tran']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent freight order or booking that this intermodal transfer is part of, providing end-to-end shipment context.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hold. Business justification: Customs holds are frequently placed during intermodal transfers at ports or border crossings. Direct link enables operations to identify which transfer point is physically blocked, calculate storage c']
  pricing_charge_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.charge_code. Business justification: Charge codes classify transfer costs (THC, handling, storage) for GL allocation and billing. Standard in intermodal operations to code each transfer charge for financial reporting. No existing pricing']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: The tariff governing transfer charges at a hub or port is needed for charge validation and regulatory compliance. Port tariffs and terminal handling tariffs govern intermodal transfer costs. No existi']
  transfer_facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Intermodal transfers occur at warehouse facilities (CFS, ICD, cross-dock terminals). Formalizing transfer_facility_id as FK (replacing denormalized transfer_location_code/name) enables facility-level ']
  transfer_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Intermodal transfers occur at network nodes (ports, rail yards, air hubs). Dwell time analysis, node throughput reporting, and SLA compliance tracking by transfer point require linking intermodal_tran']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Intermodal transfers involve specific handling assets at transfer points. Required for transfer coordination, dwell time analysis, and asset utilization at intermodal facilities. Role-prefixed to dist']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: An intermodal transfer (e.g., port-to-rail handoff) is executed as part of a specific trip. Dwell-time SLA compliance, transfer cost allocation, and operational performance reporting require linking t']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type and size classification of the container involved in the transfer, following ISO container type codes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 20ft_dry|40ft_dry|40ft_hc|20ft_reefer|40ft_reefer|flat_rack|open_top|tank — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this intermodal transfer record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts associated with this transfer.']
  customs_clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether customs clearance is required at this transfer point. True = customs clearance required, False = no customs clearance.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance process at the transfer point, if applicable.']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total duration of delay in hours beyond the scheduled transfer time, if applicable.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for any delay in the transfer process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|equipment|congestion|documentation|customs|labor|mechanical — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  dwell_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total time in hours that cargo remained at the interchange facility between inbound arrival and outbound departure, critical for SLA monitoring.']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the exception or delay encountered during the intermodal transfer, including root cause and impact.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether any exceptions or irregularities occurred during the transfer. True = exception occurred, False = normal transfer.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exception or irregularity encountered during the transfer, if any.']
  handling_equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of material handling equipment used to perform the physical transfer of cargo between transport modes.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the cargo contains hazardous materials requiring special handling during transfer. True = hazmat cargo, False = non-hazmat.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional operational notes, special instructions, or observations related to the intermodal transfer.']
  scheduled_transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The planned date and time for the intermodal transfer as per the original schedule or booking.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or cargo unit at the transfer point to ensure cargo integrity.']
  seal_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of seal verification at the transfer point, indicating whether the inbound seal was intact and whether a new seal was applied.']
  sla_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the actual dwell time met the SLA target. True = compliant, False = SLA breach.']
  sla_dwell_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable dwell time in hours as defined by the service level agreement or operational target for this transfer type.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record that originated this intermodal transfer event (SAP TM, Oracle TMS, FourKites visibility platform, or manual entry).']
  storage_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional charge for cargo storage at the interchange facility if dwell time exceeds free time allowances.']
  supervisor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the on-site supervisor or shift manager who oversaw the transfer operation.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the cargo requires temperature control during transfer. True = temperature-controlled cargo, False = ambient cargo.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'The TEU equivalent of the cargo being transferred, used for capacity planning and billing. One 20ft container = 1 TEU, one 40ft container = 2 TEU.']
  thc_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Terminal Handling Charge assessed at the interchange facility for container handling, loading, unloading, and related terminal services.']
  total_transfer_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost of the intermodal transfer including handling charges, THC, storage, and any other applicable fees.']
  transfer_facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the interchange facility type. ICD = Inland Container Depot, CFS = Container Freight Station.']
  transfer_handling_charge decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary charge for the physical handling and transfer of cargo at the interchange facility, excluding other accessorial charges.']
  transfer_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the intermodal transfer, used for tracking and documentation across systems and partners.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the intermodal transfer operation, tracking progression from scheduled through completion or exception states.']
  transfer_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the physical transfer of cargo between transport modes occurred at the interchange point.']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'The mode-to-mode transfer type indicating the inbound and outbound transport modes involved in this intermodal transfer.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous material classification, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this intermodal transfer record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Captures the physical transfer of cargo between transport modes at interchange points such as ICDs, CFSs, rail terminals, and port facilities. Records transfer location, transfer type (ocean-to-rail, air-to-road, etc.), inbound and outbound leg references, transfer date/time, dwell time, handling charges, transfer status, and any exceptions or delays encountered during the transfer. Supports cross-modal visibility and dwell time SLA monitoring.'
}

Table freight.freight_charge {
  freight_charge_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight charge line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  accessorial_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.accessorial_charge. Business justification: Accessorial charges (liftgate, residential delivery, inside delivery) must reference the accessorial charge master for standardized billing and revenue recognition. Accessorial_charge_id ensures consi']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer contract or rate agreement under which this charge is calculated. Null if the charge is based on spot rates or standard tariffs.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: Freight charges are itemized at the air waybill level (e.g., air freight charge, fuel surcharge, security surcharge). Many charges for one AWB. Nullable when charge is for ocean or other modes.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.bill_of_lading. Business justification: Freight charges are itemized at the bill of lading level (e.g., ocean freight, BAF, CAF, THC, documentation fee). Many charges for one BOL. Nullable when charge is for air or other modes.']
  billing_invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Freight charges must map to invoice lines for charge-to-bill reconciliation. Critical for freight audit, revenue assurance, and dispute resolution. Enables operational charge validation against billed']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.booking. Business justification: Freight charges can be estimated and recorded at booking stage (e.g., estimated freight charge in booking table). Many charges for one booking. Nullable when charge is for confirmed order rather than ']
  broker_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.broker_assignment. Business justification: Brokerage fees are freight charges tied to specific broker assignments. Linking freight_charge to broker_assignment enables broker fee tracking, cost allocation by broker, and reconciliation against a']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: A freight charge is assessed by or payable to a specific carrier. The existing carrier_code is a denormalized text field. A proper FK enables direct carrier-level charge reconciliation, carrier payabl']
  carrier_payable_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.carrier_payable. Business justification: Freight charges include carrier cost components requiring reconciliation against carrier payables. Charge-to-payable link enables cost-to-payable matching and supports freight margin analysis.']
  consolidation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.consolidation. Business justification: Freight charges can be consolidation-level (e.g., consolidation fee, deconsolidation fee, CFS handling charge). Many charges for one consolidation. Nullable when charge is for individual shipments.']
  contract_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.contract_rate. Business justification: Contract-based freight charges must link to specific negotiated contract rates for customer billing validation and dispute resolution. Contract_rate_id enables verification that billed rates match con']
  contract_surcharge_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.contract_surcharge_schedule. Business justification: Applied surcharges (BAF, GRI, security) must be validated against the contracted surcharge schedule for billing compliance. Linking freight_charge to contract_surcharge_schedule enables automated surc']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.billing_dispute. Business justification: Individual freight charges (surcharges, accessorials, base rates) are the specific line items disputed in billing disputes. Linking freight_charge directly to billing_dispute enables dispute teams to ']
  duty_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.duty_assessment. Business justification: Freight charges include duty and tax components assessed by customs. Linking freight_charge to duty_assessment enables accurate billing reconciliation between freight charges and customs-assessed duti']

Many charges for one carrier assignment. Nullable when charg']
  freight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_leg. Business justification: Freight charges are often leg-specific (e.g., per-leg freight charge, BAF, GRI, THC, handling charges at interchange points). Many charges for one leg. Nullable when charge is at order or shipment lev']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent freight order to which this charge applies. Links this charge to the shipment booking.']
  intermodal_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.intermodal_transfer. Business justification: Intermodal transfers generate specific charges including Terminal Handling Charges (THC), storage charges, and transfer handling charges. freight_charge already has thc_amount context via charge_type.']
  outbound_shipment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.outbound_shipment_order.   parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: Parcel-level freight charge allocation is required for merchant invoicing, carrier cost reconciliation, and profitability analysis per shipment in express logistics. freight_charge currently links to ']

  pricing_surcharge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.surcharge. Business justification: Individual surcharge line items (fuel, security, peak season) must reference the surcharge definition master for billing transparency and regulatory compliance. Pricing_surcharge_id links each charge ']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.quote. Business justification: freight_charge records itemized charge components. Many charges are derived from or validated against a formal quote issued to the customer. Linking freight_charge to quote via quote_id enables quote-']

Business justification: Freight charges must be validated against the contractual rate schedule for billing audit and dispute resolution. The contract.rate_schedule is the authoritative contractual rate basis, distinct from ']

  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated monetary value of this charge component in the specified currency. Represents the total charge before any adjustments or taxes.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this charge requires approval and its current approval state. Used for charges that exceed thresholds or require manual review before billing.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who approved this charge. Null if approval is not required or the charge has not yet been approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this charge was approved. Null if approval is not required or the charge has not yet been approved.']
  basis varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement or calculation method for the charge. Defines how the charge is computed, such as per Twenty-foot Equivalent Unit (TEU), per kilogram, per Cubic Meter (CBM), flat rate, or as a percentage of another value. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_TEU|per_kg|per_CBM|per_shipment|per_container|per_pallet|per_package|per_ton|per_mile|per_kilometer|per_day|flat_rate|percentage — promote to reference product]']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of the charge for financial reporting and analysis. Distinguishes between core freight charges, surcharges, accessorial services, regulatory fees, administrative fees, and penalty charges.']
  charge_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the charge component. Provides additional context beyond the charge type for billing transparency and customer communication.']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'The current lifecycle status of this charge. Tracks whether the charge is pending approval, approved for billing, already invoiced, paid, under dispute, waived, or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|approved|invoiced|paid|disputed|waived|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the freight charge component. Identifies whether this is a base freight rate or a specific surcharge type such as Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF), General Rate Increase (GRI), Terminal Handling Charge (THC), fuel surcharge, peak season surcharge, port congestion surcharge, documentation fee, or other mode-specific and service-specific charges. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: base_freight|BAF|GRI|THC|fuel_surcharge|peak_season_surcharge|port_congestion_surcharge|documentation_fee|customs_clearance_fee|handling_charge|storage_charge|demurrage|detention|chassis_fee|inland_haulage|security_surcharge|war_risk_surcharge|currency_adjustment_factor|low_sulfur_surcharge|emergency_bunker_surcharge|container_service_charge — promote to reference product]']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which this charge is allocated for internal cost accounting and profitability analysis. Used for operational cost tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this charge record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and charge lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the charge amount. Identifies the currency in which this charge is denominated.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the destination location (port, airport, terminal, or facility) for which this charge applies. Used for location-specific charges such as destination terminal handling or delivery surcharges.']
  direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is billable to the customer (shipper or consignee) or payable to a carrier or vendor. Determines the financial flow and accounting treatment of the charge.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'The reason provided for disputing this charge. Null if the charge is not disputed. Used for dispute resolution and root cause analysis.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which this charge rate or surcharge is applicable. Used for time-based charge validity and rate management.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The end date until which this charge rate or surcharge is applicable. Null for open-ended charges. Used for time-based charge validity and rate management.']
  exchange_rate decimal(18,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied if currency conversion was performed. Used to convert the charge from the original currency to the billing currency. Null if no conversion was required.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The General Ledger account code to which this charge is posted for financial accounting. Used for revenue recognition or cost allocation depending on charge direction.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The invoice document number to which this charge has been posted. Null if the charge has not yet been invoiced. Links the charge to the billing domain.']
  is_billable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge should be included in customer invoicing. True if the charge is billable to the customer, false if it is an internal cost or non-billable item.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge is currently under dispute by the customer or carrier. True if a dispute has been raised, false otherwise.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this charge is subject to tax (VAT, GST, sales tax). Used to determine if tax calculation should be applied during invoicing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this charge. Used to capture special circumstances, manual adjustments, dispute details, or other contextual information.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the origin location (port, airport, terminal, or facility) for which this charge applies. Used for location-specific charges such as terminal handling or origin surcharges.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center to which this charge is allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Used for business unit performance measurement.']
  quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity or volume to which the charge rate is applied. For example, number of TEUs, weight in kilograms, volume in CBM, or number of days. Used in conjunction with charge_basis and unit_rate to calculate the charge amount.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The freight service level to which this charge applies. Identifies whether the charge is for Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), express, standard, economy, or charter services. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|express|standard|economy|charter — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or code of the operational system from which this charge originated. Examples include SAP TM, Oracle TMS, or manual entry. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this charge in the source operational system. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracking.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax classification code applicable to this charge. Used by the financial system to determine the correct tax rate and treatment. Null if the charge is not taxable.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation to which this charge applies. Identifies whether the charge is specific to air freight, ocean freight, road transport, rail transport, intermodal movements, or courier services.']
  unit_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'The rate per unit for this charge. Combined with quantity to calculate the total charge amount. For percentage-based charges, this represents the percentage value.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user or system process that last modified this charge record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this charge record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user or system process that created this charge record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Itemized charge component associated with a freight order or shipment, capturing mode-specific surcharges and base freight costs. Stores charge type (BAF, GRI, THC, fuel surcharge, peak season surcharge, port congestion surcharge, documentation fee), charge basis (per TEU, per kg, per CBM, flat), currency, amount, applicable date range, and whether the charge is billable to shipper or payable to carrier. Integrates with SAP TM charge calculation and billing domain. Distinct from the billing domain invoice which aggregates charges.'
}

Table freight.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight milestone event record. Primary key.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: Freight milestones track key events in the shipment lifecycle. For air freight, milestones are associated with the air waybill (e.g., cargo acceptance, departure, arrival, delivery). Many milestones f']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.bill_of_lading. Business justification: Freight milestones track key events in the shipment lifecycle. For ocean freight, milestones are associated with the bill of lading (e.g., vessel departure, port arrival, customs clearance, delivery).']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the booking record associated with this milestone, if applicable.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Milestones are reported by carriers (carrier scan events, departure/arrival confirmations). Carrier attribution on milestones is essential for carrier OTD performance measurement, SLA breach attributi']
  consolidation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.consolidation. Business justification: Freight milestones can track consolidation-level events such as cargo cutoff, consolidation complete, deconsolidation start, deconsolidation complete. Many milestones for one consolidation. Nullable w']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Container-specific milestones (gated in, loaded, discharged) are standard in container tracking systems. Essential for container visibility and event-driven workflows. Replaces denormalized container_']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Customs milestones (Entry Filed, Customs Cleared, Released) are tracked against specific declarations. Linking milestone to declaration enables declaration-level milestone reporting for customer shipm']
  dock_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.dock_appointment. Business justification: Dock-level milestones (arrived at facility, check-in complete, loading started, loading complete) are directly tied to dock appointments. This link enables real-time dock visibility and appointment co']
  driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: POD milestones and pickup confirmations are executed by a specific driver. Driver performance tracking, POD audit trails, and e-signature compliance require linking milestone records to the driver who']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.facility. Business justification: Freight milestones like \'Arrived at CFS\', \'Departed warehouse\', \'Customs cleared at facility\' occur at specific warehouse facilities. Tracking milestone_facility_id enables facility-level milestone pe']
  freight_carrier_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_carrier_assignment. Business justification: milestone tracks key events in the freight lifecycle. Carrier assignment events (confirmation, cancellation, space confirmation) are significant milestones that should be linked to the specific freigh']
  freight_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.freight_leg. Business justification: Freight milestones track events at the leg level (departure from origin, arrival at destination, transfer at interchange point). Many milestones for one leg. Nullable when milestone is at order or shi']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent freight order for which this milestone event occurred.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hold. Business justification: Hold placement and release events generate milestone records (Hold Placed, Under Examination, Released). Linking milestone to hold enables hold lifecycle tracking as discrete milestone events, support']
  intermodal_transfer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.intermodal_transfer. Business justification: Intermodal transfers are significant operational events that generate milestones (transfer_started, transfer_completed, customs_cleared_at_transfer). Linking milestone to intermodal_transfer enables t']
  invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice. Business justification: Milestone-based billing (e.g., invoice release triggered by POD milestone) is standard in logistics contracts. Linking milestone to invoice enables automated billing triggers, milestone-to-payment tra']
  last_mile_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.last_mile_dispatch. Business justification: Milestone events (out-for-delivery, delivery-attempted, delivered) are directly associated with last-mile dispatch runs. This link enables real-time customer tracking notifications, SLA breach detecti']
  load_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.load_plan. Business justification: Load plan execution generates significant operational milestones (load_started, load_completed, weight_verified, hazmat_segregation_verified). Linking milestone to load_plan via load_plan_id enables t']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Milestones are raised at network nodes. location_code and location_name on milestone are denormalized from network_node. Node-level milestone throughput reporting, dwell time analysis, and hub perform']
  notification_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Milestone notifications (POD received, customs cleared, delivery attempted) are sent to customer contacts. milestone has notification_sent and notification_timestamp but no FK to the contact record — ']
  outbound_shipment_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.outbound_shipment_order. Business justification: Milestones (departed warehouse, in transit, delivered) must link to the originating outbound shipment order for end-to-end customer visibility reporting and OTD SLA measurement — a core KPI in logisti']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: Parcel-level milestone tracking (scan events, delivery confirmations, exceptions) is fundamental to express logistics operations. Each tracking event references a specific parcel. This link enables pa']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific organization or entity responsible for this milestone (e.g., carrier ID, warehouse ID, customs broker ID).']
  plan_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.plan_leg. Business justification: Milestones are raised against specific planned legs to track execution events (departure, arrival, customs clearance). Planned vs actual milestone timing analysis and SLA breach detection at leg level']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: Milestone events (pickup confirmation, gate-out, POD) are generated during a specific trip. Real-time tracking dashboards and SLA reporting require correlating milestones to the executing trip. A logi']
  actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the milestone event occurred, as confirmed by carrier, warehouse, customs, or tracking system.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the location where the milestone event occurred.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that created this milestone record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_number varchar [note: 'Customs declaration or clearance reference number if this milestone is related to customs processing.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System or channel through which the milestone event data was received (e.g., EDI message, FourKites real-time feed, manual entry, carrier API). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: edi|api|fourkites|manual|carrier_portal|tms|wms|customs_system — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Duration of delay in hours if this milestone was delayed compared to the planned or estimated timestamp.']
  edi_message_type varchar [note: 'EDI message type code if this milestone was received via EDI (e.g., IFTSTA, IFTMAN, DESADV for EDIFACT; 214, 315 for ANSI X12).']
  estimated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Current estimated date and time for this milestone event, updated dynamically based on real-time tracking data from FourKites and carrier feeds. Represents predictive ETA.']
  exception_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the type of exception if is_exception is true (e.g., DELAY_WEATHER, CUSTOMS_HOLD, MISSED_CUTOFF, DAMAGED_CARGO).']
  exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the exception or issue that occurred at this milestone.']
  is_critical_milestone boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is considered critical for SLA compliance and customer notifications (e.g., vessel departure, customs clearance, delivery).']
  is_exception boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone represents an exception or deviation from the planned schedule (e.g., delay, missed connection, customs hold).']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Type of facility or location where the milestone event occurred. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: port|airport|warehouse|cfs|icd|customs_facility|customer_site|carrier_terminal — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'High-level category grouping for the milestone event to support analytics and reporting segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: booking|origin|transit|customs|destination|delivery|exception — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the milestone event type (e.g., BKG_CONF, CARGO_RCV, CUST_CLR, VSL_DEP, VSL_ARR, POD_RCV). Aligns with industry EDI standards and internal TMS milestone taxonomy.']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the milestone event (e.g., Booking Confirmed, Cargo Received at Origin, Customs Cleared, Vessel Departed, Vessel Arrived, Proof of Delivery Received).']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the milestone event in its lifecycle.']
  notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a customer notification (email, SMS, portal alert) was sent for this milestone event.']
  notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer notification was sent for this milestone event.']
  planned_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Originally scheduled or planned date and time for this milestone event to occur, based on initial routing and scheduling.']
  pod_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or storage path to the electronic proof of delivery document (ePOD) or scanned signature.']
  pod_signature_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who signed for delivery, if applicable and captured.']
  proof_of_delivery_received boolean [note: 'Indicates whether proof of delivery documentation has been received for this milestone (applicable to delivery milestones).']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text remarks or notes associated with this milestone event, provided by the responsible party or operations team.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the organization or entity responsible for executing or reporting this milestone event.']
  responsible_party_type varchar [note: 'Type of organization or entity responsible for executing or reporting this milestone event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier|warehouse|customs_broker|freight_forwarder|customer|consignee|shipper|terminal_operator — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this milestone within the freight order lifecycle. Enables chronological sorting and progress tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external or internal system that originated this milestone event record (e.g., SAP TM order number, Oracle TMS shipment ID, FourKites tracking ID).']
  temperature_recorded_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading recorded at this milestone for temperature-controlled shipments.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation relevant to this milestone event.']
  updated_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user or system process that last updated this milestone record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this milestone record was last updated or modified.']
  vessel_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vessel, aircraft, truck, or rail car associated with this milestone event, if applicable.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage number (ocean), flight number (air), or trip identifier (road/rail) associated with this milestone event.']

  Note: 'Event-based tracking record capturing key milestones in the freight order lifecycle such as booking confirmed, cargo received at origin, customs cleared, vessel departed, vessel arrived, cargo available for delivery, and POD received. Each milestone stores event code, event description, planned and actual timestamps, location, responsible party, data source (EDI, FourKites, manual), and exception flag. Integrates with FourKites real-time visibility platform for predictive ETA updates.'
}

Table freight.exception {
  exception_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight exception record. Primary key.']
  air_waybill_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.air_waybill. Business justification: Freight exceptions capture disruptions and deviations. For air freight, exceptions can be specific to an air waybill (e.g., flight delay, cargo damage, customs hold). Many exceptions for one AWB. Null']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.bill_of_lading. Business justification: Freight exceptions capture disruptions and deviations. For ocean freight, exceptions can be specific to a bill of lading (e.g., vessel delay, port congestion, container damage). Many exceptions for on']
  booking_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the booking associated with this exception, if applicable.']
  broker_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.broker_assignment. Business justification: Customs-related freight exceptions require broker escalation and resolution. Linking freight_exception to broker_assignment identifies the responsible broker for accountability tracking, SLA measureme']

  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Freight exceptions are attributed to specific carriers for SLA breach tracking, claims filing, and carrier performance reporting. The existing carrier_code is a denormalized text field. A proper FK en']
  consolidation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.consolidation. Business justification: Freight exceptions can occur at consolidation level (e.g., consolidation delay, deconsolidation issue, cargo discrepancy during CFS operations). Many exceptions for one consolidation. Nullable when ex']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Container-specific exceptions (damaged, delayed, lost) require container reference for exception management and claims processing. Standard container exception handling. Replaces denormalized containe']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Freight exceptions caused by customs issues (documentation rejection, value discrepancy, classification dispute) must reference the specific declaration that triggered the exception. Enables root caus']
  dispute_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.dispute. Business justification: Freight exceptions (delays, damage, service failures) trigger billing disputes. Exception-to-dispute causality link enables operational root cause linkage to billing disputes and supports service reco']
  dock_appointment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.dock_appointment. Business justification: Dock-level exceptions (missed appointments, late arrivals, no-shows, seal failures) must link to the dock appointment for dock scheduling optimization, carrier penalty assessment, and SLA compliance r']

Business justification: Driver-attributed exceptions (cargo damage, delivery failures) feed driver performance reviews, insurance claims, and safety compliance reporting. Nullable when exception is at order or shipment']

  acknowledged_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or system that acknowledged the exception.']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was acknowledged by operations team or responsible party.']
  awb_or_bol_number varchar [note: 'Transport document number associated with the exception. AWB for air shipments, BOL for ocean/road shipments.']
  claim_filed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cargo claim was filed as a result of this exception. True if claim filed, false otherwise.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was formally closed after resolution verification and customer confirmation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this exception record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for financial impact amount.']
  customer_notification_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel used to notify customer of the exception. Includes email, SMS, customer portal, phone, and EDI.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer was notified of the exception via email, portal, or other communication channel.']
  customer_notified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has been notified of the exception. True if notification sent, false otherwise.']
  delay_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total delay caused by the exception, measured in hours. Calculated as difference between original ETA and revised ETA.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment destination.']
  detected_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was first detected or reported. Primary business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  detection_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location or facility where the exception was detected. May be port, airport, warehouse, or in-transit location.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'Origin or channel through which the exception was identified. Includes system automated alerts, carrier notifications, customer reports, warehouse scans, customs alerts, manual inspections, IoT sensors, and GPS tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_automated|carrier_notification|customer_report|warehouse_scan|customs_alert|manual_inspection|iot_sensor|gps_tracking — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  escalated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was escalated to higher management tier.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Current escalation tier for exception resolution. None indicates standard handling; tier 1-3 indicate progressive management escalation; executive indicates C-level involvement.']
  exception_category varchar [note: 'High-level grouping of exception types for reporting and analysis. Categories include operational, carrier, customs, documentation, cargo, infrastructure, and force majeure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: operational|carrier|customs|documentation|cargo|infrastructure|force_majeure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the exception, used in customer communications and tracking.']
  exception_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the exception. Tracks progression from detection through resolution.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the exception event. Includes delay, damage, missing cargo, carrier rollover, port congestion, customs hold, documentation error, weather disruption, equipment failure, and security incident. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delay|damage|missing_cargo|carrier_rollover|port_congestion|customs_hold|documentation_error|weather_disruption|equipment_failure|security_incident — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual financial impact of the exception, including additional costs, penalties, or revenue loss. Expressed in transaction currency.']
  freight_exception_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the exception event, including circumstances, impact, and any immediate actions taken.']
  impacted_leg_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the freight order leg affected by this exception. Null if exception impacts entire order.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, observations, or context related to the exception. Used for collaboration and knowledge capture.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the shipment origin where exception occurred or was detected.']
  preventable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the exception was preventable through better processes, controls, or carrier selection. Used for continuous improvement analysis.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception is a repeat occurrence on the same freight order or lane. True if recurring issue, false if first occurrence.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'Description of corrective actions taken to resolve the exception. Includes rerouting, re-booking, documentation correction, or customer accommodation.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the exception was fully resolved and normal operations resumed.']
  revised_eta timestamp [note: 'Updated estimated arrival time following the exception event. Communicated to customer as part of exception management.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the underlying cause of the exception. Captured during investigation and resolution process.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Standardized classification of root cause for trend analysis and continuous improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier_performance|customs_compliance|documentation_quality|cargo_handling|infrastructure|weather|security|process_failure — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business impact severity of the exception. Critical indicates immediate customer escalation required; high indicates significant delay or cost impact; medium indicates moderate impact; low indicates minor deviation.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this exception caused a breach of customer service level agreement commitments. True if SLA was breached, false otherwise.']
  sla_target_delivery_date date [note: 'Original committed delivery date per customer SLA. Used to calculate breach impact.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation affected by the exception. Aligns with freight order transport mode.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this exception record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Operational record capturing exceptions, disruptions, and deviations in freight order execution. Stores exception type (delay, damage, missing cargo, carrier rollover, port congestion, customs hold, documentation error), severity level, detection timestamp, detection source, impacted freight order and leg, root cause, resolution action, resolution timestamp, and SLA breach flag. Feeds into OTD and OTIF KPI tracking and customer exception notifications.'
}

Table freight.quote {
  quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the freight quote record. Primary key.']
  accessorial_charge_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.accessorial_charge. Enables accessorial charge traceability from quote through to invoi']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Quotes reference customer agreements to apply contracted rates vs spot rates.   cargo_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.cargo. Business justification: quote stores commodity_description, hs_code, and packaging_type which are authoritatively defined in the cargo master reference table. Adding cargo_id normalizes these fields. Weight, volume, teu_coun']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: The carrier buy rate underpins the cost side of a quote. Linking enables margin calculation at quote stage and buy/sell rate comparison for pricing decisions. No existing carrier_buy_rate_id on quote.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or transportation provider for the quoted service.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: A quote is provided for a specific carrier service with defined transit times and service levels. The existing carrier_service_code is a denormalized text field. A proper FK enables service availabili']
  consignee_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignee profile for the destination party of the freight movement.']
  contract_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.contract_rate.   credit_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.credit_profile. Business justification: Before issuing a quote with credit payment terms, the credit profile is checked to confirm credit status and available limit. Standard credit-to-cash process — commercial teams need this link for cred']

  rate_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_line. Business justification: The specific rate line from a rate card used to price a quote enables granular rate audit and rate line utilization reporting. Logistics pricing teams track which rate line was applied per quote for r']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.rate_schedule. Logistics pricing teams require this link for rate compliance validation, contract utilizatio']
  requestor_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Quotes are requested by and issued to a specific contact person.   revised_quote_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on quote (revised_quote_id)']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.rfq. Business justification: Carrier rate solicitation process: a freight quote is the carrier\'s response to a procurement RFQ issued during carrier sourcing events. Logistics procurement teams must link the awarded freight quote']
  service_preference_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.service_preference. Business justification: Quotes are generated against a customer\'s service preferences (preferred mode, incoterms, carrier, packaging).   shipper_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipper profile for the origin party of the freight movement.']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: Quotes must reflect the SLA tier and transit time guarantees the customer is entitled to.   spot_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.spot_quote.
