  supplier_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_quote. Business justification: In carrier procurement, a supplier_quote submitted by a carrier/3PL in response to an RFQ is the basis for generating a freight.quote. Linking these enables procurement-to-operations traceability: the']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Freight quotes must reference applicable published tariffs for regulatory compliance and customer transparency. Tariff_id documents the tariff basis for quoted rates, supports regulatory filings, and ']
  trade_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_agreement. Business justification: Freight quotes for preferential trade lanes reference specific trade agreements to apply reduced duty rates in landed cost calculations. Linking quote to trade_agreement enables automated preferential']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: Quotes are generated using transit time standards to compute estimated_transit_time_days, which is denormalized. Quote accuracy reporting and transit time standard utilization in pricing require this ']
  accessorial_charges_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount for accessorial services such as liftgate, inside delivery, residential delivery, or special handling.']
  base_freight_charge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Base freight charge amount quoted for the transportation service, excluding surcharges and accessorial fees.']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Chargeable weight used for pricing calculation, which may be the greater of gross weight or dimensional (DIM) weight.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'Type and size of container quoted for ocean freight (e.g., 20GP, 40GP, 40HC, 45HC, reefer).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight quote record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the quoted amounts.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the destination country of the freight movement.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Port, airport, facility, or city code representing the destination point of the freight movement (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE port code).']
  fuel_surcharge_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Fuel surcharge or Bunker Adjustment Factor (BAF) amount included in the quote to account for fuel price fluctuations.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo in kilograms, including packaging.']
  imdg_class varchar [note: 'IMDG classification class for dangerous goods, if applicable.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller for the quoted freight movement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_dangerous_goods boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the cargo contains dangerous goods or hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date when the freight quote was formally issued to the customer.']
  issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the freight quote was issued, capturing the business event time.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the origin country of the freight movement.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Port, airport, facility, or city code representing the origin point of the freight movement (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE port code).']
  package_count varchar [note: 'Total number of packages, pieces, or handling units included in the quote.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the freight quote, used for customer communication and reference.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the freight quote indicating its state in the quotation workflow.']
  quote_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the quote based on the commercial context: spot rate, contract rate, tender response, or Request for Quotation (RFQ) response.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of freight service quoted: Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), express, standard, or economy service level. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ftl|ltl|fcl|lcl|express|standard|economy — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Any special handling instructions, service requirements, or customer-specific notes associated with the quote.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the cargo requires temperature-controlled (reefer) transportation.']
  temperature_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature requirement in Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo.']
  temperature_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature requirement in Celsius for temperature-controlled cargo.']
  teu_count decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for container-based ocean freight quotes.']
  total_quoted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount quoted to the customer, including base freight charge, fuel surcharge, accessorial charges, and any applicable fees.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for the quoted freight movement: air, ocean, road, rail, or intermodal combination.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'United Nations number for dangerous goods classification, if applicable.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the freight quote record was last modified.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the quote validity period, indicating when the quoted rates become effective.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'End date of the quote validity period, after which the quoted rates are no longer binding.']
  volume_cbm decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo in cubic meters.']

  Note: 'Formal freight quotation issued to a shipper or customer for a specific freight movement, capturing quoted rates, validity period, service terms, and mode options prior to booking confirmation.'
}

Table freight.cargo {
  cargo_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for cargo']
  consolidated_cargo_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on cargo (consolidated_cargo_id)']
  dim_weight_rule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.dim_weight_rule. Business justification: The dim weight rule applied to cargo determines chargeable weight at the cargo level. Linking enables chargeable weight calculation audit and supports billing accuracy for dimensional cargo. No existi']
  hs_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hs_classification. Business justification: Every cargo record for international shipments requires HS classification for customs declaration, duty calculation, and export control compliance. Cargo is the natural entity for HS classification re']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Controlled goods (dual-use, weapons, chemicals) require export/import licenses tied to specific cargo. Linking cargo to license_permit enables compliance checks during booking and shipment processing ']
  origin_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.origin_determination. Business justification: Cargo origin determination drives preferential duty rates and trade agreement eligibility. Linking cargo to origin_determination enables automated preferential tariff application during customs filing']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: Inbound supply chain visibility: each cargo unit in freight corresponds to specific PO lines being shipped. Linking cargo to po_line enables shipment-to-order traceability for ETA management, receivin']
  cargo_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the cargo contents, used on shipping documents and customs declarations.']
  cargo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cargo master record indicating whether it is available for booking and operational use.']
  cargo_type varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the cargo indicating its handling category and regulatory requirements.']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The greater of actual gross weight or volumetric weight, used as the billing basis for freight rate calculations.']
  commodity_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System (HS) code classifying the cargo for customs tariff and trade statistics purposes.']
  commodity_description varchar [note: 'Standardized commodity description corresponding to the HS code, used for customs and trade compliance documentation.']
  container_type varchar [note: 'ISO container type code indicating the standard container size and specification suitable for this cargo (e.g., 20GP for 20-foot general purpose).']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the cargo was manufactured or produced, required for customs and trade compliance.']
  declared_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the cargo as declared by the shipper for customs and insurance purposes.']
  declared_value_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the declared cargo value (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this cargo master record becomes valid and available for use in booking and operational processes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this cargo master record is no longer valid for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended validity period.']
  export_license_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an export license or permit is required for this cargo based on its classification and destination restrictions.']
  gross_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the cargo including packaging, measured in kilograms. Used for load planning, carrier rate calculation, and regulatory compliance.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazardous materials classification (e.g., Class 1-9) indicating the nature of danger. Null for non-hazardous cargo. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1.1|1.2|1.3|2.1|2.2|3|4.1|4.2|5.1|6.1|7|8|9 — promote to reference product]']
  hazmat_packing_group varchar [note: 'Packing group (I, II, or III) indicating the degree of danger for hazardous cargo, determining packaging and handling requirements.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the cargo piece in centimeters, used for stacking constraints and equipment clearance verification.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Default Incoterms rule applicable to this cargo defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — promote to reference product]']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo insurance coverage is mandatory for this cargo based on value, type, or contractual requirements.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the cargo piece in centimeters, used for dimensional weight calculation and equipment compatibility checks.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Freight load classification: Full Container Load (FCL), Less than Container Load (LCL), Full Truck Load (FTL), or Less than Truck Load (LTL).']
  marks_and_numbers varchar [note: 'Shipping marks, labels, and identification numbers physically applied to the cargo packages for identification during handling and delivery.']
  max_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for temperature-sensitive cargo during transport and storage, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  min_temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for temperature-sensitive cargo during transport and storage, measured in degrees Celsius.']
  net_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Weight of the cargo contents excluding packaging, measured in kilograms. Required for customs declarations and commodity valuation.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of outer packaging used for the cargo, determining handling equipment requirements and stowage compatibility.']
  piece_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual pieces or packages comprising this cargo consignment.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business reference number used to identify the cargo across systems and documentation such as bills of lading and shipping instructions.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as fragile, keep upright, do not freeze, or live animals.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo can be stacked upon during storage and transport, affecting load plan optimization and warehouse utilization.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the cargo requires temperature-controlled transport and storage (reefer equipment).']
  teu_count decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of TEUs (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units) this cargo occupies, used for ocean freight capacity planning and terminal operations.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for which this cargo is configured, determining applicable regulations and handling procedures.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance or article, prefixed with \'UN\'. Required for dangerous goods documentation and placarding.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Total volume of the cargo measured in cubic meters. Used for volumetric weight calculations, container utilization, and load planning.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the cargo piece in centimeters, used for dimensional weight calculation and stowage planning.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for cargo. Referenced by cargo_id.'
}

Table warehouse.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warehouse or distribution center facility. Primary key for the facility master record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: 3PL-operated facilities are governed by a master logistics services agreement. Facility-level SLA compliance reporting, billing validation, and operator accountability all require knowing which agreem']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Warehouse facilities are modeled as network nodes in route planning — route plans, lanes, and hub-spoke configs reference facilities as origin/destination nodes. This link enables network topology que']
  operator_tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Many warehouses are operated by 3PL providers under management contracts. Critical for contract billing, performance SLA tracking, operational escalations, and distinguishing client-owned vs. 3PL-oper']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the facility location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, building, or unit information. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  annual_ghg_emissions_co2e_tonnes decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total annual greenhouse gas emissions from facility operations measured in tonnes of CO2 equivalent. Used for sustainability reporting and carbon footprint tracking.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  closed_date date [note: 'Date when the facility ceased operations, if applicable. Null for active facilities.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the facility. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the facility is located (e.g., USA, GBR, CHN).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customs_bonded_warehouse boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is a customs bonded warehouse authorized to store imported goods before duties and taxes are paid.']
  dock_door_count varchar [note: 'Total number of loading and unloading dock doors available at the facility for inbound and outbound freight operations.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the facility, used across operational systems and customer communications. Typically a short alphanumeric code.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the warehouse or distribution center facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the facility. DC = Distribution Center, CFS = Container Freight Station, ICD = Inland Container Depot, FTZ = Free Trade Zone, cross-dock = Cross-Docking Facility, fulfillment = E-Commerce Fulfillment Center.']
  ftz_designation varchar [note: 'Official FTZ designation number if the facility operates within a free trade zone, allowing duty deferral and other trade benefits.']
  has_cold_storage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility has refrigerated or frozen storage capabilities for temperature-sensitive goods.']
  has_hazmat_storage boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is certified and equipped to store hazardous materials per IMDG Code and local regulations.']
  is_aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds AEO certification for trusted trader status and simplified customs procedures in the EU and partner countries.']
  is_ctpat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds C-TPAT certification for enhanced supply chain security and expedited customs processing.']
  is_iso28000_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 28000 certification for supply chain security management.']
  is_iso9001_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the facility holds ISO 9001 certification for quality management systems.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this facility record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current facility lease expires, if applicable. Used for real estate planning and contract renewal tracking.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the facility in decimal degrees for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  max_daily_inbound_volume_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units (pallets, cartons, or SKUs) the facility can receive and process in a single day. Used for capacity planning and load balancing.']
  max_daily_outbound_volume_units varchar [note: 'Maximum number of units (pallets, cartons, or SKUs) the facility can pick, pack, and ship in a single day. Used for capacity planning and order fulfillment forecasting.']
  opened_date date [note: 'Date when the facility first became operational. Used for lifecycle tracking and depreciation calculations.']
  operating_hours_weekday varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekdays in format HH:MM-HH:MM (e.g., 08:00-18:00). Used for scheduling inbound and outbound operations.']
  operating_hours_weekend varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for weekends in format HH:MM-HH:MM. May be different from weekday hours or closed.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the facility.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the facility address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  renewable_energy_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of facility energy consumption sourced from renewable energy (solar, wind, etc.). Used for sustainability KPIs and environmental reporting.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the facility is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  storage_capacity_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volumetric storage capacity of the facility in cubic meters. CBM is a standard logistics measurement for warehouse capacity.']
  temperature_zone_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct temperature-controlled zones within the facility (e.g., ambient, refrigerated, frozen) for specialized inventory storage.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the facility location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for scheduling and operational hours.']
  total_floor_area_sqm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total usable floor area of the facility measured in square meters, used for capacity planning and utilization analysis.']
  wms_instance_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Manhattan WMS instance managing this facility. Used for system integration and operational tracking.']

  Note: 'Master record for each warehouse and distribution center operated by Transport Shipping. Captures physical facility attributes including location, type (DC, CFS, ICD, FTZ, cross-dock), total floor area in CBM, dock door count, temperature zones, WMS instance identifier, operating hours, capacity thresholds, and certifications (C-TPAT, AEO, ISO 9001). SSOT for warehouse identity referenced across all warehouse domain products.'
}

Table warehouse.storage_location {
  storage_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the storage location within the warehouse management system. Primary key.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent warehouse or distribution center facility where this storage location resides.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logical zone within the warehouse to which this storage location is assigned.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date when this storage location became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when this storage location was deactivated or retired from warehouse operations. Null if currently active.']
  aisle varchar [note: 'Aisle designation within the warehouse where the storage location is physically situated. Used for route optimization and labor management.']
  automation_type varchar [note: 'Level and type of automation technology deployed at this storage location. Impacts labor planning and throughput capacity.']
  bin varchar [note: 'Specific bin or slot position within the level. Represents the most granular storage position.']
  bonded_warehouse_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is within a bonded warehouse zone where goods are stored without payment of customs duties until released for domestic consumption.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was first created in the Manhattan Warehouse Management System (WMS).']
  cross_dock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is used for cross-docking operations where inbound shipments are directly transferred to outbound without long-term storage.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for physical inventory cycle counting at this storage location. Supports inventory accuracy and compliance with ISO 9001 quality management.']
  fire_suppression_zone varchar [note: 'Fire suppression system zone identifier covering this storage location. Critical for safety compliance and insurance requirements.']
  free_trade_zone_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is within a Free Trade Zone (FTZ) where goods can be stored, processed, or re-exported without customs intervention.']
  hazmat_class_permitted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of International Maritime Organization (IMO) hazard classes permitted in this storage location (e.g., 1.1, 2.2, 3, 4.1, 5.1, 6.1, 8, 9). Empty if not hazmat suitable.']
  hazmat_suitable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the storage location is certified and equipped for storing hazardous materials per International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code and International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) Technical Instructions.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical height dimension of the storage location in centimeters.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent physical cycle count was performed at this storage location.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storage location record was last updated in the Manhattan Warehouse Management System (WMS).']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical length dimension of the storage location in centimeters.']
  location_barcode varchar [note: 'Scannable barcode identifier for the storage location used by handheld Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) devices and mobile scanning equipment during warehouse operations.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Human-readable unique code identifying the storage location within the warehouse. Used for directed putaway and picking operations in Manhattan Warehouse Management System (WMS).']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the storage location indicating availability for putaway and picking operations.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Physical storage infrastructure type defining the structural characteristics and handling requirements of the location. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pallet_rack|floor_stack|shelf|bin|drive_in|push_back|flow_rack|cantilever|mezzanine|automated_storage_retrieval — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  occupancy_status varchar [note: 'Current inventory occupancy state of the storage location based on capacity utilization.']
  pick_face_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as a pick-face position for order fulfillment operations. Pick-face locations are optimized for high-velocity Stock Keeping Units (SKUs).']
  quarantine_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is designated for quarantine inventory pending quality inspection, customs clearance, or Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) processing.']
  rack varchar [note: 'Rack or bay identifier within the aisle where the storage location is positioned.']
  replenishment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location requires periodic replenishment from bulk storage. Used in First In First Out (FIFO) and Last In First Out (LIFO) inventory management strategies.']
  returns_processing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is designated for processing customer returns and Return Merchandise Authorization (RMA) inventory.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the storage location. High-value and bonded locations require additional access controls per Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism (C-TPAT) and Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) programs.']
  storage_location_level varchar [note: 'Vertical level or shelf position within the rack structure. Used for slotting optimization based on Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) velocity and weight.']
  temperature_class varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification of the storage location. Critical for cold-chain logistics and pharmaceutical shipments.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature in Celsius for the storage location. Used for cold-chain compliance and quality assurance.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature in Celsius for the storage location. Used for cold-chain compliance and quality assurance.']
  value_added_services_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storage location is designated for value-added services such as kitting, labeling, repackaging, or customization operations.']
  volume_capacity_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the storage location in cubic meters. Used for dimensional weight (DIM Weight) calculations and space optimization.']
  weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location in kilograms. Used for slotting optimization and safety compliance.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Physical width dimension of the storage location in centimeters.']

  Note: 'Granular storage location and zone master within a facility representing every bin, slot, rack, bay, aisle, floor position, and logical zone managed by Manhattan WMS. Tracks location code, zone assignment, zone type (receiving, bulk, pick-face, staging, dispatch, cold-chain, hazmat, quarantine, returns, VAS), aisle, rack, level, bin, location type, weight/volume capacity, temperature class, hazmat suitability, automation type, and current occupancy status. After merge with zone, also carries zone-level attributes (floor area, height, temperature range). Enables slotting optimization, directed putaway, and compliance segregation.'
}

Table warehouse.sku {
  sku_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock keeping unit. Primary key for the SKU master data product.']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: In 3PL transport/shipping, SKUs are customer-specific (each customer has their own product catalog). Links SKU to owning customer account, enabling customer-specific SKU management, catalog isolation,']
  hs_classification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hs_classification. Business justification: SKUs require binding HS tariff classifications for customs compliance. Links product master to approved classification for duty calculation, trade agreement eligibility, import/export license determin']
  license_permit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.license_permit. Business justification: Export/import compliance: controlled goods (dual-use, ITAR, hazmat) SKUs require a valid license permit. WMS must verify license validity and track utilized quantities against authorized quantities du']
  merchant_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.merchant. Business justification: Merchant SKU catalog management: in 3PL fulfillment, each SKU belongs to a specific merchant for catalog management, returns policy application, and merchant-specific handling instructions. A logistic']
  origin_determination_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.origin_determination. Business justification: Preferential tariff programs: the formal origin determination record establishes whether a SKU qualifies for reduced duty rates under trade agreements. Linking SKU to origin_determination replaces the']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'The ABC inventory classification (A=high-value/high-velocity, B=medium, C=low) used for prioritization and slotting optimization.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this SKU became active and available for warehouse operations.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'The date on which this SKU was discontinued or became inactive. Null if currently active.']
  barcode varchar [note: 'The primary barcode identifier (UPC, EAN, or other format) used for scanning and automated identification in warehouse operations.']
  barcode_type varchar [note: 'The barcode symbology or format type used for this SKU (UPC, EAN, CODE128, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SKU record was first created in the warehouse management system.']
  dim_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The dimensional weight of the SKU in kilograms, calculated using industry-standard DIM weight factors for freight rating and space optimization.']
  expiry_date_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU requires expiry date tracking for inventory management and FEFO rotation.']
  fragile boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU is fragile and requires special handling to prevent damage.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'The hazardous materials classification code per IMDG or ICAO standards if the SKU contains dangerous goods. Null if non-hazardous.']
  height_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'The height dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for slotting optimization and space planning.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System tariff classification code used for customs declarations, duty calculation, and international trade compliance.']
  inventory_method varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation and rotation method applied to this SKU: FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), FEFO (First Expired First Out), or standard.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SKU record was last updated or modified in the warehouse management system.']
  length_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'The length dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for slotting optimization and space planning.']
  lot_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU requires lot or batch number tracking for traceability and quality control.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the manufacturer or producer of this SKU, used for quality tracking and compliance.']
  manufacturer_part_number varchar [note: 'The manufacturers own part number or SKU identifier for this item, used for cross-referencing and procurement.']
  max_stack_height varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units that can be safely stacked vertically. Null if not stackable or no limit.']
  pick_type varchar [note: 'The standard picking method or unit used for order fulfillment of this SKU (each-pick, case-pick, pallet-pick, or bulk).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification category for this SKU used for inventory segmentation, slotting optimization, and reporting.']
  product_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed subcategory classification within the product category for granular inventory management and analytics.']
  reorder_point varchar [note: 'The inventory quantity threshold that triggers replenishment or reorder action for this SKU.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU is tagged with RFID technology for automated tracking and inventory visibility.']
  rfid_tag_code varchar [note: 'The RFID tag identifier or Electronic Product Code (EPC) associated with this SKU for automated identification.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum buffer inventory quantity maintained to prevent stockouts and ensure service level for this SKU.']
  serial_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU requires individual serial number tracking at the unit level for high-value or regulated items.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'The number of days from receipt or manufacture date that the SKU remains usable or saleable before expiration. Null for non-perishable items.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying this SKU across all warehouse operations and systems. Used for scanning, picking, and inventory transactions.']
  sku_description varchar [note: 'Detailed human-readable description of the product or item represented by this SKU, including key characteristics and specifications.']
  sku_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SKU indicating whether it is available for warehouse operations, discontinued, or pending activation.']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SKU can be safely stacked vertically in storage and transport.']
  storage_class varchar [note: 'The warehouse storage class or zone assignment for this SKU based on handling requirements, velocity, and physical characteristics (e.g., fast-pick, bulk, secure, hazmat).']
  temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable storage temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled SKUs. Null for ambient items.']
  temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum allowable storage temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled SKUs. Null for ambient items.']
  temperature_requirement varchar [note: 'The temperature control requirement for storing and handling this SKU, determining warehouse zone assignment.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'The four-digit UN number identifying the hazardous substance if applicable, used for compliance with international dangerous goods regulations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The standard unit of measure used for inventory counting, picking, and tracking of this SKU.']
  velocity_code varchar [note: 'The movement velocity classification indicating how frequently this SKU is picked or shipped (fast, medium, slow movers).']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'The code or identifier of the primary vendor or supplier for this SKU, used for procurement and replenishment.']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,4) [note: 'The total volume of the SKU in cubic meters, calculated or measured for warehouse space utilization and freight planning.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The actual gross weight of the SKU in kilograms, used for handling, labor planning, and freight calculations.']
  width_cm decimal(10,2) [note: 'The width dimension of the SKU in centimeters, used for slotting optimization and space planning.']

  Note: 'Stock Keeping Unit master — the authoritative product catalog for all items stored and handled within warehouse operations. Captures SKU code, description, product category, unit of measure, dimensions (length, width, height), weight, DIM weight factor, hazmat class (IMDG/ICAO), temperature requirement, country of origin, HS Code reference, FIFO/LIFO indicator, shelf life days, barcode/RFID identifiers, and storage class. SSOT for item identity within the warehouse domain.'
}

Table warehouse.inventory_position {
  inventory_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory position record. Primary key for this entity.']
  asn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.warehouse_asn. Business justification: inventory_position currently stores asn_number as a STRING denormalized reference to the originating ASN. Replacing this with a proper FK warehouse_asn_id → warehouse_asn.warehouse_asn_id normalizes t']
  customer_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.customer_account. Business justification: Inventory positions in 3PL warehouses are owned by customer accounts (shippers/sellers). Replaces denormalized owner_code string with proper FK. Essential for customer-specific inventory reporting, bi']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center where the inventory is held.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.hold. Business justification: Bonded warehouse management: customs holds placed on specific inventory positions (CBP exam, FDA hold) must be tracked with hold reason, issuing authority, and expected release date. WMS uses this to ']
  inbound_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.inbound_receipt. Business justification: An inventory position record is created or updated when goods are physically received via an inbound receipt event. Linking inventory_position to inbound_receipt establishes the receiving event that c']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Inventory positions must be traceable to originating POs for inventory valuation, landed cost allocation, and financial audit. purchase_order_number is a denormalized text field; a typed FK enables PO']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.sku. Business justification: Inventory positions MUST reference the authoritative SKU master. Currently inventory_position has sku STRING field (denormalized sku_code) but lacks proper FK to sku.sku_id. Adding sku_id enables JOIN']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to warehouse.storage_location. Business justification: Inventory positions MUST reference the authoritative storage location master. Currently inventory_position has storage_location_code STRING field (denormalized) but lacks proper FK to storage_location']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Supplier-level inventory reporting, returns management, and quality hold tracking by supplier are standard WMS operations. supplier_code is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to supplier enables s']
  available_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity available for allocation to new orders. Calculated as on-hand minus reserved, quarantine, hold, damaged, and expired quantities.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'Standard or actual cost per unit of the inventory item. Used for inventory valuation, cost of goods sold calculations, and financial reporting.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs declarations and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory position record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost per unit value. Standard codes include USD, EUR, GBP, CNY, JPY.']
  damaged_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity identified as physically damaged or defective. Typically segregated for disposal, return to vendor, or salvage processing.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory item expires or becomes unusable. Critical for perishable goods, pharmaceuticals, and time-sensitive products. Drives FEFO (First Expired First Out) allocation logic.']
  fifo_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned based on receipt date to enforce FIFO picking logic. Lower numbers are picked first to ensure oldest inventory is consumed before newer stock.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the inventory item is classified as hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and transportation compliance.']
  hold_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity placed on administrative hold due to customer disputes, regulatory issues, or business decisions. Not available for allocation.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International tariff classification code used for customs clearance and duty calculation. Six to ten digit code standardized by the World Customs Organization.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity currently being moved within the facility between storage locations. Temporarily unavailable during putaway or replenishment operations.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the inventory position indicating availability for order fulfillment. Available = ready for allocation; Reserved = committed to orders; Quarantine = pending quality inspection; Hold = administrative block; Damaged = physically impaired; Expired = past expiration date.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory position was last physically counted during cycle count operations. Used to schedule next count and track inventory accuracy.']
  last_movement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory transaction affecting this position (receipt, pick, adjustment, transfer). Used for slow-moving inventory analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this inventory position record. Used for real-time inventory synchronization and change tracking.']
  lifo_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned based on receipt date to enforce LIFO picking logic. Higher numbers are picked first to ensure newest inventory is consumed before older stock.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for traceability and quality control. Used for FIFO/LIFO inventory management and product recalls.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date when the product was manufactured or produced. Used for traceability, quality control, and shelf-life calculations.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total physical quantity of the SKU present at the storage location, regardless of availability status. Includes available, reserved, quarantine, hold, and damaged quantities.']
  pick_face_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this inventory position is in an active pick face location (forward pick area) versus reserve storage. Pick face locations are optimized for high-velocity picking.']
  putaway_zone varchar [note: 'Warehouse zone designation where the inventory was placed during putaway operations. Used for slotting optimization and pick path planning.']
  quarantine_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity held for quality inspection or pending release approval. Not available for order fulfillment until cleared.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the inventory was received into the facility. Used for FIFO/LIFO sequencing and aging analysis.']
  replenishment_trigger_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold that triggers automatic replenishment from reserve storage to pick face. Used to maintain optimal pick face inventory levels.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantity committed to existing orders or work orders but not yet picked. Reduces available quantity for new allocations.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for individual item-level tracking. Used for high-value or serialized inventory items.']
  temperature_controlled_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the inventory requires temperature-controlled storage (refrigerated or frozen). Drives storage zone assignment and handling requirements.']
  temperature_range_max decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable storage temperature for temperature-controlled inventory. Used to monitor cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_range_min decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable storage temperature for temperature-controlled inventory. Used to monitor cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for temperature range values. Standard units are celsius or fahrenheit.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN identification number for hazardous materials. Required for dangerous goods shipping compliance under IMDG, IATA, and DOT regulations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which inventory quantities are tracked and reported. Standard units include each (individual items), case (inner pack), pallet (shipping unit), carton, box, or generic unit.']
  volume_per_unit decimal(18,3) [note: 'Physical volume or cubic measurement of a single unit of the SKU. Used for warehouse space planning, slotting optimization, and freight rating.']
  volume_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the volume per unit value. Standard units include CBM (cubic meter), CFT (cubic foot), liter, gallon.']
  weight_per_unit decimal(18,3) [note: 'Physical weight of a single unit of the SKU. Used for dimensional weight calculations, freight rating, and capacity planning.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the weight per unit value. Standard units include kg (kilogram), lb (pound), g (gram), oz (ounce), ton (short ton), mt (metric ton).']

  Note: 'Real-time and point-in-time inventory position record for each SKU at each storage location within a facility. Tracks on-hand quantity, available quantity, reserved quantity, quarantine quantity, damaged quantity, lot number, serial number, expiry date, receipt date, FIFO/LIFO sequence number, inventory status (available, hold, damaged, expired), and last cycle count date. Core operational record powering WMS inventory control and order fulfillment allocation.'
}

  expected_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the shipment is expected to arrive at the destination facility. Enables detailed dock appointment scheduling.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Impacts customs and duty responsibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled handling (refrigerated or frozen).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ASN record was last updated. Tracks changes to status, quantities, or other attributes throughout the receiving lifecycle.']
  pro_number varchar [note: 'Progressive number assigned by LTL carriers for tracking and freight billing. Common in less-than-truckload shipments.']
  received_carton_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of cartons received and verified at the destination facility. Used for variance analysis against expected count.']
  received_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of pallets received and verified at the destination facility. Used for variance analysis against expected count.']
  receiving_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes captured by receiving personnel during the unloading and inspection process. Documents exceptions or observations.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or trailer. Used for tamper detection and customs compliance.']
  ship_date date [note: 'Date when the shipment departed from the origin facility or supplier location.']
  shipment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this inbound shipment. Determines receiving dock scheduling sequence and resource allocation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the originating system that generated the ASN (e.g., supplier ERP, TMS, 3PL system). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as fragile items, top-load only, or security protocols.']
  temperature_range_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used for cold chain compliance.']
  temperature_range_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum acceptable temperature in Celsius for temperature-controlled shipments. Used for cold chain compliance.']
  total_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or boxes included in this ASN shipment. Used for receiving verification and labor planning.']
  total_line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKU line items included in this ASN. Used for receiving complexity assessment.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this ASN shipment. Used for dock space allocation and material handling equipment planning.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning and dimensional weight calculations.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms. Used for capacity planning and freight cost validation.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for this inbound shipment.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any discrepancy between expected and received quantities or condition.']

  Note: 'Advanced Shipping Notice — inbound shipment notification received from suppliers, carriers, or origin facilities prior to physical arrival at the warehouse. Captures ASN number, origin party, expected arrival date/time, carrier reference, transport mode, total carton count, total pallet count, total weight, total CBM, associated purchase order references, and ASN status (pending, partially received, fully received, cancelled). Drives receiving dock scheduling and labor planning in Manhattan WMS.'
}

Table warehouse.asn_line {
  asn_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for asn_line']
  asn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent ASN header document. Links this line to the overall inbound shipment notification.']
  declaration_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration_line. Business justification: Import duty reconciliation: each ASN line represents a specific commodity line on the inbound shipment, corresponding directly to a customs declaration line. Linking them enables duty assessment accur']
  po_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.po_line. Business justification: ASN lines map to specific PO lines for line-level quantity reconciliation and 3-way match. purchase_order_line_number is a denormalized text field; a proper FK to po_line enables line-level PO-to-ASN ']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: ASN lines reference PO shipments — PO-to-ASN reconciliation, receiving accuracy reporting, and 3-way match are core logistics receiving processes requiring this link. purchase_order_number is a denorm']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the SKU master. Identifies the specific product item expected on this ASN line.']
  storage_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the destination warehouse or distribution center. Identifies where this ASN line will be received and stored.']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier or vendor providing this shipment. Used for supplier performance tracking and quality management.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured or produced. Required for customs compliance and trade documentation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN line record was first created in the warehouse management system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. Enables multi-currency inventory valuation and financial consolidation.']
  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the nature of any receiving discrepancy. Provides additional context for exception resolution and claims processing.']
  discrepancy_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the type of discrepancy between expected and received quantities. Used for root cause analysis and supplier performance tracking.']
  expected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of the SKU expected to be received as declared in the ASN. Used for receiving reconciliation against actual received quantity.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for perishable or time-sensitive inventory. Used for FIFO/FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory rotation and shelf-life management.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this line item contains hazardous materials requiring special handling. True if hazardous, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN line record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking and data governance.']

  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the ASN line. Tracks the lifecycle from pending receipt through completion or exception handling.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or supplier lot number for traceability and quality control. Critical for First In First Out (FIFO) inventory management and product recalls.']
  manufacture_date date [note: 'Date the product was manufactured. Used for quality control, warranty tracking, and regulatory compliance.']
  putaway_location varchar [note: 'Designated storage location (bin, slot, or zone) where the received inventory will be placed. Assigned during receiving or putaway process.']
  putaway_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the received inventory was placed into its designated storage location. Marks completion of the inbound process.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this line item requires quality inspection before putaway. True if inspection is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of quality inspection for this line item. Tracks the inspection lifecycle from pending through final disposition.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of the SKU physically received at the warehouse. Used to identify discrepancies against expected quantity.']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was physically received at the warehouse. Represents the actual business event time for receiving completion.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized inventory items. Enables unit-level tracking for high-value or regulated products.']
  storage_temperature_max_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-sensitive products. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  storage_temperature_min_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for temperature-sensitive products. Used for cold chain compliance monitoring.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this line item requires temperature-controlled storage. True if cold chain management is required, False otherwise.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying hazardous materials for transportation and storage compliance. Required for dangerous goods handling.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost per unit for the SKU on this ASN line. Used for inventory valuation, cost of goods sold (COGS) calculation, and financial reporting.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the expected and received quantities. Standard warehouse UOM codes such as Each (EA), Case (CS), Pallet (PL), Carton (CTN), Box (BOX), or Package (PKG).']
  volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the line item in cubic meters. Used for warehouse space planning, slotting optimization, and freight cost calculations.']
  weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total weight of the line item in kilograms. Used for capacity planning, freight cost allocation, and dimensional weight calculations.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail of an Advanced Shipping Notice representing each SKU expected on an inbound delivery. Captures ASN line number, SKU reference, expected quantity, unit of measure, lot number, expiry date, country of origin, HS Code, and line status. Enables granular receiving reconciliation and discrepancy tracking against actual received quantities.'
}
