  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge code was approved for production use. Used for audit trail and SOX compliance.']
  auto_apply_conditions varchar [note: 'Business rules or conditions that trigger automatic application of this charge (e.g., shipment weight exceeds 500kg, destination is residential, delivery requires liftgate).']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is automatically applied by the rating engine when applicable conditions are met, or requires manual addition by operations staff.']
  calculation_basis varchar [note: 'Unit or method by which the charge is calculated (e.g., flat fee, per kilogram, per cubic meter, per TEU, percentage of freight, per shipment, per mile, per hour). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flat|per_kg|per_cbm|per_teu|per_shipment|per_package|percentage|per_mile|per_hour — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_bearer varchar [note: 'Party responsible for paying this charge. Prepaid indicates shipper pays; collect indicates consignee pays; third_party indicates another party pays per agreement.']
  charge_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the charge type. Freight covers base transportation; surcharge includes fuel, GRI, BAF; accessorial covers value-added services; customs includes duties and brokerage; handling includes THC, CFS, ICD fees. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: freight|surcharge|accessorial|customs|insurance|handling|storage|documentation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  charge_code varchar [note: 'Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the charge type (e.g., FRT for freight, FSC for fuel surcharge, THC for terminal handling charge). Used across billing, rating, and EDI transactions.']
  charge_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the charge purpose, applicability conditions, and calculation basis. Used for customer communication and internal training.']
  charge_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the charge type (e.g., Freight Charge, Fuel Surcharge, Terminal Handling Charge, Customs Clearance Fee).']
  charge_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the charge code. Active charges are available for rating and billing; inactive charges are retired; pending charges await approval; deprecated charges are phased out; superseded charges are replaced by newer versions.']
  charge_subcategory varchar [note: 'Granular classification within the charge category (e.g., under surcharge: fuel, currency, security, peak season; under accessorial: liftgate, inside delivery, residential).']
  cost_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) cost account code for expense allocation when this charge represents a pass-through or cost recovery item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  customer_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is displayed to customers on invoices, quotes, and online portals, or is an internal cost allocation not shown externally.']
  customs_hs_code_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge requires Harmonized System (HS) code classification for customs declaration and duty calculation purposes.']
  default_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which this charge is typically denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). May be overridden at tariff or contract level.']
  dim_weight_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge calculation considers dimensional weight (volumetric weight) in addition to actual weight, common in air and express freight.']
  dispute_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge type is eligible for customer dispute and freight audit review processes.']
  edifact_charge_code varchar [note: 'UN/EDIFACT standard charge code used in electronic data interchange (EDI) messages for freight and logistics (e.g., IFTMIN, IFTMBC, INVOIC).']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this charge code becomes valid and available for use in rating, quoting, and billing operations.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which this charge code is no longer valid for new transactions. Nullable for charges with indefinite validity.']
  hazmat_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge applies specifically to hazardous materials (dangerous goods) shipments requiring special handling per IMDG, IATA DGR, or ADR regulations.']
  iata_charge_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter IATA standard charge code used in air waybill (AWB) and cargo-IMP messaging (e.g., MY for miscellaneous charge due carrier, PP for prepaid, CC for collect).']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum monetary cap for this charge. If calculated charge exceeds this threshold, the maximum is applied.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum monetary value for this charge regardless of calculation basis. If calculated charge is below this threshold, the minimum is applied.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the pricing analyst or system administrator who last modified this charge code record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this charge code record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or business context regarding this charge code (e.g., seasonal applicability, customer-specific usage, regulatory considerations).']
  published_tariff_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is part of a publicly filed tariff with regulatory authorities (e.g., Federal Maritime Commission for ocean freight, DOT for domestic trucking).']
  regulatory_filing_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the regulatory tariff filing or rate publication with governing bodies (e.g., FMC tariff number, DOT docket number).']
  revenue_account_code varchar [note: 'General Ledger (GL) revenue account code to which this charge type posts. Used for financial reporting and revenue recognition per IFRS 15.']
  revenue_recognition_category varchar [note: 'Classification determining how and when revenue from this charge is recognized per IFRS 15 and ASC 606 (e.g., service revenue recognized over time, pass-through recognized net, reimbursable recognized gross).']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code for this charge type (e.g., standard rate, reduced rate, exempt, zero-rated). Used for automated tax calculation.']
  taxable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge is subject to value-added tax (VAT), goods and services tax (GST), or sales tax per jurisdiction tax regulations.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number for this charge code. Incremented each time the charge definition is modified and re-approved. Supports change tracking and audit history.']
  waivable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this charge can be waived or discounted under certain circumstances (e.g., customer contract terms, service recovery, promotional campaigns).']
  waiver_approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether waiving this charge requires managerial or finance approval per internal controls and SOX compliance.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or name of the pricing analyst or system administrator who created this charge code record.']

  Note: 'Standardized charge code reference catalog defining all billable charge types used across freight, express, and logistics services. Captures charge code, charge name, charge category (freight/surcharge/accessorial/customs/insurance/handling), revenue account mapping, applicable service lines, taxable flag, EDI code mapping (IATA charge codes, UN/EDIFACT charge codes), and active status. SSOT for charge type standardization across billing, rate cards, and spot quotes.'
}

Table pricing.carrier_buy_rate {
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier buy rate record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the procurement contract or capacity agreement under which this buy rate is negotiated. Links to contract master.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Procurement teams managing carrier contracts link buy rates specifically to carrier agreements (vs generic agreements) for carrier performance analysis, rate benchmarking, and margin calculation on ca']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier, airline, shipping line, or subcontractor providing capacity. Links to the carrier master entity.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Buy rates are negotiated for specific carrier service products (not just carrier-level). Carrier_service_id enables precise cost matching when rating shipments, supporting accurate margin calculation ']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Carrier buy rates are negotiated per lane. Lane_id links carrier cost to specific routes, enabling buy-sell margin analysis, carrier selection optimization, and cost-to-serve reporting by lane.']
  purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Buy rates formalized through capacity purchase orders with carrier-suppliers. Business need: financial reconciliation matches buy rate commitments to PO obligations; accrual management for contracted ']
  service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: Carrier buy rates negotiated for corridors (multi-leg carrier services). Service_corridor_id links carrier cost to multi-leg routes, enabling corridor-level margin analysis and carrier performance eva']
  superseded_by_rate_carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the newer buy rate that replaces this one. Used to maintain rate lineage and history.']
  supplier_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier_quote. Business justification: Buy rates sourced from supplier quotes during carrier procurement. Business need: cost basis tracking links negotiated buy rates to original supplier quotes for margin calculation and procurement audi']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Buy-side rates paid to carriers are governed by carrier tariffs in freight forwarding. carrier_buy_rate already has carrier_id and carrier_agreement_id, but lacks a direct reference to the tariff sche']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this buy rate. Rates must be approved before use in cost calculation and freight procurement.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this buy rate was approved for operational use.']
  buy_rate_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The cost amount Transport Shipping pays to the carrier per unit. This is the base buy-side rate before surcharges.']
  commodity_group_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the commodity or product group this buy rate applies to (e.g., general cargo, hazmat, perishables, high-value).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this buy rate record was first created in the system.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this buy rate was loaded (e.g., SAP TM, Oracle TMS, Coupa). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this buy rate becomes valid and can be used for cost calculation and freight procurement.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this buy rate remains valid. Null indicates open-ended validity.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or container required for this buy rate (e.g., 20ft dry, 40ft reefer, flatbed truck, aircraft type). Relevant for ocean and road freight.']
  fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fuel surcharge (BAF for ocean, fuel surcharge for air/road) is applicable on top of this buy rate.']
  gri_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether General Rate Increase (GRI) adjustments apply to this buy rate. Common in ocean freight contracts.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the allocation of costs and risks between Transport Shipping and the carrier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  margin_floor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum margin percentage that must be maintained when pricing sell-side rates based on this buy rate. Used for margin management and pricing guardrails.']
  maximum_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Maximum charge cap for this buy rate, if applicable. Used for volume-based rate capping agreements.']
  minimum_charge_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum charge the carrier will invoice regardless of shipment size or weight. Floor for cost calculation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special conditions, restrictions, or business context for this buy rate (e.g., volume commitments, seasonal restrictions, carrier requirements).']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the buy rate (e.g., per kg, per shipment, per container, per TEU, per CBM, per mile). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_kg|per_shipment|per_container|per_teu|per_cbm|per_mile|per_km — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rate_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for this buy rate, used for operational reference and carrier invoice reconciliation.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the buy rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  rate_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the buy rate, typically including lane, mode, and service type for easy identification.']
  rate_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the buy rate (active, inactive, pending approval, expired, suspended, superseded).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of service provided by the carrier (e.g., express, standard, economy). Determines transit time and service level.']
  thc_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Terminal Handling Charges (THC) are included in the buy rate or billed separately by the carrier.']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard transit time in days committed by the carrier for this lane and service type. Used for service level planning.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this buy rate (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this buy rate record was last modified. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this buy rate record. Incremented when rate is amended or renegotiated. Supports rate history tracking.']
  volume_break_max_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volume threshold in cubic meters (CBM) for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  volume_break_min_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum volume threshold in cubic meters (CBM) for this rate tier. Used for volumetric pricing.']
  volume_commitment_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volume commitment (in rate basis units) covered by this buy rate. Exceeding this may trigger renegotiation.']
  volume_commitment_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum volume commitment (in rate basis units) required to maintain this buy rate. Part of carrier capacity agreements.']
  weight_break_max_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum weight threshold in kilograms for this rate tier. Null indicates no upper limit.']
  weight_break_min_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum weight threshold in kilograms for this rate tier. Used for tiered pricing structures.']

  Note: 'Buy-side rate representing the cost Transport Shipping pays to carriers, airlines, shipping lines, and subcontractors for capacity procurement. Captures carrier reference, service type, transport mode, origin-destination lane, buy rate amount, currency, rate basis, contract reference, validity period, margin floor, and volume tier pricing. Enables sell/buy rate spread analysis, margin management, carrier cost benchmarking, and freight audit against carrier invoices. Distinct from sell-side rate_card — this is the cost basis for all pricing decisions and margin calculations.'
}

Table route.lane {
  lane_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the origin-destination lane record. Primary key used across SAP TM, Oracle TMS, and Blue Yonder for rate lookup, carrier selection, and network optimization.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or customer contract governing rates, SLAs, and operational terms for this lane. Links to the Contract and Agreement domain for rate lookup and charge calculation in SAP TM.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary contracted carrier allocated to this lane for standard shipment execution. Used by SAP TM carrier selection engine and Oracle TMS for freight audit and load optimization.']
  contracted_carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Lanes are often contracted under specific carrier service products with defined service levels. Required for rate application during quoting, service-level routing decisions, and contracted service va']
  destination_location_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination network node (city, port, airport, ICD, CFS, or postal zone) at which this lane terminates. Paired with origin_location_id to define the directional transport corridor.']
  lane_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin network node (city, port, airport, Inland Container Depot (ICD), Container Freight Station (CFS), or postal zone) from which this lane departs. Drives carrier selection and rate lookup in SAP TM.']
  aeo_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification is required for carriers or shippers operating on this lane. Enforced during carrier selection in SAP TM and compliance screening in Descartes Customs.']
  blue_yonder_lane_code varchar [note: 'External lane identifier assigned by Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning system for network optimization, demand forecasting, and inventory optimization workflows. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and Blue Yonder.']
  co2e_emission_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Greenhouse Gas (GHG) emission factor in kilograms of Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) per tonne-kilometre for this lane and transport mode. Used for sustainability reporting under EU ETS, CORSIA, and GHG Protocol Scope 3 calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lane record was first created in the system of record and ingested into the Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and SOX compliance for master data change management.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the lane destination. Used for cross-border compliance, Importer Security Filing (ISF), and Automated Manifest System (AMS) reporting.']
  destination_unlocode varchar [note: 'United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) for the lane destination node. Used in EDI messages, Air Waybill (AWB), Master Bill of Lading (MBL), and Automated Manifest System (AMS) filings.']
  distance_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical distance of the lane corridor in kilometres. Used for freight rate calculation, Dimensional Weight (DIM Weight) pricing, Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emissions estimation, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  distance_miles decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical distance of the lane corridor in miles. Maintained alongside distance_km to support US DOT regulatory reporting and North American carrier rate negotiations where miles are the contractual unit.']
  edi_lane_code varchar [note: 'Lane identifier used in Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages exchanged with carriers, customs authorities, and trading partners. May differ from internal lane_code due to carrier-specific EDI mapping requirements.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this lane definition becomes operationally active and available for shipment booking, carrier selection, and rate lookup. Supports temporal lane versioning in SAP TM and Blue Yonder.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date on which this lane definition expires and is no longer available for new bookings. Null indicates an open-ended lane with no planned expiry. Used for lane lifecycle management and network re-optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  frequency_per_week varchar [note: 'Number of scheduled departures per week on this lane. Used for capacity planning, shipment booking availability, and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance management. Sourced from carrier schedules in SAP TM.']
  ftz_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane passes through or terminates in a Free Trade Zone (FTZ), enabling duty deferral, bonded warehouse processing, or re-export without full customs clearance. Managed in Descartes Customs.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'Applicable International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) governing risk and cost transfer on this lane. Determines customs brokerage responsibilities, insurance requirements, and freight charge allocation. Aligned with ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_capacity_freeze boolean [note: 'Indicates whether capacity on this lane is frozen, preventing new load optimization allocations. Set by Blue Yonder during peak season planning or carrier capacity constraint events. Synchronized to Oracle TMS for load optimization.']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane crosses an international border, triggering customs brokerage, Importer Security Filing (ISF), and trade compliance workflows in Descartes Customs. True for international lanes; False for domestic.']
  is_dg_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Dangerous Goods (DG) shipments are permitted on this lane. False triggers a DG prohibition restriction. Governed by IMDG Code for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR for road transport.']
  is_embargo_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a trade or operational embargo is currently active on this lane, blocking new shipment bookings. Embargo status is managed in Descartes Customs and synchronized to SAP TM for carrier selection exclusion.']
  is_seasonal_closure boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane is subject to a seasonal closure (e.g., weather-related road closures, monsoon port restrictions). When True, the lane is excluded from carrier selection and route planning during the closure window.']
  lane_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the lane, used in SAP TM and Oracle TMS for rate lookup, freight order management, and carrier selection. Serves as the canonical cross-system reference key.']
  lane_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the lane, typically formatted as origin-destination pair (e.g., \'Shanghai Port – Los Angeles Port Ocean FCL\'). Used in reporting, carrier communications, and network planning dashboards.']
  lane_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the lane. Active lanes are available for shipment booking and carrier allocation. Suspended lanes are temporarily unavailable. Under_review lanes are pending network optimization assessment in Blue Yonder.']
  lane_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification of the lane based on volume, revenue contribution, and network criticality. Tier 1 lanes are high-volume strategic corridors; Tier 3 are low-frequency secondary lanes. Used by Blue Yonder for network optimization prioritization.']
  lane_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lane by service category: express (time-definite premium), freight (standard bulk), last_mile (final delivery segment), cross_border (international), domestic (within-country), or multimodal (multiple transport modes). Drives SLA assignment and pricing tier in SAP TM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: express|freight|last_mile|cross_border|domestic|multimodal — promote to reference product]']
  last_mile_zone_code varchar [note: 'Delivery zone code for last-mile operations at the destination, used for postal zone routing, delivery slot assignment, and Door-to-Door (D2D) service configuration. Applicable when lane_type is \'last_mile\'.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable shipment volume in Cubic Metres (CBM) for a single consignment on this lane. Used alongside Dimensional Weight (DIM Weight) calculations for air freight rating and ocean FCL/LCL capacity planning.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum allowable gross shipment weight in kilograms for a single consignment on this lane. Enforced during load optimization in Oracle TMS and freight order management in SAP TM. Derived from carrier and infrastructure constraints.']
  network_region varchar [note: 'Geographic network region grouping for this lane (e.g., Asia-Pacific, Europe, Americas, Middle East Africa). Used for network planning, capacity management, and regional P&L reporting in SAP S/4HANA Finance.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the lane origin. Used for customs compliance, trade restriction screening, and regulatory reporting to WCO and Descartes Customs.']
  origin_unlocode varchar [note: 'United Nations Code for Trade and Transport Locations (UN/LOCODE) for the lane origin node. Five-character code used in EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) messages, customs declarations, and Bill of Lading (BOL) documentation.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this lane, capturing special handling instructions, carrier-specific requirements, or network planning context not captured in structured fields. Used by operations and network planning teams.']
  restriction_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body or internal authority that imposed the current lane restriction (e.g., \'IMO\', \'IATA\', \'US DOT\', \'WCO\', \'Internal Network Operations\'). Required for compliance audit and regulatory reporting.']
  restriction_effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the current operational restriction on this lane becomes effective. Used by SAP TM to exclude the lane from carrier selection and by Descartes Customs for trade compliance enforcement.']
  restriction_effective_until date [note: 'Date on which the current operational restriction on this lane expires and normal operations resume. Null indicates an indefinite restriction. Monitored by Blue Yonder for network re-optimization upon restriction lift.']
  restriction_type varchar [note: 'Categorical type of the most recently applied operational restriction on this lane. Used for exception reporting and compliance audit trails. Detailed restriction history is maintained in the lane_restriction event log.']
  sap_tm_lane_code varchar [note: 'External lane identifier from SAP TM (Transportation Management) used for route planning, freight order management, charge calculation, and carrier selection. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the Silver Layer and SAP TM.']
  standard_transit_days varchar [note: 'Contractual standard transit time in calendar days from origin pickup to destination delivery for this lane. Used as the baseline for SLA compliance measurement, On-Time Delivery (OTD) KPI calculation, and Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) computation in FourKites.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this lane: air (IATA-governed), ocean (IMO-governed), road (DOT-governed), rail, or multimodal (combination). Determines applicable regulatory framework, carrier pool, and rate structure in Oracle TMS.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this lane record. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  visibility_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FourKites real-time GPS/IoT shipment tracking and predictive ETA calculation is enabled for shipments on this lane. Drives exception management and proactive customer notification workflows.']

  Note: 'Master origin-destination lane definition representing a directional transport corridor between two network nodes (city, port, airport, ICD, CFS, or postal zone pair). Owns the canonical lane identifier used across SAP TM, Oracle TMS, and Blue Yonder for rate lookup, carrier selection, and network optimization. Captures lane type (express, freight, last-mile), transport mode (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal), lane status, standard transit time, distance (km/miles), lane tier classification, applicable Incoterms, operational restrictions (embargo, DG prohibition, seasonal closure, capacity freeze), restriction effective dates, and restriction authority. SSOT for all O-D lane master data referenced by pricing, freight, and shipment domains.'
}

Table route.network_node {
  network_node_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical or logical node in the transport network. Primary key for the network_node master record.']
  operator_network_partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.network_partner. Business justification: Many terminals, warehouses, and cross-docks are operated by third-party partners. Required for partner performance tracking, operational coordination, cost allocation, and partner SLA compliance monit']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the network node facility. Used for carrier pickup/delivery instructions, customs documentation in Descartes, and last-mile routing in Oracle TMS.']
  aeo_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node or its operating entity holds Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) certification, enabling expedited customs clearance and reduced inspections. True = AEO certified. Used in Descartes Customs for trade lane compliance scoring.']
  blue_yonder_node_code varchar [note: 'Node identifier as defined in Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning for network design, demand forecasting, and inventory optimization models. Used to synchronize network topology between the lakehouse silver layer and Blue Yonder planning outputs.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the city or municipality where the network node is located. Used for geographic display in shipment tracking, carrier selection, and last-mile delivery zone mapping.']
  co2e_emission_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Node-level Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg CO2e per CBM processed, used for Greenhouse Gas (GHG) footprint calculation in sustainability reporting. Supports EU Emissions Trading System (EU ETS) and CORSIA compliance reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the country where the node is physically located (e.g., \'HKG\', \'SGP\', \'USA\'). Used for customs compliance routing in Descartes, trade lane classification, and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network node record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  ctpat_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is C-TPAT certified, confirming compliance with US Customs and Border Protection supply chain security standards. True = C-TPAT certified. Required for US-bound shipment routing and ISF filing in Descartes.']
  customs_bonded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node operates as a customs bonded warehouse or zone, allowing storage of goods under customs control without duty payment until release. True = bonded facility. Used in Descartes Customs for trade compliance routing and ISF/AMS filing.']
  dg_handling_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is certified and equipped to handle Dangerous Goods (DG) shipments. True = DG-certified facility. Used in SAP TM carrier and route selection to ensure DG shipments are only routed through compliant nodes per IMDG Code and ICAO Technical Instructions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this network node record is valid and the node is available for route planning and shipment assignment. Used for temporal validity management in SAP TM and Blue Yonder network versioning.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this network node record is no longer valid. Null indicates the node is open-ended with no planned end date. Used for network topology versioning, decommissioning planning, and historical route analysis.']
  folio_visibility_code varchar [note: 'Location identifier assigned to this node in the FourKites Real-Time Visibility Platform. Used to correlate network node records with live shipment tracking events, predictive ETA calculations, and exception alerts from FourKites.']
  ftz_designation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node is located within or operates as a Free Trade Zone (FTZ), enabling duty-free handling and value-added services. True = FTZ-designated node. Used for trade compliance routing in Descartes and DDP/DAP Incoterms processing.']
  handling_capacity_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric throughput capacity of the node measured in Cubic Meters (CBM) per day. Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and network bottleneck analysis.']
  hub_tier varchar [note: 'Hierarchical tier of the node within the hub-and-spoke network topology. \'primary\' = global mega-hub (e.g., main international gateway); \'secondary\' = regional hub; \'tertiary\' = country-level distribution center; \'spoke\' = local delivery depot. Drives routing hierarchy in SAP TM and Blue Yonder network design.']
  iata_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter IATA airport or city code assigned to the node where applicable (e.g., \'HKG\', \'SIN\', \'LHR\'). Used for air freight routing, House Air Waybill (HAWB) and Master Air Waybill (MAWB) origin/destination coding, and IATA-compliant carrier communications. Null for non-air nodes.']
  icao_airport_code varchar [note: 'Four-letter ICAO airport designator for air cargo terminal nodes (e.g., \'VHHH\' for Hong Kong International). Used for air freight routing, MAWB/HAWB coding, and ICAO regulatory compliance. Null for non-airport nodes.']
  imo_port_code varchar [note: 'Seven-digit IMO port facility code assigned to sea port nodes. Used for ocean freight Bill of Lading (BOL) routing, vessel manifest filing, and IMO regulatory compliance reporting. Null for non-port nodes.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal latitude coordinate of the network node (e.g., 22.308901 for Hong Kong International Airport). Used for GPS-based distance calculations, FourKites real-time shipment proximity tracking, and Blue Yonder network design optimization.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'WGS-84 decimal longitude coordinate of the network node (e.g., 113.914603 for Hong Kong International Airport). Used in conjunction with latitude for geospatial route planning, ETA calculation, and IoT-based asset tracking integration.']
  max_weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum gross weight in kilograms that the node can handle per shipment or per day (context defined by operational policy). Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS and freight order feasibility checks in SAP TM.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable unique business code assigned to the node (e.g., \'HKG-HUB-01\', \'SIN-CFS-02\'). Used as the operational reference key in SAP TM route planning, Blue Yonder network design, and FourKites visibility integration.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the network node (e.g., \'Hong Kong International Gateway Hub\', \'Singapore Container Freight Station\'). Used in operational reports, carrier communications, and customer-facing shipment tracking.']
  node_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the network node. Controls whether the node is available for route planning and shipment assignment in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. \'active\' = fully operational; \'inactive\' = temporarily not in use; \'suspended\' = operations halted pending review; \'under_construction\' = not yet operational; \'decommissioned\' = permanently retired from the network.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node by its operational role in the transport network. Drives hub-and-spoke topology logic in SAP TM and Blue Yonder. Values: hub (major consolidation/distribution center), gateway (international entry/exit point), sorting_center (parcel sorting facility), warehouse (storage and fulfillment), port (sea port terminal), airport (air cargo terminal), icd (Inland Container Depot), cfs (Container Freight Station), ftz (Free Trade Zone), last_mile_depot (final delivery depot). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hub|gateway|sorting_center|warehouse|port|airport|icd|cfs|ftz|last_mile_depot — promote to reference product]']
  operating_days varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of operating days using ISO weekday abbreviations (e.g., \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI\' or \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT\'). Used for freight scheduling, carrier dispatch planning, and SLA transit time calculation in SAP TM.']
  operating_hours_end varchar [note: 'Local time at which the node ceases operations each day in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'22:00\'). Used by SAP TM for cut-off time enforcement, freight order scheduling, and SLA breach risk assessment.']
  operating_hours_start varchar [note: 'Local time at which the node begins operations each day in HH:MM 24-hour format (e.g., \'06:00\'). Used by SAP TM for freight order scheduling, carrier appointment booking, and last-mile delivery window planning.']
  oversized_cargo_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node has the equipment and infrastructure to handle oversized or out-of-gauge cargo (e.g., heavy lift cranes, wide-load bays). True = oversized cargo capable. Used in SAP TM special cargo routing.']
  parent_node_code varchar [note: 'Node code of the parent node in the hub-and-spoke hierarchy (e.g., a spoke depot\'s parent is its regional hub). Enables hierarchical network topology traversal for route planning and network performance roll-up reporting. Self-referencing relationship resolved in the linking step.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the network node facility address. Used for last-mile delivery zone assignment, carrier rate lookup, and geographic clustering in Blue Yonder network optimization.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Internal geographic region code grouping the node into a commercial or operational region (e.g., \'APAC\', \'EMEA\', \'AMER\', \'MEA\'). Used for network performance reporting, regional P&L allocation in SAP S/4HANA Finance, and Blue Yonder network design segmentation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this network node record was sourced (e.g., \'SAP_TM\', \'BLUE_YONDER\'). Used for data lineage tracking, reconciliation, and master data management governance in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  storage_capacity_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum static storage capacity of the node measured in Cubic Meters (CBM). Used for warehouse capacity planning in Manhattan WMS, inventory optimization in Blue Yonder, and network design decisions.']
  temp_range_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius that the node\'s temperature-controlled facility can maintain (e.g., 8.00 for refrigerated). Null if temperature_controlled is False. Used for cold-chain shipment routing and cargo integrity compliance.']
  temp_range_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius that the node\'s temperature-controlled facility can maintain (e.g., -25.00 for deep freeze). Null if temperature_controlled is False. Used for cold-chain shipment routing and SLA compliance validation.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node has temperature-controlled storage and handling capabilities (cold chain). True = facility supports temperature-sensitive cargo. Used for pharmaceutical, perishable, and cold-chain shipment routing in SAP TM.']
  teu_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum container throughput capacity of the node measured in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) per day. Applicable to port, ICD, CFS, and FTZ nodes. Used for ocean freight capacity planning and FCL/LCL load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  timezone_code varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the node\'s local time zone (e.g., \'Asia/Hong_Kong\', \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Critical for accurate ETD/ETA calculation, operating hours enforcement in SAP TM, and SLA compliance measurement across time zones.']
  tms_location_code varchar [note: 'Location identifier code as registered in SAP TM and Oracle TMS for route planning, freight order management, and carrier selection. Serves as the cross-system reference key for transport execution systems.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transport mode served by this network node. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (IATA for air, IMO for ocean, DOT for road/rail) and routing logic in SAP TM. \'multimodal\' indicates the node serves multiple modes (e.g., an intermodal terminal).']
  un_locode varchar [note: 'Five-character UN/LOCODE identifying the geographic location of the node (e.g., \'HKHKG\', \'SGSIN\'). Used for Bill of Lading (BOL), customs declarations in Descartes, and EDI messaging per WCO standards. Provides a globally standardized location reference across all transport modes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this network node record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse silver layer and audit trail maintenance.']
  wms_facility_code varchar [note: 'Facility identifier code as registered in Manhattan WMS for nodes that perform warehousing and fulfillment operations. Used to link network node records to WMS inventory, labor, and slotting data. Null for non-warehouse nodes.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical or logical node in the transport network including origin/destination hubs, gateways, sorting centers, warehouses, ICDs, CFSs, FTZs, ports, airports, and last-mile delivery depots. Captures node code, node type, IATA/UN LOCODE, geographic coordinates (lat/long), country, region, time zone, operating hours, handling capabilities (dangerous goods, temperature-controlled, oversized), and node status. Serves as the geographic backbone for hub-and-spoke topology and route planning in SAP TM and Blue Yonder.'
}

Table route.service_corridor {
  service_corridor_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the service corridor record. Primary key for the service_corridor data product within the route domain.']
  origin_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: service_corridor defines a named multi-leg corridor connecting network nodes. The origin_hub_code STRING field is a denormalized reference to the origin network node. Adding origin_network_node_id as ']
  trade_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_agreement. Business justification: Service corridor planning incorporates trade agreement zones for network optimization. Corridors designed to leverage FTA benefits (e.g., USMCA-compliant routing through Mexico) require explicit agree']
  awb_prefix varchar [note: 'Three-digit IATA Air Waybill (AWB) prefix assigned to the primary carrier operating this air corridor. Applicable only for AIR transport mode corridors. Used for MAWB (Master Air Waybill) and HAWB (House Air Waybill) number generation in SAP TM.']
  capacity_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weekly or per-departure capacity of this corridor expressed in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for ocean corridors, or equivalent volumetric capacity for other modes. Used in Blue Yonder capacity planning and SAP TM load optimization.']
  carrier_scac_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Standard Carrier Alpha Codes (SCAC) or IATA carrier codes for carriers allocated to operate on this corridor. Used in SAP TM carrier selection and Oracle TMS freight audit. Supports multi-carrier corridor configurations.']
  co2e_emission_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg CO2e per tonne-kilometre for this corridor, based on transport mode and carrier mix. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) emissions reporting, EU ETS compliance, CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations, and sustainability KPI dashboards.']
  corridor_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the service corridor, used in SAP TM route planning and Blue Yonder network design (e.g., \'APAC-AIR-EXP-01\', \'TPAC-OCN-FCL-02\'). Referenced in EDI messages and carrier lane allocations.']
  corridor_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the service corridor describing the route path and service product (e.g., \'Express Air Asia-Pacific\', \'Ocean FCL Trans-Pacific\', \'Road FTL Europe Central\'). Used in operational dashboards, carrier communications, and Blue Yonder network design outputs.']
  corridor_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of the service corridor. ACTIVE corridors are available for shipment routing in SAP TM. SUSPENDED corridors are temporarily unavailable (e.g., due to port congestion, regulatory hold). PLANNED corridors are in network design phase in Blue Yonder. DISCONTINUED corridors are archived.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this service corridor record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Audit trail field for data governance, SOX compliance, and lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  customs_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance is required for shipments transiting this corridor. TRUE for cross-border corridors requiring Descartes Customs processing, ISF filing, and AMS submission. FALSE for domestic or intra-customs-union corridors (e.g., intra-EU).']
  dangerous_goods_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether dangerous goods (DG) shipments are permitted on this corridor. Governed by IMDG Code for ocean, ICAO Technical Instructions for air, and ADR for road. Drives carrier selection and compliance checks in SAP TM and Descartes Customs.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the corridor\'s destination country (e.g., \'GBR\', \'AUS\', \'BRA\'). Used for customs compliance in Descartes, trade lane reporting, and cross-border regulatory requirements under WCO and WTO frameworks.']
  destination_hub_code varchar [note: 'IATA airport code or UN/LOCODE of the primary destination hub node for this corridor (e.g., \'LHR\' for London Heathrow, \'USLAX\' for Los Angeles). Anchors the corridor\'s terminal network node in SAP TM route planning and FourKites real-time visibility.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Named geographic region where the service corridor terminates (e.g., \'Europe\', \'Middle East\', \'Latin America\'). Used for network topology grouping, lane allocation, and Blue Yonder network design. Distinct from specific destination node.']
  direction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the corridor is defined for outbound (origin to destination), inbound (destination to origin), or bilateral (both directions) traffic flow. Drives lane allocation and backhaul optimization in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total great-circle or road/rail network distance in kilometres for the full corridor from origin hub to destination hub including all intermediate legs. Used for CO2e emissions calculation, freight rate benchmarking, and Blue Yonder network optimization.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messaging is enabled for carrier communications on this corridor (e.g., EDI 214 shipment status, EDI 210 freight invoice). Drives automated data exchange configuration in SAP TM and Oracle TMS freight audit.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this service corridor becomes operationally active and available for shipment routing in SAP TM. Used for corridor lifecycle management, seasonal schedule changes, and network design transitions.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this service corridor ceases to be operationally active. NULL indicates an open-ended corridor with no planned end date. Used for corridor lifecycle management and seasonal schedule deactivation in SAP TM.']
  freight_product varchar [note: 'Specific freight product type carried on this corridor. FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less than Container Load), FTL (Full Truckload), LTL (Less than Truckload), AIR_FREIGHT, PARCEL, BULK, or BREAKBULK. Governs load optimization rules in Oracle TMS and capacity planning in Blue Yonder. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FCL|LCL|FTL|LTL|AIR_FREIGHT|PARCEL|BULK|BREAKBULK — promote to reference product]']
  frequency_days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of days on which the corridor operates (e.g., \'MON,WED,FRI\' for tri-weekly ocean service, \'MON,TUE,WED,THU,FRI,SAT,SUN\' for daily express). Used in SAP TM departure scheduling and customer-facing ETD commitments.']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'Operational frequency at which the service corridor runs (e.g., DAILY for express parcel, WEEKLY for ocean FCL). Drives departure schedule planning in SAP TM, capacity allocation in Blue Yonder, and ETD/ETA commitments communicated to customers.']
  hub_spoke_role varchar [note: 'Classification of this corridor\'s role within the hub-and-spoke network topology. HUB_TO_HUB for main trunk routes, HUB_TO_SPOKE for distribution legs, SPOKE_TO_HUB for collection legs, SPOKE_TO_SPOKE for direct point-to-point routes. Used in Blue Yonder network design and SAP TM route planning.']
  incoterms_applicability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Incoterms (International Commercial Terms) applicable to shipments on this corridor (e.g., \'FOB,CIF,DDP,DAP,EXW\'). Governs risk transfer and cost responsibility rules for cross-border shipments. Aligned with ICC Incoterms 2020 and WTO trade facilitation requirements.']
  intermediate_hub_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordered, pipe-delimited sequence of intermediate hub codes (IATA or UN/LOCODE) traversed between origin and destination (e.g., \'DXB|FRA\' for Dubai then Frankfurt). Defines the multi-node path that distinguishes a corridor from a single O-D lane. Consumed by SAP TM for multi-leg route planning.']
  last_mile_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether last-mile delivery to the final consignee is included within this corridor\'s scope. TRUE for D2D (Door-to-Door) corridors. FALSE for port-to-port or hub-to-hub corridors where last-mile is handled separately.']
  leg_count varchar [note: 'Total number of transport legs in this service corridor, including origin-to-first-hub, all intermediate legs, and final leg to destination. Derived from the hub sequence but stored for query performance in SAP TM load optimization and Blue Yonder network design.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity in Cubic Meters (CBM) per shipment or per departure on this corridor. Used alongside DIM Weight (Dimensional Weight) calculations for air freight rating and load optimization in Oracle TMS and SAP TM charge calculation.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum gross weight in kilograms permitted per shipment on this corridor, based on carrier capacity constraints, regulatory limits, and infrastructure limitations (e.g., bridge weight limits for road, aircraft payload for air). Used in load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  network_design_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the Blue Yonder network design scenario that generated or last optimized this corridor configuration. Enables traceability between network optimization outputs and operational corridor definitions.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the corridor\'s origin country (e.g., \'CHN\', \'USA\', \'DEU\'). Used for customs compliance in Descartes, trade lane reporting, and cross-border regulatory requirements under WCO and WTO frameworks.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'Named geographic region where the service corridor originates (e.g., \'Asia-Pacific\', \'North America\', \'Europe\'). Used for network topology grouping, lane allocation, and Blue Yonder network design. Distinct from specific origin node — this is the macro-geographic classification.']
  primary_carrier_code varchar [note: 'SCAC or IATA code of the primary/preferred carrier for this corridor. Used as the default carrier in SAP TM carrier selection logic. Distinct from carrier_scac_codes which lists all allocated carriers.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of this corridor relative to alternative corridors for the same O-D pair. Lower values indicate higher priority in SAP TM carrier selection and load optimization. Used when multiple corridors serve the same origin-destination combination.']
  service_level_code varchar [note: 'Internal SLA (Service Level Agreement) code that maps this corridor to a defined service level tier (e.g., \'P1-NEXT-DAY\', \'P2-2DAY\', \'STD-5DAY\'). Links to SLA entitlement definitions in Salesforce CRM and drives OTD/OTIF KPI measurement.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service product offered on this corridor (e.g., EXPRESS for time-definite delivery, STANDARD for deferred, ECONOMY for cost-optimized). Determines SLA commitments, pricing tiers, and priority handling rules in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  standard_transit_days varchar [note: 'Standard total transit time in calendar days from origin pickup to destination delivery for this corridor under normal operating conditions. Used as the baseline for SLA commitments, ETA calculation in FourKites, and OTD (On-Time Delivery) KPI measurement.']
  standard_transit_hours varchar [note: 'Standard total transit time expressed in hours for sub-day precision, particularly relevant for express air corridors where transit is measured in hours rather than days (e.g., 18 hours for an overnight express air corridor). Complements standard_transit_days for precise ETA calculation.']
  suspension_end_date date [note: 'Expected or actual date on which a suspended corridor resumes operations. NULL if suspension end date is unknown. Used for proactive customer communication in Salesforce CRM and re-routing planning in SAP TM.']
  suspension_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code for corridor suspension. Used for exception reporting, root cause analysis, and regulatory compliance documentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PORT_CONGESTION|REGULATORY_EMBARGO|FORCE_MAJEURE|CARRIER_CAPACITY|INFRASTRUCTURE|SEASONAL|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  suspension_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the corridor is temporarily suspended (e.g., due to port congestion, regulatory embargo, force majeure). Populated only when corridor_status is SUSPENDED. Used for exception management in FourKites and customer notification in Salesforce CRM.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this corridor supports temperature-controlled (cold chain) shipments. Relevant for pharmaceutical, perishable food, and chemical cargo. Drives equipment type selection (reefer containers, temperature-controlled vehicles) in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  tracking_visibility_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time shipment tracking and predictive ETA via FourKites is enabled for this corridor. TRUE enables GPS/IoT-based visibility, exception alerts, and predictive ETA updates for shipments on this corridor.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this service corridor. Drives carrier selection, regulatory compliance requirements, and charge calculation in SAP TM. Aligns with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) regulatory frameworks.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this service corridor record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline and audit trail compliance.']

  Note: 'Defines a named, multi-leg service corridor connecting a sequence of network nodes for a specific transport mode and service product (e.g., Express Air Asia-Pacific, Ocean FCL Trans-Pacific, Road FTL Europe). Captures corridor name, mode, service type, origin region, destination region, intermediate hub sequence, standard total transit time, frequency (daily/weekly), and operational status. Used by SAP TM for route planning and by Blue Yonder for network design optimization. Distinct from individual lanes — a corridor is a named multi-node path, a lane is a single O-D pair.'
}

Table route.corridor_leg {
  corridor_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each individual transport leg within a service corridor. Primary key for the corridor_leg entity in the Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contracted carrier or transport operator assigned to execute this leg. Used for carrier lane allocation, freight audit, and carrier performance analytics in Oracle TMS.']
  destination_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node (hub, port, airport, depot, ICD, CFS, or warehouse) where this leg terminates. Together with origin_node_id defines the origin-destination (OD) pair for this leg.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: A corridor_leg represents a single node-to-node movement segment within a service corridor. A lane is the master origin-destination corridor definition for exactly that type of movement. Linking corri']
  primary_corridor_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node (hub, port, airport, depot, ICD, CFS, or warehouse) where this leg originates. Supports hub-and-spoke topology decomposition in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent service corridor that this leg belongs to. A corridor is composed of one or more sequential legs representing the full origin-to-destination route.']
  trade_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.trade_agreement. Business justification: Corridor leg design must incorporate trade agreement rules (USMCA, EU FTAs) to enable preferential duty routing. Network planners configure legs to maintain origin qualification and minimize duty expo']
  carrier_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the carrier operating this leg. Determines applicable regulatory frameworks (IATA for airlines, IMO for ocean carriers, DOT for road), rate structures, and liability regimes. NVOCC applies to Non-Vessel Operating Common Carriers. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AIRLINE|OCEAN_CARRIER|ROAD_CARRIER|RAIL_OPERATOR|NVOCC|COURIER|INTERMODAL — promote to reference product]']
  co2e_emission_factor decimal(10,6) [note: 'Carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emission factor in kg per tonne-kilometre for this leg\'s transport mode and vehicle type. Used to calculate shipment-level GHG emissions for EU ETS reporting, CORSIA compliance (air legs), and customer sustainability reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corridor leg record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for data lineage, SOX compliance, and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest local time (HH:MM, 24-hour) by which freight must be tendered at the origin node to be accepted on this leg\'s scheduled departure. Critical for shipment booking deadlines in Oracle TMS and customer SLA management.']
  departure_days_mask varchar [note: '7-character binary string (Mon=pos1 through Sun=pos7) indicating which days of the week this leg operates departures (1=operates, 0=does not operate). Enables precise booking calendar generation in SAP TM and Oracle TMS scheduling.']
  departure_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this leg operates departures. Drives shipment booking availability windows, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and customer-facing transit time commitments. ON_DEMAND applies to charter and FTL legs.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the leg destination. Used for import/export compliance, denied party screening in Descartes, and bilateral trade lane reporting.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized location code for the leg destination — IATA airport code (3-letter) for air legs, UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail legs. Used in AWB, BOL, and HAWB generation and customs declarations in Descartes.']
  distance_km decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical distance of this transport leg in kilometres. Used for freight rate calculation (per-km tariffs), GHG/CO2e emissions estimation per EU ETS and CORSIA requirements, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this corridor leg definition becomes operationally valid and available for shipment assignment. Supports seasonal route activation, network redesign rollouts from Blue Yonder, and historical lane analytics.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date after which this corridor leg definition is no longer operationally valid. Null indicates an open-ended lane with no planned expiry. Used for seasonal route deactivation, carrier contract expiry alignment, and network change management.']
  fourtites_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg is enrolled in FourKites real-time visibility tracking (True). When enabled, GPS/IoT telemetry is streamed for predictive ETA updates and exception management. Supports independent leg trackability as defined in the corridor_leg product scope.']
  incoterms_applicability varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) applicable to this leg, defining the point of risk and cost transfer between shipper and consignee. Governs liability allocation, insurance requirements, and customs filing responsibilities. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — promote to reference product]']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg crosses an international border (True) or is a domestic movement (False). Triggers customs declaration requirements in Descartes, ISF/AMS filing obligations, and AEO/C-TPAT compliance checks.']
  is_dangerous_goods_permitted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg is certified and permitted to carry dangerous goods (True) or not (False). Governs shipment acceptance rules per IMDG Code for ocean legs and ICAO Technical Instructions for air legs.']
  is_temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg provides temperature-controlled (cold chain) transport capability (True). Relevant for pharmaceutical, perishable, and high-value cargo routing and SLA compliance.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this corridor leg definition was last reviewed and validated by the network planning team. Supports data quality governance, periodic lane review cycles, and audit trail for network optimization decisions.']
  leg_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for this corridor leg, used in SAP TM route planning, Oracle TMS load optimization, and EDI interchange with carriers. Unique within the parent corridor.']
  leg_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of this leg within the parent corridor, starting at 1. Defines the execution order for multi-modal route decomposition (e.g., leg 1 = road pickup, leg 2 = air haul, leg 3 = road delivery). Aligns with Oracle TMS load optimization sequencing.']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational lifecycle status of this corridor leg. ACTIVE legs are available for shipment assignment; SUSPENDED legs are temporarily unavailable; DISCONTINUED legs are retired from the network. Governs availability in Oracle TMS load optimization.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Freight load configuration for this leg. FTL (Full Truckload), LTL (Less Than Truckload), FCL (Full Container Load), LCL (Less Than Container Load), PARCEL, BULK, or BREAK_BULK. Drives Oracle TMS load optimization, rate calculation, and capacity planning in Blue Yonder. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|PARCEL|BULK|BREAK_BULK — promote to reference product]']
  max_volume_capacity_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity in Cubic Metres (CBM) available on this leg per departure. Used alongside max_weight_capacity_kg for DIM weight-based load optimization and capacity utilization analytics in Oracle TMS.']
  max_weight_capacity_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum chargeable or physical weight capacity in kilograms that this leg can accommodate per departure. Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS, capacity planning in Blue Yonder, and freight booking acceptance logic.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the leg origin. Used for cross-border compliance checks, customs filing, AEO/C-TPAT screening, and trade lane analytics.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized location code for the leg origin — IATA airport code (3-letter) for air legs, UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail legs. Used in AWB, BOL, and HAWB generation and AMS/ISF filings.']
  remarks varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or remarks about this corridor leg, such as seasonal restrictions, port congestion advisories, special handling requirements, or network planning rationale. Intended for network planners and operations teams.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Service level classification for this leg indicating speed and priority tier. Determines SLA commitments, rate applicability, and carrier selection logic in SAP TM charge calculation.']
  sla_on_time_threshold_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable transit time in hours for this leg before it is classified as a late delivery for OTD (On-Time Delivery) and OTIF (On-Time In-Full) KPI measurement. Sourced from contracted SLA terms and used in FourKites exception management.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this corridor leg definition was sourced. SAP_TM for route planning lanes, ORACLE_TMS for shipment execution lanes, BLUE_YONDER for network optimization outputs, or MANUAL for analyst-entered lanes. Supports data lineage and master data governance.']
  standard_arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Standard local arrival time (HH:MM, 24-hour) at the destination node for this leg. Used as the baseline Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) for downstream leg connections, delivery scheduling, and OTD/OTIF KPI measurement.']
  standard_departure_time timestamp [note: 'Standard local departure time (HH:MM, 24-hour) for this leg at the origin node. Used as the baseline Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) for shipment scheduling, FourKites tracking initialization, and customer notification triggers.']
  standard_transit_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Contracted or standard transit time for this leg expressed in decimal hours. Used as the baseline for ETA calculation, SLA commitment derivation, and predictive ETA modeling in FourKites. Sourced from SAP TM transit time matrix.']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum temperature in degrees Celsius maintained during transport on this leg. Applicable only when is_temperature_controlled is True. Used for cold chain compliance validation and cargo acceptance rules.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum temperature in degrees Celsius maintained during transport on this leg. Applicable only when is_temperature_controlled is True. Used for cold chain compliance validation and cargo acceptance rules.']
  timezone_destination varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the destination node (e.g., Asia/Singapore, Europe/Frankfurt). Required to correctly interpret standard_arrival_time and compute accurate ETA in local destination time for delivery scheduling.']
  timezone_origin varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the origin node (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Required to correctly interpret cutoff_time and standard_departure_time for cross-timezone route planning and ETA calculations.']
  tracking_method varchar [note: 'Method used to track shipment progress on this leg. GPS for road/fleet, AIS (Automatic Identification System) for ocean vessels, EDI_MILESTONE for carrier EDI updates, API_CARRIER for direct carrier API integration, RFID for warehouse handoffs, or MANUAL for exception-based updates. Drives FourKites data ingestion configuration.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this leg. Drives carrier selection, rate calculation, customs filing requirements (IATA for air, IMO for ocean), and ETA computation. Supports multi-modal freight forwarding decomposition (air + road, ocean + rail).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this corridor leg record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance.']
  vehicle_type varchar [note: 'Type of vehicle, vessel, or aircraft used on this leg (e.g., B747F for air freighter, 20FT_CONTAINER for ocean, SEMI_TRAILER for road, FLATCAR for rail). Determines capacity constraints, dangerous goods handling rules per IMDG/ICAO TI, and emissions factors for CO2e calculation.']

  Note: 'Individual transport leg within a service corridor, representing a single node-to-node movement segment. Captures leg sequence number, origin node, destination node, transport mode, carrier type, leg distance, standard leg transit time (hours), cut-off time, departure frequency, and leg status. Enables multi-modal route decomposition for freight forwarding (air + road, ocean + rail) and supports load optimization in Oracle TMS. A corridor has one or more legs; each leg is independently trackable via FourKites.'
}

Table route.route_delivery_zone {
  route_delivery_zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the delivery zone record. Primary key for the route_delivery_zone data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — represents a geographic zone asset managed by Transport Shipping for last-mile routing and serviceability.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: route_delivery_zone is currently SILOED (no inbound or outbound FKs). It has hub_node_code (STRING) which is a denormalized code reference to the hub serving this last-mile zone. Adding hub_node_id as']
  servicing_agent_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.agent. Business justification: Last-mile delivery zones are frequently serviced by local agents. Required for agent commission calculation, service coverage validation, and agent performance tracking. Essential for agent-based dist']
  serving_depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_depot. Business justification: Delivery zones are served from specific depots; linking enables territory management, last-mile route planning, depot workload balancing, and service coverage analysis.']
  aeo_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether AEO (Authorized Economic Operator) certification is required for carriers operating within this zone. Relevant for cross-border and customs-controlled zones. Enforced during carrier selection in Descartes Customs and SAP TM.']
  applicable_service_products varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of service product codes eligible for delivery within this zone (e.g., \'EXPRESS|STANDARD|ECONOMY|FREIGHT\'). Drives service product availability validation during shipment booking and carrier selection in SAP TM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product for zone-service-product association]']
  area_sqkm decimal(12,4) [note: 'Total geographic area of the delivery zone in square kilometres. Used in network density analysis, carrier capacity planning, and cost-per-delivery benchmarking in Blue Yonder network design.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary carrier or last-mile delivery partner assigned to service this zone. Used in carrier selection logic within SAP TM and Oracle TMS. May be overridden by dynamic carrier selection based on capacity and cost.']
  city_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary city or municipality associated with the delivery zone. Used for last-mile dispatch assignment, carrier lane allocation, and customer-facing serviceability lookup.']
  co2e_factor_kg_per_shipment decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated CO2e (Carbon Dioxide Equivalent) emission factor in kilograms per shipment for last-mile delivery within this zone. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) emissions reporting, EU ETS compliance, and CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations.']
  cod_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Cash on Delivery (COD) service is restricted or unavailable within this delivery zone. COD restrictions may apply due to security concerns, carrier capability limitations, or local regulatory requirements. Referenced during e-commerce fulfillment order validation.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code where the delivery zone is located (e.g., GBR, SGP, USA, DEU). Used for customs compliance, regulatory routing rules, and cross-border serviceability validation per WCO and IMO standards.']
  coverage_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON-encoded polygon or multipolygon representing the precise geographic boundary of the delivery zone. Used for GPS-based serviceability validation, FourKites real-time visibility geofencing, and last-mile dispatch optimization. Stored as serialized GeoJSON string per RFC 7946.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery zone record was first created in the system (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for zone master data governance and SOX compliance.']
  customs_zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone from a customs and trade compliance perspective: domestic (standard domestic territory), ftz (Free Trade Zone), bonded (customs bonded area), international (cross-border delivery zone), special_economic (Special Economic Zone). Used in Descartes Customs for trade compliance routing and ISF/AMS filing requirements.']
  dg_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transport of dangerous goods (DG) is restricted or prohibited within this delivery zone. Enforced during shipment booking validation and carrier selection. Aligned with IMDG Code, ICAO Technical Instructions, and local regulatory requirements.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this delivery zone definition becomes operationally effective. Used for temporal zone management, ensuring routing and pricing rules apply only within the valid date range. Supports zone versioning and network change management.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which this delivery zone definition is no longer operationally effective. Null for open-ended zones with no planned expiry. Used for zone versioning, network restructuring, and historical serviceability audit.']
  hub_node_code varchar [note: 'Code of the hub, depot, or distribution center assigned to service this delivery zone in the hub-and-spoke network topology. Drives carrier selection, load optimization, and last-mile dispatch routing in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  isf_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an Importer Security Filing (ISF) is required for shipments entering this zone. Applicable to US import zones under CBP 10+2 rule. Used in Descartes Customs for automated compliance filing.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Decimal latitude of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone in WGS84 coordinate system. Used for distance-based routing calculations, GPS geofencing in FourKites, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Decimal longitude of the geographic centroid of the delivery zone in WGS84 coordinate system. Used for distance-based routing calculations, GPS geofencing in FourKites, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  max_dim_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum dimensional weight (DIM weight) in kilograms accepted for delivery within this zone. DIM weight is calculated from volumetric dimensions and used alongside actual weight for carrier charge calculation and capacity planning.']
  max_length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable length in centimetres for a single shipment piece accepted for delivery within this zone. Used in oversized shipment validation during booking and load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Maximum gross weight in kilograms for a single shipment accepted for delivery within this zone. Enforced during shipment booking validation and load optimization. Reflects infrastructure, vehicle, and carrier capability constraints.']
  otd_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On-Time Delivery (OTD) percentage for this zone, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 98.50 = 98.5%). Used as the KPI benchmark for carrier performance management and network optimization reporting.']
  oversized_restricted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether oversized or overweight shipments are restricted within this delivery zone due to infrastructure limitations, road access constraints, or local regulations. Used in load optimization and carrier selection in Oracle TMS.']
  peak_surcharge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a peak season surcharge is applicable for this zone during designated peak periods (e.g., holiday season, Chinese New Year). Drives dynamic pricing adjustments in SAP TM charge calculation.']
  postal_code_from varchar [note: 'Starting postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Together with postal_code_to, defines the postal code band for zone membership evaluation. Used in SAP TM zone-based routing and pricing surcharge determination.']
  postal_code_to varchar [note: 'Ending postal code of the range covered by this delivery zone. Together with postal_code_from, defines the postal code band for zone membership evaluation. Null if the zone is defined by a single postal code or polygon only.']
  remote_area_surcharge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a remote area surcharge (RAS) applies to shipments delivered within this zone. Remote area surcharges are applied by carriers for zones with limited accessibility or extended delivery routes. Referenced by the pricing and rating domain for charge calculation.']
  residential_surcharge boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a residential delivery surcharge applies to shipments within this zone. Residential surcharges are levied by carriers for deliveries to non-commercial addresses. Used in pricing and rating charge calculation for B2C and e-commerce fulfillment.']
