  service_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local time) by which a shipment must be tendered to the hub to achieve the standard transit time for this zone. Critical for last-mile dispatch scheduling and customer booking cutoff enforcement.']
  service_days varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of days of the week on which delivery service operates within this zone (e.g., \'MON|TUE|WED|THU|FRI\'). Used for ETA calculation, SLA management, and dispatch scheduling. Supports zones with restricted weekend or holiday service.']
  serviceable boolean [note: 'Master serviceability flag indicating whether Transport Shipping currently provides delivery service within this zone. A zone may be defined but temporarily unserviceable due to operational, regulatory, or capacity constraints. Used as the primary gate in shipment booking serviceability checks.']
  sla_standard_code varchar [note: 'Code referencing the standard SLA profile applicable to this delivery zone. Defines OTD (On-Time Delivery) and OTIF (On-Time In-Full) performance targets. Used in SLA entitlement validation and KPI reporting.']
  standard_transit_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of business days required to deliver a shipment from the assigned hub node to any address within this zone under normal operating conditions. Used as the baseline for SLA commitment, OTD measurement, and customer ETA communication.']
  state_province_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-2 subdivision code for the state, province, or region within the country (e.g., US-CA, GB-ENG, AU-NSW). Used for sub-national routing rules, tax jurisdiction mapping, and regional SLA configuration.']
  temperature_controlled_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether temperature-controlled (cold chain) delivery is available within this zone. Used during shipment booking to validate serviceability for pharmaceutical, perishable, and temperature-sensitive cargo.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the delivery zone (e.g., \'Europe/London\', \'Asia/Singapore\', \'America/New_York\'). Used to correctly interpret service cutoff times, ETA/ETD calculations, and SLA breach detection in FourKites and SAP TM.']
  transit_time_hours varchar [note: 'Standard transit time expressed in total hours for time-definite and express service products where sub-day precision is required. Complements standard_transit_days for same-day, next-day, and time-critical shipment SLA management.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transport mode used for last-mile delivery within this zone. Determines carrier eligibility, equipment type, and routing logic in SAP TM and Oracle TMS. Multimodal zones support more than one mode.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery zone record was last modified (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data lineage tracking, and master data governance audit.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the delivery zone across systems (SAP TM, Oracle TMS, FourKites). Used as the business key for zone-based routing, surcharge lookup, and carrier selection. Maps to the zone identifier in SAP TM zone-based routing configuration.']
  zone_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the delivery zone, including notable geographic features, operational constraints, special handling instructions, or historical notes. Used by operations and network planning teams.']
  zone_group_code varchar [note: 'Code of the zone group or zone cluster to which this delivery zone belongs. Zone groups aggregate individual zones for pricing rate card application, carrier contract negotiation, and network planning. Used in SAP TM zone group configuration.']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the delivery zone (e.g., \'Greater London Last-Mile Zone\', \'Singapore CBD Service Area\'). Used in operational dashboards, carrier communications, and customer-facing serviceability checks.']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery zone. Active zones are eligible for shipment routing and serviceability validation. Inactive or retired zones are excluded from new shipment assignments. Suspended zones may be temporarily blocked due to operational or regulatory reasons.']
  zone_tier varchar [note: 'Service tier classification of the zone reflecting delivery density, infrastructure quality, and cost profile (e.g., tier_1 = major urban centre, tier_4 = remote/rural). Used in pricing rate card assignment, SLA differentiation, and network optimization in Blue Yonder.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the zone by its definition methodology: postal (based on postal code ranges), administrative (country/state/city boundaries), custom (business-defined polygon), last_mile (final delivery leg zone), service_area (carrier or hub service coverage area). Drives routing logic and surcharge applicability in SAP TM.']

  Note: 'Geographic zone definition used for last-mile routing, service area delineation, postal zone mapping, and serviceability validation across all transport modes. Captures zone code, zone name, zone type (postal, administrative, custom, last-mile, service-area), country, state/province, city, postal code range, coverage polygon (GeoJSON), assigned hub node, standard transit time, surcharge applicability (remote area, residential), service restrictions (dangerous goods, oversized, COD), applicable service products, and zone status. SSOT for geographic coverage and serviceability master data referenced by pricing (surcharges), fulfillment (dispatch), SAP TM (zone-based routing), and carrier selection.'
}

Table route.transit_time_standard {
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the transit time standard record. Primary key for this entity in the route domain Silver layer.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Transit time standards are contractually committed SLA baselines. Linking to agreement enables automated compliance verification, breach detection, and penalty/incentive calculation. Required for cont']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or transport service provider for whom this transit time standard is defined. Used in SAP TM carrier selection logic.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the logistics service product (e.g., express parcel, economy freight) for which this transit time standard is defined.']
  origin_route_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: transit_time_standard captures origin_zone_code as a STRING, which is a denormalized reference to the origin route_delivery_zone. Adding origin_route_delivery_zone_id as a FK to route.route_delivery_z']
  primary_transit_lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination lane master record that this transit time standard applies to. Links to the lane product in the route domain.']
  service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.service_corridor. Business justification: transit_time_standard is the authoritative transit time standard for a lane, corridor, or service product combination. The network_corridor_code STRING field is a denormalized reference to a service c']
  superseded_by_transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the newer transit time standard record that replaces this one when lifecycle_status is SUPERSEDED. Enables version chain traversal for historical SLA analysis.']
  blue_yonder_standard_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from Blue Yonder Supply Chain Planning that maps this transit time standard to the network design and demand forecasting outputs. Enables traceability between the route domain SSOT and Blue Yonder optimization results.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transit time standard record was first created in the system. Audit trail field supporting SOX compliance and data lineage in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  customs_clearance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allocated for customs clearance included within the standard transit time for cross-border shipments. Used in Descartes Customs integration and trade compliance planning. Relevant for DDP, DAP, and CIF Incoterms.']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'The latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local) by which a shipment must be tendered at the origin node to qualify for this transit time standard on a given day. Used in shipment booking, load optimization in Oracle TMS, and SLA management.']
  cutoff_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the cut-off time (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of cutoff_time across global operations.']
  delivery_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the delivery time window at the destination node (e.g., Asia/Singapore, America/Los_Angeles). Ensures unambiguous interpretation of first_available_delivery_time and latest_delivery_time.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination location of this transit time standard. Used in cross-border routing, customs compliance, and trade lane analytics.']
  destination_node_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the destination hub, depot, port, airport, or facility node for this transit time standard. Aligns with IATA airport codes, UN/LOCODE, or internal network node identifiers.']
  destination_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination node (e.g., airport, seaport, Inland Container Depot (ICD), Container Freight Station (CFS), Free Trade Zone (FTZ)). Supports network topology and hub-and-spoke configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AIRPORT|SEAPORT|RAIL_TERMINAL|ROAD_HUB|WAREHOUSE|ICD|CFS|FTZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  destination_zone_code varchar [note: 'Postal or geographic zone code for the destination area, used in last-mile delivery zone mapping and zone-based transit time calculations in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  effective_from date [note: 'The date from which this transit time standard becomes valid and applicable for SLA commitments, ETA calculation, and carrier selection. Supports temporal versioning of transit standards.']
  effective_until date [note: 'The date after which this transit time standard is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended standard with no planned expiry. Used in contract SLA management and carrier selection logic.']
  first_available_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The earliest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local) at which delivery can be attempted at the destination under this transit time standard. Used in last-mile scheduling, delivery window commitments, and ePOD (Electronic Proof of Delivery) planning.']
  frequency_type varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this transit service operates on the lane (e.g., daily, weekly, bi-weekly). Used in load planning, capacity allocation, and network optimization outputs from Blue Yonder.']
  hazmat_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit time standard applies to hazardous materials shipments. When true, IMDG Code (ocean) or ICAO Technical Instructions (air) dangerous goods regulations govern the transit. Affects carrier selection and routing.']
  hub_routing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit time standard requires routing through an intermediate hub node (hub-and-spoke configuration). When true, hub transit time is included in standard_transit_days.']
  incoterms_applicability varchar [note: 'Pipe-separated list of International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) under which this transit time standard applies (e.g., DDP|DAP|FOB|CIF|EXW). Governs responsibility boundaries for transit time SLA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DDP|DAP|FOB|CIF|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|FAS|CFR|DPU|EXW — promote to reference product]']
  intermediate_hub_code varchar [note: 'Code of the primary intermediate hub node through which shipments are routed when hub_routing_required is true. Aligns with IATA airport codes or internal network node identifiers.']
  latest_delivery_time timestamp [note: 'The latest time of day (HH:MM, 24-hour local) by which delivery must be completed at the destination to meet this transit time standard. Used in SLA compliance measurement and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) reporting.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current operational state of this transit time standard in its lifecycle. ACTIVE records are used in ETA calculation and carrier selection. SUPERSEDED records are retained for historical SLA audit. DRAFT records are pending approval.']
  max_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum shipment volume in Cubic Meters (CBM) eligible for this transit time standard. Used alongside max_weight_kg and Dimensional Weight (DIM Weight) rules for load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  max_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum gross weight in kilograms of a shipment eligible for this transit time standard. Shipments exceeding this threshold require a different standard or special handling arrangement.']
  operating_days_mask varchar [note: '7-character binary string (Mon-Sun) indicating which days of the week this transit time standard is active (1=active, 0=inactive). Example: \'1111100\' = Monday to Friday only. Encodes day-of-week restrictions for scheduling and ETA calculation.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin location of this transit time standard. Used in cross-border routing, customs compliance, and trade lane analytics.']
  origin_node_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the origin hub, depot, port, airport, or facility node for this transit time standard. Aligns with IATA airport codes, UN/LOCODE, or internal network node identifiers.']
  origin_node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the origin node (e.g., airport, seaport, Inland Container Depot (ICD), Container Freight Station (CFS), Free Trade Zone (FTZ)). Supports network topology analysis and hub-and-spoke configuration. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AIRPORT|SEAPORT|RAIL_TERMINAL|ROAD_HUB|WAREHOUSE|ICD|CFS|FTZ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sap_tm_route_code varchar [note: 'External route identifier from SAP TM used to link this transit time standard to the corresponding route planning and freight order management record in SAP TM. Supports system-of-record traceability.']
  shipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of shipment load for which this transit time standard applies: Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), parcel, pallet, or bulk. Drives carrier selection and load optimization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FTL|LTL|FCL|LCL|PARCEL|PALLET|BULK — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  standard_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business code identifying this transit time standard record, used in SAP TM, Oracle TMS, and FourKites integrations for ETA calculation and carrier selection.']
  standard_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about this transit time standard, including special conditions, exceptions, or operational notes relevant to SLA management and carrier selection.']
  standard_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for this transit time standard, used in reporting, SLA contract references, and carrier selection displays.']
  standard_transit_days varchar [note: 'The planned number of calendar or business days for transit from origin to destination under this standard. Principal quantitative fact for this resource. Used in SLA commitments, ETA calculation in FourKites, and On-Time Delivery (OTD) performance measurement.']
  standard_transit_hours varchar [note: 'The planned number of hours for transit from origin to destination under this standard. Provides sub-day precision for express and same-day services. Complements standard_transit_days for precise ETA calculation in FourKites.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transit time standard applies to temperature-controlled (cold chain) shipments. Affects carrier selection, equipment requirements, and SLA commitments for perishable or pharmaceutical cargo.']
  transit_day_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether standard_transit_days is measured in calendar days, business days, or working days. Critical for accurate SLA commitment and ETA calculation, particularly for cross-border shipments with customs clearance.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this transit time standard. Aligns with IATA (air), IMO (ocean), and DOT (road/rail) classifications. Drives carrier selection and ETA logic in SAP TM and FourKites.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transit time standard record was last modified. Audit trail field supporting change management, SOX compliance, and incremental data loading in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version counter for this transit time standard record. Incremented each time the standard is revised. Supports temporal versioning and audit trail for SLA compliance and regulatory reporting.']

  Note: 'Authoritative transit time standard for a lane, corridor, or service product combination. Captures origin node/zone, destination node/zone, service level (express, standard, economy), transport mode, standard transit days, standard transit hours, cut-off time, first-available-delivery time, day-of-week restrictions, and effective date range. Feeds SLA commitments in the contract domain, ETA calculation in FourKites, and carrier selection logic in SAP TM. Distinct from actual ETA events — this is the planned standard, not the real-time estimate.'
}

Table route.carrier_lane_allocation {
  carrier_lane_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier lane allocation record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Capacity allocations are governed by master agreements with volume commitments and capacity guarantees. Needed for contract utilization tracking, volume commitment compliance, and capacity planning ag']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (airline, ocean carrier, road carrier, rail operator) allocated to this lane. Links to carrier master in procurement domain.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Capacity allocations are contracted at service-product level (express capacity vs. economy capacity are separate pools). Required for service-specific capacity management, booking validation, and allo']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination lane or corridor leg to which this carrier is allocated.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Carrier allocations reference rate cards for pricing. Rate_card_id links capacity allocation to pricing structure, enabling allocated capacity costing, carrier allocation profitability analysis, and r']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Carrier allocations reference tariffs for published rates. Tariff_id links capacity to published pricing, enabling tariff-based allocation costing and regulatory compliance for allocated capacity pric']
  allocation_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for this carrier lane allocation, used in SAP TM carrier selection engine and tendering processes.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding this carrier lane allocation, including special handling instructions, restrictions, or operational considerations.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of lane volume allocated to this carrier, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 35.50 for 35.5%). Used for load balancing and capacity planning.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier lane allocation. Active allocations are eligible for carrier selection.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Type of allocation: contracted (long-term agreement), spot (ad-hoc booking), backup (contingency), seasonal (time-bound), or preferred (strategic partner).']
  api_integration_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether API integration is enabled for real-time booking, tracking, and rate queries with this carrier on this lane.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this carrier lane allocation. Only approved allocations are activated for carrier selection.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this carrier lane allocation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier lane allocation was approved.']
  carbon_emission_factor_kg_co2e decimal(10,4) [note: 'Carbon emission factor in kilograms of CO2 equivalent per unit (per TEU-km, per ton-km, or per shipment) for this carrier on this lane. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) reporting and sustainability analytics.']
  carrier_certification_level varchar [note: 'Certification or partnership level of the carrier: standard, preferred, strategic, AEO (Authorized Economic Operator), C-TPAT (Customs-Trade Partnership Against Terrorism), or other industry certifications.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the underlying carrier contract or rate agreement that governs this allocation.']
  contracted_capacity_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Contracted capacity for ocean freight expressed in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units). Null for non-ocean modes.']
  contracted_capacity_volume_cbm decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted volume capacity in cubic meters (CBM) for freight. Used for dimensional capacity planning.']
  contracted_capacity_weight_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'Contracted weight capacity in kilograms for air, road, or rail freight. Used for capacity management and load optimization.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost per unit (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Contracted cost per unit (per TEU, per kg, per CBM, or per shipment) for this carrier on this lane. Used for freight cost calculation and carrier selection optimization.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this carrier lane allocation record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier lane allocation record was first created in the system.']
  customs_bonded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier operates under customs bond for in-transit goods, enabling duty-deferred cross-border movements.']
  cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM format, 24-hour) for booking this carrier on this lane. Bookings after cutoff are processed next business day.']
  edi_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) is enabled for automated booking, status updates, and invoicing with this carrier on this lane.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this carrier lane allocation becomes effective and eligible for carrier selection.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this carrier lane allocation remains effective. Null for open-ended allocations.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or container used by this carrier on this lane (e.g., 20ft dry, 40ft reefer, flatbed truck, aircraft type). Free-text to accommodate diverse equipment specifications.']
  fuel_surcharge_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a fuel surcharge (BAF - Bunker Adjustment Factor for ocean, or fuel surcharge for air/road) is applicable to this allocation.']
  hazmat_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier is certified and capable of transporting hazardous materials (HAZMAT) on this lane per IMDG Code or ICAO Technical Instructions.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Applicable Incoterm for this carrier lane allocation, defining the division of costs and risks between shipper and carrier (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this carrier lane allocation record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier lane allocation record was last modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Required lead time in days for booking this carrier on this lane. Used for tendering and capacity reservation planning.']
  maximum_volume_limit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum volume limit (in applicable unit: TEU, kg, CBM) that can be allocated to this carrier on this lane per capacity constraints or contract terms.']
  minimum_volume_commitment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum volume commitment (in applicable unit: TEU, kg, CBM) that the shipper must tender to this carrier on this lane per contract terms.']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Historical performance score (0-100) for this carrier on this lane, based on OTD (On-Time Delivery), OTIF (On-Time In-Full), claims rate, and service quality KPIs (Key Performance Indicators).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this carrier for the lane. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by SAP TM carrier selection engine to determine preferred and backup carriers.']
  sla_on_time_delivery_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Service Level Agreement target for on-time delivery percentage (OTD) for this carrier on this lane, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 95.00 for 95%).']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier provides temperature-controlled (reefer) transport on this lane for perishable or pharmaceutical goods.']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard transit time in days for this carrier on this lane, used for ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) calculation and service level planning.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transport for this carrier allocation (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal, parcel express).']
  visibility_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time shipment visibility is enabled for this carrier on this lane via FourKites or other visibility platforms.']

  Note: 'Defines the allocation of a specific carrier (airline, ocean carrier, road carrier, rail operator) to a lane or corridor leg, including contracted capacity, priority ranking, allocation percentage, mode of transport, service type, effective date range, and allocation status. Used by SAP TM carrier selection engine to determine preferred and backup carriers per lane. Supports tendering, spot booking, and capacity management. References carrier master in the procurement domain via FK (linked in a later step).'
}

Table route.plan {
  plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the route plan record. Primary key.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Plans execute shipments under master service agreements. Contract compliance tracking, rate application, SLA enforcement, and penalty/incentive calculation all require linking plan execution to govern']
  destination_location_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the final destination location (warehouse, port, terminal, customer site) for this route plan.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the freight order in SAP TM that triggered this route plan generation.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the predefined origin-destination lane master record that this route plan is based on.']
  plan_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the starting location (warehouse, port, terminal, customer site) for this route plan.']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary carrier (airline, ocean liner, trucking company, rail operator) assigned to execute the majority of this route plan.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Route plans use rate cards for cost estimation. Rate_card_id enables planned cost calculation during route optimization, supporting cost-based routing decisions and planned vs. actual cost variance an']
  service_corridor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the service corridor (geographic trade lane or network segment) that this route plan operates within.']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: An operational route plan is generated against a governing transit time standard that defines the SLA, cutoff times, and expected transit days for the lane/corridor combination. Linking plan to transi']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why this route plan was cancelled. Null if plan was not cancelled.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan was cancelled. Null if plan was not cancelled. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan was completed (final leg arrival at destination). Null if not yet completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan was confirmed and approved for execution. Null if plan is still in draft status. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  destination_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the destination location including street, city, postal code, and country. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the destination location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  execution_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when execution of this route plan began (first leg departure). Null if execution has not started. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterm governing the delivery terms and responsibility transfer point for this shipment: EXW (Ex Works), FCA (Free Carrier), CPT (Carriage Paid To), CIP (Carriage and Insurance Paid To), DAP (Delivered at Place), DPU (Delivered at Place Unloaded), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), FAS (Free Alongside Ship), FOB (Free on Board), CFR (Cost and Freight), CIF (Cost Insurance and Freight). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_cross_border boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route plan crosses international borders and requires customs clearance. True if cross-border, False if domestic.']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route plan involves the transport of dangerous goods or hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance. True if hazmat, False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan record was last updated. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions related to this route plan, such as handling requirements, customer preferences, or operational constraints.']
  number_of_legs varchar [note: 'Total number of transport legs (segments) in this route plan. Each leg represents movement between two consecutive nodes.']
  number_of_stops varchar [note: 'Total number of intermediate stops (hubs, terminals, cross-docks) between origin and destination, excluding origin and destination.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary optimization goal used by the planning system: Cost (minimize freight cost), Time (minimize transit time), CO2e (minimize carbon emissions), or Balanced (multi-objective optimization).']
  origin_address varchar [note: 'Full address of the origin location including street, city, postal code, and country. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the origin location (e.g., USA, DEU, CHN).']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the route plan: Draft (under planning), Confirmed (approved for execution), In Execution (actively being executed), Completed (successfully finished), Cancelled (terminated before completion), or Failed (execution unsuccessful).']
  planned_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) at the destination location. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  planned_co2e_emissions_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total greenhouse gas emissions for this route plan, measured in kilograms of CO2 equivalent (CO2e). Used for sustainability reporting and carbon offsetting.']
  planned_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total planned cost for executing this route plan, including carrier charges, fuel surcharges, and terminal handling charges. Business-confidential financial data.']
  planned_cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the planned cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) from the origin location. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  planning_source_system varchar [note: 'System that generated this route plan: SAP TM (Transportation Management), Blue Yonder (Supply Chain Planning), Oracle TMS, or manual planning.']
  planning_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this route plan was generated or last re-optimized by the planning system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the route plan, generated by SAP TM or Blue Yonder. Used for cross-system tracking and customer communication.']
  requires_customs_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route plan requires customs brokerage and trade compliance processing. True if customs clearance is required, False otherwise.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Service level for this route plan: Express, Standard, Economy, Dedicated, LTL (Less Than Truckload), FTL (Full Truckload), FCL (Full Container Load), or LCL (Less Than Container Load). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXPRESS|STANDARD|ECONOMY|DEDICATED|LTL|FTL|FCL|LCL — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Contractual SLA target timestamp for delivery at destination. Used to measure On-Time Delivery (OTD) and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this route plan requires temperature-controlled transport (refrigerated or climate-controlled). True if temperature control is required, False otherwise.']
  total_planned_distance_km decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total planned distance for the entire route from origin to destination, measured in kilometers. Sum of all leg distances.']
  total_planned_transit_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total planned transit time for the entire route from origin to destination, measured in hours. Includes transport time and planned dwell time at intermediate nodes.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this route plan: Air, Ocean, Road, Rail, Multimodal (combination), or Parcel (express delivery).']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this route plan. Incremented when the plan is re-optimized or modified. Version 1 is the initial plan.']
  visibility_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time visibility tracking via FourKites or similar platform is enabled for this route plan. True if tracking is active, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Operational route plan generated by SAP TM or Blue Yonder for a specific shipment or freight order, representing the planned sequence of nodes, legs, carriers, and timing for end-to-end movement. Captures plan reference number, planning source system, transport mode, total planned distance, total planned transit time, planned ETD, planned ETA, number of legs, optimization objective (cost/time/CO2e), plan status (draft, confirmed, executed, cancelled), and plan version. Transactional — a new plan is created per shipment or freight order planning event.'
}

Table route.plan_leg {
  plan_leg_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the route plan leg. Primary key for this entity.']
  broker_assignment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.broker_assignment. Business justification: Plan leg execution requires tracking which customs broker is handling clearance at that leg\'s port of entry/exit. Critical for coordinating documentation handoff, filing deadlines, and release timing ']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Individual leg execution must reference carrier contract for buy-rate validation, carrier performance measurement against SLAs, and cost reconciliation. Critical for carrier invoice validation and dis']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: Route plan legs use carrier buy rates for cost. Carrier_buy_rate_id enables leg-level cost calculation, supporting multi-leg cost optimization, carrier selection by leg, and leg-level margin analysis.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or transportation service provider executing this leg. Links to the carrier master data for the airline, shipping line, trucking company, or rail operator.']
  carrier_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.carrier_schedule. Business justification: plan_leg represents the planned execution of a transport leg, which is typically booked against a specific published carrier schedule (flight, vessel voyage, truck departure). Linking plan_leg to carr']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Each executed leg operates under a specific carrier service contract. Required for accurate cost calculation, service-level SLA compliance validation, and invoice reconciliation. Enables service-speci']
  container_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.container_unit. Business justification: Intermodal shipments require tracking which container is assigned to each leg for equipment positioning, empty repositioning, depot inventory management, and container lifecycle tracking.']
  corridor_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.corridor_leg. Business justification: plan_leg is the operational execution of a single transport leg within a route plan. corridor_leg is the master definition of that same leg within a service corridor. Linking plan_leg to corridor_leg ']
  customs_declaration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.declaration. Business justification: Cross-border legs trigger customs declarations. Required for leg-level customs clearance tracking, delay attribution to specific border crossings, and customs status visibility in leg execution.']
  destination_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location node for this leg. Represents the arrival point such as a warehouse, hub, terminal, or customer location.']
  driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver assigned to this leg for road transport. Links to workforce master data for driver information.']
  freight_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the freight order or shipment being transported on this leg. Enables direct linkage to the shipment execution record in Oracle TMS or SAP TM.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination lane master data for this leg. Links to the lane definition that governs transit time standards, carrier allocations, and pricing for this corridor.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent route plan that contains this leg. Links this leg to the overall multi-leg route plan for a shipment or freight order.']
  primary_plan_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin location node for this leg. Represents the departure point such as a warehouse, hub, terminal, or customer location.']
  primary_plan_transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Route plan legs specify which vehicle/trailer/container executes each leg—essential for capacity planning, asset utilization tracking, maintenance scheduling, and operational execution visibility.']
  rate_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_line. Business justification: Plan legs execute specific rate line commitments from contracts or rate cards. Enables actual-vs-quoted freight cost analysis, margin tracking, and rate accuracy validation. Critical for freight cost ']
  shipment_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to shipment.shipment_leg. Business justification: Route plan legs (strategic) map to actual shipment legs (tactical execution) when plans are executed. Essential for plan vs. actual variance analysis, performance measurement, delay attribution, and c']
  actual_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual arrival date and time for this leg. Captured from real-time tracking systems such as FourKites or carrier EDI messages. Null if leg has not yet arrived.']
  actual_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Actual departure date and time for this leg. Captured from real-time tracking systems such as FourKites or carrier EDI messages. Null if leg has not yet departed.']
  actual_transit_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual transit time for this leg in hours. Calculated as the difference between actual arrival and actual departure times. Used for performance analysis and OTD (On-Time Delivery) measurement.']
  booking_reference_number varchar [note: 'Carrier booking or reservation number for this leg. Used for ocean freight bookings, air freight reservations, and dedicated transport bookings.']
  carbon_emissions_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Estimated carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) emissions for this leg in kilograms. Calculated based on transport mode, distance, and load weight. Used for sustainability reporting and compliance with EU ETS and CORSIA.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total transportation cost for this leg. Includes base freight charges, fuel surcharges, and accessorial charges. Used for freight audit and cost allocation.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the cost amount. Indicates the currency in which the leg cost is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this route plan leg record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  customs_clearance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg crosses an international border and requires customs clearance. True for cross-border legs requiring customs declarations and trade compliance processing.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'Current status of customs clearance for this leg. Tracks the progression through customs processing from submission to release. Integrated with Descartes Customs system.']
  delay_minutes varchar [note: 'Total delay time in minutes for this leg. Calculated as the difference between actual and planned arrival times. Null if leg arrived on time or early.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for any delay on this leg. Used for root cause analysis and exception management. Examples include weather, customs hold, mechanical failure, traffic congestion.']
  distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned distance for this leg in kilometers. Used for route optimization, cost calculation, and carbon footprint estimation.']
  equipment_type varchar [note: 'Type of equipment or container used for this leg. Examples include 20ft dry container, 40ft reefer, flatbed trailer, box truck, pallet. Follows carrier and industry equipment classification standards.']
  flight_number varchar [note: 'Flight number for air freight legs. Identifies the specific airline flight carrying the shipment (e.g., AA1234, LH456).']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg transports hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance with IMDG Code or ICAO Technical Instructions for Dangerous Goods.']
  leg_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this leg within the parent route plan. Indicates the position of this leg in the multi-leg journey (e.g., 1 for first leg, 2 for second leg).']
  leg_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this route plan leg. Tracks the lifecycle state from planning through execution to completion. Updated by Oracle TMS, SAP TM, and FourKites real-time visibility events.']
  load_type varchar [note: 'Load configuration for this leg. Indicates whether the shipment is Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL), or parcel.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this leg. Used for special instructions, exception details, or operational remarks that do not fit structured fields.']
  planned_arrival_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned arrival date and time for this leg. Represents the scheduled ETA (Estimated Time of Arrival) at the destination node.']
  planned_departure_datetime timestamp [note: 'Planned departure date and time for this leg. Represents the scheduled ETD (Estimated Time of Departure) from the origin node.']
  planned_transit_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned transit time for this leg in hours. Calculated as the difference between planned arrival and planned departure times. Used for transit time standard compliance and SLA tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that created or last updated this route plan leg record. Examples include SAP TM, Oracle TMS, Blue Yonder, FourKites. Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this leg requires temperature-controlled transport (reefer). True for cold chain shipments requiring refrigeration or climate control.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for this leg. Used for real-time shipment visibility and integration with FourKites tracking platform.']
  trailer_number varchar [note: 'Trailer or semi-trailer identification number for road freight legs. Used for FTL and LTL shipments.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation used for this leg. Indicates whether the leg is executed via air freight, ocean freight, road transport, rail, parcel delivery, or intermodal combination.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this route plan leg record was last modified. Audit field for tracking changes and data quality management.']
  voyage_number varchar [note: 'Voyage or sailing number for ocean freight legs. Identifies the specific vessel voyage carrying the shipment.']

  Note: 'Individual leg detail within an operational route plan, capturing the planned execution of a single corridor leg for a specific shipment or freight order. Captures leg sequence, origin node, destination node, carrier, vehicle/vessel/flight reference, planned departure datetime, planned arrival datetime, planned leg transit hours, actual departure datetime, actual arrival datetime, leg status (planned, in-transit, completed, delayed), and delay reason code. Enables leg-level tracking integration with FourKites and exception management in Oracle TMS.'
}

Table route.eta_event {
  eta_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the ETA/ETD event record. Primary key for the eta_event product.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or Third-Party Logistics (3PL) provider responsible for transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the carrier master data. Used for carrier performance analysis and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance tracking.']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment for which this ETA/ETD event was generated. Links to the shipment master record.']
  destination_location_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location (hub, terminal, warehouse, customer site) for this shipment leg. Links to the location master data. Used for Door-to-Door (D2D) transit time calculation and last-mile delivery zone assignment.']
  driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the driver or operator responsible for transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the workforce master data. Used for driver performance tracking and compliance with Hours of Service (HOS) regulations.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.customs_hold. Business justification: ETA predictions must account for customs holds to maintain accuracy. Required for predictive ETA adjustment when holds occur, customer notification of customs-related delays, and SLA impact analysis.']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the route plan that governs this shipment leg. Links to the planned route configuration from SAP TM or Blue Yonder network design.']
  plan_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.plan_leg. Business justification: eta_event is a real-time and predictive ETA/ETD event record for a shipment leg. It already links to plan (plan_id) at the plan level, but ETA events are inherently leg-specific — each event tracks th']
  primary_eta_location_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node (hub, terminal, warehouse, Container Freight Station (CFS), Inland Container Depot (ICD), or delivery zone) where the shipment was located at the time of this event. Links to the network topology master data.']
  shipment_leg_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific shipment leg or route segment for which this ETA/ETD event applies. A shipment may have multiple legs across different transport modes.']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific vehicle (truck, trailer, aircraft, vessel) transporting the shipment on this leg. Links to the fleet master data. Used for fleet utilization analysis and Global Positioning System (GPS) tracking integration.']
  actual_etd timestamp [note: 'The actual recorded time of departure when the shipment left the origin location, hub, or intermediate node. Captured from GPS, carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), or manual confirmation. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_transit_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual transit time in hours for this shipment leg, calculated from actual Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) to actual Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA). Populated once the leg is completed. Used for Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reporting and continuous improvement.']
  awb_number varchar [note: 'The Air Waybill (AWB) number for air freight shipments. This is the Master Air Waybill (MAWB) or House Air Waybill (HAWB) number issued by the carrier or freight forwarder. Used for tracking and customs clearance.']
  bol_number varchar [note: 'The Bill of Lading (BOL) number for ocean or ground freight shipments. This is the Master Bill of Lading (MBL) or House Bill of Lading (HBL) number issued by the carrier or Non-Vessel Operating Common Carrier (NVOCC). Used for tracking and proof of shipment.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'The unique container number for Full Container Load (FCL) or Less Than Container Load (LCL) ocean freight shipments. Follows International Organization for Standardization (ISO) 6346 standard for container identification. Used for tracking and terminal handling.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ETA/ETD event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  current_predicted_eta timestamp [note: 'The most recent predicted estimated time of arrival based on real-time tracking data, traffic conditions, weather, and predictive algorithms from FourKites or SAP TM. This is the current best estimate of when the shipment will arrive. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  customer_notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether a customer notification (email, SMS, Application Programming Interface (API) push) was sent for this ETA/ETD event. True indicates the customer was notified; False indicates no notification was sent. Used for customer communication audit and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance.']
  customer_notification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the customer notification was sent for this ETA/ETD event. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and Service Level Agreement (SLA) compliance verification.']
  customs_clearance_status varchar [note: 'The current customs clearance status for cross-border shipments. Indicates whether the shipment is pending clearance, cleared, on hold, requires inspection, or has been released by customs authorities. Captured from Descartes Customs or Automated Manifest System (AMS) / Importer Security Filing (ISF) integration.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'A standardized code indicating the root cause of any delay or exception. Examples include weather, traffic, customs hold, mechanical failure, carrier capacity issue, or documentation error. Used for root cause analysis and carrier performance scorecarding.']
  delay_reason_description varchar [note: 'A free-text description providing additional context or details about the delay or exception. Captured from carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) messages, driver notes, or customer service case logs.']
  eta_variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The difference in hours between the original planned ETA and the current predicted ETA. Positive values indicate a delay; negative values indicate the shipment is ahead of schedule. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) monitoring and On-Time Delivery (OTD) / On-Time In-Full (OTIF) Key Performance Indicator (KPI) calculation.']
  etd_variance_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The difference in hours between the original planned ETD and the actual ETD. Positive values indicate a late departure; negative values indicate an early departure. Used for root cause analysis of delays and transit time performance.']
  event_source varchar [note: 'The system or channel that generated this ETA/ETD event. Indicates whether the event originated from FourKites real-time visibility platform, SAP TM route planning, carrier Electronic Data Interchange (EDI), GPS tracking device, manual operator entry, Oracle TMS, or Blue Yonder supply chain planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FourKites|SAP TM|Carrier EDI|GPS|Manual|Oracle TMS|Blue Yonder — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ETA/ETD event was generated or recorded. Represents the moment the prediction or update was made. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'The type of ETA/ETD event being recorded. Distinguishes between estimated time of arrival updates, estimated time of departure updates, predictive ETA calculations, actual ETD confirmations, exception alerts, and on-time confirmations.']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether this ETA/ETD event represents an exception condition requiring attention. True indicates a delay, early arrival, or other deviation from the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or planned schedule. Used to trigger exception management workflows.']
  exception_severity varchar [note: 'The severity level of the exception condition. Critical indicates Service Level Agreement (SLA) breach or customer escalation required; High indicates significant delay; Medium indicates minor delay; Low indicates minimal impact; None indicates no exception.']
  exception_type varchar [note: 'The specific type of exception or status condition associated with this ETA/ETD event. Categorizes the nature of the deviation (delay, early arrival, on-time, weather delay, traffic delay, customs delay, mechanical failure, route deviation, carrier issue). Used for root cause analysis and Key Performance Indicator (KPI) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Delay|Early|On-Time|Weather Delay|Traffic Delay|Customs Delay|Mechanical Failure|Route Deviation|Carrier Issue — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  location_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The geographic latitude coordinate of the shipment or vehicle at the time this ETA/ETD event was generated. Captured from Global Positioning System (GPS) or Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tracking devices. Used for real-time visibility and geofencing.']
  location_longitude decimal(11,7) [note: 'The geographic longitude coordinate of the shipment or vehicle at the time this ETA/ETD event was generated. Captured from Global Positioning System (GPS) or Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tracking devices. Used for real-time visibility and geofencing.']
  original_planned_eta timestamp [note: 'The initial planned estimated time of arrival as defined in the original route plan or shipment booking. This is the baseline ETA against which all subsequent updates and variances are measured. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  original_planned_etd timestamp [note: 'The initial planned estimated time of departure as defined in the original route plan or shipment booking. This is the baseline ETD for the origin or intermediate hub. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  planned_transit_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned transit time in hours for this shipment leg as defined in the route plan or service corridor standard. Used as the baseline for On-Time Delivery (OTD) and transit time variance analysis.']
  pod_status varchar [note: 'The Proof of Delivery (POD) status for this shipment leg. Indicates whether the shipment has not been delivered, has been delivered, delivery was attempted, was refused by the consignee, was damaged, or was lost. Used for Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) integration and claims processing.']
  pod_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when Proof of Delivery (POD) was captured. This is the actual delivery timestamp recorded by the driver or Electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for On-Time Delivery (OTD) calculation and billing.']
  prediction_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A confidence score (0-100) indicating the reliability of the current predicted ETA. Higher scores indicate greater confidence based on historical accuracy, data quality, and real-time tracking signal strength. Generated by FourKites or SAP TM predictive algorithms.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The service level or product type for this shipment. Indicates express parcel delivery, standard service, economy, same-day, next-day, freight forwarding, Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Truckload (FTL), Full Container Load (FCL), or Less Than Container Load (LCL). Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) determination and On-Time Delivery (OTD) measurement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Express|Standard|Economy|Same-Day|Next-Day|Freight|LTL|FTL|FCL|LCL — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) that identifies whether this ETA/ETD event represents a Service Level Agreement (SLA) breach. True indicates the shipment will miss or has missed the committed delivery time; False indicates on-time or early. Used for Key Performance Indicator (KPI) dashboards and customer credits processing.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The committed delivery date and time as defined in the Service Level Agreement (SLA) or customer contract. This is the contractual deadline against which On-Time Delivery (OTD) and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance is measured. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  traffic_condition varchar [note: 'A description of the traffic conditions at the time of the ETA/ETD event or along the route. Captured from traffic Application Programming Interface (API) integration, Global Positioning System (GPS) data, or driver reports. Used for delay analysis and dynamic route optimization.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation for this shipment leg. Indicates whether the leg is transported by air, ocean, road, rail, or multimodal combination. Aligns with International Air Transport Association (IATA) and International Maritime Organization (IMO) standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this ETA/ETD event record was last updated in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'A description of the weather conditions at the time of the ETA/ETD event or along the route. Examples include clear, rain, snow, fog, storm, hurricane. Captured from weather Application Programming Interface (API) integration or driver reports. Used for delay analysis and predictive modeling.']

  Note: 'Real-time and predictive ETA/ETD event record generated by FourKites or SAP TM for a shipment leg or route plan. Captures event timestamp, event source (FourKites, carrier EDI, GPS, manual), shipment reference, leg reference, original planned ETA, current predicted ETA, ETA variance (hours), ETD actual, location at time of event (lat/long, node), prediction confidence score, exception flag, and exception type (delay, early, on-time). SSOT for all ETA updates consumed by customer visibility portals, exception management, and OTD/OTIF KPI calculation.'
}

Table route.hub_spoke_config {
  hub_spoke_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the hub-and-spoke network configuration record. Primary key.']
  network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the central hub facility or terminal that serves as the consolidation and distribution center for this configuration. Links to the facility/node master data.']
  operator_tpl_provider_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.tpl_provider. Business justification: Hub consolidation operations frequently outsourced to 3PL providers. Required for operational accountability, cost allocation, performance tracking, and 3PL contract compliance validation. Essential f']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary carrier or 3PL partner assigned to operate this hub-spoke configuration. Links to carrier master data.']
  spoke_depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.fleet_depot. Business justification: Hub-spoke configurations define which depots serve as spokes (in addition to hub_node_id); critical for fleet deployment planning, asset allocation, and last-mile service design.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the configuration before it becomes active. Ensures governance and sign-off by network planning and operations leadership.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this hub-spoke configuration for operational use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration was approved. Supports audit trail and compliance reporting.']
  carrier_allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'Strategy for allocating carriers to hub-spoke lanes: dedicated carriers per lane, shared carrier pool, dynamic selection via TMS, or contract-based assignment.']
  config_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the hub-spoke configuration, used for external reference and reporting. Typically follows a naming convention combining region, service product, and version.']
  config_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name describing the hub-spoke configuration, typically including geographic scope and service type (e.g., \'APAC Express Hub-Spoke Q1 2024\').']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the hub-spoke configuration. Only active configurations are used for operational route planning and shipment execution.']
  config_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this configuration. Incremented when the configuration is updated, supporting change tracking and audit trails.']
  consolidation_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Daily cutoff time (HH:MM format, hub local time) by which shipments must arrive at the hub to be included in the next consolidation cycle.']
  consolidation_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which shipments are consolidated at the hub before distribution to spokes. Impacts inventory holding time and service level.']
  country_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes covered by this hub-spoke configuration. Defines the geographic boundary for customs and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  customs_clearance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs clearance is required at the hub for cross-border shipments in this configuration. Triggers Descartes Customs integration.']
  direct_lane_bypass_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether direct spoke-to-spoke or spoke-to-destination lanes are allowed when volume/weight thresholds are met, bypassing the hub.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which this hub-spoke configuration expires or is superseded by a new configuration. Null indicates an open-ended configuration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this hub-spoke configuration becomes active and is used for route planning and shipment execution. Supports temporal network design.']
  ftz_zone_applicable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the hub operates within or has access to a Free Trade Zone, enabling duty deferral and trade compliance benefits.']
  hub_capacity_pallets varchar [note: 'Maximum handling capacity of the hub in standard pallet positions. Relevant for LTL, parcel, and e-commerce fulfillment operations.']
  hub_capacity_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum handling capacity of the hub in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU) for containerized freight. Used for capacity planning and network design.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this hub-spoke configuration record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  max_hub_to_spoke_transit_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable transit time in hours from the hub to any spoke node. Critical for On-Time Delivery (OTD) and On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance.']
  max_spoke_to_hub_transit_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable transit time in hours from any spoke node to the hub. Used for SLA validation, route planning, and network design optimization.']
  min_volume_threshold_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum shipment volume in cubic meters (CBM) required to bypass the hub and establish a direct lane from spoke to destination. Used for load optimization and cost efficiency.']
  min_weight_threshold_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Minimum shipment weight in kilograms required to qualify for direct lane bypass, avoiding hub consolidation. Supports FTL and FCL optimization logic.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system that last modified this hub-spoke configuration record. Supports accountability and audit trails.']
  network_design_model_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the Blue Yonder network design model or optimization run that generated or validated this hub-spoke configuration.']
  network_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market area covered by this hub-spoke configuration (e.g., \'North America\', \'APAC\', \'EU West\'). Used for regional network planning and optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this hub-spoke configuration, including special handling instructions, seasonal adjustments, or operational constraints.']
  optimization_objective varchar [note: 'Primary objective used by Blue Yonder and SAP TM for network optimization: minimize cost, maximize speed, maximize reliability, or balanced multi-objective.']
  predictive_eta_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether predictive ETA calculation using FourKites or Blue Yonder is enabled for this configuration, supporting proactive exception management.']
  real_time_visibility_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FourKites real-time visibility tracking is enabled for shipments routed through this hub-spoke configuration.']
  service_product_scope varchar [note: 'The service product line or business segment this hub-spoke configuration applies to. Determines operational rules, SLA targets, and routing logic.']
  sla_target_otd_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On-Time Delivery percentage for shipments routed through this hub-spoke configuration. Used for performance monitoring and carrier evaluation.']
  sla_target_otif_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target On-Time In-Full percentage for shipments routed through this hub-spoke configuration. Measures both timeliness and completeness of delivery.']
  spoke_count varchar [note: 'Total number of spoke nodes included in this hub-spoke configuration. Used for network complexity analysis and capacity planning.']
  spoke_nodes_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of spoke node codes or identifiers that are served by this hub. Represents the delivery/pickup points connected to the hub in this configuration.']
  sustainability_target_co2e_kg_per_shipment decimal(10,3) [note: 'Target carbon emissions in CO2e kilograms per shipment for this hub-spoke configuration. Supports CORSIA and EU ETS compliance and sustainability reporting.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation used for hub-to-spoke and spoke-to-hub movements in this configuration. Aligns with freight forwarding mode definitions.']

  Note: 'Configuration record defining the hub-and-spoke topology for a specific network region or service product. Captures hub node, spoke nodes list, service product scope (express, freight, e-commerce), transport mode, maximum spoke-to-hub transit time, consolidation frequency, minimum volume threshold for direct lane bypass, effective date range, and configuration status. Managed in SAP TM and Blue Yonder for network design. Distinct from service_corridor (which defines named multi-leg paths) — this defines the structural hub-spoke relationship.'
}

Table route.lane_performance {
  lane_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each lane-period performance measurement record in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Primary key for this operational measurement entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (transport service provider) whose performance is being measured on this lane for the defined period. Supports carrier scorecard and selection analytics.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Performance metrics must be measured against specific service-level commitments (express vs. standard SLAs differ). Required for service-level SLA compliance reporting, carrier scorecard generation, a']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination lane master record that this performance measurement covers. Links to the route domain lane master.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Lane performance metrics inform rate card adjustments. Rate_card_id links performance to pricing decisions, enabling performance-based rate optimization, rate card effectiveness analysis, and dynamic ']
  transit_time_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.transit_time_standard. Business justification: lane_performance captures actual vs planned transit performance for a lane over a measurement period. Linking lane_performance to transit_time_standard via transit_time_standard_id enables direct comp']
  actual_transit_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The measured average actual transit time in hours for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from FourKites actuals and Oracle TMS freight audit data. Compared against planned_transit_hours to derive delay.']
  avg_delay_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average number of hours by which shipments on this lane exceeded the planned transit time during the measurement period. Positive values indicate late delivery; negative values indicate early delivery. Key input for SLA breach analysis.']
  avg_dim_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average dimensional (volumetric) weight in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. DIM weight is used by carriers to price low-density shipments. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit.']
  avg_freight_cost decimal(14,2) [note: 'Average freight cost per shipment on this lane during the measurement period, in the reporting currency. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit. Used for cost-per-lane benchmarking and network optimization analysis.']
  avg_shipment_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Average actual gross weight in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. Used for load optimization, carrier rate benchmarking, and CO2e intensity calculations.']
  carrier_invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of carrier invoices for shipments on this lane that matched the contracted rate without dispute during the measurement period. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit. Supports carrier financial performance evaluation.']
  carrier_performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite carrier performance score for this lane-period, typically on a 0–100 scale, derived from OTD rate, OTIF rate, exception rate, and transit time adherence. Primary input for carrier scorecard and SAP TM carrier selection decisions.']
  co2e_per_shipment_kg decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average CO2e emissions in kilograms per shipment on this lane during the measurement period. Calculated from fuel consumption, transport mode, and distance. Supports EU ETS, CORSIA, and GHG sustainability reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the avg_freight_cost and total_freight_cost monetary fields on this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_clearance_delay_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average hours of delay attributable to customs clearance processes for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from Descartes Customs. Used for cross-border lane performance root cause analysis.']
  damage_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane that incurred cargo damage during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal. Used for carrier liability assessment, insurance claims analysis, and lane risk scoring.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the lane destination. Used for cross-border compliance, customs reporting, and geographic performance segmentation.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the destination hub, port, airport, or facility of the lane (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE). Identifies the arrival point for transit time and OTD measurement.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'Total number of shipment exceptions (delays, damages, misroutes, customs holds, etc.) recorded on this lane during the measurement period. Sourced from FourKites exception management. Triggers network optimization review.']
  exception_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane that incurred at least one exception during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal (exception_count / shipment_count). Used for lane risk scoring and network optimization triggers.']
  folio_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the primary operational system of record from which actuals for this lane-period measurement were sourced (FourKites, Oracle TMS, SAP TM, or manual entry). Supports data lineage and audit traceability.']
  foukites_tracking_coverage_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane that had active FourKites real-time GPS/IoT tracking during the measurement period. Low coverage rates may indicate data quality issues in actuals-based metrics.']
  last_mile_delay_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average hours of delay attributable specifically to the last-mile delivery segment for shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Enables root cause decomposition of total delay into linehaul vs. last-mile components.']
  measurement_period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date of the performance measurement window for this lane-period record. Combined with measurement_period_start_date defines the exact reporting interval.']
  measurement_period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date of the performance measurement window for this lane-period record. Combined with measurement_period_end_date defines the exact reporting interval (e.g., weekly, monthly).']
  measurement_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this lane-period performance measurement record. Draft = initial load; Confirmed = validated by freight audit; Published = available for scorecard and SLA reporting; Superseded = replaced by a corrected record.']
  network_optimization_trigger boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lane-period measurement has triggered a network optimization review in Blue Yonder based on SLA breach, high exception rate, or cost threshold exceedance. True = optimization review triggered.']
  on_time_shipment_count varchar [note: 'Number of shipments on this lane that were delivered on or before the planned delivery date/time during the measurement period. Numerator for OTD rate calculation.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the lane origin. Used for cross-border compliance, customs reporting, and geographic performance segmentation.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the origin hub, port, airport, or facility of the lane (e.g., IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE). Identifies the departure point for transit time and OTD measurement.']
  otd_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane delivered on or before the planned delivery date/time during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9500 = 95%). Core KPI for SLA monitoring and carrier scorecard.']
  otif_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane delivered both on time and in full (no quantity shortfall) during the measurement period, expressed as a decimal. Composite SLA metric used for customer contract compliance and carrier evaluation.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the measurement window for this lane-period record. Determines the aggregation cadence used for SLA monitoring and carrier scorecard reporting.']
  planned_transit_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The contracted or standard planned transit time in hours for this lane as defined in the SLA or service corridor standard. Sourced from SAP TM route planning master data. Baseline for OTD and delay calculations.']
  pod_completion_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane for which an electronic Proof of Delivery (ePOD) was captured and confirmed during the measurement period. Supports compliance with customer SLA documentation requirements.']
