  predictive_eta_accuracy_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Proportion of shipments on this lane for which FourKites\' predictive ETA was within the agreed tolerance window of the actual delivery time during the measurement period. Measures the reliability of the real-time visibility platform\'s ETA predictions.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lane-period performance measurement record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and incremental processing.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lane-period performance measurement record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Used for change tracking, SLA audit, and incremental ETL processing.']
  shipment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of shipments executed on this lane during the measurement period. Denominator for OTD, OTIF, and exception rate calculations. Sourced from Oracle TMS shipment execution records.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lane\'s OTD rate fell below the contracted SLA threshold (sla_threshold_otd) during the measurement period. True = SLA breached; False = SLA met. Triggers escalation and network optimization review workflows.']
  sla_threshold_otd decimal(5,4) [note: 'The contracted minimum OTD rate threshold for this lane as defined in the applicable SLA or carrier agreement, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.9500 = 95%). Used to determine SLA compliance status for the period.']
  source_system_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native record identifier from the operational source system (FourKites or Oracle TMS) from which this lane-period measurement was extracted. Enables back-tracing to the system of record for audit and reconciliation.']
  total_co2e_kg decimal(14,4) [note: 'Total CO2e emissions in kilograms for all shipments on this lane during the measurement period. Aggregate of co2e_per_shipment_kg × shipment_count. Used for sustainability reporting and carbon offset calculations.']
  total_freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total freight cost for all shipments on this lane during the measurement period, in the reporting currency. Sourced from Oracle TMS freight audit and SAP S/4HANA Finance. Used for P&L lane-level cost analysis.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this lane (air, ocean, road, rail, or multimodal). Aligns with IATA for air, IMO for ocean, and DOT for road/rail classifications.']

  Note: 'Operational performance record for a lane over a defined measurement period, capturing actual vs. planned transit time, OTD rate, OTIF rate, average delay hours, shipment volume, exception count, carrier performance score, and CO2e per shipment for that lane. Sourced from FourKites actuals and Oracle TMS freight audit data. Used for lane-level SLA monitoring, carrier scorecard inputs, and network optimization trigger analysis. This is an operational measurement record, not an analytics aggregate — each row represents a lane-period measurement.'
}

Table route.carrier_schedule {
  carrier_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the carrier schedule record in the Transport Shipping lakehouse Silver layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Carrier schedules (vessel/flight rotations) operate under specific carrier agreements defining rates, capacity, and service terms. Essential for schedule-to-contract reconciliation, rate validation, a']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier (shipping line, airline, road carrier, or rail operator) that operates this schedule. Used by SAP TM for carrier selection and Oracle TMS for freight audit.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Carrier schedules represent specific service offerings (express, economy, etc.). Link enables service-level performance tracking, rate application, and SLA validation against contracted service commit']
  destination_node_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node (port, airport, hub, depot, ICD, CFS) at which this schedule arrives. Used for ETA/ETD management and last-mile delivery zone assignment.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the origin-destination lane master record that this schedule serves. Links the schedule to the route domain lane definition for load optimization and carrier selection in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  primary_carrier_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network node (port, airport, hub, depot, ICD, CFS) from which this schedule departs. Supports hub-and-spoke topology and FourKites real-time visibility integration.']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Carrier schedules reference tariffs for published rates. Tariff_id links schedule to pricing, enabling schedule-based tariff rate lookup, carrier schedule costing, and published rate validation agains']
  alliance_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the ocean carrier alliance (e.g., \'2M\', \'THE Alliance\', \'Ocean Alliance\') or air cargo alliance to which this schedule belongs. Used for slot-sharing and co-loading capacity management in Blue Yonder and Oracle TMS.']
  arrival_days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO weekday numbers (1=Monday through 7=Sunday) on which this schedule arrives at the destination node. May differ from departure days for multi-day transits. Used for ETA calculation and last-mile delivery zone scheduling.']
  arrival_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled local arrival time at the destination node in HH:mm (24-hour) format. Combined with arrival_days_of_week to determine the Estimated Time of Arrival (ETA) for delivery planning and SLA entitlement matching.']
  arrival_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the destination node (e.g., \'Europe/Rotterdam\', \'Asia/Tokyo\'). Required to convert local arrival time to UTC for cross-border ETA management and FourKites real-time visibility integration.']
  capacity_teu decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum container capacity of this schedule expressed in Twenty-foot Equivalent Units (TEU). Applicable for ocean FCL/LCL services. Null for non-ocean modes. Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS and capacity planning in Blue Yonder.']
  capacity_volume_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity in Cubic Meters (CBM) for this schedule. Used alongside DIM Weight (Dimensional Weight) calculations for air freight and LTL/LCL load optimization in Oracle TMS.']
  capacity_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum payload weight capacity in kilograms for this schedule. Applicable across all transport modes (air, ocean, road, rail). Used for load optimization in Oracle TMS and freight order management in SAP TM.']
  co2e_emission_factor decimal(10,4) [note: 'Carrier-published or calculated Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) emission factor for this schedule in kg CO2e per tonne-km. Used for GHG (Greenhouse Gas) reporting, EU ETS compliance, CORSIA carbon offsetting calculations, and sustainability analytics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier schedule record was first ingested into the Transport Shipping lakehouse Silver layer. Audit trail field for data lineage and SOX compliance.']
  cutoff_hours_before_departure decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cargo receipt deadline expressed as the number of hours before scheduled departure by which cargo must be tendered to the carrier at the origin node. Used by SAP TM for freight order management and Oracle TMS for shipment execution planning.']
  dangerous_goods_accepted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier schedule accepts dangerous goods (DG) shipments (True) or not (False). Drives compliance routing in Descartes Customs and shipment eligibility checks against IMDG Code (ocean) and ICAO Technical Instructions (air).']
  departure_days_of_week varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO weekday numbers (1=Monday through 7=Sunday) on which this schedule departs (e.g., \'1,3,5\' for Mon/Wed/Fri). Used by SAP TM for departure planning and Blue Yonder for network optimization.']
  departure_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled local departure time at the origin node in HH:mm (24-hour) format. Combined with departure_days_of_week to determine the Estimated Time of Departure (ETD) for shipment planning in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  departure_timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the origin node (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Asia/Shanghai\'). Required to convert local departure time to UTC for cross-border schedule coordination and FourKites predictive ETA calculation.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the destination node (e.g., \'GBR\', \'SGP\', \'BRA\'). Used for import customs declaration routing in Descartes Customs, denied party screening, and WTO trade compliance.']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail destinations or IATA 3-letter airport code for air destinations. Denormalised from the destination network node for EDI messaging, customs filing, and carrier schedule matching.']
  doc_cutoff_hours_before_departure decimal(5,2) [note: 'Deadline for submission of shipping documents (AWB, BOL, customs declarations) expressed as hours before scheduled departure. Required for Descartes Customs AMS/ISF filing compliance and carrier EDI submission.']
  edi_source_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the EDI feed or carrier data exchange channel from which this schedule record was sourced (e.g., EDIFACT IFTMCS, ANSI X12 204, carrier API). Used for data lineage, reconciliation, and Silver layer ingestion audit in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Calendar date from which this carrier schedule is valid and available for booking. Used for schedule versioning, seasonal service management, and SAP TM departure planning.']
  effective_until_date date [note: 'Calendar date after which this carrier schedule is no longer valid. Null indicates an open-ended schedule with no defined end date. Used for schedule lifecycle management and carrier contract alignment.']
  frequency_per_week varchar [note: 'Number of departures per week for this schedule on the specified lane. Used by Blue Yonder for supply chain planning, capacity allocation, and network design optimization.']
  fuel_type varchar [note: 'Primary fuel type used by the vessel, aircraft, or vehicle operating this schedule. Used for BAF (Bunker Adjustment Factor) surcharge calculation, GHG emissions reporting, and EU ETS compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: VLSFO|HFO|MGO|LNG|SAF|DIESEL|ELECTRIC|HYBRID — promote to reference product]']
  is_direct_service boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule operates as a direct (non-stop) service between origin and destination nodes (True) or involves intermediate port calls / transshipment stops (False). Used for transit time accuracy and customer SLA commitments.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the origin node (e.g., \'CHN\', \'USA\', \'DEU\'). Used for trade compliance routing in Descartes Customs, C-TPAT/AEO screening, and cross-border regulatory reporting.']
  origin_port_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE (5-character) for ocean/road/rail origins or IATA 3-letter airport code for air origins. Denormalised from the origin network node for EDI messaging, AMS filing, and carrier schedule matching without requiring a node lookup.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the carrier published or last updated this schedule via EDI feed. Represents the principal business event time for this record. Used for schedule freshness validation and EDI reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.']
  ref varchar [note: 'Carrier\'s own internal reference or booking system identifier for this schedule, as received via EDI. Enables round-trip reconciliation with the carrier\'s system and supports freight audit in Oracle TMS.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known carrier schedule reference number or code as published by the carrier via EDI feed. Used as the business identifier for matching against carrier EDI messages (e.g., EDIFACT IFTMCS) and for reconciliation in SAP TM and Oracle TMS.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier schedule record. ACTIVE: available for booking and departure planning. SUSPENDED: temporarily unavailable (e.g., port congestion, force majeure). CANCELLED: permanently discontinued. DRAFT: pending carrier confirmation. EXPIRED: past effective_until_date.']
  schedule_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number for this carrier schedule record, incremented each time the carrier publishes an updated schedule via EDI. Supports schedule change management and historical comparison in the Silver layer.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule provides temperature-controlled (reefer/cold chain) capacity (True) or ambient-only service (False). Used for perishable cargo eligibility and specialized equipment allocation in SAP TM.']
  transit_time_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard published transit time in hours from origin node departure to destination node arrival for this schedule. Used as the baseline for SLA (Service Level Agreement) commitments, OTD (On-Time Delivery) KPI measurement, and Blue Yonder network optimization.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transport for this carrier schedule. Drives carrier selection logic in SAP TM, load optimization in Oracle TMS, and customs declaration routing in Descartes Customs.']
  transshipment_port_code varchar [note: 'UN/LOCODE of the primary transshipment hub for indirect services (is_direct_service = False). Used for multi-leg ETA calculation, customs transit declaration in Descartes Customs, and FourKites milestone tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this carrier schedule record was last modified in the Transport Shipping lakehouse Silver layer. Used for incremental processing, change data capture, and audit trail maintenance.']
  vgm_cutoff_hours_before_departure decimal(5,2) [note: 'Deadline for submission of Verified Gross Mass (VGM) declaration expressed as hours before scheduled departure. Mandatory for ocean shipments under SOLAS Amendment 2016. Null for non-ocean modes.']
  voyage_rotation_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-assigned voyage or rotation number for ocean services, or equivalent rotation identifier for air/rail. Used for Bill of Lading (BOL) and Master Bill of Lading (MBL) cross-referencing and port authority reporting.']

  Note: 'Published carrier schedule for a specific lane or corridor leg, capturing carrier reference, service name, origin node, destination node, transport mode, vessel/flight/train service identifier, departure day(s) of week, departure time, arrival day(s) of week, arrival time, frequency, transit time (hours), capacity (TEU/kg/CBM), cut-off time (cargo receipt deadline), effective date range, and schedule status. Sourced from carrier EDI feeds and used by SAP TM for departure planning and Oracle TMS for load optimization.'
}

  consignee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.consignee. Business justification: Last-mile dispatch delivers to consignees (end recipients). The recipient_name and recipient_contact_phone should be normalized to consignee_id FK referencing consignee.consignee_id. This enables leve']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignment being dispatched for final delivery. Links to the shipment or parcel being delivered.']
  delivery_driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: Last-mile delivery agent assignment requires driver profile lookup for license validation, HOS compliance verification, performance tracking, and route authorization. Existing delivery_agent_employee_']
  dispatch_run_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.dispatch_run. Business justification: A dispatch_run is the operational route/courier trip that groups multiple last-mile dispatches. last_mile_dispatch assigns a single consignment to a driver, while dispatch_run represents the full sche']
  fulfillment_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_zone. Business justification: Last-mile dispatch assignments are zone-based. Adding delivery_zone_id FK enables proper zone-based routing, SLA tracking, and delivery agent assignment. The delivery address fields remain (execution-']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that this dispatch is executing. Links to the e-commerce or D2D order.']
  hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customs.customs_hold. Business justification: Last-mile dispatch is blocked when customs holds exist. Enables delivery agents to understand delay root cause, provide accurate customer updates, and reschedule delivery attempts based on expected cu']
  origin_network_node_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.network_node. Business justification: Dispatches originate from specific network nodes (depots, hubs). Enables dispatch origin tracking, node-level performance analysis, and depot capacity planning. Standard in last-mile operations manage']
  plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.plan. Business justification: Last-mile dispatches execute the final delivery leg of multi-leg route plans. Linking enables end-to-end plan execution tracking, actual-vs-planned variance analysis, and closed-loop feedback for rout']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Last-mile dispatches require rate card linkage for delivery zone pricing application, COD fee calculation, accessorial charge determination, and driver settlement in final-mile delivery operations.']
  shipment_leg_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to shipment.shipment_leg. Business justification: Last-mile dispatch executes a specific shipment leg. Operations teams link dispatch events to shipment legs for SLA breach attribution, delay root-cause analysis, and carrier performance reporting. A ']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Last-mile dispatch operations assign specific fleet vehicles to delivery routes. Dispatch planning, vehicle utilization tracking, and operational reporting require authoritative link to fleet.transpor']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the consignment was successfully delivered to the recipient. Null if delivery not yet completed. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  actual_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the delivery agent picked up the consignment from the depot or hub. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  cod_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cash on Delivery amount to be collected from the recipient at the time of delivery. Null if not a COD shipment.']
  cod_collected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the COD amount was successfully collected from the recipient. True if collected, False if not collected or not applicable.']
  cod_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the COD amount. Format: ISO 4217.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispatch record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the delivery destination address. Typically contains street number and street name.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the delivery destination address. May contain apartment, suite, or building information.']
  delivery_attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential count of delivery attempts for this consignment. Increments with each failed delivery attempt. First attempt = 1.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the delivery destination address.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the delivery destination. Format: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3.']
  delivery_failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for delivery failure. Null if delivery was successful. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recipient_unavailable|address_incorrect|access_denied|refused_by_recipient|damaged_package|weather_delay|vehicle_breakdown — promote to reference product]']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the customer or shipper for the delivery. May include gate codes, safe drop locations, or contact preferences.']
  delivery_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  delivery_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for GPS tracking and route optimization.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the delivery destination address.']
  delivery_route_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the planned delivery route or zone for this dispatch. Used for route optimization and territory management.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the delivery destination address.']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the consignment was dispatched for last-mile delivery. Primary business event date for this transaction.']
  dispatch_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique dispatch reference number used for tracking and communication. Format: DSP-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  dispatch_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential position of this dispatch within the delivery route. Determines the order in which deliveries should be attempted.']
  dispatch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch. Tracks progression from dispatch through delivery or return. Values: dispatched (assigned to driver), in_transit (en route to delivery area), out_for_delivery (active delivery attempt), delivered (successfully completed), failed (delivery unsuccessful), returned_to_depot (consignment returned).']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the consignment was dispatched from the depot or hub for final delivery. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  epod_photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a photo was captured as proof of delivery. True if photo captured, False otherwise.']
  epod_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether an electronic signature was captured as proof of delivery. True if signature captured, False otherwise.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this dispatch record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  planned_delivery_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the planned delivery time window communicated to the customer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  planned_delivery_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the planned delivery time window communicated to the customer. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  return_to_depot_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consignment was returned to the depot after a failed delivery attempt. Null if not returned. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery missed the SLA target delivery timestamp. True if breached, False if met or not yet determined.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The committed delivery date and time per the service level agreement. Used to measure on-time delivery performance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that created this dispatch record. Primary source is Oracle TMS last-mile dispatch module.']

  Note: 'Last-mile delivery dispatch record assigning a consignment to a delivery agent/driver for final delivery execution. Captures dispatch date, delivery agent employee ID, vehicle registration, delivery route code, dispatch sequence number within the route, planned delivery time window, actual pickup time from depot, delivery attempt number, dispatch status (dispatched/in_transit/delivered/failed/returned), and return-to-depot timestamp if undelivered. Sourced from Oracle TMS last-mile dispatch module. Enables D2D delivery performance tracking and driver productivity measurement.'
}

  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the fulfillment order, used for tracking and customer communication.']

[ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: received|validated|allocated|picking|packed|manifested|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|cancelled|returned — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fulfillment order by business channel and fulfillment mode.']
  original_order_reference varchar [note: 'For return or exchange orders, the reference to the original fulfillment order being returned or exchanged.']
  packed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the order was packed and ready for dispatch.']
  picked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when warehouse picking was completed for this order.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'The date promised to the customer for delivery, forming the basis of the Service Level Agreement (SLA).']
  received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the fulfillment order was first received into the system from the merchant or customer platform.']
  return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this order is a return merchandise authorization (RMA) order (True = return, False = forward shipment).']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'The return merchandise authorization number issued for return orders, linking back to the original order.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'The delivery service level selected for this order, determining speed and handling priority.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the order delivery breached the promised SLA window (True = breach occurred, False = SLA met).']
  sla_window_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The end of the promised delivery time window for SLA compliance tracking.']
  sla_window_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The start of the promised delivery time window for SLA compliance tracking.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'The technical channel through which the order was received (EDI, API, web portal, mobile app, manual entry).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the upstream system of record that originated this order (WMS, TMS, merchant platform).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing any special handling requirements for this order (fragile, temperature-controlled, signature required, etc.).']
  total_item_quantity varchar [note: 'The total number of individual items (SKUs) included in this fulfillment order across all order lines.']
  total_package_count varchar [note: 'The total number of packages or parcels created for this order during the packing process.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total volumetric measurement of the order in cubic meters (CBM), used for dimensional weight calculation.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total gross weight of the order in kilograms, including packaging materials.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned tracking number for shipment visibility and customer tracking.']
  validated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the order passed validation checks (inventory availability, address verification, payment authorization).']
  value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total declared value of goods in this fulfillment order, used for insurance and customs purposes.']
  value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order value amount.']

  Note: 'Core master entity representing an e-commerce or D2D fulfillment order received from a merchant or customer platform. Captures the full order lifecycle from receipt through completion, including order source channel (EDI/API), merchant reference, service type, fulfillment mode, order status, promised SLA window, COD flag and amount, special handling instructions, and omnichannel integration metadata. This is the SSOT for fulfillment order identity within the fulfillment domain — distinct from freight forwarding orders owned by the freight domain.'
}

  order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the individual order line item within the fulfillment system. Primary key for the order line entity.']

Business justification: Order line items need HS classification for automated customs documentation generation. Enables duty calculation, restricted goods screening, and customs declaration pre-population from order data, cr']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: Order line items are packed into parcels during the pack stage. The carton_id and tracking_number strings should be normalized to parcel_id FK referencing parcel.parcel_id. This enables tracking which']
  back_order_expected_date date [note: 'Expected date when back-ordered inventory will be available for fulfillment. Used for customer communication and inventory planning.']
  back_order_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is on back order due to insufficient inventory. True if the item will be fulfilled in a subsequent shipment.']
  bin_location varchar [note: 'Specific bin, shelf, or slot location within the warehouse where the SKU is stored. Used by WMS for pick path optimization and inventory accuracy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order line record was first created in the fulfillment system. Used for audit trail and order lifecycle analysis.']
  dangerous_goods_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item contains dangerous goods or hazardous materials requiring special handling, labeling, and carrier compliance per IMDG or IATA regulations.']
  declared_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared monetary value of the line item for insurance, customs declaration, and liability purposes. Used for cargo insurance and claims processing.']
  declared_value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the declared value. Supports multi-currency e-commerce fulfillment operations.']
  dimension_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for length, width, and height dimensions. Common values include CM (centimeter), IN (inch), M (meter), FT (foot).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration or best-before date for perishable or time-sensitive products. Used for FEFO (First Expired First Out) picking strategies and quality control.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Customer-provided gift message to be included with this line item. Used for personalized fulfillment in e-commerce gift orders.']
  gift_wrap_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether gift wrapping service was requested for this line item. Triggers special handling instructions during packing operations.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this order line record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data synchronization across systems.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the fulfillment order. Determines the ordering and processing sequence of line items within a multi-line order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of the order line. Tracks progression through pick-pack-ship workflow and supports partial fulfillment scenarios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled|returned|back_ordered — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number for the picked inventory. Critical for traceability, recalls, and FIFO/LIFO inventory management.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity of the SKU ordered by the customer or merchant. Represents the target fulfillment quantity before any adjustments or substitutions.']
  original_sku varchar [note: 'The originally ordered SKU if this line represents a substitution. Null if no substitution occurred. Used for customer communication and substitution analytics.']
  pack_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was packed and prepared for shipment. Used for pack productivity analysis and order cycle time measurement.']
  packed_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Quantity successfully packed and prepared for shipment. May differ from picked quantity due to quality control rejections or packing errors.']
  pick_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was picked from warehouse inventory. Used for pick productivity analysis and SLA tracking.']
  picked_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Actual quantity picked from warehouse inventory for this line. May differ from ordered quantity due to stock availability, damage, or operational constraints.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the product being fulfilled on this line. Used for pick-pack documentation and customer communication.']
  quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the ordered quantity. Common values include EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), LT (liter), GL (gallon). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PK|BX|PL|KG|LB|LT|GL — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item has been returned by the customer. True if an RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) was processed for this line.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for return. Values include DAMAGED, WRONG_ITEM, NOT_NEEDED, DEFECTIVE, SIZE_ISSUE, LATE_DELIVERY. Used for returns analytics and quality improvement.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized inventory items. Enables unit-level tracking for high-value goods, electronics, and regulated products.']
  ship_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the line item was dispatched from the fulfillment center. Used for carrier handoff tracking and delivery ETA calculation.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Final quantity dispatched to the customer. Represents the actual fulfilled quantity for billing and delivery confirmation purposes.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier representing the specific item to be fulfilled. Used for inventory lookup, pick instructions, and product master reference.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the upstream system or channel that originated this order line (e.g., WMS, OMS, e-commerce platform). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_handling_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating special handling requirements for this line item. Values include FRAGILE, HAZMAT (hazardous materials), REFRIGERATED, HIGH_VALUE, OVERSIZED, or NONE.']
  substitution_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a substitution for an out-of-stock SKU. True if a substitute product was picked instead of the originally ordered SKU.']
  un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying dangerous goods classification. Required for hazmat shipping documentation and carrier acceptance.']
  unit_height decimal(10,2) [note: 'Height dimension of a single unit of the SKU. Used for dimensional weight calculations and warehouse slotting optimization.']
  unit_length decimal(10,2) [note: 'Length dimension of a single unit of the SKU. Used for dimensional weight (DIM weight) calculations and packaging optimization.']
  unit_weight decimal(10,3) [note: 'Weight of a single unit of the SKU. Used for dimensional weight calculations, carrier selection, and freight cost estimation.']
  unit_width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width dimension of a single unit of the SKU. Used for dimensional weight calculations and carton selection during packing.']
  warehouse_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the warehouse or fulfillment center location where this line item is being processed. Used for inventory allocation and pick routing.']
  weight_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for unit weight. Common values include KG (kilogram), LB (pound), G (gram), OZ (ounce).']

  Note: 'Individual line item within a fulfillment order representing a specific SKU, quantity, unit weight, dimensions, declared value, and fulfillment instruction. Tracks per-line status (picked, packed, shipped, returned), substitution flags, and back-order indicators. Supports multi-line order orchestration and partial fulfillment scenarios common in e-commerce fulfillment operations.'
}

Table fulfillment.parcel {
  parcel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the parcel. Primary key for the parcel entity.']
  billing_invoice_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to billing.invoice_line. Business justification: Each parcel generates specific invoice line items for weight-based charges, dimensional weight surcharges, and accessorial fees. Real business process: parcel-level freight audit, billing reconciliati']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Parcels are physical units tendered to carriers. Direct FK enables parcel-level carrier performance analysis, invoice reconciliation, service level compliance tracking. Business process: carrier billi']
  consolidation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to freight.consolidation. Business justification: In LCL and express consolidation operations, individual parcels are grouped into a consolidation for transport. Parcel must reference its consolidation for deconsolidation planning, freight charge all']
  fulfillment_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_zone. Business justification: Parcels are routed to specific delivery zones based on destination address. Adding delivery_zone_id FK enables zone-based routing, carrier selection, and SLA management. Destination address fields rem']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent fulfillment order that this parcel is part of. Links parcel to the order orchestration workflow.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Individual parcels are routed via specific lanes for carrier assignment and transit time calculation. Critical for parcel routing optimization, carrier selection algorithms, and delivery promise calcu']
  packaging_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Tracks packaging material supplier for each parcel. Critical for quality control, cost analysis, and supplier performance evaluation in logistics operations. Enables root cause analysis when packaging']
  parcel_manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel_manifest. Business justification: Parcels are grouped into carrier manifests for scheduled dispatch. Adding parcel_manifest_id FK is critical for tracking which manifest a parcel belongs to, enabling manifest reconciliation, carrier h']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Parcels need rate card linkage for chargeable weight calculation using dim weight rules, service-level pricing application, and carrier billing reconciliation in multi-carrier fulfillment operations.']
  rate_schedule_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.rate_schedule. Business justification: Parcels are rated against specific carrier rate schedules from agreements. Billing audit, rate verification, and dispute resolution require direct schedule reference to validate applied rates match co']
  tariff_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.tariff. Business justification: Parcels are rated against published tariffs for carrier billing reconciliation. Business process: tariff-based rating validation ensures billed rates match published tariff schedules for audit complia']
  actual_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Actual physical weight of the parcel measured in kilograms. Used for carrier rating and freight charge calculation.']
  carrier_service_code varchar [note: 'Specific service level selected for parcel delivery (e.g., express, standard, economy, next_day, two_day). Determines transit time and service level agreement (SLA).']
  carrier_service_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the carrier service (e.g., FedEx Priority Overnight, UPS Ground, DHL Express Worldwide).']
  chargeable_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'The greater of actual weight or dimensional weight, used for carrier billing and freight charge calculation.']
  cod_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount to be collected from the recipient at delivery. Applicable only when cod_flag is true.']
  cod_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the COD amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cod_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this parcel requires cash on delivery (COD) collection at the time of delivery.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parcel record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  declared_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared value of the parcel contents for insurance and liability purposes. Used to determine carrier liability limits and insurance premiums.']
  declared_value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the declared value amount.']
  destination_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the delivery address (street address, building number). Used for last-mile routing and electronic proof of delivery (ePOD).']
  destination_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the delivery address (apartment, suite, floor). Optional field for additional address details.']
  destination_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the delivery address. Used for geographic routing and service area determination.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the delivery destination (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for customs clearance and cross-border compliance.']
  destination_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Latitude coordinate of the delivery address. Used for GPS-based route optimization and real-time tracking.']
  destination_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Longitude coordinate of the delivery address. Used for GPS-based route optimization and real-time tracking.']
  destination_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the delivery address. Critical for carrier routing and service level determination.']
  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the delivery address. Used for tax calculation and regulatory compliance.']
  dimensional_weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Dimensional weight (DIM weight) calculated based on parcel volume and carrier-specific DIM factor. Used for freight rating when DIM weight exceeds actual weight.']
  hazmat_class varchar [note: 'UN hazard class for dangerous goods (e.g., 3 for flammable liquids, 9 for miscellaneous). Applicable only when hazmat_flag is true.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the parcel contains hazardous materials (dangerous goods) requiring special handling and compliance with IMDG Code or ICAO Technical Instructions.']
  hazmat_un_number varchar [note: 'Four-digit UN number identifying the dangerous goods substance (e.g., UN1203 for gasoline). Required for hazmat compliance and carrier acceptance.']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the parcel in centimeters. Used to calculate cubic meter (CBM) and dimensional weight (DIM weight).']
  insurance_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether additional cargo insurance is required for this parcel beyond standard carrier liability.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parcel record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  length_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Length dimension of the parcel in centimeters. Used to calculate cubic meter (CBM) and dimensional weight (DIM weight).']
  manifest_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parcel was manifested and handed over to the carrier. Used for carrier billing and service level agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  origin_facility_code varchar [note: 'Code of the warehouse or fulfillment center where the parcel was packed and dispatched. Used for operational reporting and network optimization.']
  pack_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the parcel was packed and sealed at the fulfillment center. Marks the completion of the pack stage in the pick-pack-ship workflow.']
  packaging_material varchar [note: 'Primary material used in the packaging. Relevant for sustainability reporting and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions tracking.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Type of packaging material used for the parcel (e.g., box, envelope, poly bag). Influences handling requirements and carrier service eligibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: box|envelope|poly_bag|tube|pallet|crate|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  parcel_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the parcel in the pick-pack-ship and last-mile delivery workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: packed|manifested|dispatched|in_transit|out_for_delivery|delivered|failed_delivery|returned|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  recipient_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the recipient for delivery notifications and electronic proof of delivery (ePOD) transmission.']
  recipient_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the recipient for delivery coordination and failed delivery notifications.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the person or organization receiving the parcel. Used for delivery verification and electronic proof of delivery (ePOD).']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique seal or tamper-evident identifier applied to the parcel for security and customs compliance. Required for high-value or regulated shipments.']
  signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a recipient signature is required for proof of delivery (POD). Used for high-value or regulated shipments.']
  sla_target_delivery_date date [note: 'Target delivery date committed to the customer based on the selected carrier service and service level agreement (SLA).']
  sla_target_delivery_time_window_end timestamp [note: 'End of the committed delivery time window for time-definite services (e.g., 12:00 PM for morning delivery).']
  sla_target_delivery_time_window_start timestamp [note: 'Start of the committed delivery time window for time-definite services (e.g., 9:00 AM for morning delivery).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that created this parcel record (e.g., Manhattan WMS, SAP TM). Used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements (e.g., fragile, keep upright, temperature-sensitive, signature required).']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Unique barcode or tracking number assigned to the parcel for end-to-end visibility and proof of delivery (POD). Used by carriers and customers to track parcel movement.']
  volume_cbm decimal(10,6) [note: 'Total volume of the parcel in cubic meters (CBM). Calculated as length × width × height. Used for warehouse space planning and carrier capacity management.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the parcel in centimeters. Used to calculate cubic meter (CBM) and dimensional weight (DIM weight).']

  Note: 'Physical parcel or package created during the pack stage of pick-pack-ship workflow. Captures parcel dimensions (CBM), actual weight, DIM weight, packaging type, barcode/tracking number, seal number, hazmat flag (IMDG/ICAO compliance), and assigned carrier service. Each parcel is linked to one or more order lines and is the unit of last-mile dispatch. Distinct from freight containers managed in the freight domain.'
}

  carrier_iata_code varchar [note: 'Two or three-character airline designator code assigned by IATA for air freight carriers.']

  cod_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the total COD amount.']
  contains_hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this manifest contains any parcels with hazardous materials requiring special handling and regulatory compliance.']

  destination_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the destination zone.']

  discrepancy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting any discrepancies found during reconciliation (e.g., missing parcels, weight variance, damaged items).']
  dispatch_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when the manifest is to be dispatched to the carrier.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the manifest was handed over to the carrier. Principal business event timestamp for this transaction.']
  edi_transmission_status varchar [note: 'Status of EDI manifest transmission to the carrier system. Tracks whether manifest data has been successfully communicated via EDI 214, 315, or API.']
  edi_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manifest was transmitted to the carrier via EDI or API.']
  estimated_arrival_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Predicted date and time when the manifest will arrive at the destination zone or carrier hub. May be updated in real-time via carrier tracking systems.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this manifest record was last modified.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique manifest number assigned to this carrier dispatch grouping. Used for carrier handover documentation and freight audit reconciliation.']
  manifest_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the manifest. Open: accepting parcels; Closed: no further additions; Dispatched: handed to carrier; In Transit: en route; Delivered: completed; Reconciled: audit complete; Cancelled: voided. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|closed|dispatched|in_transit|delivered|reconciled|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  manifest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manifest based on its operational purpose. Outbound: customer deliveries; Inbound: returns; Transfer: inter-facility; Return: RMA; Cross-dock: direct transfer.']
  planned_departure_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the vehicle or carrier to depart from the origin facility.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of freight audit reconciliation comparing manifest details with carrier billing and delivery confirmations. Discrepancy indicates count or weight mismatch.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manifest reconciliation process was completed.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the delivery route or service lane assigned to this manifest for network optimization and performance tracking.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Unique security seal number applied to the container or vehicle for tamper-evidence and customs compliance.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that created this manifest record (e.g., WMS, TMS, OMS).']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Instructions for carrier regarding special handling requirements for this manifest (e.g., temperature control, fragile, hazardous materials, high-value).']
  total_chargeable_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total chargeable weight used for billing, calculated as the greater of actual weight or volumetric weight.']
  total_cod_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of all COD collection amounts for parcels in this manifest. Carrier is responsible for collecting and remitting these funds.']
  total_declared_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of declared values of all parcels in the manifest. Used for insurance coverage and liability determination.']
  total_parcel_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual parcels included in this manifest. Used for carrier handover verification and freight audit.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total cubic volume of all parcels in the manifest, measured in cubic meters. Used for load planning and carrier capacity allocation.']
  total_volumetric_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total dimensional weight (DIM weight) of all parcels calculated using carrier-specific volumetric divisor. Used for freight rating when volumetric weight exceeds actual weight.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total actual weight of all parcels in the manifest, measured in kilograms. Used for carrier billing and capacity planning.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this manifest.']

  Note: 'Carrier manifest grouping parcels for a scheduled dispatch run to a specific carrier, service lane, or last-mile zone. Records manifest number, carrier SCAC/IATA code, dispatch date-time, origin facility, destination zone, total parcel count, total weight, total CBM, manifest status (open, closed, dispatched, reconciled), and EDI transmission status to carrier. Supports carrier handover and freight audit processes.'
}

Table fulfillment.delivery_attempt {
  delivery_attempt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each physical delivery attempt. Primary key for the delivery attempt record.']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent consignment being delivered. Links this attempt to the shipment package.']
  delivery_driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: Each delivery attempt is performed by a driver whose profile (license validity, certifications, performance rating, HOS status) must be validated for operational compliance and audit trail. Existing d']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the e-commerce fulfillment order driving this delivery. Links attempt to the original order.']
  last_mile_dispatch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the last-mile dispatch run that included this delivery attempt. Links to the route execution.']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: A delivery attempt is fundamentally the act of delivering a specific physical parcel to the consignee. delivery_attempt already links to fulfillment_order and last_mile_dispatch, but has no direct FK ']
  route_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.route_delivery_zone. Business justification: Delivery attempts occur within specific delivery zones for performance tracking. Enables zone-level delivery performance analysis, failure rate tracking, and zone-based service improvement initiatives']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: delivery_attempt stores vehicle_registration_number as a denormalized string. A FK to fleet.transport_asset enables per-vehicle delivery performance reporting, incident correlation with fleet records,']
  access_code varchar [note: 'Building or gate access code provided to enable delivery agent entry to restricted premises. Confidential information for delivery execution.']
  attempt_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which this delivery attempt occurred. Used for daily delivery performance reporting.']
  attempt_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS latitude coordinate captured at the moment of delivery attempt. Used for geolocation verification and route optimization analysis.']
  attempt_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'GPS longitude coordinate captured at the moment of delivery attempt. Used for geolocation verification and route optimization analysis.']
  attempt_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of this delivery attempt for the consignment (1 for first attempt, 2 for second, etc.). Used to track re-delivery attempts.']
  attempt_outcome_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the result of the delivery attempt. Core field for OTD and OTIF KPI calculation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: delivered|failed|refused|no_access|redirected|damaged|address_incorrect — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  attempt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the delivery agent arrived at the delivery location and initiated the attempt. Primary business event timestamp for SLA calculation.']
  cod_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount to be collected from the recipient at delivery. Null if this is not a COD shipment.']
  cod_collected_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the COD payment was successfully collected from the recipient. True if collected, false if not collected or not applicable.']
  cod_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the COD amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Null if not a COD shipment.']
  cod_payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used by recipient to pay the COD amount (cash, credit card, mobile payment, check). Null if COD was not collected.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery attempt record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line where the delivery attempt was made. May differ from consignment address if redirected.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (apartment, suite, building) where the delivery attempt was made.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the delivery attempt was made.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code of the delivery location (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the consignee or shipper for this delivery (e.g., leave at door, call on arrival, deliver to reception). Guides delivery agent behavior.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the delivery location. Used for geographic analysis and route optimization.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the delivery attempt was made.']
  depot_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the depot or distribution center from which this delivery attempt was dispatched. Used for operational reporting and network analysis.']
  epod_photo_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a photo of the delivered parcel was captured at the delivery location. True if photo was taken, false otherwise.']
  epod_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number linking to the electronic proof of delivery record (signature, photo, barcode scan). Null if delivery was not completed.']
  epod_signature_captured_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a digital signature was captured from the recipient at delivery. True if signature was obtained, false otherwise.']
  failure_reason_code varchar [note: 'Detailed reason code explaining why a delivery attempt failed (e.g., recipient_not_home, business_closed, access_denied, incorrect_address). Null if attempt was successful. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: recipient_not_home|business_closed|access_denied|incorrect_address|security_restriction|weather_delay|vehicle_breakdown — promote to reference product]']
  failure_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about the delivery failure. Captured by delivery agent for customer service follow-up.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this delivery attempt record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  next_action_instruction varchar [note: 'Instruction code indicating the next step to be taken following this attempt (e.g., schedule re-attempt, hold at depot, return to sender). Drives workflow automation.']
  reattempt_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date on which the next delivery re-attempt is scheduled. Null if no re-attempt is planned or if delivery was successful.']
  recipient_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the person who received the parcel or was contacted during the delivery attempt. Used for delivery confirmation and follow-up.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person who received the parcel or who was present at the delivery location during the attempt. May differ from consignee if alternate recipient accepted delivery.']
  recipient_relationship varchar [note: 'Relationship of the actual recipient to the consignee (e.g., consignee themselves, family member, neighbor, building security). Used for delivery authorization validation.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this delivery attempt resulted in an SLA breach (delivery not completed by target time). True if SLA was breached, false otherwise. Critical for customer service and penalty tracking.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Contractual target date and time by which delivery must be completed to meet SLA commitments. Used for OTIF and OTD KPI calculation.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that captured this delivery attempt record (e.g., mobile delivery app, handheld scanner, TMS). Used for data lineage and quality tracking.']
  time_at_location_minutes varchar [note: 'Duration in minutes that the delivery agent spent at the delivery location during this attempt. Used for productivity analysis and route optimization.']
  weather_condition_code varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of delivery attempt. Used to analyze weather impact on delivery success rates and SLA performance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|storm|extreme_heat|extreme_cold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Records each physical attempt to deliver a parcel to the consignee address. Captures attempt sequence number, attempt date-time, delivery agent ID, GPS coordinates at attempt, outcome code (delivered, failed, refused, no-access, redirected), failure reason, next-action instruction (re-attempt, hold at facility, return to sender), and ePOD reference. Central to last-mile SLA tracking and OTD/OTIF KPI computation.'
}

Table fulfillment.fulfillment_carrier_assignment {
  fulfillment_carrier_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier assignment record. Primary key for this entity.']
  carrier_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.carrier_agreement. Business justification: Carrier selection must reference specific agreement for automated rate lookup and contract compliance validation. Essential for TMS rate shopping, capacity allocation, and ensuring assignments comply ']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the selected carrier organization responsible for transporting this fulfillment order or parcel. Links to the carrier master data.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Carrier assignments select specific services (not just carriers). Business process: rate shopping, service level selection, transit time commitment, carrier service performance tracking. Removes denor']
  consignment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the consignment or parcel shipment unit for which the carrier is assigned. May be null if assignment is at order level rather than parcel level.']
  contract_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.contract_rate. Business justification: Carrier assignments under contract agreements directly reference specific contract rates for pricing execution, volume commitment tracking, and contracted service-level enforcement during carrier sele']
  fulfillment_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fulfillment order for which the carrier is being assigned. Links this assignment to the parent order being fulfilled.']
  lane_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to route.lane. Business justification: Carrier assignment decisions require lane-specific routing rules, capacity allocations, and contracted service levels. The assignment engine evaluates carrier performance and availability per lane to ']
  parcel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel. Business justification: fulfillment_carrier_assignment records carrier selection for a fulfillment order OR a specific parcel. The product description explicitly states \'for a fulfillment order or parcel\'. Currently it has f']
  parcel_manifest_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parcel manifest or carrier manifest document that includes this carrier assignment, used for batch handover and carrier reconciliation. May be null if not yet manifested.']
  rate_card_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.rate_card. Business justification: Carrier assignments validate quoted rates against applicable rate cards during carrier selection optimization, ensuring pricing compliance and enabling margin analysis for multi-carrier routing decisi']
  sla_entitlement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.sla_entitlement. Business justification: Carrier selection must satisfy the customer\'s contracted SLA entitlement (transit time guarantee, delivery window). fulfillment_carrier_assignment carries sla_compliant_flag and sla_target_delivery_ti']
  assignment_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable unique identifier for this carrier assignment, used for tracking and reference in operational systems and customer communications.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the carrier assignment indicating whether the assignment is pending confirmation, confirmed and active, dispatched to carrier, cancelled, or failed.']
  assignment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the carrier was assigned to this fulfillment order or parcel, representing the moment the selection decision was made.']
  cancellation_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for cancellation of the carrier assignment (e.g., order_cancelled, carrier_rejected, capacity_unavailable, customer_change, address_correction). Populated only when assignment is cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: order_cancelled|carrier_rejected|capacity_unavailable|customer_change|address_correction|service_downgrade|cost_optimization — promote to reference product]']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this carrier assignment was cancelled. Null if the assignment has not been cancelled.']
  carrier_account_number varchar [note: 'The specific carrier account number or contract identifier under which this shipment is being tendered, used for billing reconciliation and rate application.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the carrier assignment was confirmed, either by the carrier accepting the tender or by system validation. May be null if not yet confirmed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this carrier assignment record was first created in the data platform, used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  estimated_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time of arrival (ETA) when the carrier is expected to deliver the shipment to the destination, used for SLA tracking and customer communication.']
  estimated_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The estimated date and time when the carrier is expected to pick up the shipment from the fulfillment center or origin location.']
  estimated_transit_time_hours decimal(8,2) [note: 'The estimated transit time in hours from pickup to delivery as quoted by the carrier or calculated by the TMS at the time of assignment.']
  integration_reference_code varchar [note: 'External reference identifier from the source system or integration layer, used to trace this record back to the originating transaction in SAP TM, Oracle TMS, or other operational systems.']
  label_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the shipping label has been generated for this carrier assignment, signaling readiness for physical dispatch.']
  label_generated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shipping label was generated for this carrier assignment. Null if label has not yet been generated.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this carrier assignment record was most recently updated in the data platform, used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  master_tracking_number varchar [note: 'The master tracking number or Master Air Waybill (MAWB) / Master Bill of Lading (MBL) number when this shipment is part of a consolidated load. May be null for direct shipments.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this carrier assignment was a manual override of the system-recommended carrier selection, requiring additional justification and audit.']
  override_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for manual override of the carrier selection (e.g., customer_request, capacity_constraint, service_failure, cost_exception, regulatory_requirement). Populated only when override_flag is true. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|capacity_constraint|service_failure|cost_exception|regulatory_requirement|quality_issue|strategic_partnership — promote to reference product]']
  override_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context and justification for the manual override of carrier selection. Populated only when override_flag is true.']
  quoted_rate_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The freight rate amount quoted by the carrier for this shipment at the time of assignment, used for cost estimation and freight audit reconciliation.']
  quoted_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the quoted rate amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  selection_method varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the carrier was selected automatically by the Transportation Management System (TMS) optimization engine, manually by an operator, or as a manual override of the system recommendation.']
  selection_priority_score decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numerical score calculated by the carrier selection algorithm representing the priority or ranking of this carrier for this shipment, used for optimization and decision audit.']
  selection_rule_applied varchar [note: 'The specific carrier selection rule or optimization criterion that was applied to choose this carrier (e.g., lowest_cost, fastest_transit, sla_compliant, preferred_carrier, customer_specified, zone_based).']
  sla_compliant_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the selected carrier and service level are expected to meet the SLA target delivery timestamp based on estimated transit time.']
  sla_target_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The contractual or committed delivery timestamp per the Service Level Agreement (SLA) with the customer, used to determine SLA compliance and breach detection.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record that created this carrier assignment (e.g., SAP_TM, ORACLE_TMS, MANHATTAN_WMS), used for data lineage and integration troubleshooting.']
  tender_status varchar [note: 'Status of the carrier tender process indicating whether the shipment has been tendered to the carrier and the carriers response (not tendered, tendered, accepted, rejected).']
  tender_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the shipment was tendered to the carrier via EDI, API, or manual process. Null if not yet tendered.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The unique tracking number or waybill number assigned by the carrier for this shipment, used for shipment visibility and proof of delivery (POD) tracking.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Primary mode of transportation for this carrier assignment (air, road, rail, ocean, multimodal).']

  Note: 'Records the carrier selection decision for a fulfillment order or parcel, capturing the selected carrier, service level (express, standard, economy), carrier account number, rate quoted, selection rule applied (cheapest, fastest, SLA-compliant), assignment timestamp, and override flag with override reason. Supports carrier selection logic driven by SAP TM and Oracle TMS and enables freight audit reconciliation.'
}

Table fulfillment.dispatch_run {
  dispatch_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dispatch run. Primary key for this entity representing a scheduled last-mile delivery route or courier trip.']
  carrier_buy_rate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to pricing.carrier_buy_rate. Business justification: Dispatch runs require carrier buy rate linkage for cost allocation per route, profitability analysis, carrier settlement reconciliation, and margin tracking in last-mile delivery operations.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier. Business justification: Dispatch runs are executed by carriers (or internal fleet). Business process: carrier performance by run, carrier payment reconciliation, carrier capacity utilization tracking. Removes denormalized SC']
  courier_driver_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.driver_profile. Business justification: Courier assignment for last-mile dispatch runs requires driver profile validation including license type, HOS compliance status, performance rating, and certification eligibility (hazmat, temperature-']
  fulfillment_delivery_zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.delivery_zone. Business justification: Dispatch runs operate within specific delivery zones. The service_area_code STRING should be normalized to FK referencing delivery_zone.fulfillment_delivery_zone_id. This enables proper zone-based rou']
  origin_depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.depot. Business justification: Dispatch runs originate from a fleet depot; origin_depot_code is a denormalized string on dispatch_run. A proper FK to fleet.depot enables depot-level dispatch volume reporting, capacity planning, and']
  parcel_manifest_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fulfillment.parcel_manifest. Business justification: A dispatch_run (operational driver route) is associated with a parcel_manifest (carrier manifest grouping parcels for a scheduled dispatch). The parcel_manifest defines what parcels are loaded onto th']
  transport_asset_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.transport_asset. Business justification: Dispatch run planning assigns specific fleet vehicles to courier routes. Fleet utilization analysis, vehicle maintenance scheduling coordination, and operational cost allocation require authoritative ']
  trip_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.trip. Business justification: A last-mile dispatch run IS a fleet trip. Linking dispatch_run to fleet.trip enables reconciliation of delivery performance against fleet actuals (fuel consumed, distance, emissions, cost) — essential']
  actual_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'The actual total distance traveled in kilometers during this dispatch run, captured from GPS or vehicle telematics.']
  actual_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The actual total duration in minutes for this dispatch run, calculated from dispatch timestamp to return timestamp.']
  actual_stop_count varchar [note: 'The actual number of delivery stops completed during this dispatch run, which may differ from planned due to failed deliveries or route changes.']
  cod_collection_count varchar [note: 'The number of Cash on Delivery transactions collected during this dispatch run.']
  cod_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for COD collections on this dispatch run.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispatch run record was first created in the system.']
  dispatch_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text operational notes or special instructions for this dispatch run, used for communication between dispatch coordinators and couriers.']
  dispatch_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the dispatch run departed from the origin depot or facility, marking the start of the last-mile delivery route.']
  dispatch_type varchar [note: 'The type or service level of this dispatch run, indicating the delivery speed and priority classification.']
  edi_transmission_status varchar [note: 'The status of EDI manifest transmission to the carrier system, indicating whether the dispatch run manifest has been successfully communicated.']
  edi_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the EDI manifest was transmitted to the carrier system for this dispatch run.']
  epod_capture_count varchar [note: 'The number of electronic proof of delivery records captured during this dispatch run, including signatures and photos.']
  failed_delivery_count varchar [note: 'The number of parcels that could not be delivered during this dispatch run due to recipient unavailability, address issues, or other delivery exceptions.']
  hazmat_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this dispatch run includes hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this dispatch run record was most recently updated in the system.']
  manifest_number varchar [note: 'The manifest document number generated for carrier handover, listing all parcels loaded onto this dispatch run for last-mile delivery.']
  manifest_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The status of manifest reconciliation indicating whether all parcels loaded have been accounted for through delivery, return, or exception processing.']
  planned_distance_km decimal(10,2) [note: 'The planned total distance in kilometers for this dispatch run based on route optimization.']
  planned_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'The planned total duration in minutes for this dispatch run based on route optimization and historical performance.']
  planned_stop_count varchar [note: 'The number of delivery stops planned for this dispatch run based on route optimization and order allocation.']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the manifest reconciliation was completed for this dispatch run.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The actual date and time when the dispatch run returned to the origin depot or facility, marking the completion of the last-mile delivery route.']
  returned_parcel_count varchar [note: 'The number of parcels returned to the depot at the end of this dispatch run due to failed delivery attempts or customer refusal.']
  route_code varchar [note: 'The predefined route code or zone identifier for this dispatch run, used for route planning and optimization.']
  run_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for this dispatch run, representing the business day on which the last-mile delivery route is planned to execute.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or manifest number for the dispatch run, used for operational tracking and carrier handover documentation.']
  run_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the dispatch run indicating its operational state in the last-mile delivery workflow.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this dispatch run breached its SLA target completion time.']
