Business justification: Blocked space agreements are typically tied to specific carrier services (e.g., weekly Asia-Europe ocean service, daily air freight service). This link enables service-level capacity management and re']
  renewed_blocked_space_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on blocked_space_agreement (renewed_blocked_space_agreement_id)']
  trade_lane_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific trade lane (origin-destination corridor) covered by this blocked space agreement, such as Asia-Europe or Trans-Pacific.']
  agreement_reference_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known business identifier or contract number for this blocked space agreement, used in communications with the carrier and internal stakeholders.']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the blocked space agreement by transport mode and service type. Ocean FCL (Full Container Load), Ocean LCL (Less Than Container Load), Air Freight, or Multimodal.']
  allocation_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which the guaranteed capacity is allocated or refreshed (e.g., weekly allocation of 100 TEU, monthly allocation of 50,000 kg).']
  base_rate_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'The base freight rate per unit of capacity (per TEU, per kg, per CBM) agreed in this blocked space agreement. Subject to surcharges and adjustments.']
  blocked_space_agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the blocked space agreement. Draft indicates under negotiation; Active indicates in force; Suspended indicates temporarily paused; Expired indicates past end date; Terminated indicates cancelled before expiry; Pending Renewal indicates approaching expiry with renewal in progress.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Primary unit of measure used to express the guaranteed capacity in this agreement: TEU for ocean containers, kg or CBM for air freight, or pallets for specialized services.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user who created this blocked space agreement record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this blocked space agreement record was first created in the system.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or port cluster to which the blocked space capacity is destined (e.g., Mediterranean, Middle East, US East Coast).']
  dispute_resolution_mechanism varchar [note: 'The agreed mechanism for resolving disputes arising from this blocked space agreement: Arbitration, Litigation, Mediation, or Negotiation.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this blocked space agreement becomes binding and capacity reservations take effect.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this blocked space agreement grants Transport Shipping exclusive access to the specified capacity on the service. True if exclusive; False if shared with other parties.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this blocked space agreement ceases to be in force. Nullable for open-ended agreements subject to notice periods.']
  force_majeure_clause varchar [note: 'Summary of the force majeure provisions that excuse performance under extraordinary circumstances (e.g., natural disasters, war, strikes, pandemics).']
  governing_law varchar [note: 'The jurisdiction and legal framework governing this blocked space agreement (e.g., English Law, New York Law, Singapore Law).']
  guaranteed_capacity_cbm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total volume capacity guaranteed to Transport Shipping measured in cubic meters. Applicable to both ocean LCL and air freight agreements.']
  guaranteed_capacity_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total weight capacity guaranteed to Transport Shipping measured in kilograms for air freight agreements. Null for ocean agreements.']
  guaranteed_capacity_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total capacity guaranteed to Transport Shipping measured in TEU (Twenty-foot Equivalent Units) for ocean agreements. Null for air freight agreements.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Identifier or name of the user who last modified this blocked space agreement record in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this blocked space agreement record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_utilization_commitment_absolute decimal(12,2) [note: 'The minimum absolute quantity of capacity (in the agreement\'s unit of measure) that Transport Shipping commits to utilize. Alternative or complementary to percentage-based commitment.']
  minimum_utilization_commitment_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of guaranteed capacity that Transport Shipping commits to utilize over the agreement period. Failure to meet this threshold may trigger penalties.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or operational instructions related to this blocked space agreement.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or port cluster from which the blocked space capacity originates (e.g., Far East, North Europe, US West Coast).']
  penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed or maximum monetary penalty amount for under-utilization, if applicable. Null if penalty is variable or non-monetary.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  penalty_for_underutilization varchar [note: 'Description of the financial or operational penalty imposed if Transport Shipping fails to meet the minimum utilization commitment (e.g., payment for unused capacity, rate adjustment, loss of priority).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The booking priority or service level that Transport Shipping receives under this blocked space agreement relative to other shippers (e.g., guaranteed space, preferred allocation, standard queue).']
  rate_basis varchar [note: 'Description of the pricing or rate structure associated with the blocked space (e.g., fixed rate per TEU, discounted rate schedule, spot rate with floor). Distinct from the rate schedule itself which may be stored separately.']
  rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the base rate (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to expiry that renewal notice must be provided by the party holding the renewal option or to prevent automatic renewal.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'The renewal mechanism for this blocked space agreement: Automatic renewal unless notice given, Mutual consent required, Shipper (Transport Shipping) holds renewal option, Carrier holds renewal option, or No renewal (one-time agreement).']
  rollover_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused capacity from one allocation period can be rolled over to the next period. True if rollover is permitted; False otherwise.']
  rollover_limit_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage of unused capacity that can be rolled over to the next allocation period, if rollover is allowed. Null if no limit or rollover not allowed.']
  signed_by_carrier varchar [note: 'Name and title of the carrier authorized signatory who executed this agreement.']
  signed_by_transport_shipping varchar [note: 'Name and title of the Transport Shipping authorized signatory who executed this agreement.']
  signed_date date [note: 'The date on which this blocked space agreement was formally executed by both Transport Shipping and the carrier.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate this blocked space agreement prior to its natural expiry.']

  Note: 'Master record for blocked space agreements (BSAs) between Transport Shipping and ocean or air carriers guaranteeing a fixed volume of capacity on specific services or trade lanes. Captures BSA reference, carrier, trade lane, transport mode (air/ocean), service or vessel/flight, guaranteed capacity (TEU, kg, CBM), minimum utilization commitment, penalty for under-utilization, rate basis, effective and expiry dates, and BSA status. Distinct from interline agreements (which govern revenue proration) — BSAs govern capacity reservation and utilization obligations.'
}

Table network.carrier_service {
  carrier_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier service product. Primary key.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier partner offering this service. Links to the carrier profile master data.']
  connecting_carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on carrier_service (connecting_carrier_service_id)']
  carbon_neutral_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is certified as carbon-neutral through offsets or sustainable fuel programs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this carrier service record was first created in the system.']
  customs_clearance_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether customs brokerage and clearance services are included in this service offering.']
  cutoff_day_offset varchar [note: 'The number of days before departure that the cutoff time applies (0 = same day, 1 = one day before, etc.).']
  cutoff_time_local varchar [note: 'The local time by which cargo must be delivered to the carrier facility to be accepted for this service (format: HH:MM).']
  dangerous_goods_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is certified to handle dangerous goods (DG) or hazardous materials (HAZMAT).']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'The specific destination location code (IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE port code, or city code) where the service ends.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or country code where this service terminates. May be a specific port, city, or broader region code.']
  dg_classes_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of UN dangerous goods classes supported by this service (e.g., 1, 2.1, 3, 4.1, 5.1, 6.1, 8, 9).']
  documentation_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'The local time by which shipping documentation (AWB, BOL, customs declarations) must be submitted (format: HH:MM).']
  door_to_door_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service includes door-to-door pickup and delivery, or is port-to-port / terminal-to-terminal only.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this service definition becomes valid and available for booking.']
  equipment_types_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of equipment types supported by this service (e.g., 20GP, 40HC, 45HC for ocean; ULD types for air; truck types for road).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this service definition expires or is no longer available for booking. Null for open-ended services.']
  flight_service_identifier varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned flight number or air service identifier (for air freight).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'The operational frequency of this service: daily, weekly, biweekly (fortnightly), monthly, or on-demand.']
  frequency_per_week varchar [note: 'The number of service departures per week. Used for capacity planning and booking availability.']
  incoterms_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Incoterms (International Commercial Terms) supported by this service (e.g., FOB, CIF, DDP, DAP, EXW).']
  insurance_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether cargo insurance is available or included with this service.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this carrier service record was last modified.']
  live_animal_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service is certified to transport live animals.']
  max_volume_capacity_cbm decimal(15,3) [note: 'The maximum volume capacity in cubic meters (CBM) that this service can handle per shipment or booking.']
  max_weight_capacity_kg decimal(15,2) [note: 'The maximum weight capacity in kilograms that this service can handle per shipment or booking.']
  origin_location_code varchar [note: 'The specific origin location code (IATA airport code, UN/LOCODE port code, or city code) where the service begins.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or country code where this service originates. May be a specific port, city, or broader region code.']
  oversized_cargo_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service can handle oversized or out-of-gauge cargo that exceeds standard equipment dimensions.']
  perishable_cargo_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service supports perishable cargo requiring time-sensitive handling.']
  service_code varchar [note: 'The unique alphanumeric code assigned by the carrier to identify this service product. Used in booking and rating systems.']
  service_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the service offering, including unique features, routing details, and value propositions.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The quality tier of the service offering: premium (highest priority, fastest), standard (regular service), economy (cost-optimized), or basic (minimal service).']
  service_name varchar [note: 'The commercial name of the carrier service product as marketed by the carrier (e.g., FedEx Priority Overnight, Maersk AE7 Asia-Europe Express).']
  service_status varchar [note: 'The current operational status of this carrier service: active (available for booking), suspended (temporarily unavailable), discontinued (permanently ended), seasonal (operates during specific periods), or planned (future service).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service delivery model: direct (non-stop), transshipment (hub-and-spoke), feeder (connecting service), express (time-definite), economy (cost-optimized), or standard (regular service).']
  temperature_controlled_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this service supports temperature-controlled (reefer) cargo.']
  tracking_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time or milestone-based shipment tracking is available for this service.']
  tracking_granularity varchar [note: 'The level of tracking detail provided: real-time (GPS/IoT), milestone (event-based), daily (batch updates), or none.']
  train_service_identifier varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned train service number or rail service identifier (for rail freight).']
  transit_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard transit time in days from origin to destination for this service, excluding customs clearance and last-mile delivery.']
  transit_time_hours varchar [note: 'The standard transit time in hours for express or time-critical services where hour-level precision is required.']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'The primary mode of transportation for this service (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal, courier).']
  vessel_service_identifier varchar [note: 'The carrier-assigned identifier for the vessel service string (for ocean freight) or vessel rotation code.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the specific transport services offered by a carrier partner, representing the named service products available for booking. Captures carrier reference, service name, service code, transport mode, service type (direct, transshipment, feeder, express, economy), origin and destination regions, transit time, frequency (daily, weekly, fortnightly), vessel/flight/train service identifier, cut-off times, and service status. Enables service-level carrier selection and route planning. Distinct from route.carrier_schedule (which captures published timetables) — this is the service product definition.'
}

Table network.carrier_contact {
  carrier_contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the carrier contact record. Primary key.']
  carrier_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier or network partner organization this contact represents.']
  reports_to_carrier_contact_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on carrier_contact (reports_to_carrier_contact_id)']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the contact indicating whether they are actively available for communication and liaison activities.']
  contact_first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person.']
  contact_full_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the individual contact at the carrier or partner organization.']
  contact_last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contact role indicating their primary area of responsibility and liaison function with Transport Shipping. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: commercial|operations|claims|documentation|it_edi|technical|customer_service — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this carrier contact record was first created in the system.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'External identifier linking this contact to their record in the Salesforce CRM system for unified relationship management.']
  department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area within the carrier organization where the contact works (e.g., Operations, Commercial, Claims, IT).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact ceased to be active or available for liaison, if applicable. Null indicates the contact is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contact became active and available for liaison with Transport Shipping.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for contacting this individual at the carrier or partner organization.']
  emergency_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact should be reached for emergency or critical operational issues requiring immediate attention.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Numeric indicator of the contact\'s position in the escalation hierarchy, where lower numbers indicate first-line contacts and higher numbers indicate senior management (e.g., 1=frontline, 2=supervisor, 3=manager, 4=director, 5=executive).']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the contact, used for document transmission where required by legacy processes or regulatory requirements.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact within the carrier or partner organization.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred language for communication with this contact, specified as ISO 639-1 two-letter language code (e.g., EN, ES, FR, DE, ZH).']
  last_contact_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent communication or interaction with this contact.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this carrier contact record was most recently updated.']
  mobile_number varchar [note: 'Mobile or cellular phone number for the contact, used for urgent or after-hours communication.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional information about the contact, such as special instructions, availability constraints, or relationship history.']
  office_location varchar [note: 'Physical office location or branch where the contact is based (e.g., city, country, or facility name).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for reaching the contact, including country and area codes.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred method of communication for routine business interactions.']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for their contact type at this carrier or partner organization.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Time zone where the contact is located, specified in IANA time zone database format (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London, Asia/Singapore).']

  Note: 'Master record for key contacts at carrier and network partner organizations, capturing the individuals responsible for commercial, operational, and technical liaison with Transport Shipping. Captures carrier or partner reference, contact full name, job title, department, contact type (commercial, operations, claims, documentation, IT/EDI), email, phone, preferred communication channel, and active status. Enables relationship management and escalation routing across the partner network.'
}

Table network.network_event {
  network_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the network event record. Primary key.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the agent or correspondent office affected by this network event. References the agent master entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the carrier affected by this network event. References the carrier master entity.']
  carrier_service_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.carrier_service. Business justification: Network events often affect specific carrier services (e.g., flight delays, vessel schedule changes, service suspensions). This link enables service-level event tracking and impact analysis. Nullable ']
  depot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to fleet.depot. Business justification: Network disruption events (port closures, carrier strikes, weather events) directly affect specific depots, triggering rerouting, asset redeployment, and customer notifications. Operations centers nee']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to network.partner. Business justification: network_event currently stores partner_name as a denormalized STRING column but has no FK to the partner master record. Adding partner_id normalizes this relationship — the partner\'s legal_name, trade']
  affected_airports varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of IATA airport codes affected by this network event (e.g., JFK, PVG, AMS).']
  affected_countries varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes for countries impacted by this network event.']
  affected_ports varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of port codes or names affected by this network event (e.g., USNYC, CNSHA, NLRTM).']
  affected_regions varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic regions impacted by this network event (e.g., APAC, EMEA, Americas).']
  affected_trade_lanes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of trade lanes or shipping routes impacted by this network event (e.g., Asia-Europe, Trans-Pacific, North Atlantic).']
  affected_transport_modes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of transport modes impacted by this event (e.g., air, ocean, road, rail).']
  alternative_carrier_engaged boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alternative carriers have been engaged to provide substitute capacity during this network event.']
  alternative_routing_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether alternative routing options are available to bypass the affected network segment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this network event record was first created in the system.']
  customer_notification_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when customer notifications were sent regarding this network event.']
  customer_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether affected customers have been notified about this network event and its impact on their shipments.']
  end_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network disruption or event was resolved and operations returned to normal. Null if event is ongoing.']
  escalation_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this network event was escalated to higher management levels.']
  escalation_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this network event requires escalation to senior management or executive leadership.']
  estimated_capacity_loss_teu decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated loss of container capacity measured in TEU resulting from this network event.']
  estimated_delay_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated average delay in hours to shipments affected by this network event.']
  estimated_shipments_affected varchar [note: 'Estimated number of active shipments impacted by this network event.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code assigned to this network event for external reference and tracking.']
  event_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the network event, including the nature of the disruption, root cause, and contextual information.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the network event indicating the stage of resolution and operational response.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network event was first detected or reported in the operational system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the network event indicating the nature of the disruption or change affecting the partner network. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: carrier_service_suspension|capacity_disruption|port_congestion|strike_action|natural_disaster|regulatory_change|partner_status_change — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  financial_impact_estimated_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of this network event in USD, including revenue loss, additional costs, and penalties.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this network event record was last updated or modified.']
  lessons_learned varchar [note: 'Documentation of lessons learned and recommendations for preventing similar network events in the future.']
  mitigation_actions_taken varchar [note: 'Detailed description of mitigation actions and contingency measures implemented to minimize the impact of this network event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes and comments related to this network event for internal reference and collaboration.']
  operational_impact_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the operational impact on service delivery, capacity, transit times, and customer commitments resulting from this network event.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to this network event for resource allocation and response planning.']
  regulatory_authority_name varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory authority or governing body notified about this network event (e.g., IMO, IATA, FMC, DOT).']
  regulatory_authority_notified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether relevant regulatory authorities have been notified about this network event as required by compliance obligations.']
  resolution_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of how the network event was resolved and operations were restored to normal.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the root cause of this network event for trend analysis and risk management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: weather|labor_dispute|equipment_failure|port_congestion|regulatory|security|pandemic|geopolitical|infrastructure|other — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  root_cause_details varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of the root cause analysis findings for this network event.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity level of the network event indicating the magnitude of operational impact on the partner network and service delivery.']
  sla_breach_count varchar [note: 'Number of SLA breaches resulting from this network event across affected customer contracts.']
  start_datetime timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the network disruption or event began affecting operations.']

  Note: 'Transactional event log capturing significant operational events affecting the partner network — including carrier service suspensions, capacity disruptions, port congestion alerts, strike actions, natural disaster impacts, regulatory changes affecting trade lanes, and partner status changes. Captures event type, affected partner or carrier, affected trade lanes or regions, event severity, start and end datetime, operational impact description, mitigation actions taken, and resolution status. Enables proactive network disruption management and partner communication.'
}

Table network.partner_onboarding {
  partner_onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner onboarding record. Primary key for tracking the end-to-end onboarding lifecycle of a network partner from initial qualification through activation.']
  contract_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to contract.agreement. Business justification: Partner onboarding is governed by a formal contract agreement. Currently has contract_reference_number (string) but needs proper FK for referential integrity. The contract_reference_number can remain ']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the partner entity being onboarded (carrier, agent, 3PL, correspondent office). Links to the master partner record in the network domain.']
  reactivation_partner_onboarding_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on partner_onboarding (reactivation_partner_onboarding_id)']
  actual_activation_date date [note: 'Actual date when the partner was activated and began operational activities. Nullable until activation is completed.']
  api_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of API connectivity setup and testing. Tracks technical integration for real-time system-to-system communication.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when final approval for partner activation was granted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Final approval status for partner activation. Indicates whether the partner has been approved to go live.']
  certification_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of industry certification verification (ISO, AEO, C-TPAT, dangerous goods, etc.). Confirms partner holds required certifications.']
  certification_verified_date date [note: 'Date when required certifications were verified.']
  contract_execution_date date [note: 'Date when the partnership contract was formally executed by all parties. Marks legal commitment.']
  contract_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed partnership contract document. Links onboarding to the legal agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this onboarding record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  due_diligence_completed_date date [note: 'Date when comprehensive due diligence checks (financial, operational, compliance) were completed and approved.']
  edi_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of EDI connectivity setup and testing. Tracks technical integration for automated data exchange.']
  financial_assessment_date date [note: 'Date when the financial assessment was completed.']
  financial_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Status of the financial viability and creditworthiness assessment. Evaluates partner financial stability and risk.']
  geographic_scope_authorized varchar [note: 'Geographic regions or countries the partner is authorized to operate in. Defines territorial scope of the partnership.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the partner officially went live and began processing live transactions. Marks operational commencement.']
  initiated_date date [note: 'Date when the onboarding process was formally initiated. Marks the beginning of the partner onboarding lifecycle.']
  insurance_verification_status varchar [note: 'Status of insurance coverage verification. Confirms partner has adequate liability, cargo, and other required insurance.']
  insurance_verified_date date [note: 'Date when insurance coverage was verified and approved.']
  kyc_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of the Know Your Customer compliance check. Verifies partner identity, ownership structure, and legitimacy.']
  kyc_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the KYC compliance check was completed and approved.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this onboarding record was last updated. Audit trail for record changes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special conditions, escalations, or other relevant information about the onboarding process.']
  onboarding_duration_days varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days from initiation to activation. Used for process performance measurement and continuous improvement.']
  onboarding_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for this onboarding case. Used for tracking and communication with the partner and internal stakeholders.']
  onboarding_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the onboarding workflow. Represents the specific phase of the structured onboarding process the partner is currently in. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pre_qualification|due_diligence|contract_negotiation|contract_execution|system_integration|operational_readiness|go_live|post_activation — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the onboarding process. Tracks the current state from initiation through completion or termination.']
  onboarding_type varchar [note: 'Type of onboarding activity. Distinguishes between new partner onboarding, expansion of existing partner capabilities, or reactivation of dormant partnerships.']
  operational_readiness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score (0-100) assessing the partner readiness across all onboarding dimensions. Used for go/no-go decision making.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the partner being onboarded. Determines the onboarding workflow, compliance requirements, and integration scope.']
  pre_qualification_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the pre-qualification stage was completed. Indicates the partner met initial screening criteria.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation if the onboarding was rejected or cancelled. Captures business rationale for non-activation.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment of the partner based on due diligence, financial assessment, and compliance checks. Influences monitoring and oversight requirements.']
  sanctions_screening_date date [note: 'Date when sanctions screening was performed and cleared.']
  sanctions_screening_status varchar [note: 'Status of sanctions and denied party screening. Ensures partner is not on restricted or prohibited lists.']
  service_modes_authorized varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of transport modes the partner is authorized to operate (air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal). Defines operational scope.']
  system_integration_completed_date date [note: 'Date when technical system integration (EDI, API, TMS connectivity) was completed and tested successfully.']
  target_activation_date date [note: 'Planned date for the partner to go live and begin operational activities. Used for project planning and stakeholder communication.']
  tms_integration_status varchar [note: 'Status of TMS system integration. Tracks connectivity to core transportation management platform for order and shipment processing.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking the end-to-end onboarding lifecycle of a new network partner (carrier, agent, 3PL, correspondent office) from initial qualification through activation. Captures partner reference, onboarding type, onboarding stage (pre-qualification, due diligence, contract execution, system integration, go-live), stage completion dates, responsible onboarding manager, compliance checks completed (KYC, sanctions screening, certification verification), IT integration status (EDI, API), and overall onboarding status. Ensures structured and auditable partner onboarding.'
}

Table network.partner_settlement {
  partner_settlement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner settlement transaction. Primary key for the partner settlement record.']
  interline_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the interline agreement governing the prorate methodology and revenue split for this settlement, if applicable.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network partner (carrier, agent, 3PL provider) for whom this settlement is being processed.']
  reversed_partner_settlement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on partner_settlement (reversed_partner_settlement_id)']
  actual_payment_date date [note: 'Actual date on which payment was successfully made to the partner, as confirmed by the payment system.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement was formally approved for payment by authorized personnel.']
  awb_count varchar [note: 'Number of air waybills included in this settlement period for interline traffic settlements.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Partner bank account number to which the settlement payment is made. Masked or encrypted for security.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Name of the financial institution holding the partner account for settlement payment.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center or business unit code responsible for this settlement expense or revenue allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this settlement record was first created in the system.']
  deduction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of deductions applied to the gross settlement, including penalties, chargebacks, claims offsets, and other adjustments.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner has raised a dispute or disagreement regarding this settlement transaction.']
  dispute_raised_date date [note: 'Date on which the partner formally raised a dispute regarding this settlement.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute raised by the partner, including details of the discrepancy or disagreement.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which the dispute was resolved and both parties agreed on the final settlement terms.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied when converting from original currency to settlement currency, if applicable.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'General ledger account code to which this settlement transaction is posted for financial accounting purposes.']
  gross_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total settlement amount before any deductions, adjustments, or withholdings are applied.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this settlement record was last updated or modified.']
  net_settlement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final settlement amount payable to the partner after all deductions, adjustments, and withholdings have been applied. Calculated as gross amount minus deductions minus tax withholding.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information regarding this settlement transaction.']
  original_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction currency before any currency conversion was applied.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number or transaction identifier from the payment system used to track and reconcile the payment.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from settlement approval date within which payment must be made to the partner, as per contractual agreement.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date on which the settlement was successfully reconciled and confirmed by both parties.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of the reconciliation process indicating whether the settlement has been matched and verified against partner records and source transactions.']
  scheduled_payment_date date [note: 'Target date on which payment is scheduled to be made to the partner based on payment terms and approval date.']
  settlement_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the settlement is denominated and will be paid.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Payment mechanism used to settle the financial obligation with the partner. CASS refers to IATA Cargo Accounts Settlement System. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cass|bank_transfer|wire_transfer|ach|netting|offset|check — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  settlement_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this settlement (e.g., end of the month or billing cycle).']
  settlement_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this settlement (e.g., beginning of the month or billing cycle).']
  settlement_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique reference number for this settlement transaction, used for partner communication and reconciliation.']
  settlement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the settlement transaction indicating its processing stage and payment state. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_payment|paid|disputed|reconciled|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  settlement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the settlement transaction indicating the nature of the financial arrangement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: interline_prorate|agent_commission|3pl_service_fee|capacity_penalty|handling_charge|fuel_surcharge|volume_incentive|performance_bonus|demurrage_charge|detention_charge — promote to reference product]']
  shipment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of shipments or consignments included in this settlement calculation.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the partner bank for international wire transfers.']
  tax_withholding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of tax withheld at source as required by local tax regulations or international tax treaties.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume in cubic meters for all shipments included in this settlement.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total chargeable weight in kilograms for all shipments included in this settlement.']

  Note: 'Transactional record managing financial settlement between Transport Shipping and network partners for interline traffic, capacity utilization, agent commissions, and 3PL service fees. Captures partner reference, settlement period, settlement type (interline prorate, agent commission, 3PL service fee, capacity penalty), gross amount, deductions, net settlement amount, currency, settlement method (CASS, bank transfer, netting), settlement date, and reconciliation status. Enables accurate partner financial settlement and dispute resolution.'
}

Table network.partner_sla {
  partner_sla_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the partner service level agreement record.']
  agent_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the agent if the partner is an agent entity.']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the carrier if the partner is a carrier entity.']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network partner (carrier, agent, 3PL provider) to whom this SLA applies.']
  superseded_partner_sla_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on partner_sla (superseded_partner_sla_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which this partner SLA was formally approved and authorized.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SLA automatically renews upon expiry (True) or requires explicit renewal action (False).']
  breach_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Performance level at which the SLA is considered breached, triggering penalties or remediation actions.']
  contract_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent contract or agreement document under which this SLA is governed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner SLA record was first created in the system.']
  destination_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or country code for the destination scope of this SLA.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this partner SLA commitment becomes active and enforceable.']
  escalation_procedure varchar [note: 'Description of the escalation process to be followed when SLA performance issues or breaches occur.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which this partner SLA commitment expires or is scheduled for renewal. Nullable for open-ended SLAs.']
  incentive_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary incentive or bonus amount awarded for exceeding SLA performance targets.']
  incentive_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract clause or document section that defines incentives or bonuses for exceeding SLA targets.']
  incentive_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the incentive amount.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this partner SLA record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal SLA review meeting or assessment.']
  measurement_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the SLA performance is measured and reported (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, per shipment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: real_time|daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly|per_shipment|per_transaction — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  measurement_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which performance is aggregated for SLA evaluation (e.g., rolling 30-day average).']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the performance metric (e.g., percentage, hours, days, count).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal SLA review meeting or assessment.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special conditions related to this partner SLA.']
  origin_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or country code for the origin scope of this SLA.']
  penalty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty amount applied per SLA breach occurrence or per measurement period.']
  penalty_clause_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract clause or document section that defines penalties for SLA breach.']
  penalty_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the penalty amount.']
  performance_metric varchar [note: 'Specific metric or Key Performance Indicator (KPI) being measured under this SLA (e.g., on-time delivery percentage, average transit days, documentation error rate).']
  renewal_notice_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required before SLA renewal or termination.']
  reporting_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which SLA performance reports are provided to stakeholders (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly).']
  review_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which the SLA terms and performance are formally reviewed with the partner (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annually).']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Type of service to which this SLA applies (e.g., express, standard, economy, Full Truckload (FTL), Less Than Truckload (LTL), Full Container Load (FCL), Less Than Container Load (LCL)). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: express|standard|economy|freight|ltl|ftl|fcl|lcl — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sla_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name or title of the partner SLA commitment.']
  sla_reference_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique reference number or code for this partner SLA commitment.']
  sla_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the partner SLA (e.g., active, suspended, expired, terminated, draft, under review).']
  sla_type varchar [note: 'Category of operational performance standard governed by this SLA. Defines the specific performance dimension being measured (e.g., transit time, pickup compliance, documentation accuracy, claims response time, capacity fulfillment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: transit_time|pickup_compliance|documentation_accuracy|claims_response_time|capacity_fulfillment|delivery_performance|tracking_update_frequency|customs_clearance_time — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'Target performance threshold that the partner is committed to achieve. Represents the agreed-upon service level standard.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which this partner SLA was terminated or cancelled, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why this partner SLA was terminated.']
  trade_lane varchar [note: 'Specific trade lane or route corridor to which this SLA applies (e.g., Asia-Europe, Trans-Pacific, North America-Latin America).']
  transport_mode varchar [note: 'Mode of transportation to which this SLA applies (e.g., air, ocean, road, rail, multimodal). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: air|ocean|road|rail|multimodal|courier|parcel — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  warning_threshold decimal(18,4) [note: 'Performance level that triggers a warning notification before a full SLA breach occurs. Allows proactive intervention.']

  Note: 'Master record defining the service level agreement commitments agreed with network partners (carriers, agents, 3PL providers) governing operational performance standards. Captures partner reference, SLA type (transit time, pickup compliance, documentation accuracy, claims response time, capacity fulfillment), target metric, measurement unit, threshold values (target, warning, breach), measurement frequency, penalty or incentive clause reference, effective date, and SLA status. Distinct from contract.sla_commitment (which governs customer-facing SLAs) — this is the partner-facing operational SLA.'
}

Table network.edi_connection {
  edi_connection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the EDI or API integration connection record. Primary key.']
  fallback_edi_connection_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on edi_connection (fallback_edi_connection_id)']
  partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the network partner (carrier, agent, or third-party logistics provider) for whom this EDI connection is established.']
  acknowledgment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the partner is required to send functional acknowledgments (e.g., CONTRL, 997) for messages received through this connection.']
  authentication_method varchar [note: 'Method used to authenticate and authorize access to the partner\'s EDI or API endpoint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: BASIC_AUTH|OAUTH2|API_KEY|CERTIFICATE|SSH_KEY|USERNAME_PASSWORD|TOKEN|NONE — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  average_daily_message_volume varchar [note: 'Average number of messages exchanged per day through this connection, used for capacity planning and performance monitoring.']
  compression_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether message compression is enabled to reduce bandwidth usage and improve transmission efficiency.']
  connection_health_status varchar [note: 'Current health status of the EDI connection based on recent transmission success rates, latency, and error patterns. Used for proactive monitoring and alerting.']
  connection_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the EDI connection, typically including partner name and connection type for easy identification.']
  connection_reference_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique reference number or code for this EDI connection, used for operational tracking and support.']
  connection_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the EDI connection. Reflects the current operational state from initial setup through active use to eventual termination.']
  connection_type varchar [note: 'Type of electronic integration connection established with the partner. Includes traditional EDI standards (TRADACOMS, EDIFACT, X12) and modern API protocols (REST, SOAP) as well as file transfer methods (SFTP, AS2, FTP). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EDI_TRADACOMS|EDI_EDIFACT|EDI_X12|API_REST|API_SOAP|SFTP|AS2|FTP|FTPS — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDI connection record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this EDI connection configuration expires or is scheduled for decommissioning. Null indicates an open-ended connection.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this EDI connection configuration is valid and authorized for use.']
  encryption_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether message encryption is enabled for this connection to protect data in transit.']
  encryption_protocol varchar [note: 'Encryption protocol or standard used to secure messages transmitted through this connection (e.g., TLS 1.2, TLS 1.3, PGP, S/MIME).']
  endpoint_host varchar [note: 'Hostname or IP address of the partner\'s EDI or API server. Confidential business integration detail.']
  endpoint_port varchar [note: 'Network port number used for the EDI or API connection (e.g., 443 for HTTPS, 22 for SFTP).']
  endpoint_url varchar [note: 'The URL or network address of the partner\'s EDI or API endpoint where messages are sent or received. Confidential business integration detail.']
  go_live_date date [note: 'Date when the EDI connection was activated for production use and began processing live business transactions.']
  interchange_control_reference_prefix varchar [note: 'Prefix or pattern used for generating unique interchange control numbers in EDI message headers for this connection.']
  last_failed_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent failed message transmission attempt, used for troubleshooting and alerting.']
  last_health_check_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent automated health check performed on this connection.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this EDI connection record was most recently updated.']
  last_successful_transmission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful message transmission through this connection, used for health monitoring.']
  max_retry_attempts varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times the system will attempt to retransmit a failed message before escalating to manual intervention.']
  message_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this connection is used for sending messages to the partner (outbound), receiving messages from the partner (inbound), or both (bidirectional).']
  message_format varchar [note: 'Data format or serialization method used for messages exchanged through this connection.']
  message_types_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of message types or transaction sets supported by this connection (e.g., IFTMBF booking confirmation, IFTSTA status update, AWB data, manifest, invoice, DESADV despatch advice).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional information, special configuration details, known issues, or operational notes related to this EDI connection.']
  peak_message_volume varchar [note: 'Maximum number of messages processed in a single day through this connection, representing peak capacity utilization.']
  production_environment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the production environment connection. Indicates whether the connection is actively processing live business transactions.']
  protocol_version varchar [note: 'Version of the EDI standard or API protocol being used for this connection (e.g., EDIFACT D.96A, X12 4010, REST API v2.1).']
  receiver_identifier varchar [note: 'EDI receiver identification code or qualifier assigned to the partner for inbound messages (e.g., partner\'s SCAC code, IATA code, GLN, DUNS number).']
  retry_policy varchar [note: 'Description of the retry logic and parameters applied when message transmission fails (e.g., number of retries, backoff intervals).']
  sender_identifier varchar [note: 'EDI sender identification code or qualifier used by Transport Shipping in outbound messages to this partner (e.g., SCAC code, IATA code, GLN, DUNS number).']
  sla_response_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum response time in seconds agreed with the partner for message acknowledgment or processing, as defined in the integration SLA.']
  sla_uptime_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum uptime percentage committed for this connection as defined in the integration SLA (e.g., 99.9%).']
  technical_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary technical contact at the partner organization for EDI support and troubleshooting.']
  technical_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary technical contact at the partner organization responsible for this EDI connection.']
  technical_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary technical contact at the partner organization for urgent EDI issues.']
  test_environment_endpoint_url varchar [note: 'The URL or network address of the partner\'s test/sandbox EDI or API endpoint used for integration testing before production go-live.']
  test_environment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the test/sandbox environment connection for this partner integration. Indicates whether test connectivity and message exchange have been successfully validated.']
  timeout_seconds varchar [note: 'Maximum time in seconds to wait for a response from the partner endpoint before considering the transmission failed.']

  Note: 'Master record for electronic data interchange (EDI) and API integration connections established with network partners for automated message exchange. Captures partner reference, connection type (EDI TRADACOMS, EDIFACT, X12, API REST, SFTP), message types supported (booking confirmation, status update, AWB data, manifest, invoice), connection endpoint details, protocol version, test and production environment status, go-live date, message volume, and connection health status. Manages the digital integration layer of the partner network.'
}
